### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-26T01:52:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kickstarter-promoting-light-could-care-less-about-emf.17089/post-195362>

look at the emission spectra carefully and then listen to April 2016 again and then consider all the data in Roeland van Wijk book on light emission from cells. Why do all cells release ELF-UV?

Evolution is physics in motion. From the mixing of motion and quantum mechanics emerges the a new emergent concept we now refer to as epigenetics. Epigenetics, not natural selection is driving evolution forward. This is completely consistent with what Darwin believed in 1859, but it completely at odds with Modern Synthesis of net-darwinism. It is a huge point, because modern medicine is broken because of net-darwinism. We continue to look inside the nuclear genome and avoid looking at the mitochondrial genome. The mitochondrial genome is where epigenetic acts to speed up or slow down energy flows which speed up of slow down circadian clock genes that are located in front of every human gene in the nuclear genome. These genes are swimming in cell water (EZ) which has a constantly varying of flickering in the hydrogen bonding network around DNA/RNA and in between the two helices. Light frequencies are the levers that turn the gears of water to make life possible. They key it appears in cells is to slow light down. When we slow light down the circadian clock genes in front of every human gene can become synched to act as the ideal metronome for creation of life.

Cells are transparent for one reason. Optics. All stressed cells emit uncolored ELF-UV. All transparent substances show some dispersion in the visible spectrum, and, if uncolored, have a higher index of refraction for purple (UV) then the red light. This difference implies that higher visible frequencies are slowed down more by the medium than are the red frequencies. This explains why cells are designed to release UV light, it also explains why they EZ has a higher refraction index than bulk water, and why red light penetrates tissues 10-30 cm’s to work directly on the ATPase to run it as a quantum torque nanomachine. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4701522/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4701522/)

# Thread: Down the Rabbit Hole I Go... Again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/down-the-rabbit-hole-i-go-again.17091/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jwhb77 — 2015-11-04T07:22:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/down-the-rabbit-hole-i-go-again.17091/post-181115>

Hi Folks,

I just wanted to introduce myself, my name is Wil. I just finished Dr. Kruse's EpiRx book for the first time, and it really blew my mind! I will no doubt be reading through it again (SLOWLY this time), and I'm hoping my joining the forum here will help me as I wade into the deeper waters of its content.

I have some background in the sciences, though my university degree is in Letters & Philosophy. As I was finishing up at the university, my father began showing the signs of early-onset Alzheimer's disease, and I ended up spending the next 8 years of my life helping my mother care for him in their home. After he passed away, I decided I would go on to nursing school... determined to help people who were going through what my family had endured during that time.

Three years, all of the nursing prerequisite sciences, & one semester of nursing school later, I crashed & burned... not fully sure what happened or when it happened, but my health & my cognitive abilities plummeted. I knew intuitively something was very wrong, and it was interfering with my ability to plan, remember, & even basic day-to-day functioning. I'd been diagnosed with ADHD/ Inattentive type back in undergrad, but it was nothing on the scale of what I was experiencing. So I reluctantly withdrew from my nursing program until I could get a handle on & do something about whatever it was that was going on with me.

Over this past year & a half, I've seen more doctors than I can count on two hands... most were content with the status quo that just wasn't working to get me where I want/need to be. So, I started digging deeper & doing my own research. I started seeing an MD that specialies in integrative medicine & nutrigenomics. She has helped me a lot... after much testing, we uncovered toxicity from heavy metals in my system & especially from mercury. I've been doing chelation therapy ever since, using DMPS & EDTA mainly, along with a slew of nutritional supplements, and we've managed to get my mercury levels down to within the lab reference range... still working on getting lead & aluminum down, but getting close to the rr's on those too.

Still facing a lot of the cognitive issues that forced me out of school, & praying that they don't develop into what my Dad went through. In the meantime, in my search for more answers, I encountered Jack's material online, bought his EpiRx, & have started implementing the diet a bit at a time. I've been eating mostly Paleo-style for almost 3 years, but hardly any seafood before the past few weeks. Haven't started any CT yet, but looking to make that happen someday soon.

Anyway, I better stop myself before this gets to be a novel... that's me, in a nutshell. I welcome any comments or input, & I'm certain I'll be plying the forum with lots of questions before long. My thanks to Dr. Kruse for sharing his wealth of knowledge with us all, & my thanks to anyone who's read this far...

Blessings,
Wil

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-04T15:11:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/down-the-rabbit-hole-i-go-again.17091/post-181135>

One bit of early advice that is complex........and you may not understand it until you read more of the blog regarding mercury. I have been recommending black light for a long time to my members but you need to be careful with it if you are mitigating Hg toxicity.

Hg light is complex answer because of how complex light is. The reason is Hg attunement by resonance. Man has changed the source of the sun for its species by addicting itself to indoor light over sun light. This has huge implications.

I only tell folks to avoid mercury vapor fluorescent bulbs if they have a very well documented mercury toxicity. Hg bulbs are a light source that can act as a sender of resonant energy. Hg in your body can act as the receiver of resonant energy if it is present. This is one of the reasons I am not in favor of amalgam removal for most people. It seems counterintuitive to my core beliefs, but let me explain. In its free form, Hg easily vaporizes at room temperatures. It likes to find harbor in fatty tissues like When Hg is charged by current is has a spectral array that give two lines in the violet at 404.66 And 407.79, one in the indigo range at 435.84, one in turquoise at 490.05, one in lemon at 546.08 and two in the yellow range at 576.92 and 579.03. It binds to sulfur enzymes to remove them from use and interferes with ECT directly by lowering energy production. I am not worried about Hg resonance with indoor lights because UV light does not penetrate our surfaces deeply as the picture shows.

But I worry about it a lot in dental reconstructions because most biologic dentists are completely unaware of the risks of resonant light transfer from fluorescent or LED bulbs in their dental lights, offices, or their oral work lamps. The source of their light in CFL bulbs is due to Hg electrification. LED bulbs have a massive high color temperature that presents other issues. And then there is the flicker effect of both bulbs no one seems to talk about.

If you have Hg toxicity and they are going to work on you in this kind of lighted environment, you should request a red lit environment when undergoing removal of amalgams. Their offices are often built with blue/white LED and fluorescent lights with high color temperatures. This is akin to putting a match to gasoline with respect to Hg generated lighting.

Blue light can penetrate into fat layers where Hg lies, and dentists wear lamps on their heads that have extreme blue spikes at 435 nm-465nm with high color temperatures that can photoelectrically energize Hg in your tissues. This risk is much greater with blue artificial light because it penetrates far deeper that UVA light can as the picture above shows. Hg is rarely found in surfaces of the body. There is speculation it maybe in the retina, but if true I should be dead considering how much DHA is in my RPE. For ten years I have followed this effect in many of my own patients and not one has developed a toxicity in their retina. In fact most of their eye complaints improve. This tells me a lot about blue light being the worse EMF we face............

Food for thought as you learn more.

# Thread: Metal Clothing and Back-reflecting RF-EMF: Potential Biohack fail?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-clothing-and-back-reflecting-rf-emf-potential-biohack-fail.17093/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-11-04T23:39:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-clothing-and-back-reflecting-rf-emf-potential-biohack-fail.17093/post-181166>

New thoughts about back-reflecting RF...

I'm very curious about the field-interactions when metal or metal-clothing is within an electrical or magnetic EMF. Can anyone point me to anything?

It hit me that if a person were to be inside of an electrical or magnetic EMF, and also wearing RF-shielding (like a copper-thread shirt and hat) to back-reflect RF, is it possible that the electric/magnetic EMFs might interact more with the clothing to draw undesired energy into those body at those locations?

Thx!

### Jonathin — 2015-11-08T02:26:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-clothing-and-back-reflecting-rf-emf-potential-biohack-fail.17093/post-181359>

I just read about the up and coming 6G specs. The world is going to be even scarier if that gets implemented.

How about measuring body voltage before and during the wearing of the metal duds? Maybe a full body "wetsuit" that you can keep moist to block emf. Probably not a good fashion look, tho.

### cygnario — 2016-01-07T10:38:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-clothing-and-back-reflecting-rf-emf-potential-biohack-fail.17093/post-185629>

The problem with RF protective clothing is that unless you make a faraday cage (whole body covered from head to toe), the RF will reflect off _either_ sides of the cloth, meaning that if you do it incorrectly the waves will enter your body through a gap that isn't well covered and will start bouncing around on your insides. In other words, you're constantly microwaving yourself. That's probably also the reason why people feel worse after trying it. You have to be very careful and really know what you are doing.

### Scompy — 2016-01-07T19:00:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-clothing-and-back-reflecting-rf-emf-potential-biohack-fail.17093/post-185665>

In other words, you're constantly microwaving yourself. That's probably also the reason why people feel worse after trying it. You have to be very careful and really know what you are doing.

Yeah, was getting sick-feeling after about 2 weeks of doing it, plus gained 5+ pounds in that period of time even though I was eating the same, actually eating even better! I swithed over to RF-absorbing clothing and wear a thick, hoodie jacket while at work, then strip down when I go outside.

### Scompy — 2016-01-09T00:06:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-clothing-and-back-reflecting-rf-emf-potential-biohack-fail.17093/post-185805>

> [lilreddgirl said:](/goto/post?id=185787)
>
> Scompy what material is your RF absorbing clothing made from?

It's a hoodie with a thicker, inner lining of meshed cotton/fleece. It also breathes well, so I don't get too warm in it. I based choosing it on my own data. I used my cornet to test about 4 jackets in the past (using a constant location away from my Xbox's RF-EMF), and found this jacket reduced the nnEMF the most. I will test more jackets, jeans and clothing with denim/polyesters mixtures to see the differences in readings. One thing I look out for at the store--hoodies with plastic zippers instead of metal ones. As time goes on, I'll have to test new clothing materials that might block even better.

However, I think it depends. I have heard that EHS (Electro Hypersensitiviy Syndrome) patients (who suffer miserably from even the ever so slightest EMF exposure-- they are our canaries in the coal mine) have gotten relief wearing these body shielding clothing and suits. They are even able to go out in public areas without crippling symptoms.

However, 5G millimeter waveforms are likely a game changer for existing shielding. Now, it's time for me to crawl back into my burrow.

I had to fly this past week (microwave dungeon with nearly every passenger around me on cell with bluetooth headphones an easy 15k micros from every direction versus about 1.5k micros behind my clothing) and first time since I began my mitodoka journey my Bristol chart wasn't ruined the day after. Also wearing a skull cap now instead of the hoodie so the microwaves going through my face can exit out my nape or vice versa.

I recommend being careful about exposing emf meters on flights now. One captive idiot (wearing bluetooth headphones of course) sitting across the aisle from me thought my Trifield was a bomb detonator and alerted the flight attendant about me being a terrorist. The look of fear in his face was almost equally matched by my anger that a phone zombie was going to create a serious hassle.

### Saichi — 2019-09-29T19:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-clothing-and-back-reflecting-rf-emf-potential-biohack-fail.17093/post-279205>

### Pawel Wypychowski — 2019-09-29T21:29:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-clothing-and-back-reflecting-rf-emf-potential-biohack-fail.17093/post-279210>

Shielding clothing (made of shielding fabrics – usually thin copper or silver threads) in RF and Microwave range works predominantly by reflection, although reflection decreases with frequency, while absorption increases. At higher frequencies, like millimeter waves in 5G, absorption loss will be much more significant. The effect of the shield is a combination of reflection and absorption in the shield material. That is for the shield which is relatively well sealed electromagnetically – that means no gaps larger than 1/20 of the wavelength (i.e., about 6 mm = a quarter of an inch for Wi-Fi 2,4 GHz). For shielding clothing it is impossible to execute such a shield, so the performance of the shield will be significantly affected by unavoidable gaps. Nevertheless, my measurement shows the body gets 10 to 50 times reduction of radiation (power density) when measured under covered parts (i.e. chest area under shielding shirt or hoody, or genital area covered with shielding shorts). Does that help? Yes. Is it enough to mitigate RF and microwave radiation impact on your health long term? Absolutely no. Shielding clothing should not be considered a solution, but rather a damage limitation armor when entering the area of high radiation for limited time.

There is another big electromagnetic problem with shielding clothing, which is mostly overlooked by everyone. Our skin is not evolutionarily designed to be short-circuited by conductive clothing, and this is precisely what most of the shielding fabrics do. Years of experience in working with electrosensitive individuals and my experiments led me to use, recommend and offer shielding clothes made of carefully chosen shielding fabrics, constructed in a way, that there is no electrical contact between the metallic threads and the skin. The other electromagnetic drama of shielding clothing is pretty apparent – when used, it blinds your skin to see The Light. This one alone should be enough to understand that shielding clothing is only a temporary armor. Yes, it may help limit damages temporarily if well designed. Is it long term solution for your health and vitality in “modern” world? No.

Thx for one of the best discussions on shielding clothing.

I agree it is only a stopgap mitigation solution, principally for EHS individuals who would otherwise suffer terribly or be confined or housebound.

I am still waiting for someone to do an exhaustive study of shielding clothing using sensitive RF and body voltage meters to get some hard numbers evidence of what really happens in body under shielding fabrics. The problem of RF leakage around these fabrics and RF penetration from different angles into body is worrisome.

Hopefully one day a genius will design a dense carbon-based cloth fabric that will only safely absorb and not reflect radiation. That is the holy grail.

# Thread: NASA's new EM Drive (Electromagnetic Drive) - The electormagnetic force is primordial
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nasas-new-em-drive-electromagnetic-drive-the-electormagnetic-force-is-primordial.17096/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeff Power — 2015-11-05T22:58:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nasas-new-em-drive-electromagnetic-drive-the-electormagnetic-force-is-primordial.17096/post-181231>

Interesting article, if proved true, it appears once again science is lacking in understanding the laws of the universe in many respects. This EM Drive is defying the laws of the universe as it is classically known in mainstream science. Maybe this some how ties back in to what Jack has said about the only true primordial force being the electromagnetic force. Gravity is only emergent.

**
EM Drive is reportedly still producing thrust after another round of NASA testing
The "impossible" engine continues to surprise.**

FIONA MACDONALD
5 NOV 2015
[the past few months about the EM Drive](http://www.sciencealert.com/independent-scientists-confirm-that-the-impossible-em-drive-produces-thrust) – an "impossible" engine that, if functional, could supposedly power a spacecraft to Mars in just [10 weeks, without any rocket fuel](http://www.sciencealert.com/nasa-has-trialled-an-engine-that-would-take-us-to-Mars-in-10-weeks).

It's pretty easy to understand the appeal. Science can't explain how the engine could possibly work and yet, somehow, throughout a range of tests at NASA's Eagleworks Laboratory, the engine reportedly continues to produce thrust. But here's the catch – so far none of these results have been peer-reviewed, and it can't be ruled out that the thrust isn't the result of some type of experimental error. So for now, we remain skeptical.

But in the meantime, Paul Mach, one of the principal investigators at the Eagleworks Lab, has provided the first public update about their tests on the EM Drive in months, and has admitted that the team has upgraded their experimental protocol and mitigated some of the errors that people were concerned about in prior tests.

"And yet the anomalous thrust signals remain..." Mach [wrote on the NASASpaceFlight forums.](http://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=38577.msg1440938#msg1440938)

His comment was in response to an unpublished paper, which claimed that the propulsion seen in earlier trials of the EM Drive were a result of Lorentz force – which means the force generated by interactions between the EM Drive and the Earth's magnetic field.

[Mach explains](http://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=38577.msg1440938#msg1440938) that he can't comment on this work in detail or provide any photos, as his lab is in the process of getting a peer-reviewed paper published, but that he can shed some light on the issue.

“I will tell you that we first built and installed a 2nd generation, closed face magnetic damper that reduced the stray magnetic fields in the vacuum chamber by at least an order of magnitude and any Lorentz force interactions it could produce,” [wrote Mach.](http://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=38577.msg1440938#msg1440938)

[He also admits](http://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=38577.msg1440938#msg1440938) that there are still traces of contamination caused by thermal expansion in the system, and the team is now developing an advanced analytics tool to try to work out where this is coming from.

The Em Drive stands for electromagnetic propulsion drive, and the reason it's so controversial is that it defies one of the fundamental concepts of physics – the conservation of momentum, which states that for something to be propelled forward (like a spacecraft), some kind of propellant (like fuel) needs to be pushed out in the opposite direction.

But the EM Drive, which was invented by English researcher Roger Shawyer in the early 2000s, works [in theory by bouncing microwaves back and forth inside](http://www.sciencealert.com/independent-scientists-confirm-that-the-impossible-em-drive-produces-thrust)an enclosed chamber in order to generate thrust.

And so far a range of scientists, including the NASA team, have shown that the engine "is producing a force that is not attributable to any classical electromagnetic phenomenon," as the Eagleworks researchers [wrote in an unpublished paper last year](http://ntrs.nasa.gov/search.jsp?R=20140006052). In other words, it seems to work, but they can't explain why.

So far, this thrust has only been produced in incredibly small amounts, but given the fact that the theoretical system – once scaled up – would be able to power a spacecraft without the need for heavy rocket fuel on board, that's not an issue. The EM Drive is still predicted to be able to carry humans great distances in[incredibly short periods of time.](http://www.nasaspaceflight.com/2015/04/evaluating-nasas-futuristic-em-drive/)

Of course, that's all dependent on the thrust the scientists are detecting being an actual result of the EM Drive's bouncing microwaves, and not an experimental artefact. And despite reports that more and more stringent testing is being conducted to rule this out, we still need to see some results in peer review.

So please, Eagleworks scientists, while we love your interaction on the forums and the feedback, get cracking with the journal articles and those replicable results, we beg of you, because we really would love something concrete to go on sometime soon!

[http://www.sciencealert.com/the-em-...ious-thrust-after-another-round-of-nasa-tests](http://www.sciencealert.com/the-em-drive-still-producing-mysterious-thrust-after-another-round-of-nasa-tests)

# Thread: A Newborn Story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-newborn-story.17097/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-11-06T19:06:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-newborn-story.17097/post-181277>

Yesterday walking through a parking lot to get my lunch, I saw two new mothers holding their newborns in front-slings. That was pleasant to see, until I realized both of them were on their cells talking to different people. So the mothers were holding their phones in their hands away from their heads--perfectly placing the cell phones a few inches from their babies' heads. I shook my head knowing the damage going on, only hoping that the mothers were acting more as an insulator for their babies. But what really made my internal-blood-boil was that after I got my lunch 15mins later, there they were again standing around talking on their cell phones.

So here's the question...Should I have interrupted their phone calls and given them a quick public service announcement by:

1\. Interrupting them and saying, "Hi, you have a beautiful child...You also might want to go online later and research what cell phone EMFs can do to the mylen in the brain to reduce autism and other risks. Have a nice day."

2\. Or just keep walking.

Do we have a moral obligation to share knowledge to the clueless when it **doesn't** present itself? I'm thinking no. But if the situation **does** present itself and if I would have gotten eye contact, I would have turned on my 'cheery-fun' mode attempting to relate their children to my own and then given them the nugget of truth.

Then it becomes up to the individual to either dismiss what was heard or take it in as a possibility, and if that nugget is seeded, take-action. In this situation, I ended up walking by because they were so involved with their cell conversations. I was hoping one of them would see me and it would be an invitation so I could tell them what they were doing. In part, it was the cell phone's built-in ability to distract that may give her child a neurological disease in the future.

### Linz — 2015-11-06T19:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-newborn-story.17097/post-181279>

Hi Scompy,

I was in a queue in the chemist recently and overheard a request for advice and otc medication for IBS. I didn't think I could talk to the customer but when it was my turn to speak to the chemist I suggested next time she might recommend turning off the blue lights after dark and the wi-fi. She was very interested! I think there is just about enough public information about shift work and living in a constant summer causing diabetes etc that people do stop and listen if they have a little awareness already.
Seem to remember Caroline has a great way of talking to young lads with phones in their pockets!

we have a new baby in the family, on DH's side. Mom wears baby, and puts the phone in her bra btwn her and baby. or will but the phone in the pocket in the front of the baby carrier.

I will say I too made similar errors as a new mom with both of my kids letting them play with my phone or ipad. But I know better now.

As for letting them know, we didn't in our family - we're to crazy with our orange glasses at family get togethers... and not letting our kids use devices... they think its ok, and even when we've explained the risks previously, they've told us we "know nothing"

in the end... we'll be standing... can't say much about the rest of humanity...

### Scompy — 2015-12-31T04:40:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-newborn-story.17097/post-185143>

New Story to tell.

Today, I went to Old Navy not realizing it was a mistake before I got in line because everyone was coming into the store to use their after Xmas card presents. Anyway, I got in a LONG line that was 15mins long. The line was filled with people on their cell phones, including children ages about 5, 7, and 10 also playing games on cell phones. Only myself and a nice Asian man in front me were the only two people in that entire line under strong fluorescent lights that didn't have an electrical device. I was totally biohacked in-line, heavy EMF absorbing coat and pants and back-reflecting EMF cap to also help with the overhead lights.

As I scanned the massive line, everyone suddenly looked up from their cell phones. It was like a bunch of sheep that just heard the bark of a dog. It's not what you think. It was a small girl probably less than a year and a half sitting in her mother's shopping basket. The girl screamed out in pure terror because mommy took away the cell phone from her. Mommy wanted to go online herself to scan her own stuff while waiting so long, so baby was now infuriated. This baby girl had a massive skin-condition all over her body--dry, flakey skin, and her face was beat-red with splotches. In my mind I was thinking, "She could really use some massive rehydration." The girl wouldn't stop yellilng... she continued to scream and scream and scream to no end.

This next bit was classic, in a face-palm kind of way. I knew what was coming. Mommy decides to grab bottle to give to baby. Baby does a non-verbal reaction to shove back the bottle in total disgust. After she pushed the bottle aside, she went to grab the cell phone back from mom. Then baby screams out in total horror that she can't have her EMF, blue-screen generating toy. Mother continues to 'act cool' like nothing is going on. Meanwhile, everyone in-line goes back to looking down to their cell phones trying to ignore the situation at hand, except for an older grandmother that says in a whisper to a daughter in-line behind me, "Oh, she must be getting close to her 'terrible 2s' right now" (in reference to the baby screaming in fury).

About 2 more minutes of this, the mother trying to juggle and put everything else possible into the baby's grasp, nothing will calm down the kid. Finally, the mother gives-in and puts the cell back in the baby's hands. The baby calms down instantly. But something strange I perceived, the mother realizes that the baby is totally addicted to the cell. Mother is trying to hide the baby's TOTAL ADDICTION from the public eye. The mother and calm child get to the cashier, but the mother takes back the cell. The baby screams now the loudest of all, and grabs at the next best things... all of the electronics at the front counter. Mother reluctantly gives back the cell after about 30 more seconds of this. Cashier calms down a bit seeing child is distracted again.

Scompy perceives that mother is using the cell-phone all of the time as a crutch for parenting at home. Mother has been told by society that technology makes kids smarter, bought into half-truth and knows nothing about daily melatonin cycling or the physics going on around her. Mother does realize she has a huge problem, but clearly doesn't have the time, and especially the dopamine, to deal with it. Scompy keeps mouth shut through it all wanting to see a moment to , gets through the line, watches several other kids in-line semi-sick coughing, while playing on their cells. Asian man smiles back to me, with wisdom in his eyes. I smile back and leave the store 15 minutes later after buying some socks for my son, extremely grateful that I didn't take him into the store with me.

Overall, I left lamenting the nnEMF soup this baby girl must live in... the electron theft, and the parents completely clueless about what's going on in their environment.

### caroline — 2015-12-31T05:15:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-newborn-story.17097/post-185146>

I find young men are the easiest to talk to about this ....especially since they usually carry their phone in their pants pocket! They will listen to Anything that may affect the Crown Jewels!

It doesn't seem right to say nothing.....but everyone Has to get there when they are ready....just like we did....

So many people raked JK over the coals for his weird, stupid, nonsensical ideas .....these were people who were basically on board until he mentioned nnEMF .....and then all hell Broke lose! You have no idea how ugly it got......

Even a moron had to figure out that there may be something in this. There is a neurosurgeon in Australis who has been saying this for more than ten years and his peers ostracised him and he was denied hospital privileges. He moved to the U.S. For some time before returning to OZ.

> He once told me he began to outfit his home using Apple devices and then switched to Google’s Android operating system since the software was easier and more extensible to develop on."

I live in San Mateo, and saw this in the news today too.... The tech and nnemf are just part of his death story I'm sure there was an entire recipe. Sadly this leaves people puzzled with no clue what went wrong.

### Sean L. — 2016-08-10T23:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digital-disruption-6th-extinction.17102/post-199497>

Interesting observation of the effects of EMF when driving home today. It's the middle of the Summer here in New Hampshire so all the leaves are a deep, dark green however when passing by the transmission towers it looked like mid November underneath the wires. Most of the leaves were a dark red or yellow with some brown or even dead. Perhaps a microcosm of what EMF is doing to our cells...

# Thread: Awesome Kid lesson by Tracy Guild
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-kid-lesson-by-tracy-guild.17104/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-09T16:37:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-kid-lesson-by-tracy-guild.17104/post-181478>

Tracy said, "Waiting for a store to open this morning, my son and I strolled over to Starbucks to get a cold drink and there was a really long line. The woman in front of us was glued to her phone and after a few minutes, walked over to wait in the line where they give you your drink. Noah - "She just used her app to order and pay and jumped the line...she is better than us (snarky kidding voice)." I said - no, Son. She does not realize that she is desperate for time because she is glued to her toxic blue poison. She is living on fast forward. To wait, to slow down is devine because we have leveraged the light to give us more time."

This is a starfish action here folks.

### thomas — 2015-11-22T17:59:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-kid-lesson-by-tracy-guild.17104/post-182500>

Something I can relate to.. the whole notion of "saving time" is complete bullshit. Like, you need to go faster somewhere, WHY?? Why do you need to follow after something you can't ever catch?
Conformity is a symptom of brain atrophy. Conformity leads to brain atrophy. Brain atrophy leads to conformity. Vicious cycle.
Me I do better when I forget the notion of time entirely. Measuring time leads to unhapiness. Being slave to time measuring machines is the opposite of how I want to live.
At the end of the day, you're still gonna be you. Sometimes I sit on some bench for hours and I get some thoughts, "well actually, I don't need to go anywhere", "I can sleep there if I want to. Home is wherever I am","I don't have to do anything, I chose what I do".. some thoughts to cure the zombie mindsets.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-22T18:19:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-kid-lesson-by-tracy-guild.17104/post-182501>

Disturb conformity with curiosity. Paleo biochemists have been making fun of me for 5 years while I just sit back and smile at them. Why do I smile. It's just a matter of time before my version of crazy replaces their arcane ideas of how life works. Crazy has some benefits. Insanity about nature beauty requires patience. In this sense, I am very happy to be called insane. Ignore your critics, only mediocrity is safe from ridicule. You should always dare to be different.

[http://phys.org/news/2015-11-quantum-entanglement-room-temperature-semiconductor.html](http://phys.org/news/2015-11-quantum-entanglement-room-temperature-semiconductor.html)

Here is the rub people: If if it works at room temperature, it can damned well work at body temperature, doubly so for the conducive environments in nerve, skin, and fascial tissues.Your docs, alternative docs, and you paleo biochemists might not know where all the latest science is headed but you do. This is why I persist. Ignore the critics, only mediocrity is safe from ridicule. You should be dare to be different. A lot of this basic research is already worked out and spoken about clearly in "Life on the Edge" by Jim Al-Khalili. My job is to bring you the biologic applications and implications of what the experimental bio-physicists are showing us. The paleo biochemists cannot explain enzymatic flux and reactions rates. Some of us can. How does the enzyme know where the substrate is? It follows the the path that light leaves in its wake in a cell. That pathway is made by electrons, because light only interacts with electrons instantaneously. It requires no time. Enzymes all work by proton tunneling as you learned in "Life On the Edge". So the pathway where a connection needs to be made is lined with protons and neutrons in our protein lattice's. It is almost like having a canyon carved into the cell for running water to follow. Cell water (EZ) also acts as a molecular mirror for infrared light. Therefore, the enzyme gains knowledge of the path it should take because light leads it to its partner molecule. EZ water is the ideal chromophore for light. This happens are lightening fast speeds because the interaction between light and electrons is instantaneous. This means an enzyme knows the path through a "quantum observation or measurement", by cell water. The enzyme, a protein, has alpha helices that absorb light in specific spectral frequencies. They are tuned to the frequency of the substrates of the reactants. Infrared spectroscopy demonstrates that each organic molecule has its own unique signature. Amazingly, the alpha-helices are just the right size coils to be easily tuned to the entire infrared and visible spectrum. The enzymes often organize as dimers for depth-perception, just as we have two eyes. This is how life works; what is published in a biochemistry book is yesterday's news that cannot help tomorrow's patients. We have to go deeper to uncover nature's rules. My goal is not to be accepted by the status quo, but to bury it altogether.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-22T18:20:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-kid-lesson-by-tracy-guild.17104/post-182502>

The new reality will be this soon = Paleo bro science = Alchemist & Metaphysician

Adapt or get replaced

# Thread: 2.5 year old, can't get her eating DHA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sean Shick — 2015-11-09T17:42:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181500>

I saw the thread that contained some recipes, there were 2 in there I know we can try. But at this point I am really focused on getting some DHA in her, and it just hasn't happened so far.

Is a supplement completely out of the question? She has taken no supplement so far...and I know food is always better. Thoughts?

Almost at the point of holding her down and shoving a sardine in there............j/k of course

### Lahelada — 2015-11-09T18:00:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181507>

What is she eating at the moment?

### Sean Shick — 2015-11-09T22:37:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181515>

Her diet is not the best, not the worst, but here it is.

Very little processed food at all.

\- cheese, best we can find.
\- yogurt
\- organic beef, not 100% grass
\- organic bison, same
\- corn tortilla fried in coconut oil(taco nights)
\- apples, banana, pomegranate, carrots
\- pretzels, goldfish crackers, Cheerios
\- a little dark chocolate
\- very little chicken( we don't really eat chicken )
\-homemade pancakes with coconut oil
\-some toast with good butter

And that's really about it. No fish

### fitness@home — 2015-11-09T23:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181516>

How frequently do you try introducing fish into her meals? Keep trying and let her watch you enjoying _your_ meal. Maybe offer her bites of your seafood first.

My kids are all grown up, but I recall my youngest (as a toddler) being excited because she was eating shark . We lived in Florida then and had ample seafood available to experiment with.

Bottom line... don't give up!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-09T23:53:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181521>

If she won't eat it........dont let her eat anything else. You are the adult and set the environment. Use the leverage to change the behavior.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-09T23:59:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181523>

When your proteins are lacking electrons from a lack of DHA putting someone in sun does little. Sunlight needs DHA to change magnetic signaling in our free radicals. This is what creates the triplet state. Sunlight cannot innovate change in us to alter our trajectory based upon our habits. Why? Simple habits are magnetic chemical interactions that occur time and time again and we revert to them because our dopamine levels sets them in stone. So if you keep letting the kid doing what they have done the kid will get what they have and you will deal with a child who becomes a troubled teen. Do something to change the habit. That something is limiting their choices........and going hungry. No one dies from fasting for a couple of days to set the new rules of existence.

Wake up. You're the parent. Stop trying to be their friend. Be the difference in their life.

### Sean Shick — 2015-11-10T00:31:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181528>

Baby and Mom and just now going through the weening process and I think that I have been rationalizing the poor eating because Mom has done such a good job with the breast feeding; thinking that's shes getting what she needs from Mom. But all along I've been worried, made it to the front burner though now that the breast milk is stopping.

"You'll Eat This Sardine AAAaand You Will Like IT!!!!! lol

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-10T06:52:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181557>

Stay here.......you can become brighter than me.......I had to over come my professional beliefs. Unlearning to relearn is a lot harder than learning........trust me.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-10T06:52:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181558>

I am not so bright............took me 40 yrs to figure it out.

### nicld — 2015-11-10T17:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-181574>

What worked with our daughter and eating new foods is present it to her, tell her she has to try two big bites of it, then she can have something else. Keep doing that and up the bites each time, it will work. The fits that were thrown in our house were epic but like Jack said, you are the parent and hunger will force her to eat. Also maybe start with tuna, salmon or a mild white fish like Christine said. Sardines have a pretty strong taste to me.

Good luck, you are on the right track.

### Linz — 2015-11-16T14:13:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-182060>

Sean,
I always told my children they should taste everything but never made them eat it all so they were not afraid they would have to eat much of the things they really disliked. Incredible how healthy their own choices were when there was no pressure (and not too much temptation from unhealthy alternatives).
Over the last few years my health crashed and I changed diet and habits for myself. As I improved I realised that both teenagers were gradually doing more and more the same as me without prompting.

You can catch up. Farmers know a lot more about light and seasons than most .......... and lots about keeping things healthy rather than salvaging the sick!

### Sean Shick — 2015-11-16T21:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-182097>

Thanks for the advice, love it. There hasn't been any major progress made yet. Biggest success was some wild alaskan salmon mixed up in some rice. I was actually super stoked to get that done. We have a long road ahead of us. I keep telling myself that she would fine living on bread and water, if thats all we had. But I think anyone here on this forum probably isn't the type of person that settles in ignorance. When ya love these little buggers so much that words can't describe, you just want them to eat some fish darn it...IS THAT SO WRONG!!!! lol

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-11-18T05:16:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-182251>

Just keep introducing it. Make no issues about it, just offer. Here's food!
My kids love salmon cooked on the grill, or trout, or snapper, or tuna. They love oysters. In fact, I was so astonished when my oldest about 2.5 and she totally macked down our friend's tin of smoked oysters. I'd never eaten oysters as a kid, and was like, wow!
Other things my kids have always liked: tuna salad--get creative! Crab cakes, salmon cakes, salmon burgers, tuna burgers, oyster po'boys (breaded with almond flour and baked and served on lettuce or coconut wraps), fish and sweet potato chips...there are many options out there. Get creative, don't stress. If you make food a "thing" they will resist more. Just offer, offer, offer.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-11-18T05:16:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-182252>

Oh! And bacon wrapped anything.

### Coreena — 2016-02-08T23:54:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-188569>

My son LOVES these salmon cakes I make. Super simple and delicious:

Sautee in butter/ghee:
1 onion, diced
Same amount of celery, diced
When they are soft, add:
1 package frozen organic butternut squash until very soft and mashable.

Meanwhile cook at 350 for 25 minutes:
1-2 lbs. salmon in oven, with salt

Allow everything to cool, then mix it all in a bowl. Add 1-2 eggs if your child doesn't have any problem with eggs. Add some himalayan salt, a few spices (whatever you like), some good organic mustard to taste, and let chill in fridge if you have time.

Form patties, then fry up in more ghee/butter.

I always make a big batch so we can eat them for breakfast and lunch the next day.

I also use all of these same ingredients and make a salmon chowder. But I add bacon to that recipe too. I get salmon carcasses from our local fish store and I save all the skin/shells from my hubby's oysters and shrimp and make up a yummy fish stock. Pretty easy if you just look at some recipes online.

### Scompy — 2016-02-09T02:10:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-5-year-old-cant-get-her-eating-dha.17105/post-188580>

Great point about shrimp...! And who on earth doesn't like shrimp? Talk about a perfect protein, selenium, B12 and iodine source as well!

Here's my spin: I was successful by referencing **_Spongebob Squarepants_** about the fabled Krabby Patties, telling my son I could also cook up the major secret ingredient. BOOM!

### Optimalbound — 2015-11-13T12:35:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-a-for-members-in-europe.17106/post-181892>

> [lilly antic said:](/goto/post?id=181879)
>
> Is THAT correct, Optimalbound????
> I understand it to be 1 pm CST= 6pm GMT(London time) = 7pm central European Time
> Please help!!!!

Sorry, my bad! I actually cross-checked with two different on-line time services, and it turns out that either they both were wrong, or I somehow managed to misread them both!

As can be seen on this page:
[http://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/](http://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/) :

[GMT](http://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/uk/london) = [CST](http://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/usa/new-orleans) + 6
[CET](http://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/france/paris) = [CST](http://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/usa/new-orleans) + 7
​

So: 1PM (CST) = **7PM (GMT)** = **8PM (CET)**

​

(CST = New Orleans; GMT = London, Lisbon, Reykjavik; CET = Paris, Madrid, Berlin, Oslo etc.)

In other words, all three of us were wrong...

### Optimalbound — 2015-11-13T12:45:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-a-for-members-in-europe.17106/post-181893>

> [lilly antic said:](/goto/post?id=181886)
>
> Yes, I was right-the time difference is 7 hours, so Optimalbound it will be at 7 pm central eurpean time.

No, I don't think so. The time difference is 7 hours, but 1pm + 7 = **8pm
**
I think some of the confusion here might have been caused by CST and CET going off daylights saving time at different dates, so for a while last month, the time difference was one hour less than it is now.

# Thread: CLOUDY DAYS STILL PROVIDES UV LIGHT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cloudy-days-still-provides-uv-light.17108/>

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-10T15:05:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cloudy-days-still-provides-uv-light.17108/post-181561>

Today's PSA: even in cloudy weather one can get UV. You need to be outside in it no matter what. Can you get UV in the AM when it is cloudy?
Most of the UV light is UVA in this instance. Those are the same frequencies in black lights. Clouds absorb UV radiation, reducing ground-level UV intensity. Clear skies allow virtually 100% of UV to pass through, scattered clouds transmit 89%, broken clouds transmit 73%, and overcast skies transmit 31%.

Even on a cloudy day, visible sunlight outdoors is at least 10 times brighter than the light indoors. The interesting part for modern man is that the color temperature (blue light at 435-465nmn) is way higher than sunlight. Since humans now spend 95% of their time indoors you might begin to realize this could affect dopamine levels and maybe limit your time on Earth? Modern medicine remains clueless about the UV effect but you should not be. To use the UV outside you need DHA within your RPE of the retina. Everything requires the proper connection to get optimal.

# Thread: Biohacking Post-surgery Procedures
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-post-surgery-procedures.17112/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-11-11T02:23:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-post-surgery-procedures.17112/post-181623>

I was discussing with a friend of a friend last week about his son that survived after almost drowning and suffered some brain damage as a result. This got me thinking about what solutions we all have for regeneration, but are **_prevented_** from having access to after a surgery or accident because we're stuck in a hospital.

Awhile back, I posted about an ideal hospital to build, but I'm also thinking what can be done practically and even to request of hospital staff. Some basics would include:

1.  Natural sunlight timing, not from behind glass, and more skin exposure
2.  Minimizing nnEMF in the hospital
3.  Minimizing fake lighting in the hospital and blue-blocking in the evening
4.  Access to a grounding cord and being as close to the ground after recovery
5.  Access to better water sources (RO, biohacked EZ, etc.)
6.  Access to better foods (DHA, veges, etc.)

Patients can implement these things themselves, but often in the few days after an operation, they are stuck in bad conditions. And it's exactly in that time-window that the body needs the most healing to fight infection, etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-17T03:26:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-post-surgery-procedures.17112/post-182153>

there is a blog on this one.........

# Thread: My Vacuum Biohack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-vacuum-biohack.17113/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-11-12T04:25:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-vacuum-biohack.17113/post-181726>

#1. Rip out the freaking carpets!

If you have a house and can do it, rip out as much carpet as possible and go hardwood floors if you can. I ripped the carpet from my whole lower-level and about 9/10ths of the top level of my home. I had mine resurfaced a year ago so it's minimal work to keep them clean. Usually, I just need to use a damp cloth to clean the hardwoods, and I have a few strategic area carpets where I like them. Overall square footage, I have about 85% less the carpet-area compared to when I moved into the home. And less carpet-area means less time spent vacuuming sucking up EMF with the dust.

Granted, I still own a vacuum and sometimes it's hard to avoid using it. Tonight was one of those nights. I went an additional step and wore rubber gloves and kept the cord as far away as I could while doing it. The rubber gloves so when touching the cord, I'm minimizing the EMF. Off of the vacuum I own, I get 0.5 to 0.7 Gauss near the motor compartment. The cords are around 0.2 to 0.4 Gauss on average.

Any other vacuum hacks appreciated.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-12T05:03:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-vacuum-biohack.17113/post-181732>

I love my 82 husband and married for better or worse and he says moving is not an option. Regardless of these non essential details of life. I will Trust in God because I am only passing this way for my mission not for my life but because of His plan for me.

# Thread: Arsenic is high
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### david weiss — 2015-11-13T00:31:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-181830>

On my recent blood test my arsenic level was 11 ng/ml. That is alarmingly high. What could have caused that and what should I do about it? I've been following the Epi-Paleo diet for over a year. Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-13T03:26:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-181858>

You must have it somewhere in your environment. I hope your relationship are fine...........Arsenic poisoning is common among humans. Smells like almonds. Causes peripheral neuropathy too.

### Jonathin — 2015-11-13T04:04:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-181861>

FYI: Arsenic was a common pesticide in old fruit growing areas. There may be high levels in soil of these areas.

### david weiss — 2015-11-13T15:23:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-181903>

Thanks for the replies. I don't think anyone is trying to poison me.
We live out in the country and our water is from a well. We've had it checked a number of times and it always checks out okay. Still, I guess there's a possibility.
I don't think there was much agriculture in my immediate area, but nearby are a lot of former fruit growing areas. I know they did a lot of chemicals there in the past.
Beside eliminating the source once I figure it out, what is the best way to eliminated it? I do have an infra red sauna.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-13T18:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-181908>

something has to be behind it.

### Jonathin — 2015-11-13T21:00:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-181921>

I am wondering whether this was a coincidental one off exposure (food or environment) with your test. Was it a testing error? Or what repetitive habit, food, or environmental contact? I would want to get tested again in a few weeks.

### david weiss — 2015-11-13T23:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-181926>

I also wondered if the test was erroneous. I'm going to test again in a week or two. Meanwhile I'm spending time thinking about what in my environment could be the culprit. Haven't come up with anything so far. I'd like to also get my wife to test, she has the same exposures that I do.

### JanSz — 2015-11-21T16:54:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-182475>

> [david weiss said:](/goto/post?id=181830)
>
> **On my recent blood test** my arsenic level was **11 ng/ml.** That is alarmingly high. What could have caused that and what should I do about it? **I've been following the Epi-Paleo diet for over a year.** Thanks

Arsenic is not likely to be detected in blood specimens drawn more than 2 days after exposure.

[http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Clinical and Interpretive/8645](http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Clinical+and+Interpretive/8645)

**So, you must have got arsenic less than 2 days before blood draw,
or have constant supply of it (seafood?)
Where are you getting your seafood from?
Consider changing your fishmonger at least for a while, then re-test.**

What caused you or your doc to test for arsenic?

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**
0-12 ng/mL

Reference values apply to all ages.

**Interpretation

**
Abnormal blood arsenic concentrations (>12 ng/mL) indicate significant exposure.

\--------------
Diagnosis and treatment of
chronic arsenic poisoning
[http://www.who.int/water\_sanitation\_health/dwq/arsenicun4.pdf](http://www.who.int/water_sanitation_health/dwq/arsenicun4.pdf)

,
Arsenic level in hair and nail above 1 mg/kg and 1.08 mg/kg respectively and/or arsenic
level in urine, above 50μg/L (**without any history of taking seafood**).

..............
\---------------------------------
I've been following the Epi-Paleo diet for over a year.
(without any history of taking seafood)

### JanSz — 2015-11-21T17:21:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-182478>

[http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Clinical and Interpretive/8645](http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Clinical+and+Interpretive/8645)

**Cautions

**
Measurement of urine arsenic is the preferred method of screening for arsenic exposure.
Blood is not a good specimen to screen for arsenic.

This test is not useful for evaluation of chronic arsenic exposure.

High concentrations of gadolinium and iodine are known to interfere with most metals tests. If either gadolinium- or iodine-containing **contrast media** has been administered, a specimen must not be collected for 96 hours

\---
\---

### david weiss — 2015-11-22T16:17:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arsenic-is-high.17115/post-182499>

Great information, Thanks. I think my seafood source is pretty good. I eat a sardines, fresh wild caught shell fish, low mercury fish from Whole Foods, etc.

The high arsenic showed up on some blood work. It doesn't make a lot of sense because I cannot imagine any way that I had a high arsenic exposure, My water is clean and I live in the country, not near any industry or agriculture.

I just took a urine metals test with a kit from Doctors Data. I don't have the results yet.

# Thread: How does ocular melatonin influence pineal melatonin at night?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-ocular-melatonin-influence-pineal-melatonin-at-night.17116/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 46
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Curves — 2015-11-13T00:32:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-ocular-melatonin-influence-pineal-melatonin-at-night.17116/post-181831>

I'm trying to get a better grasp of the relationship between retinal/ocular melatonin and pineal melatonin.

I know UV light hits the retina in the morning making ocular melatonin and turning off production of pineal melatonin.

What I'm wanting to know more about is how does ocular melatonin influence the production of pineal melatonin at night. Quoting you from the Health and Wellness Show "**The latest research in probably the last 8 or 9 years shows that full spectrum sunlight in the morning actually increases ocular melatonin. Without ocular melatonin made in the eye, you cannot release pineal melatonin after four hours of darkness**. "

[http://www.sott.net/article/304590-Health-and-Wellness-Show-A-conversation-with-Dr-Jack-Kruse](http://www.sott.net/article/304590-Health-and-Wellness-Show-A-conversation-with-Dr-Jack-Kruse)

This one links the eye to the pineal.........

[http://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-1-4615-5051-8\_59#page-1](http://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007%2F978-1-4615-5051-8_59#page-1)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-13T03:21:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-ocular-melatonin-influence-pineal-melatonin-at-night.17116/post-181856>

[http://www.sciencemag.org/content/210/4475/1267](http://www.sciencemag.org/content/210/4475/1267)

Melatonin concentrations decreased 10 to 20 minutes after the subjects were exposed to 2500-lux incandescent light and reached near-daytime levels within 1 hour (Fig. 1). After the subjects resumed sleeping in the dark, the melatonin concentrations increased immediately and within 40 minutes were at the levels measured before exposure. The fluorescent light (500 lux) did not reduce melatonin, and there was no change after the return to darkness. In the two subjects who were exposed to 1500-lux incandescent light, melatonin concentrations decreased to levels intermediate between those measured during exposure to 500 and 2500 lux (Fig. 2). The return to normal nighttime concentrations after subjects were exposed to 1500 lux was similar to that occurring after their exposure to 2500 lux. The concentration of melatonin in subjects awakened and exposed to 500-lux fluorescent light did not differ significantly from that measured while they were asleep in the dark.

### Lahelada — 2015-11-13T22:14:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-ocular-melatonin-influence-pineal-melatonin-at-night.17116/post-181922>

500 lux = 500 lumens over an area of a square meter
You cannot make that calculation add up though as this would mean that melatonin is not affected when sleeping with a 60 w CFL considering an average size bedroom of 14sqm or 150 sqft.
While that is low lighting in interior design terms surely it is still too much? You could read by that light.
For reference full moon light is 0.25 lux roughly,full daylight 2000 lux.
Are they confused with the lumen specification that manufacturers do put on the package?
Someone check my maths please as it is important for the red/ purple light overnight biohackers.

### Curves — 2015-11-13T23:38:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-ocular-melatonin-influence-pineal-melatonin-at-night.17116/post-181928>

**Tying some pieces together:**

· Melatonin is a hormone, which is mainly produced by the **pineal gland, a vestigial eye**. . . . **very rarely are we reminded of its phylogenetic origin as the third eye** . . .
· This very ancient molecule exists in organisms as simple and primitive as prokaryotes and as complex as humans.2 It acts as a free radical scavenger and an antioxidant, possibly its initial function **2–3.5 billion years ago.**
· The retina has a light-mediated feedback system; **melatonin mediates darkness-related adaptive changes, and dopamine mediates light-related changes.**
· **Dopamine has the opposite effect to melatonin and can mimic light in entraining and phase shifting the circadian oscillator which controls melatonin rhythmicity**​

**Still not yet understanding the causal relationship between ocular daytime melatonin and pineal nightime melatonin. Do they aggregate? Do different things? Signal each other? Scientific third grader here still trying to piece the puzzle together.**

### Curves — 2015-11-13T23:54:47+0200

@[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) I surely won't be the one to confirm your math  but this study says it's < 200 lux. This still seems high to me considering the retina responds to 5 photons of light. I bolded the area in the abstract that I found interesting with respect to this discussion.

JOURNAL OF BIOLOGICAL RHYTHMS

[http://jbr.sagepub.com/content/12/6/537.abstract](http://jbr.sagepub.com/content/12/6/537.abstract)

**Photic Regulation of Melatonin in Humans: Ocular and Neural Signal Transduction**
**1\. George C. Brainard**

1\. Department of Neurology, Thomas Jefferson University, Jefferson Medical College, Philadelphia, PA 19107
**1\. Mark D. Rollag**

1\. Department of Anatomy, Uniformed Services University of Health Sciences, Bethesda, MD 20814
**1\. John P. Hanifin**

1\. Department of Neurology, Thomas Jefferson University, Jefferson Medical College, Philadelphia, PA 19107

Light is a potent stimulus for regulating the pineal gland's production of melatonin and the broader circadian system in humans. It initially was thought that only very bright photic stimuli (≥ 2500 lux) could suppress nocturnal melatonin secretion and induce other circadian responses. It is now known that markedly lower illuminances (≤ 200 lux) can acutely suppress melatonin or entrain and phase shift melatonin rhythms when exposure conditions are optimized. T**he elements for physical/biological stimulus processing that regulate photic influences on melatonin secretion include the physics of the light source, gaze behavior relative to the light source, and the transduction of light energy through the pupil and ocular media.** Elements for sensory/neural signal processing become involved as photons are absorbed by retinal photopigments and neural signals are generated in the retinohypothalamic tract. Aspects of this physiology include the ability of the circadian system to integrate photic stimuli spatially and temporally as well as the **wavelength sensitivity of the operative photoreceptors**. Acute, light-induced suppression of melatonin is proving to be a powerful tool for clarifying how these elements of ocular and neural physiology influence the interaction between light and the secretion of melatonin from the human pineal gland.

### Curves — 2015-11-17T02:02:01+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-17T02:07:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-ocular-melatonin-influence-pineal-melatonin-at-night.17116/post-182139>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=182132)
>
> That's what I'm discovering.... as I study this stuff.
>
>
> I still have a question regarding biopolar neurons. How do they specifically capture and transimit the UV frequency to the SCN? Is it more than the stimulus of the UV hitting the retnia? or does it go beyond? Trying to understand how CNII plays into this???

Light responses in bipolar cells are initiated by synapses with photoreceptors. The bipolar cells then transmit the signals from the photoreceptors or the horizontal cells, and pass it on to the ganglion cells directly or indirectly via amercing cells in the retina. Unlike most neurons, bipolar cells communicate via graded potentials and not action potentials. This means light frequency is on a slope. That slope is related in quantum fashion to the neurotransmitters in the brain. All NT are also quantized to light. This is why dopamine and UV light are linked. Like horizontal cells, amacrine cells work laterally also. However, horizontal cells are connected to the output of rod and cone cells. These are the main photoreceptor of the eye clock and camera. Amacrine cells affect the output from bipolar cells directly (they are inhibitory), and are therefore more specialized. The specialization comes in the form of light frequencies they respond too. Each type of amacrine cell releases one or several NT's where it connects with other cells in the brain to do what they do. This is why there are 33 types. If you understand factorial math, that means within our octave of the visible spectrum amacrine cells can handle
8,683,317,618,811,886,495,518,194,401,280,000,000 different frequencies. So when you realize that biochemistry only uses 100,000 reactions per second light frequencies can easily handle this task via your retina. Biochemists have no clue what controls enzymatic flux but now you do.........

This is how sorting out the visible spectrum of light occurs. We operate in jut one octave of 73 octaves of light total spectrum. Within that one octave are hundreds of thousands of frequencies of light. Amacrine cells determine what frequencies we pay most attention to and link them to NT that respond to these frequencies. They are respond in quantized fashion controlled by light alone. The rest of the frequencies we remain oblivious too in our retina because our visual sense is not attuned to them.

Everything we put on our bodies distorts our sensory perception. This alters our reality. The senses are specific to our morphologic development and present for us to use the specific spectrum of our local environment in the best ways ways possible. When you move away from your ideal adapted environment things change. Why? Blocking one part of the spectrum alters biochemistry because it often regulates another. This is akin to having sex with your clothes on. We can do this, but nature wants us naked for a reason. Moreover, it's probably not a good thing to consider. Mother Nature perspective is the one makes the point we should adhere to. Our surfaces are designed to decipher wave forms from the space around us; they are invisible to us unless we have our senses intact.

# Thread: TV bands for Wifi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tv-bands-for-wifi.17118/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-11-13T08:55:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tv-bands-for-wifi.17118/post-181876>

Came across this one that made me go, 'Crap!'

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/11/141124092402.htm](http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/11/141124092402.htm)

Takeaways:

"...propose to extend the frequencies for free communication to include lower ranges and even **increased transmission power**."

"These bands are being used less and less for the transmission of TV signals. They are **highly suited for penetrating obstacles such as walls**. Depending on the ambient conditions and by automatically adapting transmission power to prevent interference, such WLAN networks might even reach communication partners at a distance of several kilometers. Even in cities where the transmission capacity will be limited due to the large number of transmission stations, **the range of wireless networks could be extended significantly**."

"Examples besides wireless computer networks are wireless loudspeakers and cameras, remote controls for garage doors, transponders, baby phones, and Bluetooth."

### Scompy — 2015-11-13T09:04:15+0200

# Thread: Binge Eating/Cravings=Leptin Resistance

**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 25

### NadoRo — 2015-11-14T01:57:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181939>

I've been on the Reset protocol for 3 months..sleep has drastically improved, and I thought I'd try cutting the BAB over the past few days. The unfortunate result has been a strong uptick in cravings, and I'm all of sudden finding myself hungry at all the wrong times again. This has lead to mediocre food choices and a strong feeling that I'm backsliding!

Question: Does the inability to reduce the BAB without triggering cravings mean I am still Leptin Resistant? My guess is..YES. Or, putting it another way, if you can cut the BAB down to the Post RX 30 grams of protein and not trigger cravings or binge eating, does that mean you are finally Leptin Sensitive? Is THIS the marker I'm looking for and not sleep improvements??

### caroline — 2015-11-14T03:01:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181944>

My opinion - keep doing the BAB until you naturally feel like you need a lot less food. Not everyone goes down to 1 o.r 2 meals a day. Do what works for you.
You are getting lots of sunlight?
nnEmf?
Drinking lots of good water?
Lots of good fat?
Remember - fat doesn't make you fat!
Let your appetite be your guide.
Could something be triggering your cravings?

### Penny — 2015-11-14T04:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181947>

Maybe it means your environment is still bad?

>
> Cravings for me are triggered by carbs. If I eat nothing for breakfast (had to today because I was fasting for bloodwork) or if I eat fat and protein of whatever amount, I don't get those cravings. What are you having for breakfast?

Breakfast is some form of beef or pork. Seafood for one or both of the other meals. My carbs are very low (except when I fall into a bowl of potato chips!), likely no more than 20-30 grams per day. I've been waking earlier with the time change, so breakfast comes around 6-6:30 am. This means I'm pretty hungry by 10:30-11 am. I still can't go longer than 4-5 hours without hunger. I'd love to be able to skip lunch, but that triggers cravings too.

### NadoRo — 2015-11-14T05:27:23+0200

> Maybe it means your environment is still bad?

For sure my environment is not optimal. I struggle to achieve hydration at altitude, even in the traditional CW sense of "hydration." I have to drink a gallon of spring water daily to feel like I'm getting there. I thought I was ok with spring water, but it's been suggested to me that RO water hydrates faster. Maybe I need to switch for while until the tank is refilled, so to speak.

> Drinking lots of good water?
> Lots of good fat?
> Remember - fat doesn't make you fat!
> Let your appetite be your guide.
> Could something be triggering your cravings?

My understanding was that most people were eventually able to eat less, but that has not been the case for me. I have a history of disordered eating and strong carb cravings, so I get anxious when that feeling comes on.. I think dairy is a factor here. It's crept back in for me. I was planning to wait until I saw my latest round of tests to eliminate it. I wanted to see how much of a dent I could make in my HS-CRP and other markers just by diligently following no gluten and focusing on getting more seafood. I guess the fact that I don't want to get rid of it suggests that it should be next on my list!

### caroline — 2015-11-14T06:05:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181954>

A lot of us here had disordered eating - are you eating enuf fat?

I found recently I became really obsessed with sushi and my hunger was out of control - it was definitely the rice I finally figured out! Talk about a slow learner!

It seemed like sushi should be good for me .....all the seafood - not!

How much dairy are you having?

Tell us what you are eating again?

Are you eating enuf for your BAB? I had to eat at least 80 gr of protein to knock out the carb cravings.....and I am not all that big....

### caroline — 2015-11-14T06:07:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181955>

Change of seasons wreck havoc with a lot of us. My sleep goes down the tubes etc.

JK has told us if we fall of the wagon (potato chips) we need a kick in the butt. You kinda set yourself back to the beginning. That always scared the heck out of me!

### NadoRo — 2015-11-14T06:15:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181956>

I'm having generous amounts of dairy in my coffee (HWC or half/half), eat grass fed butter, and have noticed some cheese sneaking in. I love heavy cream and could drink it straight out of the container! My breakfast is always pork or beef with eggs to get the protein count higher. I'd estimate I eat about 50 grams of protein, so you're comment about 80 grams makes me think I need to go up way higher than I have been. I don't have much of an appetite in the morning, so I've struggled to adhere to the 50 grams.

How did you get up to 80 grams with your BAB? What did you eat? Did you find that you were able to pare down the BAB and still not have cravings? I'm wondering if, for me, that development will mark the turning point in leptin signaling.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-14T16:04:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181963>

cravings are due to a light problem......and sleep is altered as a result............implication nation: ...We don't need to improve sleep........we need to innovate darkness and sleep comes naturally. Big difference. Focus in on what matters and not on what doesn't

### NadoRo — 2015-11-14T16:23:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181967>

> [thisbirdhaswings said:](/goto/post?id=181961)
>
> That should have read "wouldn't worry". Can't edit responses here anymore for some reason.

Thank you! I'm mulling over your comment about needing a larger dinner. Post dinner snacking was never an issue for me, and I'm able to keep the dinners small..wondering now if that's a mistake. Ideally, I'd like to see my eating go to two meals a day, where I drop lunch. I think what's happened here is that I'm waking earlier (now up at 6 am naturally, just as the sun starts to rise) but I'm eating dinner too late. There's too much of a lag between lunch and dinner. I'm also thinking that CT initially raises hunger levels, and that could be contributing to the cravings.

Given a history of binge eating and a naturally large appetite (even when I was thinner, I could eat whatever I wanted..and generally did), I need to keep the BAB going. Lunch can be the meal I drop down the road, if and when I reach the point of getting down to two meals a day. I think it's particularly important to keep the BAB going in the face of coming holidays. These meals will have all of my favorite comfort foods...I can resist if I have a large breakfast under my belt.

Also, I had tea this morning instead of coffee. I use markedly less creamer in tea than coffee, but get the same pleasure from drinking the cup of tea as I do the coffee. Gotta cut the dairy again.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=181963)
>
> cravings are due to a light problem......and sleep is altered as a result............implication nation: ...We don't need to improve sleep........we need to innovate darkness and sleep comes naturally. Big difference. Focus in on what matters and not on what doesn't

Yep, cravings went up this week as my light exposure and grounding went down due to our first big snow and plunging temps mid-week (low 30's until midday, 20's at sunrise). I got out in the daylight as much as possible, but it was waaay less than last week when we were in the 50's and 60's. Temps are warmer today, and I'll make it a point to get outside today. Just another way deep winter is not optimal when you aren't fully cold adapted!
