### Penny — 2015-11-15T03:52:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-181996>

I think it's direct - I just recently learned this myself - I got the blue light blocking at night part down - although at the moment that is a fail - but you need sunlight during the day to make serotonin so you have a substrate to make melatonin - pretty weird if you ask me... or at least counterintuitive - vitamin d boosts dopamine so yes on that one too - the gut flora thing was discussed in Ubiquination 24 I think - pretty cool - I don't remember how the bugs get light - I vaguely remember him saying when you swallow a fecal transplant pill, it adds light and that's how they get results - but as far as cravings and gut flora go, bugs can't digest fat - it's too big, so if you eat fat, they starve. If you add broths, they don't escape the gut lining - if you add chitosan and almonds, it feeds the distal flora - so, no blue light because the pathogenic bugs grow - so, kill, repopulate, cage them, don't feed them simple carbs because they love that - if you do that, they will die and you will have no more cravings - in fact, I vaguely recall Jack telling someone to drink a bottle of olive oil for cravings - which I thought was strange at the time, but what he was doing was sending something down there that was indigestible - and you don't have to buy any expensive stuff - garlic/ginger/rosemary/oregano/coconut oil - not black pepper, that makes membranes more permeable and they'll seep through - in fact, I'm drinking an oregano/rosemary/ginger tea right now and I've been also drinking cranberry juice (zero sugar) because cranberries make it so the bugs can't stick... I'm currently sporting an infection - it's my standard go-to along with gobs of garlic to kill anything - bee propolis/elderberries/echinacea/oregon grape (berberines) are great also - if you do this before bed, bugs that key off of melatonin and wake up to party won't keep you up...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-15T06:51:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/binge-eating-cravings-leptin-resistance.17122/post-182006>

Nado........everything is a light issue. Welcome to the new world order.

What does light change in you?

Light helps you lead a life of your own choosing. Not the one mapped out for you by others.

We become what we think about when we are under the sun.

The sun's light allows you to be of service to others.

Build. Work. Dream. Create using light

Be kind is easier under t.he sun's direction

Be charitable is easy under the sun because you see others in their natural light

Forgive yourself every morning to renew. This is the power of AM light

Make your past serve your present because the only part of you life you can change is the present using light

The problem with most people is their desire to conform to a life designed by someone else. Use your light not theirs.

There was a time you had dreams and ambitions that kept you awake at night. Now the only thing that keeps you awake is fear.

Where did you get the idea you can’t be successful? Might it be your alien sun worship?

Ask yourself, “Why do you work? Why do you get up in the morning?” Is it to use tech gadgets or to see the sun?

Successful people who those who do deliberately that which they want to do well because the sun build their ability to choose wisely consistently

Have goals not whims, the sun builds that ability in you

Most people work harder to prevent themselves from losing than they do to develop their dreams because they are missing purple and red light.

You have to decide. Will I invest in myself to build the life I deserve or will I stay the course? The decision rests on the light that turns on or off your frontal lobes.

What do you want? Do you want to be wealthy so you can do more good or set an example of making excuses?

Good luck can work for you. Doubt and fear can be the things of the past. Since we become what we think about it makes sense for us to change our thoughts regardless of our circumstances. Transformation is easiest in nature when the sun gives you signs of what to do.

If you wish to be rich you can be rich. If you think about poverty you will be poor. Energy flows to where you think.

If you think in negative terms you will reap negative results. If you think in positive terms you will achieve success. Light is the key to the direction and flow of this energy.

A life of frustration is determined by what we think about. Light cuts energy vampires around you. You see them in the light of day and no longer let them pull your strings to the darkness.

It is up to you to make of your life what you must.......everyday the sun rises.

It is not fate or divinity that keeps you in poverty. It is your thinking that cloaks you in confusion and poverty because you live in your shadows. Live in your light and your shadows will disappear.

What you focus on has to produce an outcome. Just as light focus through a magnifying glass to turn a spark to a flame you too can ignite your life with the focus of light.

If you think negative thoughts about your relationship you will help realize a relationship that is less than you deserve.

You are where you are because you do more to stay there than change your conditions of existence. The light you allow determines the reality you get.

You can change everything in your life with a simple power only you can control unless your quantum yield: Your thoughts are tied to the light your eye senses.

Very few people accept the fact that they can have anything they want if they think in faith that they deserve to be.

We convince ourselves that we are victors or victims by our thoughts. Purple light gives wins and blue light build victims.

Nothing comes unannounced, but many can miss the announcement. So it’s very important to actually listen to your own intuition rather than driving through it………our own inner GPS is the best guidance of all. Purple and red are the key to you inner GARMIN.

As you believe it is revealed whatever it truly is. No one can control you unless you give them the remote.

Have courage. Take action. Ideas are meaningless unless you take forward action. Ideation with execution leads to deletion of all good ideas.

What is it you want? What is you would improve about your life if you had a magic wand? That wand is your light saber.

You have as much right to being a success as anyone else. But to do so you have to stop thinking about what you fear. Bend the bars of your cage.

Remove every negative thought because you deserve better. Light allows your to become un-stuck.

Take control of your mind before your beliefs take control of you.

Do more than you have to do because your reality depends upon it.

I want you to think as though nothing can be denied you. Certain light denies you to natural truth.

You can't get away from yourself by moving from one place to another because the light still follows your every move.

Remove every fear from you by shining sunlight on the problem.

Be calm and cheerful when you are drinking sunlight.

You have a dream for a reason. Don’t leave it buried. It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live.

That is all. Night. Carry On.

# Thread: DHA: A Cancer Causing Killer? Ray Peat says...

### Brother John — 2015-11-14T23:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-a-cancer-causing-killer-ray-peat-says.17123/post-181984>

Ray Peat is a very interesting writer and has a lot to say about DHA and other Puffa's.
Here is the link for his DHA article: [http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsaturatedfats.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsaturatedfats.shtml)

I would love to read a serious point by point rebuttal to his argument!

For myself and my own N1: After about 2 years of eating a dozen or so oysters a day and sushi 2 0r 3 times a week... I was very fatigued regardless of external stressors or workload. So I stopped eating anything with DHA. My energy level climbed steadily for the past several weeks. So here I am, aware of 2 points of view, Jack's and Ray's and they are 180 degrees apart.

I am considering the idea that DHA and light must work together or DHA is toxic. And that like anything... there can be "too much".
Thanks,
Brother John

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-17T00:11:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-a-cancer-causing-killer-ray-peat-says.17123/post-182111>

Everything Peat says are studies linked to supplemental form of DHA. I advocate seafood not a damn pill. I have told you this many times on the forum over and over. Seafood not a pill. Why? The fish oil used in most studies is not in triglyceride form and is in phospho-ethyl ester form which increases inflammation. This means it cannot be in the SN-2 position in our body. And the SN-2 is required to get into the CNS and PNS. What good is the pill or studies that use this. There is another problem. DHA in the TG form or the ethyl ester form outside of its other chemicals photo-oxidizes. This means the light the study is done in is critical. Peat never mentions that how the experiments are done affects the results. He made his living doing these flawed studies as a Phd so he never thought to control for them himself. And HE NEVER feels about how that lack of light control affect the results, the data and what he believes. I do. The study must be done in the cold and dark to have scientific power. There are good quality SN-2 natural triglyceride fish oils, granted eating clean seafood is always optimal over supplementation. Just say the studies are a weak basis of that because they use ethyl ester.

My forum and website are not a magnum opus as some believe, it is preliminary, not absolute and designed to be subject to change as data and ideas evolve our perceptions of human biology. This was outlined in the Quilt, which is an adaptable document. That was the origin, but the destination is determined by the redox potential of new material brought to bear on older ideas. In this way it becomes a a creative common for the biophysical sciences. In this way, I break through academias and peer review the current limitations of journals and the scientific mentality of the day. Academia must engage the aberrant and far-from-equilibrium ideas that challenge their present draft as a finished project. and they won’t because they do not want to subject themselves to this scrutiny, which is a blow to themselves. Therefore, we must do it for them. We must realize that we observe things through the lens through which we see them. We need to examine that lens as we collect data that puts a haze on that lens of understanding.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-11-17T00:47:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-a-cancer-causing-killer-ray-peat-says.17123/post-182120>

[http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/85/6/1457.full](http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/85/6/1457.full)
**Docosahexaenoic and arachidonic acid concentrations in human breast milk worldwide**

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-11-17T00:53:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-a-cancer-causing-killer-ray-peat-says.17123/post-182121>

"The whole adult human brain was estimated to consume the equivalent of 4.6 mg/day of DHA and 17.8 mg/day of AA.

This technique was recently repeated by Rapoport’s group \[[80](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257695/#B80-nutrients-03-00529)\] in 14 human volunteers with consistent findings; the global rate of DHA incorporation in the human brain was the equivalent of the consumption of 3.8 ± 1.7 mg/day DHA. Further, they used published estimates of the total amount of DHA in the human brain (5 g) and the daily incorporation rate of 0.076% per day, to determine that the half-life of brain DHA was approximately 2.5 years in humans."

Well, ultimately, I use the CFB, contrast showers, get in the sun all the time, especially in the AM, etc. etc. etc. so practice some of your ideas.

But here is what Dr. Peat typically will talk about doing --> **Move to a location that has lots of light** (when I emailed him about my issues that was the FIRST recommendation he gave me); **utilize sunlight as much as possible.** Utilize sources of light indoors that has less blue and lots of red and infrared. Participate in "meaningful activity" such as learning languages, utilizing the non-dominant hand in already mastered skills, practice painting, etc. Create an "enriched environment" via plants, art, light, etc. Practice "the marital arts" (lol). eat/drink: egg yolks, shrimp, oysters, cod, scallops, milk, cheese, butter, ghee, coconut oil, liver, green veggies like spinach and kale (though well cooked), lots of fruit, honey, maybe some starch. Maybe meat. Coffee. Increase dopamine. Increase cholesterol, pregnonolone, DHEA, and Progesterone. **Utilize diatomic hydrogen.** Avoid PEMF devices. Maybe use some methylene blue and sulfated compounds.

Doesn't sound too far off from some of your recommendations, outside of him promoting a massive decrease in PUFAs, Iron, and endotoxin promoting substances; for me, having PUFAs below 2% of my calories, with linoleic acid almost 0%, while getting **roughly 1 gram** or 2 of DHA+EPA from egg yolks, cod, and oysters, led to me being able to tolerate massive amounts of sun with NO problem, whereas before my skin would get dry and "ashy" (lol), I would get immensely fatigued, etc. I can now effectively allow sunlight to supercharge my brain.

### Penny — 2015-11-17T01:49:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-a-cancer-causing-killer-ray-peat-says.17123/post-182136>

Well I might as well as this question here - is DHA from eggs and/or lamb (grass fed meat) in the SN2 position?

What about phosphatidyl choline or krill oil?

Vaisman et al reported that ingestion of phospholipid-conjugated EPA and DHA increased the scores for a continuous performance task compared with scores obtained when the fatty acids in the ingested oil were stored as triglycerides. This finding indicates that n-3 PUFAs incorporated in phosphatidylcholine act on brain function more efficiently than those incorporated in triglycerides.[35](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3789637/#b35-cia-8-1247) Brossard et al[36](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3789637/#b36-cia-8-1247) and Lemaitre-Delaunay et al[37](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3789637/#b37-cia-8-1247) reported that lysophosphatidylcholine, which is derived from phosphatidylcholine by phospholipase A2, is the preferred physiological carrier of DHA. Thies et al found that PUFAs bound to lysophospholipids at the sn-2 position are preferentially incorporated into the brain.[38](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3789637/#b38-cia-8-1247) PUFAs are most often bound to the sn-2 position in phospholipids.[39](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3789637/#b39-cia-8-1247) The n-3 PUFAs bound to phosphatidylcholine in krill oil may thus be taken up by the brain tissues more readily than the n-3 PUFAs of sardine oil, most likely bringing about the observed difference in their effects. However, the relationship between brain function and fatty acid composition in brain tissue has not been clarified, so further studies need to be carried out.

The major limitations of the present study are its small sample size and inclusion of male subjects only. However, its results suggest that n-3 PUFAs activate cognitive function of the brain in the elderly. The differences in the effects obtained by krill oil and sardine oil seem to be related to differences with regard to incorporation of fatty acids into lipids. It is concluded that fatty acids present as phosphatidylcholine have the potential to bring about more beneficial effects on cognitive function. Further studies using large sample sizes and including female subjects are needed to confirm the results of this study.

I have NO problem disagreeing with him, you or Yew. High color temperature light changes the biochemistry of DHA. Simple. I don't even think Peat knows that the absorption spectra of DHA is 240 nm. This changes what DHA requirements are needed as one is in a world built around nnEMF and blue light DEVOID of all UV light. With no light controls in the studies Peat and his followers are twisting in the wind of precepts. But there is another more significant problem for Peatatarins to overcome. 600 million years of exclusive use in LIFE. That is a huge wall to climb of any theory on DHA.

Bio-fractal, continue your search for your Rx, is all I can say. But I am done discussing where he falls short. This site is not about Peat and never will be after my time spent with him. If you want it to be, please go to his site and do your thing there. Here, we understand that the light environment has a massive role in DHA biochemistry. Bazan's work shows that.

We know you like Peat's ideas.........but a guy like Feynman made the best point every about theories and reality. In nature, we have the best results from the experiment on DHA. **DHA has been present for 600 million years in the eukaryotic tree and NEVER REPLACED EVEN ONCE, even by DPA. If this lipid is bad in anyway, as Peat advocates, just by chance alone you'd expect something else to have been naturally selected for to replace it.** Even though DHA has other competing PUFA's like DPA, that are easier to acquire and create on an energy basis 9thermodynamics), it remains unchallenged in eukaryotic cell membranes.

This raises the question of why nature has specficially continued to use it exclusively even when centralized uncontrolled experiments show it has an ugly side. Feynman's insight here bears repetition. When you realize that is nature's choice for this extended, any theory that bastardizes DHA like Peat's has has to explain this result. Nothing Peat has proposed can describe it. No matter how many bad things he finds in lab experiments, he matters under the crucible of nature. His ideas and theories are attractive to centralized thinkers like you and Yew, and many may find the experiments "good in theory", but fails under Feynman's test mentioned above. If nature has used it exclusively for over 600 million years, we should not question its use, regardless of whether we have contrary proof from a lab experiment that it might be a problem. This tells us that the experiments in some way are profoundly flawed and we are missing the reason why. That is why I cannot in ANY way support Peat's theories on DHA. Why do I echo Feynman's viewpoint here? Life is lived in nature, under solar and darkness and not in a lab under blue light.

Are you a bit lost because everything in life seems contradictory? In QED, all possibilities exist, so there is no contradiction. However, there is a lapse in understanding the implications of the QED mechanisms. That is where Peat and I differ. Understand them, and contradictions vanish if you don't persist with posts like this.

If you don't want someone sucking the life out of you....take out the straw.

The straw has been removed, sir.

# Thread: G'day from Perth, Western Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-perth-western-australia.17124/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chris Houston — 2015-11-15T04:43:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-perth-western-australia.17124/post-182000>

Hi all, very pleased to join the forum.

My name is Chris, and I'm living in Perth, WA. I work a FIFO job and spend half of my time in Leonora in a mining town. I work in I.T, so needless to say I'm a little worried about my career choice.

My journey began a few years ago after I hurt myself at the gym yet again. Every 6 months at the gym i would inevitably do some kind of injury to myself. This time my shoulder was completely messed up. I spent thousands of dollars on Physio visits, and countless hours doing my own personal pain relief exersizes.

After doing this for a year and having only slight improvement in my pain, i by chance went to see Damian Porter @ Eat Well Move Well as recommended by an old friend from School. He shed light on the Ketogenic diet, amongst other things, which i had never heard of in my life.

I followed the Ketogenic diet strictly for 3 months. After this 3 months, my pain and inflammation causing movement restriction was 90% subsided. I had a severe case of Keto Flu for nearly the entire 3 months, and I left like i was on another planet mentally, but physically the results were miraculous.

Since that time I have been doing a lot of research, and Jack's information i find to be the most insightful. The interviews between Jack and Tristan blew my mind. I had to know more. I haven't jumped all the way down the rabbit hole yet, but I am focusing my energy on moving in the right direction.

I eat a Ketogenic diet most of the time, i do however slip up occasionally. I've started cold showering fairly regularly and will start using face dunking soon. I use Flux on my computer screen at night, or wear orange glasses to limit blue light intake. I sleep very well (always have) but still felt like crap in the morning. I am feeling a lot more refreshed in the morning now. I find that i don't even need coffee, but i use it at around 9:00 for my bullet proof coffee.

Now that my pain is fixed, my focus is to keep my skin clear from Acne. I was always troubled by Acne during my teenage years and into adulthood. I tried all of the medications on the market. I had a reaction whilst taking minocylin and got jaundice and was really sick in hospital with a poorly liver. I then took Roaccutane which worked effectively, but had extremely nasty side effects. I started getting very bad cystic acne whilst it was clearing and a few years after. I have been on 3 courses of Roaccutane since around 2005. They say it gives you depression, which i did eventually get diagnosed with, although i don't think it was because of that medication. I didn't agree with that diagnosis and refused to take any anti depressants. I fixed the issues in my life at the time and my anxiety/depression subsided.

I am moving to Bali, Indonesia for a year to practice Yoga and meditation to improve my mitochondria and overall health. I will be much nearer to the ecuator so hopefully that will also be beneficial with the amount of sunlight i can get. (Not that i have a huge issue with that in WA). It will be an interesting journey and I am hoping that persisting with the Ketogenic diet over there will be easy enough. I'm sure there will always be plenty of Fish and Avocados!

Lastly my primary focus, which is a dream more than anything else, is to improve my brain function and memory. A lot of my depression stems from my constant battle with myself for not being clever enough. I've always felt like i was destined for great things but i wasn't born with the capacity to achieve it. I used to be a clever kid and after taking the Epilepsy medication throughout my childhood my grades plummeted and i was never the same. I try not to be too hard on myself but it's a thought in the back of my mind at all times which I may never be able to let go of. I keep thinking that maybe eventually i'll discover the keys to the kingdom and open up what I've been waiting for for so long. Here's to hoping.

Sorry for the life story.
Pleased to meet you.

Chris

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-15T06:29:17+0200

### Simon Wright — 2015-11-16T14:52:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-perth-western-australia.17124/post-182062>

Chris,

If you are moving to Indo you will find the we get ample sun......and its better quality sun than Au ie you wont burn as much.

UV light doesnt come into the picture until 8-8.30am so you can do alot of morning exposure. If you greet the sun this will give you 2 hours of quality AM sun.

I suggest you load up on DHA and eat loads of goood quality fish and oysters in the meantime before you move here. I get my fish (and occasional king prawns...you gotta make sure they come from the ocean) from a place called Sakanaya on Jl Sunset in Seminyak. Its the only place i trust for seafood here (have lived here 15 years)

Get a house with a bath and you can do CT after March.

Water here is way better than Aus and there is heaps of mgnetite sand here so you can use this to structure your water as well.

Food here is great....eat local....and organic if u can

You can really load yourself UV here as well.

CHeers

SImon

# Thread: Where to live in this world, if there were no other humans?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-live-in-this-world-if-there-were-no-other-humans.17125/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-11-15T19:19:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-live-in-this-world-if-there-were-no-other-humans.17125/post-182034>

Simple question:What are the best places(aka most optimal for biology) to live in this world, if there were either no other humans? or if other humans didn't change the natural environment at all, if we forget all manmade bs, alterations to the ground, surface, atmosphere.

I am guessing water would be ok everywhere.
I am guessing in between tropics would be better for light.
I am guessing places with increased magnetism. Places with big recent craters like:mexico with chicxlub crater(kt), india with shiva crater(kt) Or along volcanoes, and ocean ridges/trenches.
Guessing along the sea for easy seafood.
Probably place with lot of rainfall, dense vegetation.

# Thread: Women exposing to morning sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2015-11-15T20:11:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182036>

Other than some of the men here that I see mention hanging out in shorts only in the mornings to get sun, are there any Women on the forum who are exposing more than their eyes to the morning sun?

Please share! What and how do you do it?

If it's raining, do you go out and let it rain on you?
Do you tolerate feeling cold if it is or are you cold adapted?

Please ladies tell it would feel very encouraging.

### nicld — 2015-11-15T21:25:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182041>

I do on the weekends. Now that my "safe" spot is gone I sit on my back steps (which is somewhat protected) with a tube dress on and let the girls and lady bits get some sun. Recently even though it had been colder, the sun is so warming it feels awesome. We will see how it goes when it gets colder.

### Patty Cakes — 2015-11-15T22:05:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182042>

I am in So Cal and the cooler weather is just starting but still is warm. I lie out without anything on, on a part of the deck that cannot be seem by anyone. Sometimes I just go out on the big part of the deck and sit with shorts and a camisole to get the most sun.

### thomas — 2015-11-15T22:34:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182044>

As my friend nike would say: just do it.

### Lahelada — 2015-11-15T23:32:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182045>

Hello,
Yes I go out when it rains. As far as cold adaptation goes it is a gradual thing. Starts s low as you think you want to and then go a bit lower. You want to be uncomfortable but not freezing to death .
I have garden and I sit out with large pareo on a director chair. From behind you have no idea that Iam in the altogether and from the front I cannot be overlooked. If anybody comes in to the shared garden the pareo closes in no time. On the floor in front of an open window /patio door is also a good way if you cannot be outside fully unclothed.

### Penny — 2015-11-16T02:29:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182050>

I go out au natural in my back yard from the time I wake up until I have to cart someone somewhere... I was out in the rain this morning - I was cold at first when I got out there, but gradually I adapted - the key is to move around - that has always been the key for me anyways... sitting is the kiss of death - my yard is looking a lot better now There is always something to do out there -

I was sunbathing on the trampoline this summer but then a bee crawled into my shorts - well, I don't think I can take another hit like that anytime soon... it was an unholy reaction - but nettle stopped it almost instantly - or it was done by then who knows -

### Mystic Rose60 — 2015-11-16T12:51:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182059>

I'm in Michigan and per Jack's advice, basked in the nude during the spring, summer and part of the fall, but now that the trees, etc. have shed their leaves, I can be seen. So, since I'm cold adapted now, I sit out in the A.M in my bikini or some short shorts and a tank top and bask and am very comfortable. I prefer the bikini though because I can at least maneuver some of my girls out of the top to the sun that way. I plan on spending much of the winter months outdoors and harvesting as much uv as I can that way.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-16T14:58:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182063>

cold, sunlight with Visible and UVA still perform the quantum magic in you..........because NO is still released from your skin.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-16T14:58:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182064>

They make UV permeable suits for ladies........FYI

### thomas — 2015-11-16T15:34:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182068>

check tanthrough.com
Everyone on this forum should have those clothes for the times when we can't be all naked. Just saying.

### nonchalant — 2015-11-16T17:58:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182077>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC382790/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC382790/)

> That textile which has the largest openings between its threads transmits the most ultra-violet rays.

I think water is probably the best transmitter of UV light. Maybe there's something to those Spring Break wet T-shirt contests?

### Marbear — 2015-11-18T00:36:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182223>

I laid out all summer naked to get my UVB - on weekends and during lunch breaks. On weekends now, I've been sitting outside on the east side of the house first thing in the morning to get sun in my eyes and with my jacket open to let the girls have sun too. It has been low 30s lately but the sun feels nice. I spend 30 minutes to an hour out. I'm not cold adapted but trying to work my way to it. Hubby knows how much I don't like being cold so he's pleasantly surprised at the sight he now gets to see

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-11-18T00:56:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182226>

Depending on whether I go to work or not, I either sit outside in the morning sun while drinking my coffee, or I'll CT in the morning sun, and then warm up in the sun wearing a bikini. I'm in NM and we've had a fair amount of rain this fall while I was cold adapting, so I would just CT in the tank in the rain. Now, it's full on winter with snow last night. It was sunny this morning, so I shoveled the snow with no jacket, no hat. This afternoon I soaked in the CT tank in the sun, and then warmed up in the sun. I'm getting a nice tan!

### Curves — 2015-11-18T20:15:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182273>

Thanks ladies for making me feel both inspired and wimpy at the same time LOL.

For me cold-adapting has been in stages. In San Francisco, in the low 50s, swimming in the ocean -- no problem. In Oregon, in the 40s and 30 still working on it. When it's not raining, I sit outside first thing in the am, in my open bathrobe and wait for the sun to rise. Maybe half an hour. Although I have privacy to sit as naked as I want, I'm not fully adapted so not fully naked. I'm meticulous about getting sun in my eye first thing in the morning but then, I start to feel "unproductive" and conflicted, like I should be inside writing and working. I must admit that that conflict messes with my head.

Once thing that helps me with me minimize my conflict" is sitting outside with a note pad (I really am brilliant first thing in the morning), reading a book or one of Jack's blogs. A

This morning, at 9:00 my bf and I went mushroom hunting outside for about half an hour and it was awesome.

Right now, I'm watching the light reflect off the water droplets from the rain yesterday and it's a truly spectacular light show.

Who knows, maybe in a few months, I'll be sitting outside in the rain like @[Chrysanthemama](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1795/), @[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) and @[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/)

### Lahelada — 2015-11-19T14:17:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182320>

@[Curves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1418/) just one minute then two...

### Curves — 2015-11-19T22:25:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182376>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=182320)
>
> @[Curves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1418/) just one minute then two...

@[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) you made me smile and motivated me this morning -- 5 minutes outside in the rain in a short-sleeved T-shirt. I wasn't naked but not entirely covered up either. I must admit, it did feel great. Thanks for the encouragement.

### lilreddgirl — 2015-11-21T00:06:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182436>

wow Thank you okie dokie this does feel encouraging. I hope we can keep it going to keep track of how all of us are doing.

So far myself I'm working on just staring at the sun when I wake up (it's not strong now) and blinking. Built up from 5 to 10 to 20 to 30 minutes now. I am not honestly looking forward to shivering in the cold out there but am thinking of shorts and short tanktop when I start. (I had just built up my cold tolerance with ice baths but let that go while weaning off some medicine that I'm almost off of... then back to the ice baths).

Question: does it have to be direct sunlight on our skin? Or do we just have to be outside... the sun shines into my yard more directly starting around noon, I don't catch the sunrise in the east...

Second question: will I actually start to Feel the benefits/effects of the UV? So far I'm just noticing that I'm starting to 'enjoy' blinking at the sun...

### Scompy — 2015-11-21T05:12:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-exposing-to-morning-sun.17126/post-182456>

> [lilreddgirl said:](/goto/post?id=182436)
>
> wow Thank you okie dokie this does feel encouraging. I hope we can keep it going to keep track of how all of us are doing.
>
> So far myself I'm working on just staring at the sun when I wake up (it's not strong now) and blinking. Built up from 5 to 10 to 20 to 30 minutes now. I am not honestly looking forward to shivering in the cold out there but am thinking of shorts and short tanktop when I start. (I had just built up my cold tolerance with ice baths but let that go while weaning off some medicine that I'm almost off of... then back to the ice baths).
>
> Question: does it have to be direct sunlight on our skin? Or do we just have to be outside... the sun shines into my yard more directly starting around noon, I don't catch the sunrise in the east...
>
> Second question: will I actually start to Feel the benefits/effects of the UV? So far I'm just noticing that I'm starting to 'enjoy' blinking at the sun...

Great to hear! I always go for the direct sunlight, and the morning sun is really weak rignt now here in Seattle. When I got out into the sun earlier in the week around 10am, I felt like the sun was powering me through the CT and helping the workout. I think that was the major factor for zapping the cold I had coming on. For 'feeling the effects of the UV', I'm feeling a good dopamine hit from being in the morning sun, especially in the first few minutes after waking up. Tomorrow morning looks to be clear and 29F, so I'm combining four things at the same time after a large glass of water: CT, light morning weight-lifting/anaerobic routine, grounding and skin/eye exposure to sunlight in just shorts. Then about 30mins later, I'll protein-up with the leptin RX. I've noticed that the weight-lifting in frigid weather really helps to take the edge off of the CT. I'm also going to cold dunk my face before getting out into the 29F.

I like to pan around the sky to get light shining onto all parts of my RPE. Once the sun is visible, I don't stare into it directly, but look towards it and I keep a motion going while doing a lot of blinking. I'm also curious what others do. I get a lot of fog up here like in the Bay area (used to live there), so it can be entrancing when the sun is a disc through it, but still don't stare directly at it for more than 5-10 seconds or so.

# Thread: IR sauna and UV light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-sauna-and-uv-light.17128/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2015-11-16T15:33:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-sauna-and-uv-light.17128/post-182067>

Hi All,

I have purchased an IR sauna and have been trying different things with it. One thing i do twice sometime three times a week is take a 10-15min sauna at between 50-55c (between 11am to 12 pm), lay in the sun (naked) for 15-20 min, then jump back in the sauna for 10 mins then lay the otherside of my body in the sun for 15-20 mins. I do not use soap on any part of my body for 2 days after this.

My general feeling from this is i really feel charged from this the next day In addition i do not burn at all and my colour is a much darker chocolate than usual. (Although i am caucasian i am rather dark skinned)

To note i live 7 degrees south of the Equator so there is abundant UV.

My rationale with this is as i am bringing more of my blood to the surface via the action of the sauna and i am charging my plasma with the abundant UV light. (i usually cover my face and only expose my body).

In addition this seems to affect my redox potential alot as i can have a few (3) wines after and it doesnt really affect me.

As well to note i am having a few moles (dark spots) dissappearing from my body doing this.

Regards

Simon

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-16T17:34:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-sauna-and-uv-light.17128/post-182073>

Simon be careful with too much NIR sauna.......long term chronic over use leads to fibrosis of the muscles.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-16T17:35:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-sauna-and-uv-light.17128/post-182074>

If there is abundant UV at your location there is a huge amount of IR too......so you dont need a NIR sauna in my opinion. May 2015 webinar was clear message........nothing replaces the sun

>
> Simon be careful with too much NIR sauna.......long tern chronic over use leads to fibrosis of the muscles.

HI Jack,

Many thanks for the clarification. It is a Far Infrared Sauna. But thanks i will bring the use of it down.

Also i have listened to all your last 6 months blogs repeatatively and am going to turn my sauna into a light /mediation box. I am going to fit it out with 650nm, 680nm and 880 nm and am also looking at the ultra violet (Purple) range to mitigate my work hours. I have a clothing factory and spend a quite a few hours in front of my computer everyday.

Best

Simon

### Matt Justice — 2019-02-26T16:48:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-sauna-and-uv-light.17128/post-263983>

> [jewishhacker said:](/goto/post?id=263973)
>
> I thought most sauna studies show that sauna almost everyday is great for long term health, or is that because most saunas studies are done in dry saunas?

Dr. Rhonda Patrick usually points to some studies of reducing all cause mortality in other parts of the world. My hunch is that you have to consider the ritual/social aspect in other regions, way of life, and simply heat stressing the body regularly compared to sitting in a chair all day in a dense city. (which is what most of them are comparing this to)

Of course I'm not a doctor, so I can't explain it in medical terms, and am naive when it comes to the science... but sweating 4+ times per week, temp heat shock, intermittent CT, and the community/social aspect typically done with your tribe alone... is WAY more connected than sitting in a cubicle for 8 hours a day, unwinding with a bottle of wine after your stressful commute, and "talking" to friends on Facebook.

Physical. Mental. Emotional. Community.

# Thread: Yes so I'm a horse's ass!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes-so-im-a-horses-ass.17129/>

### cmegan — 2015-11-16T21:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes-so-im-a-horses-ass.17129/post-182090>

The importance of reading all blogs!
So, I realized last week that I missed a few very important blogs posts by Jack...and now I know that contact lenses and RX glasses block UV light. I've been gazing at the sun every day in the morning and spending so much time outside for the past 3 years with my contact lenses on. Well, every morning now I drive to the beach wearing my old RX glasses, take them off when I get there, and allow my eyes to get a few hours of sun. What a difference! For the first few days I could hardly even look at the sky! I feel like my whole world has opened up, thank you Jack.

Jack's recent blogs have really been blowing me away...it's all about the sun...so simple. When I think about so many people walking around with RX glasses and contacts in, coupled with EMF, not grounding, being in office environments all day, no wonder everyone is getting so sick!

My new goal is to get RX glasses without any UV coating (does that even exist? I haven't been able to find any companies stating so) and work on improving my eyesight...

Has anyone found any companies with making glasses without the UV coating?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes-so-im-a-horses-ass.17129/post-182094>

Blocking one part will regulate another.

### prAna303 — 2015-11-16T21:34:32+0200

# Thread: Dona's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/donas-journal.17131/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dona Inman — 2015-11-16T22:17:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/donas-journal.17131/post-182101>

INTRODUCTION
Name: Dona (pronounced Donna)
Age: 55yrs
Height: 5' 7" Weight: 317lbs (down from 404 on Jan 17, 2015)
General Complaints: Morbid Obesity, Peripheral Circulatory Insufficiency (dark, dry, itchy spots on both shins), Chronic Plaque Psoriasis & (back of head & eyebrows)

I live in NE Oklahoma. Rural 15 acres. Small house on an unsealed concrete slab. I live with my two youngest children (ages 17 & 20). My husband and I have been separated for nearly 4yrs.....both are comfortable leaving things just separated. Stupid relationship.
I still raise a bottle calf or two every year. Just filled the freezer w/ beef & bones last month. We also keep a heritage breed of hog that yields a large pork roast 1-2 times each week. I make 2-3 gallons of Roasted Bone Broth at least once a week and have just recently begin using it to fast 2-3 days each week. Lots of seasonal forages during warm weather, but nothing in great quantity.
Retail-wise, in-season, I buy raw milk & eggs from a neighbor. I manage a grocery buying club drop site for Azure Standard.com and so typically always have organic eggs, butter & cheese on hand to compliment my homegrown meats. I dont like veggies very much....except avocados and tomatoes of course.

Im a student online in the Nutritional Counseling program offered ny Trinity School of Natural Health. Im learning to read my body chemistry via urine & saliva pH, conductivity, metabolic sugars, salts & proteins. Well aware I need more water, more greens and probably a calcium supplement. I HATE pills though, so hope to make up for the supplement w/ sunshine. I say that as I listen to the rain outside though....0 out of 10 UV here most of this week. ::sigh::

I am collecting the equipment needed to install an indoor NIR sauna and an outdoor shower/bath inside the next year. Ive changed out most all the lightbulbs in the house, but really just prefer only using red light & candles after sunset. Family complains a lot, but i just send them to their rooms and go about my business most of the time - lol. Im drinking 10oz while standing outside barefoot every morning now days...often times in my nightgown - lol. Good thing I dont have nearby neighbors.

One step at a time. Got rid of my cell phone and turned off wifi at the house last month. Now only have a land line phone....dont miss the cell. Now when i leave home its more of an adventure - lol. Nobody can find me <wink>. Today Im shopping for an affordable water distiller. Just stopped in here to read a bit more and see if a brand was suggested. Looking forward to chatting soon.

### FutureVision — 2015-11-16T23:15:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/donas-journal.17131/post-182104>

Welcome Dona. Sounds like you are off to a great start! I can tell you now, being someone that also has psoriasis, that living up north is a challenge and a detriment especially since we need the sun to heal and it just isn't in sufficient quantities or quality up north. Jack suggested to me that I need to looking into reptile bulbs and/or tanning beds over the winter. Probably something you need to look into as well.

Have you looked at the Leptin RX yet and cold thermogenesis?

# Thread: A greater Curiosity has brought me here.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8

### Sabertooth — 2015-11-18T01:59:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/post-182228>

Greetings y'all, I have been following a low carb raw paleo diet for over 6 years now, which has helped me resolve and recover from a number of health issues.

I have would like to continue my learning and thought it would be helpful cruse the forum here for a while and see what wisdom may come of it. I am fascinated by the ways in which the body is able to heal and rejuvenate itself, and wish to apply what I learn toward developing lifestyle practices which promote optimal health. I am already fairly familiar with much of the subject matter being presented hear, and look forward to joining some of the cutting edge discussions.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-18T02:00:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/post-182229>

### Josh Rosenthal — 2015-11-18T02:02:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/post-182230>

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-18T02:15:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/post-182233>

You too Josh.........get a pic up.

### Sabertooth — 2015-11-18T02:45:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/post-182241>

It is done,

I have found the interviews, and much of the information here, to be very much in-tune with my own intuitions and am very interested in joining these discussions.

My personal experiences regarding the paleo diet may also be of interest to some here, being a person who has lived on a diet where most of my calories coming from whole raw sheep, with roughly 60% of total calories coming from sheep fat. Very low carb, mostly from greens, some vegetables, and coconut.

A short summary of my past issues- I was diagnosed chronic fatigue, with autoimmune issues, chronic viral infections (including viral meningitis, and shingles) fungal overgrowth, weak digestion, elevated blood sugar. I was working as an electrician and believe I was exposed to environmental toxicity,, electromagnetic overload( I've been hit with high voltage more times than I can count) as well as suffering from food intolerance, and many other subtle issues which I'm still not sure how to describe.

Though diet is of great importance, my primary interest in coming here is learning how to best mitigating harmful electrical exposures, and working on ways to align myself with the cycles of the earth, and the energy of the cosmos.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-18T03:42:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/post-182242>

Sabertooth........everything funnels to 3 core things: Light, water, and magnetism. When you get to the ubiquitination series you will find that food is not as important in the grand scale of things. In fact, only DHA matters a great deal.

Here is a sample of what those blogs contain:[http://www.beastified.com/bp1/2015/9/19/bp3-dr-jack-kruse](http://www.beastified.com/bp1/2015/9/19/bp3-dr-jack-kruse)

Even sleep is about light. Ironic huh? Or maybe quantum?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-18T03:43:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/post-182243>

The EMF bootcamp is an ideal way for you to begin. I would also suggest reading Dr. Andrew Marino's book called Going Somewhere.

### Sabertooth — 2015-11-19T04:45:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-greater-curiosity-has-brought-me-here.17135/post-182304>

Loved the Quantum sleep episode, this is definitely inspiring some quantum shifts in my own realm of awakening.

I would be curious as to your opinion on if it is possible to get optimal DHA from sheep?- keep in mind that I eat whole fully mature mutton- all the glands , organs, fats, blood, ect... It just seems that I am so uniquely adapted, and I don't like eating much fish or sea food. On occasion I do eat and enjoy shellfish, and even the occasional mackerel, but there seems to be an instinctive "stop" which limits how much I consume. Often times after eating shellfish, I will have a huge uplift in mental energy and libido, followed by a kind of crash, and then lose of apatite for any sea food. Its a very unique situation, and I am not sure if there is an easy explanation, but my body prefers Mutton fats to fish fats.... or even to beef, other land animals, including immature sheep(Lamb) which seem to be lacking in something?
My occasional cravings for sea food may just as much be because of a need for trace minerals, as the need for fatty acids.

I have been recently attempting to reset my sleep cycle, after falling into the habit of staying up past midnight and sleeping in to 10am. I have typically been in tune with the solar cycle, and have awaken at dawn for years, but since getting with my girlfriend three years ago, and becoming self employed I've gotten out of tune. She stays up late typically, with all the lights and devices on, and because I am no longer having to be a a job at a certain time, I have been staying up late with her and sleeping in(even though it hasn't been feeling optimal)

I am also really focusing on finding ways to get healthy light exposure through the up coming winter. I have gotten rid of all the florescent lights in the house and am experimenting with mixing different types of full spectrum and infrared lighting........... I was wondering what the opinion is on red light therapy beds? I have used them in the past and they feel wonderful, but the only one in town went out of business a couple of years ago.....There is a new one just opening up and I was thinking about using its regularly through the winter, as a supplement to what ever other natural sun I can get?

I had S>A>D as a teenager, and have always been extra sensitive to being cooped up indoors under florescent lighting all day, like I was forced into being, during my school years. I also suffered from insomnia , which was probably not helped by having a TV in my room as a child. Eventual I began to understand more about what was going on and learned intuitively in my early adult life about sun gazing in the morning and would always find time to be in the sun whenever possible, but now it seems that simply spending a day here or a day there doing shirtless yard work, isn't enough sun exposure to optimize ones energy, and so much of my work is inside.

Being just recently in London on a journey reminded me of the discussion in the interview regarding the UK... I concur with there being some kind of environmental overload of bad energy in places like London. It seemed that every passer by in the street had a kind of sadness or anxiousness that seemed ubiquitous to the area. Besides being cloudy during the day, it seemed that every place I went at night was lite with compact florescent bulbs, which give off a very dull and depressing spectrum of light, the air also seemed bad, and lord knows the kind of EMF tidal waves bathing over every square inch of everybody ( It was always absurd to me, as an electrician when even people that could afford more natural lighting would chose to live their lives under depressing florescent lighting in order to save a few dollars a month in energy cost)

Paris wasn't much better, and I am beginning to suspect that the dark energy of our technological machinery is looming heavily over the mega cities of the world, keeping the inhabitants from living in vibrant fullness which comes from being nourished being nourished by less polluted environmental conditions.

The real eye opening experience that has inspired me to make some changes was the three days I spent in Nice France, with a couple of Paleo friends, one of whom is a disciple here. We spent all day out on the beach in the sun, swimming in the cold water, everything was absolutely beautiful. After Ten days under the clouds of northern Europe, I was absolute starving for the sun, and after one day of basking on the beach felt instantly replenished. At night we used candles and slept in darkness, without any electronic devices and awoke with the sun and went straight to the beach and ate breakfast outside.

After returning to Kentucky, despite the jet lag, it seems that those three days of basking in the sun and following some basic light hygiene protocol has reset my circadian rhythm, and since I have been back, I've been waking at dawn without any effort. I would like to continue progressing toward optimizing my home environment, and strengthening these habits.

# Thread: Redox is a function of time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-is-a-function-of-time.17138/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-19T15:00:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-is-a-function-of-time.17138/post-182321>

Education formats us and places limits on our ideas. Innovation invites failure because you never know what is next. Evolution welcome mistakes because they allow rapid adaptations to alien environments to lead to stability. Evolution rewards errors. Curiosity spurs innovative thinking because it invites risk and embraces failure. Innovation allows us be the first, be unique, and to be different. Innovation is more important than knowledge because it extends what we know into areas where there is a wisdom vacuum. You cannot lose and you can only win when you push boundaries. What is time? Where does it come from? Is it primordial or is it emergent? Is your time the same as everyone else? Is time the basis of N=1 in the quantum world? Today you get to unlearn some old stuff to re learn some new ideas. [https://www.jackkruse.com/time-2-how-is-time-built/](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-2-how-is-time-built/)

### Penny — 2015-11-20T01:49:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-is-a-function-of-time.17138/post-182390>

I can't believe that SCIO spectrometer... unreal - someone gets it at least...

[https://www.consumerphysics.com/myscio/scio](https://www.consumerphysics.com/myscio/scio)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-20T15:56:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-is-a-function-of-time.17138/post-182423>

Now that you have learned about gravitational lensing today and how it affects light, does anyone want to solve why astronauts eyes get ruined in space due to refractive and accommodation errors? Might it be that space dictates the eye can and cannot sense when under the influence of an "alien sun" 24/7? This environment affects the size and shape of the globe, lens and retina to give you a new reality of symptoms. Might is be why astronauts get pseudo tumor like eye findings? You bet your ass it does, you environment creates the reality you get.

# Thread: Labs and Reactions to Everything
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### krw2586 — 2015-11-21T22:39:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-182486>

I was hoping someone could share their thoughts on my labs. Sorry in advance for the long post. I'm just at a loss for what to do!

I've had health issues since 2012 including Lyme, severe chemical sensitivity, toxic mold exposure, methylation issues, heavy metals, etc. I have seen many amazing functional medicine doctors over the years and they have helped some issues, but not most of it. This past February, I had a sudden crash and have not been able to recover. It followed a ketogenic diet experiment, which was suggested for some neurological issues. Ironically, it helped a lot of things so I wish it didn't crash me. At the same time I had also been on way too high of a dose of T3 for supposed "thyroid resistance". I think it damaged something... Anyway the keto diet made me sicker than I have ever felt, I believe due to dumping an excessive toxin load. Since then, my adrenals are completely shot, my POTS has returned, my energy is basically zero, my gastroparesis / paralytic ileus is the worst it's ever been, I don't even tolerate simple vitamins and minerals, and I've been gaining weight rapidly (45 pounds since February after weighing the same (about 105) basically my whole life). I truly don't know what to do. I have even tried a ketogenic diet again, hoping it would undo whatever it did in the first place, but I have a hard time getting into ketosis despite eating the same as I did before. I'm also staying very puffy on a low carb diet - I'm not letting go of excess fluid like I usually do when I enter ketosis. Fluid is a big issue in general - every morning I wake up swollen and my lymph nodes hurt. Once I'm up and moving around, it drains down to my legs and then they feel swollen... if I lay flat my legs feel better, but then my face and underarm lymph nodes swell again. The fluid just shifts. It's also noticeably worse if I react to something, like a food, chemical, or EMFs. I know inflammation is a problem. I can't take electrolytes because they give me instant low blood sugar (I think it's the potassium) and a ragey, burst-into-tears type of adrenaline surge. Minerals do the same thing. Earthing also does the same thing, and so do ionic foot detox baths. I think there is a problem with electrons?? It's a fairly severe and immediate response - within 30 seconds of all of these things.

I also react with major adrenaline, rage, feeling overwhelmed, worse POTS to anything that is supposed to be energizing - like ubiquinol, pqq, vitamin d (and major fluid retention on that one), amino acids, coconut oil, coleus, B6, etc. I've been trying to follow the Leptin Rx, but eating first thing in the morning completely wipes me out even with zero carbs. I feel almost drugged/drunk from it, even taking high dose enzymes and ox bile. And my attempts at Cold Thermogenesis are equally as difficult. I actually like the cold (heat makes my BP way too low and my POTS much worse), but I tend to react sort of extreme after being very cold. Once I'm warm again, I get way too hot, flushed, sweaty, anxious, irritable, worse POTS.... my system tends to overreact to everything. And sunlight gives me migraines with lots of neuro sx like dizziness, slurred speech, vision loss, etc. I feel high from it. Help!

Anyway, here are some labs with their ranges and with notes. Does anything make sense? I have been reading/researching for years and am more than ready to learn. I've just reached a point where I need help. I did recently discover mold in my apartment, but I've lived here for almost 2 years so I'm not sure why it took about a year to crash.... could that be causing all of this?

TSH: 1.31 \[0.45-4.5\] This was suppressed (about 0.04) for about 2 years from thyroid meds. I'm still taking the same dose, but it's suddenly not suppressed anymore... why? I take 1 grain of WP Thyroid plus 25mcg T3 SR. Thyroid labs were done fasting, before taking meds.
Free T4: 0.76 LOW \[0.82-1.77\]
Free T3: 3.0 \[2.0-4.4\]
Reverse T3: 8.9 LOW \[9.2-24.1\]
Cortisol am, fasting: 21.2 \[4.0-22.0\] This is higher than my norm
Aldosterone: 19 \[<= 28\] This used to be high, over 50. Suddenly it dropped and cortisol went up.
Estradiol: 22 \[19-144\] On day 7
Progesterone: 0.1 \[<= 2.7\] On day 7. Very low for me, but I react to progesterone supplementation
Prolactin: 16.6 \[4.8-23.3\] This is a bit higher than normal
Pregnenolone: 55 \[7-188\] I get migraines/seizure activity from pregnenolone supplementation
hs-CRP: 0.2
Insulin: 6.7 \[2.0-19.6\]
DHEA-S: 63 \[18-391\] This is lower than normal
A1c: 5.7 High \[<5.7\]
Vitamin D3 1,25 (OH)2: 53 \[no range given\]
Vitamin D, 25 OH, total: 18 LOW \[30-100\] I've never had low D before
Sodium: 139 \[135-146\]
Potassium: 4.5 \[3.5-5.3\]
Chloride: 104 \[98-110\]
Carbon Dioxide: 21 \[19-30\]
Glucose: 95 \[65-99\]
BUN: 10 \[7-25\]
Creatinine: 0.87 \[0.5-1.1\]
Calcium: 9.9 \[8.6-10.2\] My calciums is frequently a little high
Protein, total: 7.3 \[6.1-8.1\]
Albumin: 4.5 \[3.6-5.1\]
Globulin, calculated: 2.8 \[1.9-3.7\]
Bilirubin, total: 0.3 \[0.2-1.2\]
AST: 34 HIGH \[10-30\] Both AST and ALT are often high
ALT: 23 \[6-29\]
Alkaline Phosphate: 65 \[33-115\]
VIP: 29 \[<75\] This used to be 41.8, it has gotten worse
VEGF: 44 \[31-86\] This used to be 10, so it has gotten better
Leptin: 34.8 HIGH \[4.7-23.7\] This used to be 4.4! Much worse?!
ADH: 0.9 \[0.0-4.7\]
MSH: 18 \[0-40\] This has been low for years

Saliva Cortisol:
6-8am: 8 LOW \[13-24\]
11-1pm: 4 LOW \[5-10\]
4-5pm: 1 LOW \[3-8\]
10-midnight: 1 NORMAL \[1-4\]

THANK YOU THANK YOU THANK YOU to whoever was patient enough to read all of this

### kyrakitty — 2016-10-15T18:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-202853>

I had these exact same issues as you quoted below:

Mine started in 2009 "I've had health issues since 2012 including Lyme, severe chemical sensitivity, toxic mold exposure, methylation issues, heavy metals, etc."

In 2014 like you "I also react with major adrenaline, rage, feeling overwhelmed." It would feel like someone injected epinephrine into me and other times the rage was occurring with little cause and not under my control. It wasn't a mental state something in my environment was messing with the part of the brain that controls rage.

Keto did the same to me "Anyway the keto diet made me sicker than I have ever felt"

It's something in your environment at home or work. The way to test it if you are able is to avoid your home and work area for a minimum of a week by staying somewhere else that is clean. You could try to book a space for a week or two that is specifically for people with mold, EMF and chemical sensitivities. If you feel better then you have your answer.

When the above was happening to me it was my environment. When I would leave for many days I would get better when I returned I got worse.

CT, keto or any diet, supplements, medications won't help if your environment is bad.

Also I had... and still struggle with too much blue light exposure from computers and phones and hardly spent enough sun. I am currently working on figuring out how to get more sun during the right times for my latitude and how to use supplemental lights to give my body more uvb, uva, red and infrared light spectrums. I haven't figured it out yet but this is necessary along with living and working in a safe clean environment.

### Huck — 2016-10-15T20:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-202862>

Keto was making me sick, although I didn't realize it for years. I recently found in my 23andme genetics lab, that I likely had a problem digesting saturated fat and metabolizing fat. What was happening to me was that after a few weeks on keto diet, I would start having blood pressure spikes and heartburn which mimicked heart problems. Docs kept prescribing more and more meds.

If you are not digesting food properly, all kind of bad things can happen, with inflammation at the top of the list. Plus it can lead to bad bacteria getting into the blood stream.

I decided on my own to cut way back on saturated fats. Immediately the heartburn stopped, blood pressure spikes completely disappeared over the next 3 weeks, inflammation went way down, back pain went away, I started losing weight at a rapid pace, I reduced my bp meds, my PSA dropped from 33 to 10 in 3 weeks (which is an indicator of reduced inflammation.

I have taken thousands of dollars worth of supplements over the years with no improvement in my health. When you throw in insurance and doctors fees, labs, etc I'm guessing the amount is close to $75,000 without a single doctor having a clue.

Now that I have reduced sat fat, I can tell some of the supplements are doing what they are supposed to do. I find I can't eat much coconut oil, eggs, bacon, etc that are high in sat fat or symptoms start coming back. I can use a little coconut oil to cook, but no more eating 2 or 3 tbsp a day.

I'm not saying your problem is saturated fat, but that looking at your genetics might lead to the clue you need to figure out what is causing your problems. For example, my 23andme report also showed I likely had trouble converting sunlight to vitamin d and might have to supplement at higher doses than most people. I already knew that - I take 10,000 IUs in summer and 15,000 IUs in winter, plus get as much sun as I possibly can. If I don't do that, my vitamin d drops below 30.

Hopefully, when we regain our health, our epigenetics will overrule our genes. But when we are in an unhealthy state, our genes may be running things.

ETA: The keto diet and Adrenalin were what got my attention in op. When I was having the BP spikes, my heart rate would jump up to 135, which I assumed was accompanied by a surge in Adrenalin. It would stay there for hours and then slowly come down. Now my heart rate varies from 65 to 85 over the day - higher when exercising.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-211801>

Just wondering what low hanging fruit you can get right...

How is your water intake?
How much seafood do you eat?

Those may be some easy places to start.

Grounding + Sun is giving you some trouble... have you perhaps tried a minimal dose, preferably at sunrise? When I first began, grounding actually increased my inflammation; now it drops it quite quickly.

I have a client with POTS... mitigation of nnEMF has been critical for her. Getting her a magnetico made things even better. A magnetico may be too big of jump for you to start... but ask yourself where you can limit nnEMF. Start with the cell phone and wifi at home.

**ADH: 0.9 \[0.0-4.7\]**

Also known as: Vasopressin; AVP
Formal name: Antidiuretic Hormone; Arginine Vasopressin
Related tests: Osmolality, BUN, Creatinine, Sodium, Urinalysis

ADH deficiency is often seen with one of two types of diabetes insipidus. Central diabetes insipidus is a decrease in the production of ADH by the hypothalamus or in the release of ADH from the pituitary while nephrogenic diabetes insipidus is characterized by a decrease in the kidney's response to ADH. Both types of diabetes insipidus lead to the excretion of large quantities of dilute urine.

diabetes insipidus results from a lack of production of arginine vasopressin (also called antidiuretic hormone or ADH) by the pituitary or
a lack of response of the kidneys to ADH, .......

**DDAVP® Nasal Spray** (desmopressin acetate)

**DDAVP Nasal Spray (desmopressin acetate nasal spray)** is provided as an aqueous solution for intranasal use.

\====================================================

Saliva Cortisol:
6-8am: 8 LOW \[13-24\]
Cortisol,serum am, fasting: 21.2 \[4.0-22.0\]

High totalcortisol when free cortisol is low=high TBG
Thyroid Binding globulin

Elevated TBG levels are associated with influences such as pregnancy, genetic predisposition, oral contraceptives, and estrogen therapy. TBG levels can decrease with androgenic or anabolic steroids, large doses of glucocorticoids, hypoproteinemic states, liver disease, nephrotic syndrome, and congenital TBG variants.

\======================================================

Pregnenolone: 55 \[7-188\] I get migraines/seizure activity from pregnenolone supplementation

DHEA-S: 63 \[18-391\]

Then try to supplement with progesterone and Dhea

### krw2586 — 2017-04-05T20:22:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-211822>

Thanks for the responses! I wasn't getting alerts until today so I'm sorry for missing some of the older replies.

Since the first post I moved out of that apartment and have slowly (painfully slowly!) improved. I think my body had an actual nervous system breakdown. I weaned off my thyroid meds and that helped a lot. I basically had to take away all stress, including meds and supplements. My new home has low RF/EMF readings and I still shut everything down at night. There are lots of windows so my artificial light exposure is much less. And I have been taking extremely small doses of cholestyramine for mold exposure (it binds to toxins). It has helped a lot, but I don't want to stay on it for a long time because it can lower cholesterol and thus interfere with hormones and fat soluble vitamins.

I am now drinking only spring water (so no chlorine or fluoride) and I add sea salt to it, which helps the extreme thirst. I drink about 80-90 oz a day. But last time my ADH was checked it had dropped to 0.5.

I actually feel better eating LESS protein, and it has helped with the ammonia smell in my sweat. I still eat one serving a day and it's usually seafood (mostly shrimp because fish causes histamine reactions).

I still have issues with electron donors. Sun, IR light (mostly just near IR), grounding, and certain supplements can crash me quickly. I'm going to try the very very gradual sun approach.

The saturated fat thing is interesting. I realized I also wasn't handling fat well and cut back on it. I need to look at my 23andMe results and see if I can figure out why. Coconut oil seems to be extremely hard for me to take. It gives me immediate low blood sugar and after some research I discovered that it actually triggers the release of insulin (somewhat strongly). So I think my whole issue has something to do with the interplay between insulin and cortisol. Just recently I tried taking Lauricidin (an MCT) for a gut infection and even an extremely low dose (2 pellets with each meal) messed with my blood sugar and insulin and made my weight jump up. A few years ago I took Lauricidin with no problems, and I was taking 30 pellets with each meal! So it's very odd.

I still cannot take any hormone supplements or vitamin d supplements (vit d supps shut down my kidneys and I stop peeing- even as low as 40 ius a day). And my weight/puffiness is still being stubborn. Oddly, coming off of high dose thyroid meds made no change in my weight at all. Whatever is going on feels bigger than thyroid, like there is an even greater controller of metabolism.

Hopefully if I can start to slowly get more sun and maybe do some very light strength exercises to help any possible insulin resistance, more of this will turn around.

Like you,...I have been plagued by Biotoxin mold illness, lyme and its coinfections for over 40 years and I just learned that this was the root cause of my chronic fatigue all these years.

I also have the CBS mutation which affects my sulfur metabolism and my detox pathways. Like you , i cannot handle coconut oil or coconuts. I believe the reason why you cannot handle them is because you may have sulfur metabolism issue as well that will negatively affect your detox pathways. Because of my CBS mutation I need to limit sulfur containing foods (ketogenic diet makes me ill because of the over consumption of sulfur rich foods) and limit supplements like MSM, NAC, Milk thistle, chlorella, ALA, Garlic, Glutathione, HCL.

By what you mentioned, I believe that you may have the same sulfur issue because you mentioned in a previous post that your sweat smells like ammonia and that is a good sign of sulfur metabolism issues as well.

First off, I believe that the Mold illness needs to be adressed first and foremost. In my opinion, my mold symptoms have been worse than the lyme. The mold issue needs to be addressed ASAP with a super agressive approach especially if you have the HLA Dr1 gene, which I also have. You should have had the full labs done from both Labcorp and quest, and the Marcons should have been competed as well. You should know your VCS (vision contrast test) results so you have that benchmark to go by.

One reason you may feel ill when you eat fat is because of the release of bile will be loaded with mycotoxins. If you don't bind them, as you know they are going to be reabsorbed and cause a cascade of inflammation throughout the body. You could also do the NeuroQuant MRI like i have to see the extent of inflammation in the brain. this can tell you if its Mold related or lyme related.

I believe that you need to follow the protocol to the fullest extent with labs along the way to chart your progress. After staying on cholestyramine for a month or so, then start the nasal spray for the marcons. Once marcons is gone, and you pass your VCS vision test, then you can start the VIP nasal spray. I have made the mistake of quiting before the VIP and I wont do that again.

You said that you moved from one apartment with mold to a new apartment. Did you take all your moldy belonging with you and cross contaminate your new apartment? Have you did the mycometrics.com HERTSMI 2 test in you new apartment? Also, because you have the HLA DR1 gene, are you super cautious of building before entering them. It only takes 10 minutes to reinfect you system with mold. I wear a hepafilter mask when walking into suspicious or old buildings. I don't take any chances now.

This will be a battle for the rest of your life since you have that HLA DR1 Gene. So its always better to be safe than sorry.

Just a note, ...Cholestyramine works well but according to Dr Klinghardt,.. Russian research has shown that Zeolite actually works better. I tried chlorella and because of the sulfur content it made things worse. Garlic which is great for mold, also made me ill because of the sulfur.

Pots is an issue with the autonomic nervous system and is related to mold, Lyme and its coinfections. According to Dr Klinghardt, unless you take care of the Mold first, you won't be able to address the Lyme and coinfections because they tend to hide out in the mold and fungus. This chronic inflamation will destroy you and your mitochondria.

I am a Holistic Health Practitioner, who utilizes ART developed by Dr Klinghardt. Autonomic Response Testing ART does an amazing job of detecting and addressing hidden stressors affecting the Autonomic Nervous System ANS which include a variety of structural, chemical, emotional, EMF, spiritual, stressors. These stressors can cause the chromatin in the cells to emit incoherent light affecting the regulation of the biophoton field around the body. Klinghardt calls this "blocked regulation" of the biophoton field. When this occurs, the Autonomic Nervous System ANS can get stuck in this blocked regulation condition and the result is the body will be stuck in sympathetic fight or flight mode.

This sympathetic mode means that the majority of the blood flow is to the musles, heart, and brain for fight or flight and very little is supplied for healing detoxing , digestion, and restoration. This constant stress may cause adrenal fatigue as well and a cascade of other health issues. Also combined with this blocked regulation, many times a patient will have a scar or autoimmune issue that will cause lateral and bilateral switching which is electrical chaos in the nervous system.

The very first step in healing is addressing the underlying stressors that are causing this incoherent light emissions which are blocking regulation of the biophoton feild and causing the sympathetic response,...... once addressed this turns the incoherent light emissions into coherent light which opens regulation of the biophoton field and moves the Autonomic Nervous System into a parasympathetic healing a restoring mode. Next the switching issues will need to be addressed.

Besides ART,...I personally utilize high quality structured water to form EZ water , maximize the photo electric effect through Natural sun exposure, Ozone therapy for oxygen utilization and to modulate the immune system, HBOT, Eng3 Nano Vi (to repair and refold damaged protiens in the mitochondria), magnetico mattress, near infrared sauna, Distructive Interference Techniques to energetically neurtralize chronic infections, food sensitivity elimination, address genetic mutations, and am off high sulfur foods, (use Molybdemum and magnanese arginine )
