I read on some threads, that members putting iodine on their skin for absorption.
My question would be, is it better absorbed through the skin like magensium? Or is the oral way better?

### Butters — 2016-12-13T22:30:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-to-this-short-panel.17148/post-205233>

I have another question. I checked some things up and it seems in europe they dont measure BUN but urea.
Can you translate urea/creatinine into BUN/creatinine?

urea: 4,5mmol/l - 27,03 mg/dl
creatinine: 91 µmol/l - 1,03 mg/dl

I found, you can calculate BUN= urea x 0,467. That would be 12,62.
That would be a BUN/creatinine of 12,2.
Is that correct?

Hi there,

just introducing myself. You can find info in my bio/below. Nice to meet every one.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

**Age: 22
Sex: M
Joined JackKruse.com: 2015
Location: Rural fishing village, Edinburgh, Scotland, UK

Aiming to cure: suspected dry eye syndrome, Joint pains (wrists, knees, shoulders & back)

Surgeries: One shoulder bone removal operation a few years back on my scapula for rotator cuff pain related issues

Current Rx: Redox (I haven't purchased this but am implementing what I have learned), leptin

Current Therapies / Protocols: magnetico charged/vortexed water | using circuit breaker at night | leptin Rx | magnetico 20 gs | redox Rx | Himalayan crystal | AM UV/IR | Red plastic blue blockers | artificial IR light 140 LED illuminator light 850nm | cold showers | ketogenic diet | seafood every meal (anchovies, mackerel, salmon, herring, sardines, oysters & mussels)

Health Goals: white healthy looking eyes, painless joints, weight loss, fitness, better skin & improved energy (goals not in any particular order).**

### Calum — 2015-11-24T15:24:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calum-from-scotland.17150/post-182570>

Thank you jack. I have moved house to my Dad's mainly because of suspected black mold toxicity (there is visible mold in numerous rooms and water coming in through ceilings which environment I have been living in for over 2 years) and because my mum wouldn't let me turn the circuit breaker off at night which my Mum and Dad think I am off my rocker for lol! There is some black mold in one of the showers I shower in in my new place that I need to irradiate. I am currently planning on purchasing some exo terra lamps once I get the money which I will use when I am not outside which ideas have been transferred from your most recent podcast where you spoke to that guy from the UK. Here is a list of the lamps:

\- [http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/reptile\_uvb100.php](http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/reptile_uvb100.php) (UV)
\- [http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/reptile\_uvb150.php](http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/reptile_uvb150.php) (UV)
\- [http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/reptile\_uvb200.php](http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/reptile_uvb200.php) (UV)
\- [http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/sunray\_bulb.php](http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/sunray_bulb.php) (UV)
\- [http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/solar\_glo.php](http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/solar_glo.php) (UV)
\- [http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/infrared\_basking\_spot\_lighting.php](http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/infrared_basking_spot_lighting.php) (IR)

I won't be buying them all. I haven't decided which ones to buy yet. I am planning on using them them on the natural light cycles here in Scotland when the sun is out. I haven't analysed the specification on all the bulbs, but if I can get a bulb with low UVB or no UVB, I was going to use that when the sun is not at its peak in the sky to simulate natural sun since the UVB is present when the sun is at its peak in the day cycle - I think. I need to do more research into this therapy as I am unsure the duration I should expose myself to "alien" sun and what time to begin therapy would be best.

I think I heard you saying you suggested using blueblockers when using "alien" sun indoors? Is this to block the blue light or is it because "alien" light is harmful to the eyes or some other reason? My eye sight has declined and the next pair of prescription glasses I buy will be blueblockers which I am only going to wear when I need to which will be mostly indoors. I currently use the blueblockers attached after sundown until sleep. I have been using f.lux (maximum minimization of blue light 24/7) on my laptop and a blue light filter app on my phone. At night I have been using red incandescent bulbs or candle light (I open my windows slightly if I have flames on the go) which is attached below also.

I am currently living on the third floor (attic conversion) where I have my desk under my velux to get as much natural light when indoors as possible. I have also been going off my head picking materials up off the beach and decorating my room with them - see pictures - in combination with walks to increase my sun exposure. I went a 30mile walk the other day there with my friend where we caught the sunrise and where outdoors the whole day catching the rays and getting some exercise which I am surprised my knees dealt with. I also have a stool so that I can gain additional height to pop my head out my eastern facing velux to see the sun rise in the morning. And I I have also started eating breakfast in the garden sometimes. I have lived next to one of the most polluting power stations in Europe for my whole life (Cockenzie power station) which is currently getting knocked down - watch the chimneys come down here if you like --->

. It has a huge sub station that I think may be a problem due to its proximity to my household (see picture below) in conjunction with the pylons.

As far as my tap water goes, I am charging it "under" the magnetico with an artificial IR light 140 LED illuminator at 850nm (I need to watch Gerrard Pollock's interviews or read his book to find out what level of IR is best for structuring or at least scientifically proven) shining on the water through the bottles they are contained in which is currently polymer, but I am going to start collecting glass bottles since they are less toxic; after that, I then vortex them using a kids vortexing device which I am going to substitute for another homemade structuring device using spherical objects non-toxic objects and PVC tubing because of the vortexing device's impracticality (it takes too long). Does anyone know if water charges underneath the magnetico just as it supposedly does on top? I need to get a filter to get out fluoride, chlorine, psychotropic drugs and other contaminants. I plan on doing a ton of research before I cash out hundreds of pounds on one which I plan on doing very soon. When I am not drinking tap water I drink polymer bottled Scottish "highland spring" water that is certified organic by the soil association - it undergoes the same treatment as my tap water.

I'm going to make my own soap from lye and grass-fed suet to get away from SLS and paraben. I am also going to make my won toothpaste to to get away from fluoride too which I need to find a recipe for.

My biggest problems are my wrists atm which I need most help with. They have been causing debilitating pain for about 2 years now which is pretty much constant which makes my MSc in civil engineering and construction management really hard. I have realized recently that if I get cold they get sorer which is a annoying because I wanted to start CT but this is keeping me back. I think it has something to do with blood circulation because if I am active and my hands get cold they don't get sore which is because of blood circulation in my opinion. The pain source is presently undiagnosed so I need to get a scan or something. I got an x-ray and my ulnar's are said to be elongated more than normal which may have predisposed me to the injuries I have. I have been to a physiotherapist but that didn't work and wrist operations don't look promising which is why I am so big on self-healing/alternative practices to get myself rehabilitated. I think it is something to do with the tissues so that is where I need to capitalise on most to get the most benefit I think. How can I optimise tissue regeneration or healing rather in my body? I'm trying to get my body in a parasympathetic state.

I only started taking control of my health in about August this year and I have been on a research rampage since then. I went from 96kg about then to 76kg at present but quite a bit of that is water/glycogen weight. I basically went on a full organic wholefoods diet to do that with blueblockers at the time - without exercise. I'm not going to speak about all the things I ate/drank, that would take too long.

I know a fair bit about muscular imbalances and have formulated myself a self-tailored program to deal with my imbalances which should also help - it's mostly stretching.

\- Here is some contextual information for you Jack or anyone else of the forum who thinks they can give me some beneficial advice. I have exams for uni coming up which I am short for time for and this typing ain't making my wrists better so catch you all later

### JanSz — 2015-11-24T22:01:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calum-from-scotland.17150/post-182597>

Cockenzie power station latitude
55.9683° N, 2.9717° W
That is high up North, not much light there, specially not much UV light

concentrate on using mostly your "highland spring" water
remember, EZ water need tight confinement (nanometers) to happen or at least be next to one surface
another way of saying, drink spring water and leave the rest to mother Nature

sore wrists - CT ok, add lots of red led
RubyLux ALL RED High Intensity LED Bulb 640 to 660nm
also IR-A comfortable amount
after XXX time on red light, reduce exposure (not sure of time, weks or months)
likely, when it stops hurting, reduce exposure

black mold ----> UV-Light (A & B), dehumidifier (installed low, in the basement, crawl space or bottom floor)
these days be careful when going to the woods, wear long pants (Ticks--->Lyme)
UV-C could be helpful in getting rid of mold, if there is a way to have them on when you are not there
See this thread for idea about UV-C and other lighting.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-26#post-181917](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-26#post-181917)
and this
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/warren-found-hope.17020/page-3#post-182580](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/warren-found-hope.17020/page-3#post-182580)

Currently you skin is not accustomed to light (high latitude, indoor living, clothes).
It takes at least one month of daily small incremental light increases to get where you want to be.
After that time, still be aware about how you feel due to high intensity (full spectrum) light exposure. Solar Glo 160W.
Reduce light when in doubt.
\-------
Good idea to have your cortisol tested, blood or saliva
Saliva better, blood may be easier to get.
Most important is shape, high in the morning, still rather substantial at noon, lower late afternoon
Ideally you want all readings real close to their respective top ranges.

...
Sunlight when first happens does not have any UV
latter comes UVA
latter comes UVB
think of UVA & UVB being present 10AM-2PM

yearly average of UVB exposure, at 60deg latitude is about 20% relatively to equator
good place for Seasonal affective disorder (SAD)

...
Keeping general temperature at night 2-4 deg C lower than (general) day temperature helps in improving on Circadian Rhythm.
...

These days even young people have DHEAs(serum) deficiency.
Sum of DHEAs and Cortisol hormones in the blood is real close to 100% of steroid hormones.
Sum of all other steroid hormones fits in last about 1/4%.

When considering Vit D think vit D(sulfate) and eat lots of sulfur, onions, garlic, MSM

.............

IR-A ---- 800nm - 1400nm

Utilize your available natural light.

Divide 24 hr day into
14 hr designated as a day (be around 3-4 Solar Glow 160W for 10hrs, reduce to 1-2 in last 4 hrs)
10 hrs designated as a night (no lights, red when going to bathroom)

RubyLux ALL RED High Intensity LED Bulb 640 to 660nm on wrists, alternate with CT.

I drink Spring Water.

1

There is no added fluoride to my tap water which is good! But there is chlorine and other stuff I can imagine which I don't want! If my water was fluoridated, then spring water would be a good option but since water is cheap here with minimal amount of natural fluoride, maybe I am better off with a filter financially at least. Is there any scientific evidence behind the fact that reverse osmosis water charges easier than spring water? Btw, highland spring has a little natural chlorine in it.

'fluoride Low levels of fluoride can occur naturally in water. The regulatory limit is set at 1500 microgrammes per litre. We do not add any additional fluoride to your water.' - ScottishWater ([http://www.scottishwater.co.uk/asse...e/water quality/yourwaterqualityexplained.pdf](http://www.scottishwater.co.uk/assets/domestic/files/you%20and%20your%20home/water%20quality/yourwaterqualityexplained.pdf))

-   [Anglian Water](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anglian_Water) Services Ltd

-   [United Utilities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Utilities) Water plc ... In 2004, following a public consultation, Scotland's parliament rejected proposals to fluoridate public drinking water' - wikipedia ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fluoridation\_by\_country](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fluoridation_by_country))

# Thread: Hi hi from Brisbane Australia ✌️
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Haley_Pendragon — 2015-11-24T11:29:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-182567>

Hi all!

My name is Haley and I'm a 32yo Naturopath in North Brisbane. I'm also studying my post grad Bachelor of Medicines Management with Professional Honours in Complementary Medicine part time.

I currently run a health food store where I spend 5 days/wk, and spend the remainder of my time seeing clients in my clinic ([www.facebook.com/aliveinwonderlandhealth](http://www.facebook/aliveinwonderlandhealth)), writing articles (I'm a paid health blog writer), studying, and of course spending time with my kids (mum of 2 kids under 10)... Occasionally if I'm lucky I'll take my motorbike for a cruise up the mountain.

I was drawn to Dr Kruse's work through the paleo scene... People such as Chris Kresser were on my reading list... Then I began delving deeper, seeing Joshs's (Paleo Osteo's) comments on our practitioner networks and read of Jack's Epi-Paleo Rx - I have been immersed in reading & listening to Jack's work ever since (big podcast fan as I find podcasts easier in my busy life!). I tend to spend the majority of my lunch breaks reading Dr Kruse's work from his blog and I often have "sunlight moments" (no longer known as "light bulb moments") that I share with my colleagues & clients regularly.

My end of year gift list this year consists mostly of books, recommended by Jack on podcasts. I am always open to more reading suggestions as knowledge is power! I have read many of the blogs, and often re-read them.

I'm grateful to live on the east coast of sunny Australia where fresh seafood is rife. My 9yo son is a big fan of oysters in particular!

My main interests lie within mental health (neurotransmitters, anxiety, depression, stress, PTSD), heavy metals, gut health, addiction, diabetes, adhd and improving people's wellness in general through education. Most of it is all linked anyway.

Glad to be here on the forum. Being so busy I will likely not spend too much time 'foruming' but I am always keeping up as best I can.

Thanks for having me!

Warm regards,
Haley ✌️

### Haley_Pendragon — 2015-11-24T22:02:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-182598>

Thanks! Looking forward to more learning ahead.

### caroline — 2015-11-25T01:18:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-182616>

Welcome Haley. We have a few members in Brisbane and I am a little south of Sydney.

### Jenny S — 2015-11-25T05:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hi-from-brisbane-australia-%E2%9C%8C%EF%B8%8F.17153/post-182624>

Hi Haley, I'm in Hervey Bay at present.
Sounds like you are very busy but doing well!

### Haley_Pendragon — 2015-12-08T12:33:04+0200

Hi Jenny! That's not too far from me at all.

I've been looking through some posts on this from 2013 to try and find suggestions for something like the Magnetico available locally, and if any of you lovely Aussies can give me some guidance on that I would really appreciate it! Thanks!

### KetoKatie — 2015-11-24T19:24:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geomagnetic-maps-if-everything-else-is-good-is-living-in-the-red-still-bad.17154/post-182582>

For a variety of reasons, we are still not moved, I will update my journal soon. (We live in nnEMF hell that seems to rivals NYC or SF.) Just found a good place that seems to have all the right things...price is excellent and would allow me to stay a member, homeschool my kids next year like I hope to, while paying down our bills (student loans mostly) and it's low nnEMF, great yard for grounding/CT/fire pit/garden etc, low stress for hubs commute.

BUT it's in a pink/red area of this map:
[http://www.mngs.umn.edu/magnetics.htm](http://www.mngs.umn.edu/magnetics.htm)

Do I hold out for something better in a lower magnetic area or jump on this? We are still renting because we want to be able to move in a year or two out of Minnesota, it's not happening now like we had been hoping for.

### Penny — 2015-11-25T00:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geomagnetic-maps-if-everything-else-is-good-is-living-in-the-red-still-bad.17154/post-182608>

I googled "geomagnetic health" and it was rather terrifying...
[https://www.google.com/search?q=geomagnetism+health&ie=utf-8&oe=utf-8](https://www.google.com/search?q=geomagnetism+health&ie=utf-8&oe=utf-8)

I would say pass, but what do I know... I once listened to a podcast where a lady was sick for years and whose hubby died because their house was on top of some geo-something-or-other - awful - one of these links says it equates to strokes - not good -

# Thread: How do I improve fingers and toes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 493403g494f99539 — 2015-11-24T20:38:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-182588>

Been dipping into the lake for a while now. The weather is ~0C, don't know about water temp. I feel like I could swim around there for a good 30min without shivering, except after 5-10min my fingers and toes are toast. I mean the opposite of that. Popsicles. If I stay in any longer than that, then putting on clothes, tieing shoelaces, etc, is gonna be a real hassle afterwards, fingers just stop functioning.

Can any pros tell me, will it eventually go away?

If I fill the tub with ice cold water and just soak my feet and hands in it, can this improve the situation? Since I would do it at home I could even push it further and get them really frozen and numb. How risky is that tho? Can I damage them?

I don't want to resort to wearing gloves or something since that would just delay progress, no?

### Ralef — 2015-11-24T21:22:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-182592>

Hi, i'am doing the same, but just staying in the water for a few minutes. Body is fine, but on toes and fingers i had the same problems as you have. Now i'am using light neopren gear for hands and feet, bought it in a seadiver shop. It works great.

### Butters — 2015-11-25T11:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-182638>

Same here. Hands and feet are always cold, despite of feeling warm over all and emitting heat.

### seanb4 — 2015-11-25T14:56:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-182643>

Nice username

I still have trouble with my hands and feet however as my body overall adapts my extremeties can tollerate more.

I think you need a really strong DC to have warm hands / feet

### thomas — 2015-12-03T19:07:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183190>

Put some godamn gloves and ct longer goddamnit.

### nicld — 2015-12-03T19:22:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183191>

I wear scuba boots too. One can do damage to toes and fingers in the cold but take ALOT longer for the rest of the body.

### Dennis Clark — 2015-12-05T20:30:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183403>

SCUBA boots are a lifesaver. Gloves, too, if you are actually swimming around.

### 493403g494f99539 — 2015-12-05T21:06:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183409>

I don't know... I feel like I'm making very slight progress (at least in post-ct recovery speeds) and don't want to use gloves or boots. Hell, I don't even use underwear, towels, caps or anything, I like to keep it "au naturel" so I can just spontaneously jump in whenever I feel like it.

Just want to know from the pros:
Does it eventually go away on it's own?
Should I do extra CT specifically for extremities or is that wasting my time? _(When it starts snowing I could try bare-feet walking/making snowballs without gloves and whatnot)_
How dangerous is it to ignore "the needles" and push it further?
Any other things that could help? "Hot-cold contrast shower" for cardiovascular health? Some sort of [finger workout regiment](http://i.imgur.com/K0upy2B.jpg)?

### Peertje80 — 2015-12-05T23:39:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183431>

I have some sort of Raynauds in my fingers. I'm only bothered under 12 degrees C or when I'm leaving the office in the afternoon to go home after work (which is actually weird because it seems to be unrelated to temp). So I get my 'KI' flowing to my fingers before I do CT. Loosening up the neck, shoulder, elbows. And than swing firmly but relaxed arms up en down and left to right until you feel a tingle in the hands. Get some deep breathing going on. And then they are fine during CT. Don't know how to do this for toes, maybe swing legs?

But I would really advice neopreen too. I would choose time submerged before being fully naked. Vasoconstriction in hands and feet is a pretty normal response to sudden drop in temp. Don't know if it will go away. Maybe you can try to experiment with more gradual drop in temp?

### 493403g494f99539 — 2015-12-06T20:54:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183543>

> [Peertje80 said:](/goto/post?id=183431)
>
> Loosening up the neck, shoulder, elbows. And than swing firmly but relaxed arms up en down and left to right until you feel a tingle in the hands. Get some deep breathing going on.

Thanks, will give that a try.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-07T06:07:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183595>

> Nice username
>
> I still have trouble with my hands and feet however as my body overall adapts my extremeties can tollerate more.
>

### 493403g494f99539 — 2015-12-09T17:43:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183801>

So light + DHA = DC, right?
It's winter so not much I can do about the light.
Also I don't have oysters... Mackrel should be pretty high in DHA... quantity > quality apparently... so I'll eat 5 cans of that shit and you're telling me my hands and feet will last noticeably longer the next time I go in?

Edit: wait, what... _"DHA turns light to a DC electric signal"_... so I can't just increase the DHA due to lack of light. If a bloody contact lens in my eye blocks my whole brain from knowing what time of year it is, then surely these bigass clouds blocking that tiny far-away sun won't be letting me convert a damn thing. Also, like, I tend to wear clothes...

Should I eat 5 cans of mackrel, stand outside naked in the snow from sunrise to sunset and then I will be able to last 5 extra minutes in the water?

### Inger — 2015-12-10T13:34:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183843>

Yep, that sounds right

### 493403g494f99539 — 2015-12-11T10:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-improve-fingers-and-toes.17156/post-183906>

Random question, but... can we create DC with breathing/meditation also? Is that what this "inner fire" and whatnot stuff is about?

When I do deep breathing I do get more energy but not really in the fingers or toes. If I do push-ups after hyperventilating for a while I do feel "the feeling of electricity" in my fingers. How cool is that? I doubt it's actual electricity though.

# Thread: Sea water - deadly or just another myth?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-water-deadly-or-just-another-myth.17161/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-27T08:31:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-water-deadly-or-just-another-myth.17161/post-182736>

It seems to me that there is a pattern emerging -almost everything that we are taught about in health ends up being vague or just dead wrong. (Lack of UV light for instance seems to be at the root of almost all chronic illness.)
Since our ancestors in the East African Rift lived off of seafood and probably spent most of their time in the water wouldn't it make sense that they probably could drink it too (?). I really can't see them hunting for a fresh water creek everytime they got thirsty.
Or maybe they just absorbed water through their skin(?) I've seen sea gulls gulp the shit down like no tomorrow..and that really got me thinking that , just like the ability to tolerate cold is in us, maybe our ability to handle salt water is too.

Maybe people die because of an intense Herx reaction. Any thoughts? Possible biohack?

(Or is this idea just ridiculous)

### PaulG — 2015-11-27T09:41:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-water-deadly-or-just-another-myth.17161/post-182737>

Found this article [what-will-happen-if-you-drink-a-glass-of-salt-water-every-morning](http://positivemed.com/2015/07/22/what-will-happen-if-you-drink-a-glass-of-salt-water-every-morning/)

### Erik — 2015-11-27T17:22:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-water-deadly-or-just-another-myth.17161/post-182742>

i drank 2 glasses of sea water every day for a year. I got some health problems but my environment was TERRIBLE, so i dont think the sea water was hurting me.... but who knows, i have to try again with a good environment

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-28T07:34:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-water-deadly-or-just-another-myth.17161/post-182763>

> [Erik said:](/goto/post?id=182742)
>
> i drank 2 glasses of sea water every day for a year. I got some health problems but my environment was TERRIBLE, so i dont think the sea water was hurting me.... but who knows, I have to try again with a good environment

Thanks for sharing your experience... two glasses..that's a fair a bit. I bet you would have accumulated more health problems if you didn't drink the water!But that is just my current thinking based on how introducing 'evil' UV light has increased my brain power HUGELY!(Thanks to Dr Kruse's emphasis on UV light..I really cannot thank him enough) A great many things that we have learned about health are false. It seems that the more people believe something to be true, the less that it IS true!

### Martha Ray — 2022-08-26T17:29:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-water-deadly-or-just-another-myth.17161/post-315269>

At least cheaper than Asea.**

### JanSz — 2022-08-27T02:16:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-water-deadly-or-just-another-myth.17161/post-315286>

> [Martha Ray said:](/goto/post?id=315269)
>
> **Plan to collect Seawater next trip to beach and take 1 oz of it a few days a week for 6 weeks and see what I might notice.
> At least cheaper than Asea.**

Bad idea.
Go on the boat, at least few miles away from the shore, get your water there.
The idea is to collect clean sea water from large depths, because, hopefully, it is clean.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------
There is a skimming effect in places where water meets land.
Sea is getting rid of its debris that way.
Aquarium skimmers works using this premise.

\====================================
[Aquarium Skimmer | Black Hole Skimmers](https://www.blackholeskimmers.com/aquarium)

//////////////////

**\----------------------------------**
​

**Yes, I've taken a Boron** supplement regularly in the past to try to balance my Ca / Mg ratio due to bad leg and foot cramps when taking Ca and or some forms of Mg- strange symptom to try and tract down. I did not take it long and it did not resolve my bad leg and foot cramps.

I've also took a form of BORON last year per the instruction of Michael McEvoy a Metabolic counselor on the west coast to address my 15+ years high my C-RP marker (for years it had been as high as up to 18). He told me he would be on the look out for a way to lower my C-RP and about 4 weeks after I had met with him he called to share this research paper (see below) that used **Calcium Fructoborate form f BORON** to lower C-RP. **He suggested I try it, I did and it worked!**

**_Calcium Fructoborate for Bone and Cardiovascular Health_**
Biological Trace Element Research
[Biol Trace Elem Res.](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4930945/#) 2016; 172: 277–281.
Published online 2015 Dec 21. doi: [10.1007/s12011-015-0590-2](https://doi.org/10.1007%2Fs12011-015-0590-2)
PMCID: PMC4930945

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4930945/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4930945/)​

Only one time before the marker went down when I was vegetarian 6 months then vegan for 2 months. The Boron I took for the C-RP marker was
**_Vibrant Health_ Calcium Fructoborate Super Natural BORON**
[**https://www.vitacost.com/vibrant-health-super-natural-boron?ta=Vibrant+Health+Calcium+Fructoborate+Super+Natural+BORON.&t=Vibrant+Health+Calcium+Fructoborate+Super+Natural+BORON**](https://www.vitacost.com/vibrant-health-super-natural-boron?ta=Vibrant+Health+Calcium+Fructoborate+Super+Natural+BORON.&t=Vibrant+Health+Calcium+Fructoborate+Super+Natural+BORON.)It has Calcium Fructoborate providing **6 mg BORON** and it also has 676 mg Broccoli powder certi. organic. I still have some on hand and plan on taking it on when I have leg and foot cramps. I have also taken tonic water, pickle juice and or ACV when I have those cramps.

6\. It supports short-term memory and short-term memory loss

8\. It can increase sex hormones (estrogen, progesterone, and testosterone)
9\. It can help prevent certain cancers​

# Thread: Wil's Journal: Stumbling Forward One Day-At-A-Time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 51
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jwhb77 — 2015-11-28T02:10:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-182757>

2015-11-27. Friday

My name is Wil.

Simply put, this is a record of my journey towards better health and a better life... a state of being & function that I would call 'optimal'. It's a journey I've been on for some time already, and those interested can get a bare sketch of where I'm coming from here:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/down-the-rabbit-hole-i-go-again.17091/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/down-the-rabbit-hole-i-go-again.17091/)

To try & boil it down:
\--I'm 38 years old, male, primarily of Irish, Welsh, & French stock...
approx 5'11'' tall, weighing 205 lbs... athletic build, but not 'muscle-bound'...
family history of depression & anxiety, and my father died from early-onset Alzheimer's disease...
\--personal history of depression & anxiety since puberty...
\--played competitive football (American) throughout middle school & high school (varsity)... collision-related black-out during my senior season/possible concussion (never diagnosed)...
\--diagnosed ADHD/Inattentive at age 23... taking antidepressants, anxiolytics, & ADHD meds on-and-off since then (and currently)...
\--had to drop out of nursing school at age 36, due to cognitive dysfunction & memory problems...
\--heavy metal toxicity detected in blood labs at age 36... mercury & lead esp., but also antimony, tin, aluminum, et al.)
\--also detected heterozygous MTHFR gene mutation at that time...
\--undergoing chelation tx for the past 2 yrs to alleviate the heavy metal toxicity... mercury is down from sky-high to within reference range, lead is getting close to reference range, as are a few of the others that were too high...
\--Syncopal episode in June 2014, after a 2-mile run... passed out in the middle of Hobby Lobby...
\--MD diagnosed hypertension (had been borderline before), Rx'ed Lisinopril + (more recently) Amlodipine besylate...

Lots going on, as you can see, but even after all of that, I'm still doing everything I can manage to try to improve my situation & my understanding of it... in doing so, I managed to stumble across Jack Kruse's work online... needless to say, my mind = blown! Jack gave me lots of things to consider, to re-think, & new things to try... I'm truly grateful for the opportunity he's afforded me!

### jwhb77 — 2015-11-28T03:21:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-182759>

2015-11-27. Friday (continued)

So, what I'm up to at this point:

\- Continuing weekly chelation tx (DMSA + calcium disodium EDTA), until blood tests show they're no longer needed...
\- Re-reading the EpiRx book & SLOWLY, for digestion...
\- Trying to establish a new habit of waking at dawn & getting direct sunlight for at least 15-20 mins, while juggling...
\- Eating a variety of new (to me) seafoods... trying to eat at least a tin of sardines, salmon, or mussels everyday... just ordered some salmon roe... sadly I've no access to the fresh stuff locally, so I gotta get my seafood canned or online...
\- Getting started with sea veggies... sushi nori is the first & only thing I've done so far in this regard...
\- Cleaning up other areas of my dietary intake, already gluten-free & tomato-free... but stopping dairy is going to be a tough one... always been a big milk & cheese guy... a work in progress.
\- Toying with CT & trying to get myself into a more regimented program with it...

If anyone reading this journal knows, I'm presently trying to figure out the following:

\- My local water supply doesn't treat the water with fluoride, fortunately... I have a carbon filter in the fridge water at home, so would I need to go all-in with a RO filter system that filters fluoride?
\- Are there any more cost-effective means of getting a regular supply of good water than RO systems (they're kinda pricey)? What about ozonating carbon-filtered water?
\- Should the water I use for CT also be RO and/or non-fluoridated? Just curious...
\- Is there a way to figure out how much DHA/EPA I should be consuming per day, as a bare minimum? I've read elsewhere on the forums that there's not likely an upper limit for contemporary, nnEMF-saturated people like us, but nothing specifically commenting on a bare minimum to shoot for...

Anywho, that's all for the moment... I'll be back with more

### caroline — 2015-11-28T05:13:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-182761>

Welcome to journal land Wil!

Eat as much seafood as possible - a lot of us try to eat mostly seafood

Start with bottles of spring water. Don't worry about what water for C/T .... Just do it!
Have you read the easy start guides?
Do you listen to Jacks podcasts? They are an easier way to get quick info.

The most important thing is the three legged stool ......light, water, magnetism

Eat seafood
Drink bottled water
Get outside as much as possible with as little clothes as possible - without scaring the neighbours! You need lots and lots of outdoor time ....in bare feet. I think of my hands and feet as solar panels!

Tell us more about where you live and work. Your post code is a big deal

Tell Dr K. More about your Moms and grandmas health history. Siblings?

nnEMF?

Having had a concussion is huge
Are you still exercising???

If you can swing it ..... Become a gold member then you will get all the webinars and the live monthly Q&A where you can ask Jack anything.

### jwhb77 — 2015-11-29T04:38:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-182816>

Thank you, Caroline!

Are there any particular/preferred sources for spring water you recommend?

I've caught a few of Jack's podcasts & interviews, which have been helpful. I'm curious if there's an optimal order in which I can read Jack's blog posts & podcasts... my intuition is to just read & process them in chronological order.

Thank you for the advice! Sounds like I'm on the right track, just need to up my time outdoors in the sun.

My postal code is 74055. I live on the outskirts of one of Tulsa, OK's larger suburbs... it's not rural, but not exactly urban/suburban, either... it's "booming" growth-wise, & presently somewhere inbetween.

As for work environment, the past 5-6 years I've spent most of my time in healthcare... hospitals & an optometrist's office. I also have spent lots of time at institutions of higher education over the past 20 or so years... nursing school being the most recent. I haven't worked consistently since the crash-and-burn at nursing school, in part due to the health & cognitive function issues involved & partly b/c I've lost confidence in myself & my abilities. Not a good place to be.

I'm not comfortable sharing a detailed family health history on a public forum like this, but if Dr. Kruse is interested, I'd share everything I know via private message. I'll just share here that both of my parents struggle/struggled with similar anxiety & depression issues, & that my younger brothers do to varying degrees as well. My Dad passed away at 60 years old in 2011, from early onset Alzheimer's disease, though his mother (my paternal grandma) lived to see 89 years & was sharp as a tack for all but the last few of them. I never knew much about my maternal grandma, who passed away before I was born.

As for nnEMF's, I've lived with WiFi at home for the past 3 yrs continuously, & on-and-off for several yrs before that... my wife & I just got rid of it. Immediate neighbors still have it, though. I've spent lots of time around & in front of computers over the years...

I've been an athlete most of my life... mostly running in recent years... inconsistent of late, though, due to my running partner (wife) being in an auto accident. Also wanting to get back to weightlifting again, just haven't yet.

I'd love to become a gold member, but won't be able to swing it until I'm earning a consistent income again. Definitely on the radar, though...

Thanks for your interest!
Wil

### caroline — 2015-11-29T09:27:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-182820>

I forgot to ask you if there is a smart meter on your house?

Others will have info about brands of water ... I am in Oz.

C/T is really important ....read the easy start guide and follow exactly. Dr. K. Is always very specific.

Your work environment ... hospitals are very toxic. Dr. K. Has to do extra ordinary things to compensate for his day job.

Maybe don't worry about ecercise for now .... Get outside walking etc. as much as possible.

NnEMF is very toxic for you no doubt ...... You need to really examine your home

Do you have all electronics out of your bedroom? iPhone, iPad, TV, clock radio

### jwhb77 — 2015-11-30T20:49:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-182891>

Caroline,

I'm not certain, but the pics of smart meters I've seen online look very similar to what is on my home... not sure if we need to try to have it removed, or to try to institute a barrier along the inside of the wall it is located on. I need to read up on it.

I'm definitely going to run... just need to get us going consistently & habitually. My wife & I are pretty cold-sensitive, though, which makes consistency throughout the winter that much more challenging. I'm starting CT today with my face... hopefully the adaptations will come relatively quickly & make me less resistant to running in the cold, in the process. I can't say I'm optimistic about getting my wife to do CT anytime soon, but maybe she'll be more inclined if we start seeing benefits from it.

I've eliminated all electronics from our bedroom, with the exeptions of our bedside lamps, our phones (which we use as alarms), and my wife's insulin pump (non-negotiable). Perhaps we should keep the phones across the room from where we sleep?

I've been looking into Earthing as I sleep, as a possibility... also contemplating construction of a Faraday enclosure around the bed. We'll see...

Lastly, I am looking at our arificial light sources inside the house... we have GE Reveal bulbs in most of our living areas, which are supposed to be natural & full-spectrum light, but I'm re-considering whether this is a good choice, since we use artificial lights mostly at night. I have a 250 watt red heat lamp I've been using on-and-off for the past year or two, which does seem to help when I shine it on myself from a distance... I do this mostly in the evening... been wondering whether I ought to add a UV lamp alongside it, but I'm hesitant at this point b/c of the mercury resonance issues Dr. K warned me about. I'm within the RR for Hg now, so technically I'm not at a 'elevated' or 'toxic' level anymore, but the urine tests really only measure systemic levels (not sure I trust them to be accurate & representative of the Hg levels in my CNS tissues, which seem to be at the crux of a lot of my issues). For now, I'm resisting the temptation to risk it.

### caroline — 2015-11-30T22:59:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-182901>

As Dr. k. Would say ....... Phones in the bedroom - no Bueno!

Get an old fashioned alarm clock.

Most of us wake up without an alarm now ...... Early to bed - early to rise!

Once you re set your circadian Rythm - it isn't a problem.

Please get your self on track first and hopefully your DW will follow. You really can't do it for someone else.

Dr. k. Tells all of us repeatedly ... You need to care care of you. It is wonderful if our partners are on board but often they aren't .... So we have to step up and do what we need to do for ourself.

C/T will make a huge difference.....

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-01T02:47:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-182924>

Key thing is adding purple light back to your life and cutting out the blue.........after that its off to limit nnEMF and then you can deal with the mundane aspects of this like diet. DHA is key for most people who are microwaving their SCN and shining blue light into their RPE. Once you get that remedied you can then ease back into a seasonal life for what the sun calls for where you live.

### jwhb77 — 2015-12-03T21:10:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-183210>

Thanks to you both...

I just received a pair of UV bulbs in the mail that project 365 nm wavelength & a reptile light that is supposed to replicate full-spectrum sunlight. I've also gotten red glasses to use with blue light-emitting screens, esp at night...

Today also marked my second day of spending an hour outside in the sunlight, trying to get as much of its light into my eyes as possible, w/o staring directly at it. First day I did this at sunrise, today it was closer to noon... not sure what difference that makes, but I did notice that the sunrise spectrum felt a lot more energizing in effect & left me feeling a lot less sluggish throughout the day & evening hours than I have been otherwise, in recent memory.

I find myself tending to stare off to the side of the sun, 30-50°or so, vs trying to stare directly at it (feels to intense)... I also find that blinking helps as my gaze gets closer to the sun's periphery... I'm curious as to what others do & experience in this regard. I also wonder if the light will still have the same effects if I close my eyes while keeping them pointed in the direction of the sun... would the light get through to the retina, or be attenuated too much by passing through the eyelids, to remain effective? Reason I'm curious is that it seems a lot more relaxing to let them close than to struggle to keep them open the entire time...

### Penny — 2015-12-04T00:56:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-183232>

First day I did this at sunrise, today it was closer to noon... not sure what difference that makes, but I did notice that the sunrise spectrum felt a lot more energizing in effect & left me feeling a lot less sluggish throughout the day & evening hours than I have been otherwise, in recent memory.
>
> I find myself tending to stare off to the side of the sun, 30-50°or so, vs trying to stare directly at it (feels to intense)... I also find that blinking helps as my gaze gets closer to the sun's periphery... I'm curious as to what others do & experience in this regard. I also wonder if the light will still have the same effects if I close my eyes while keeping them pointed in the direction of the sun... would the light get through to the retina, or be attenuated too much by passing through the eyelids, to remain effective? Reason I'm curious is that it seems a lot more relaxing to let them close than to struggle to keep them open the entire time...

Yeah - I found that to be the case when it was really bright and past 11:00 - so much more comfy to just close my eyes - I thought sunlight must be getting in if that silly little led I bought can penetrate tissue - I also think the color of your eyes and how long you've kept them covered up is huge in how painful a process this will be for you - mine are brown so I could not care less how much brightness I'm exposed to but my Nordic hubby from a baby has had sunglasses or he can't even see in the sun - he has grey/green eyes - I was also wondering if the VDR SNP would be the only bummer for one to have and more difficult to remedy than the others - probably means you just need more sun...

### jwhb77 — 2015-12-26T08:29:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-184960>

2015-12-26. Saturday

Merry Christmas, fellow travelers... haven't posted over the past few weeks, too much holiday hustle & bustle going on...

I've managed to stay on track thus far with my daily seafood/DHA intake... tried mackerel for the first time this week & was pleasantly surprised... not as 'fishy' as I had anticipated. Still wondering how many mg of daily DHA I should seek as a bare minimum...

Most mornings I've been successful in getting up at sunrise & absorbing an hour of the morning sun with my eyes & skin. Eyes are still a bit too sensitive to keep my gaze closer than 20-30° from the sun's edge, esp. later in the AM, but I do seem to be squinting less as the days go by.

I've also been working to improve my artificial lighting situation, & I've started regularly incorporating a pair of UV light bulbs when indoors throughout the day, especially when I'm using the CPU. At night I've been using them together with my 850nm infrared LED light, along with wearing some orange shades to block out any blues encountered. This has helped reduce fatigue noticeably & keeps me more relaxed at night, esp. when I'm in front of a screen. In addition, I ordered a RubyLux infrared bulb & one of their 640-660nm red bulbs to try indoors as well. We'll see...

In terms of learning, I've started collating the blogs chronologically, & plan to print them for easy screen-free reading this week. Looking forward to it!

Signing off, Wil

### jwhb77 — 2015-12-31T08:14:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-185151>

2015-12-31. Thursday

Just a quick update here... received the RubyLux 640-660nm All Red bulb in the mail this afternoon (Dec 30). Lit it up in concert with my RubyLux UV bulb, and all I can say is 'WOW'! This feels GREAT!!!

### jwhb77 — 2016-01-01T22:24:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-185242>

lilreddgirl,

Thanks for the tip on daily seafood... that's something I can take action on

In answer to your questions...
On the UV bulbs mentioned in my Christmas update, I was referring to a pair of Sunlite UV 265nm bulbs.

The orange shades I referenced are safety glasses, and do block UV wavelengths, in addition to blue... I wear them primarily at night, & especially at night when in front of screens. During daylight hours I seldom wear them & try to accumulate as much UV on my eyes as possible (sun 1st priority, bulbs 2nd). I figure that the negatives of blue light on my eyes at night outweigh the positives of additional eye exposure to UV during those hours, hence the orange shades. I include the UV bulbs in that setup b/c the UV will still get absorbed through the skin with benefit.

In my later quick update, I was referring to a RubyLux High-Intensity UV LED bulb being used in tandem with the RubyLux 640-660nm High-Intensity All Red LED bulb (not the Sunlite UV bulbs)... when I said 'GREAT' I meant that I felt mentally 'clear', 'calm', 'still', 'more present', 'coherent', etc... energized, but not buzzed or jittery... almost as if there is less mental & emotional 'static' going on when I'm under these lights. As someone who has coped with anxiety in excess most of his adult life & tried lots of solutions to little avail, this is huge... I had noticed similar, but much less intense & extensive benefits with just the UV bulbs (both kinds), but the combo with the red is on a whole other level IME... while my N=1 with this is of course extremely limited at this point, I've had the same experience each time I've used this combination of light sources so far

As for usage, I've been using this combo during the day & evening hours... pretty much any time I can when I'm awake & indoors at home, & with the bulbs about 2-4 feet away. Haven't tried any form of light while sleeping & am very hesitant to do so... not sure if I will test that out or not, probably not.
Anyway, hope this helps... good luck!

Sincerely,
Wil

### jwhb77 — 2016-01-16T02:52:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-186432>

I ordered a coolfatburner & coolgutbuster from Coolfatburner.com & received it yesterday... wore them both today while doing my 'Power Hour' at sunrise... felt great. No shivering or strong discomfort, & feelings of serenity overtook me as I lay there, staring up towards the morning sun. So peaceful... which kind of makes me wonder if these garments were making me cold enough... I don't have much CT experience under my belt, so I'm not sure what it's supposed to feel like done properly. Anyways, it was a nice experience.

I've also started making a smoothie every morning before sunrise with fruit, almond milk, & a tablespoon of salmon roe in it for the DHA. Not 'fishy' tasting at all & I feel better/less anxious in the morning hours since taking up the practice. I can feel the difference on mornings I've skipped this. I really want to up my daily seafood/DHA volume, & these smoothies are one easy way to do that... I'm also looking into some online options for getting live mussels & oysters airshipped to my landlocked self in OK (Vital Choice, et al.), but I'm a little apprehensive about storing these properly. We'll see...

Another devleopment: while my wife & I were down in Dallas last week, I underwent a battery of aptitude testing at AIMS... they give testees (not to be confused with testis, lol) a list of careers that fit well with their individual aptitude & interest profiles as part of the service, and one of the suggestions they gave me was diagnostic medical sonographer. I've read on the forum here that ultrasound can create EZ water in the body, but the prospect of working in an environment with potentially-high nnEMF exposures scares me (I don't know much about what the exposure might be for someone operating an ultrasound machine). If anyone knows anything about this, please do chime in here... it would help me evaluate whether to pursue this career path.

### jwhb77 — 2016-01-22T21:08:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-187112>

Did CT 3x this past week-and-weekend using the coolfatburner & coolgutbuster... the first day I did for 2.5-3 hrs, out in the late morning-early afternoon sun, and I think I may have gotten ahead of myself there. The skin of my chest, back, & abdomen were a nice, rosy pink for approx 6 hours afterwards, and I had no shivering while in the garments or for the first few hrs after. I did, however, have a hard time remaining asleep that night in bed when the shivers hit. The next afternoon, I also noticed a few areas of skin on my belly felt numb... not completely without sensation, but definitely less than normal. I decided I'd better wait another day & scale waaay back, more in line with recommendations. Lesson definitely learned. Follow the protocol & build gradually.

The other two times I CT'ed were 30 min or less, and I had none of the same issues come up.

I've also been keeping up with my sunrise hour of power on most mornings. It's definitely felt like winter here in OK, USA this week, so on the few mornings that've been wet + cold, I rationalized staying inside in front of my various artifical lamps. No comparison there, really, but better than nothing I suppose.

On a brighter note, my wife told me that I do seem to be functioning better... getting more done, taking on more repsonsibility, keeping a better attitude towards things & life. That was really encouraging for me to hear, & I think I'm on the right track here. I hope the improvements continue!

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-23T02:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-187127>

I think Dr. K mentioned on the blogs on CT a few years back that he spent some months with his torso feeling numb 24/7 and this was not a problem. I think as long as you don't see white skin instead of cherry red it's supposed to be ok. I'm surprised that you got shivers during sleep though!

### jwhb77 — 2016-01-30T23:30:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-187698>

2016-1-30. Saturday

After seeing a slow but certain improvement in my symptoms & overall cognitive functioning during the past month-and-a-half of following Dr K's recommendations, I fell off the wagon this past week with my morning hour of power, starting with a very late night early in the week. I stayed up too late, overslept, spent most of my time indoors, and got very little accomplished. I rationalized some of it by substituting an array of full-spectrum sunlamps, UV lamps as much as I could. As the week progressed, I felt myself slipping increasingly into a state of frustration, shame, anger, & depression/desperation. High levels of anxiety re-asserted themselves with vigor. It's very hard to think clearly when you're in that state, but by yesterday (Friday) morning, I could no longer deny that, by lapsing, I was in effect doing this to myself.

I got my rear out in the sun at about 4:30-5 pm that evening, about an hour before sunset. What happened? After about 20 minutes of sungazing, all of those problem symptoms that seemed so overwhelming & hopelessly permanent the day before dissipated. The longer I was out there, the better I felt & the clearer my mind got. My dog even hopped up on the hammock with me, and we gazed at the sun together until it descended past the horizon.

What my experiences this week taught me, in a way that word of mouth simply cannot, is how powerful & vital our (or, at the very least, _my_) connection to the sun is. My biology wasn't fooled by my rationalizations, arguments, or my artificial light sources... it was hungry for sunlight, and sunlight is all that would do. Simple as that, & I'm taking it to heart.

### jwhb77 — 2016-01-30T23:33:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-187699>

2016-1-30. Saturday (cont)

In other news, while I was on the trip to Dallas for aptitude testing (I mentioned a few posts back), one of the most interesting takeaways that my wife & I learned is that I test low for 'perceptual speed', a trait that is essentially a measure of our ability to quickly & accurately see & compare the similarities & differences among sets of visual stimuli (e.g. letters, numbers, pictures, objects, or patterns) that are presented to us. Though this trait is related to intelligence & IQ, is not the same thing... and it's very common among those who, like me, are diagnosed with ADHD. It made a lot of sense of my life experiences... especially my struggles in keeping up with notetaking during lectures in school, and in my struggles with staying on top of the high volume of paperwork demanded by during nursing school.

All of this got me to wondering how myelination in the CNS might be related to variations in perceptual speed. As a trait, it's supposed to be more or less stable throughout a person's lifetime, but it does generally decline across the board with age... in other words, the older you get the slower your perceptual speed tends to get, & this is relative to the individual. I'm wondering if this decrease in relative perceptual speed might be tied to the myelination decreases commonly associated with aging. I also think it'd be interesting to see data on the perceptual speeds of those suffering diseases of myelination like MS... wonder whether there might be correlations there.

For myself, I'm quite curious to see if consistent seafood & sunshine might be enough to eventually increase my perceptual speeds over time... it'll be intersting to see.

### jwhb77 — 2016-02-16T04:31:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-189157>

2016-2-15. Monday

Since my last update, I've been to see my doc. We're going to continue my chelation tx another 6 months with Ca disodium EDTA & DMPS taken in 6 week cycles... she also said I've cleared enough of the heavy metals to begin IV phospholipid therapy whenever I'm ready. I told her about my experiments with light & seafood, & she was on board, esp when she saw a lot of my symptoms had improved on my implementation of Dr K's recommendations.

She also started me on a Ca-Mg Butyrate supplement (by BodyBio) to help combat excessive metabolic peroxide production that's shown up in some of my testing (related to my heterozygous MTHFR genetics). In addition, she is starting me on BodyBio's Phosphatidyl choline oral supplement. I need to read up on it, but a college level physics class I'm taking this spring keeps getting in the way...

Still not getting consistent CT like I want too, not sure what my hangup there is... but this one of this week's goals will be to decide on what form of CT I'll be most likely to follow through with consistently (& to git 'r done).

If anyone has any suggestions on this, or how I might otherwise fast track my progress & recovery, I'm all ears & would be much obliged. Thanks for reading

### jwhb77 — 2016-02-17T21:29:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-189259>

2016-02-17. Wednesday

Been doing some research & planning on my CT options, and just wanted to get some opinions on what is the best 'bang for my buck'... budget is tight right now, but I could make some sacrifices over the next several months to stretch it up to the neighborhood of $500-600 USD. I've narrowed it down to 2 main options at this point: Option #1- purchasing a 150 gal stock tank + upright freezer to keep an ice supply flowing for CT, or Option #2- purchasing the Quantlet as my main CT investment. Both seem like good options to me, with the Quantlet seeming more convenient, but possibly less effective for CT (??? I have no experiential frame of reference upon which to compare them in this regard). I know the Quantlet is more than just a CT device, but it does loosely fall into that category for my purposes...

I'm hoping someone with relevant experience and/or understanding might chime in here with some sage advice? Thanks.............

Wil

I do CT cheaply in any bathtub by running cold water and freeze a couple 2 Gallon jugs of water in a regular freezer to use as your ice.

### jwhb77 — 2016-03-24T19:26:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-191455>

2016-03-24. Thursday

As I was sunning this week, it occurred to me that the heavy manhole cover (all metal, probably iron) in the back corner of my backyard might make a great place to sit/stand while getting my daily sunlight.

My reasoning: being metal, it's highly conductive... being lodged in the earth itself, & connected to a network of metal pipes buried in the earth, it seems like this would make an extremely effective source for grounding. As I'm trying to maximize my ground state while sunning, this seems like a free way of enhancing my process & results, but I'm not certain as I don't feel I know enough about the issues involved in grounding/earthing yet.

I've also been wondering if the timing of my DHA consumption could be optimized or not... for instance, would it make any difference if I consumed it at some interval before sun exposure, vs during?

Just curious...

### jwhb77 — 2016-04-10T07:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-192449>

2016-04-08. Saturday

I am grateful & blessed to have gotten a new job recently, & so I decided it was high time to secure a Quantlet & got one of the last reduced-price units available via the Indiegogo campaign... really excited about this device & hoping it will provide some dramatic improvements to my energy levels & cognitive function! I'd love to hear from anyone who's used one for these purposes, and what it's done for them... or anyone who knows of related stories. Eagerly awaiting the September ship date!

### jwhb77 — 2016-05-14T02:42:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-194756>

2016-05-13. Friday

It's been too long since I've posted, but it's been a crazy month with the end of the semester in my Physics class going on, along with getting up to speed at my new job.

Anyways, as far as my Kruse-in' goes, I've managed to get outside in the sunrise light at least 20 or so min most mornings... on working days, this is mostly highway drive-time w/ my window or sunroof open, on my way to work, and on non- work days I've mostly done 30 min sessions standing on the grass in my backyard. I'm not satisfied with my consistency yet, but I'm giving myself points for being about 70-80% of the way there.

In my Kruse-inspired fantasy world, I'd be spending the hour before sunrise doing CT submerged in 55 degree-or-cooler water, then hop out of the tub, go outside, & gaze at the AM sunrise for an hour everyday, while slurping down a dozen or more fresh oysters. I'd also catch the PM sunset for 30 min to an hour, maybe after CT'ing again...

In reality, this hasn't happened yet, but I'm getting bits & pieces of this vision in place... trying to build one little aspect/habit in at a time, until I'm doing all of this. We'll see... I've tended to go in spurts with this.

I just received a shipment of freshly- shucked Hama-Hama oysters that I ordered online, & am pumped to have something closer to live than the smoked, canned-in-cottonseed oil oysters that I have local access to in my area. I'm going to try to keep better records of how everything is affecting me as I go, and try to make this journal more consistently current too, now that I'm done with school for the summer.

Still counting the days till the Quantlet arrives... can't get here soon enough! LOL...

Peace, & We Out!
Wil

### jwhb77 — 2016-05-16T06:41:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-194875>

2016-05-15. Sunday

Saturday for lunch I ate 3 dozen petite oysters (canned... eating through my canned stock at home before reaching for the new, fresh stuff I received Friday), and over the course of the next 3 hours I could literally feel the dopamine levels in my body & brain rising... and my anxiety levels concomitantly dropping to nihil. Having over a decade of experience in my n=1 of taking adhd meds that target dopamine specifically, with this identical set of sensations, I feel I've got a pretty good handle on what this 'feels like'... and it was unmistakable. Taken together with the fact that this was a completely blind/unintentional effect on my part, I tend to towards thinking it was more than simple placebo effect. It completely caught me off guard... and, looking back on it, the experience reinforces for me that Jack Kruse is so much more than just a mad genius shouting at the wind, desperate to be right & desperate to be heard... he's onto some deep, deep things that we ignore to our own chagrin. In this age of internet gurus & 'experts' aplenty, his advice is so much more than an overblown ego trip... even if (& perhaps especially when) it 'blows' your mind!

Just wanted to share...

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-16T19:10:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-194900>

ADHD = bad lit environment = John Ott's Book, Health and Light

### jwhb77 — 2016-06-04T04:39:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-195959>

2016-05-03. Friday

Following Dr. K's suggestion, I got John Ott's book Health and Light... started digging in today. It's interesting from the first chapter... looking forward to plowing through the rest this week.

In other news, I've been having my salmon roe in the AM & oysters after I get home from work this week... feeling much more coherent today. Brain fog & anxiety are much improved over the past few weeks, & I think it was my spending too much time in front of blue-lit screens & slacking off with my oyster-slurping regimen.

### jwhb77 — 2016-06-21T04:51:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-196983>

2016-06-20. Monday

Been reading Ott's Health and Light book, and I'm almost to the chapters that cover human photobiomodulation... hard to put the book down, but since I'm aiming to digest the info I'm pacing myself.

Went to a family reunion of sorts this weekend, and several people mentioned my weight loss & wanted to know what I'd been doing. My aunt kept telling me how good I looked, lol. I take it in stride mostly, since that's all peripheral as far as I am concerned. All I care about is getting better, and by that I mean getting my brain & mind & memory purring along like a well-oiled machine. As it stands, I've got a long ways to go before I reach that goal... my near-daily forgetfulness & struggles to make connections keep me quite humble (if not outright depressed at times). The biggest struggles for me right now are being faithful & consistent with all of my Krusing practices, and staying in a positive frame of mind.

This week, I plan on watching Dr. K's webinar on mitochondria hacking that was just recently posted, & hope to pick up some gems there... also, I'm debating whether I need to pony up & get the Magnetico Super Sleep Pad, or if maybe I'm just feeling impulsive/impatient lately. I definitely see its value theoretically, it's just a stretch for my budget right now... we'll see.

Anywho, that's all for now.

Shalom Friends,
Wil

### caroline — 2016-06-21T06:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-196988>

What protocols do you have in place now?

### jwhb77 — 2016-06-21T15:57:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-197012>

Caroline,

I'm spending 30 min to an hr watching the sun rise, eating a tablespoon or two of salmon roe, & eating a dozen shucked oysters from HamaHama oyster company on most days. Other than that, I eat a standard Paleo-style diet most of the time. I avoid all gluten, beef, nightshades (the majority of the time), and peanuts (I've tested allergic to all of these). I've tried CT on and off, but seem to have a hard time implementing it consistently... especially when life gets busy.

I've also limited my time in front of blue-lit screens quite a bit, though there's room for improvement. I put a blue light filter on my laptop & phone screens, which doesn't eliminate all blue light, but which does seem to help. I also have a pair of 265 nm UV LED bulbs that I run whenever I'm on the laptop, along with some 660nm red bulbs that I often use with it (the laptop) as well. I sometimes chew nicotine gum while studying, which helps.

### jwhb77 — 2016-07-15T00:20:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-198367>

2016-07-14. Thursday

I bit the bullet about 3 weeks ago & ordered a 20 gauss Magnetico, and I've been sleeping on it for the past 2 weeks. Within the first 30 min of lying on it, my body was tingling all over. Within the first hour I started getting dry-mouth. I unintentionally fell asleep on it for a few hours in the late afternoon on that first day, then had a hard time falling asleep on it later that night. The first week was rough, with a lot of flu-like symptoms... runny nose, 'hangover' feelings upon waking, loose stools, etc.

The frequency & intensity of these symptoms diminished after the first week, though, and the second week I started feeling more normal & coherent. Been chugging water like no tomorrow, and I still feel like my mouth & throat are always dry. I would say my energy levels are a little bit better throughout the daytime, though nothing dramatic. What has changed noticeably about my energy is that it's been much more even-keel throughout most days... which is unusual for me. I typically have energy in spurts, with low levels during the afternoon hours... I haven't noticed the drop-off since starting with the Magnetico.

I've experimented a little with keeping my water jug (of spring water) over the Magnetico for a few hours at a time, & it does seem to change something in the character of the water when I do this. It seems to absorb in my mouth much more, and I like the mouthfeel compared to just plain spring water. I also notice having a clearer mind after drinking it.

That's all for now....

### jwhb77 — 2016-07-17T22:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wils-journal-stumbling-forward-one-day-at-a-time.17163/post-198497>

2016-07-17. Sunday

Anyone know if storing drinking water in copper vessels would be a no-go for those with heavy metals/copper toxicity issues?

I also wonder if the light will still have the same effects if I close my eyes while keeping them pointed in the direction of the sun... would the light get through to the retina, or be attenuated too much by passing through the eyelids, to remain effective? Reason I'm curious is that it seems a lot more relaxing to let them close than to struggle to keep them open the entire time...

Hey Wil,

Which red glasses did you get? I've been looking around for some but haven't had any luck yet?

# Thread: very successful hack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-successful-hack.17164/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 46
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Melanie Procter — 2015-11-28T16:39:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-successful-hack.17164/post-182768>

Hi,
I want to report the success of my recent bio hack. It was all very simple. I started out with a number of issues--insulin resistance, bad vision, numbness, leaky gut, tons of allergies.

I am in Phuket Thailand. It is very hot and sunny here so this was a great place to get sun.

The hack was simple.

1\. 3-4 hours of sun a day, primarily morning sun and some daily afternoon su
2 lots of hydration through fresh tropical juices and green juices
3\. 1 serving a day of smoked salmon
4\. CT on my eyes several times a day
5\. stayed away from computer. cell phones and artificial light
6\. During my sun sessions, I always looked into the direction of the sun

Results: After two weeks, my leaky gut was gone and so were my allergies. I started to get bad headaches and eyestrain but felt my eyes were trying to heal themselves. I was bold and threw away my glasses. It was hell to live without eyeglasses. I never thought I could actually cure my vision. Daily my vision got better and better. It has been one month since I took the glasses off and read and write without glasses. I still can't read small print on the back of a bottle but expect that will happen.
