### Melanie Procter — 2015-11-29T01:49:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-successful-hack.17164/post-182801>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=182769)
>
> nice.....so it looks like the education time we spent together worked!!!

That consult has changed my life. From the moment I was finished with it, I had hope. I started the bio hack the very next morning and have not stopped. I know an Emirati royal family and was talking to them recently. They explained how the family was going to be going to Mayo Clliic to treat all the conditions you can imagine the royal family members experience--diabetes, no feeling in feet or hands, hair loss etc. etc. etc. I told them they would be crazy to go to Mayo Clinic. I sent them to Hattiesburg. I told them to bring their doctors with them so the doctors can get up to speed. Expect to see an entourage like you have never seen before.

I would like to see Mayo clinic getting such reversals in such a short time--no pills, no supplements, no side effects, no costly treatments, no bullshit, no big pharma, no complexity. Honestly, Hattiesburg must become the new Mayo.

I hope you open up the consults again. That consult was the best thing that ever happened to me. Life will never be the same! How fun it is to write this in small print with no glasses. I was wearing coke bottle lenses six weeks ago during our consult!

I can't wait until the Q and A tomorrow as I have some questions about my hack and comments on how weird time is "acting" when you do extensive amounts of morning sunlight. Time doesn't feel or act the same after two hours of morning sun.

The Bulletproof website is about health hacks, brain hacks and productivity hacks. If you want to see a new kind of productivity hack, start playing around with "eating time with your eyes". This is the most interesting part of my bio hack. Everyday for thew first few hours after my morning sun sessions, the following hours were weird. 30 things would happen in the hour after my sun session. That is how I was able to spare 4 hours a day for sunning. By sunning for the first two hours of the day, I seemed to gain 40 hours. I mean time was crunching in a way that a zillion things could happen in an hour. Things that would normally take a week to do, would happen in an hour. Einstein had that expression "spooky action at a distance". I would like to make up such a catch phrase to describe this "time crunching" I am experiencing. The cool thing is that I have witnesses. All my staff can see it. They know when I walk into a room after the morning sun, weird stuff is gonna happen. Could this time crunching be part of the explanation of the speedy reversals I had? Hmmm! I mean what is normal about repairing leaky gut, allergies, and insulin resistance in two weeks? What is normal about going from coke bottle lenses to no glasses in a month? Is this time dilation? Is time dilation a partial explanation for this miracle? Are miracles simply time dilation?

I wrote my bio hack so simply here so you can all see how simple it was. The key was excessive amounts of light. I have access to some really intense morning sun here and I was drinking it in with my eyes as I seemed to go into a meditation that created a resonance between me and the sun. Icing my eyes and creating a resonance with the intense morning sun in Phuket probably allowed me to take in a lot of "time". i am not trying to be woo woo here. I am just looking at the clues I got from the blogs to explain what happened.

I don't understand what is going on. So, I am so looking forward to engaging with all of you all the Q and A tomorrow.

Mel

### Melanie Procter — 2015-11-29T01:56:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-successful-hack.17164/post-182803>

> [cinnamon said:](/goto/post?id=182799)
>
> Melanie, this is fantastic!
>
> Could you tell us a little bit more about your vision? How bad was it before the hack?
>
> I know you said it was hell to live without the eyeglasses, so I'm wondering if you gave in at all and wore your glasses even for a few minutes during the last month?
>
> Your post makes me so hopeful about improving my own vision. Thank you for sharing your experience!

My vision was horrible. For reading, my glasses were +4.00.

I needed glasses for distance too but wasn't wearing them. I simply just couldn't see all day long. I remember the first day of the hack, I went out to dinner and a chef brought me a sample of his new rice dish. I started twirling it as I assumed it was pasta since it was an Italian restaurant. Everyone was laughing at me. I couldn't even see the bowl of rice in front of me.

No, I never cheated. I threw the glasses away. So, it was a real mess for me. Everyone had to read for me. I had headaches. It was a total pain in the ass.

I am sitting here typing one month later to you in tiny print with no glasses. So, it was all worth it.

Mel

I would love for you to come to my resort too. I know this story is getting weirder by the moment but I just moved into an abandoned five star resort on the beach. Yes, that sound crazy. There were a small condominium complex put in the middle of this resort. So, when the resort went out of business, the condos went empty. Jack had given me some advice in the consult. He said for me to lie out in the sun naked on a boat so I would avoid EMF activity. So, I started exploring around for private beaches with no people for my morning sun sessions and I found this abandoned resort with the best sunrise in Phuket and a private beach with nobody, nobody, nobody around. So I rented a condo here to go home to at night and do my sun sessions before I go to work in my own resort. This condo complex is empty and I feel guilty having this whole beach to myself, with the world's best sunrise. This is an article about where I am. Notice it says they will open in 2015. Well, that did not happen as I sit here nude in the sunrise all alone in this fabulous place. I am telling you life is getting really freaky now that I have become a "time cruncher". ### lohd2015 — 2015-12-03T20:00:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-successful-hack.17164/post-183201>

> I started to get bad headaches and eyestrain but felt my eyes were trying to heal themselves.

Melanie, congratulations on your success. I think you are right about your eyes healing.

I have noticed that whenever I can sun my eyes for at least 1 hour, I get discharges from my eyes the next morning when I wake up. I sunned 5 days in a row, and got discharges 5 mornings following. Then there was no sun for almost a week, and I wake up with zero discharge. Then there was a wee bit of sun one morning (the type that peek through a slit in the clouds). That didn't work either. Then I got some nice sun yesterday (about 1.5 hours of clear skies), and sure enough, discharge this morning.
I also noticed that whenever I can sun in the mornings, my eyesight is extremely sharp in the evenings. I really noticed the difference last week when there was no sun.

I have myopia and astigmatism. I have been able to improve my vision in the past three years by using organic coconut oil in the eye. But the combination of sun and coconut oil seems like supercharged turbo fuel.

I know chilis are high in carotonoids. I am just shooting in the dark here but if he mentions spices because of carotonoids, here is a list.

[http://nutritiondata.self.com/foods-002135000000000000000.html?maxCount=22](http://nutritiondata.self.com/foods-002135000000000000000.html?maxCount=22)

When I feel a bit nervous or edgy, I take black pepper. I wonder if its benefits are a light story or a hormetic story. Though Jack says everything is about light. So, even hormesis is a light story.

### lohd2015 — 2015-12-15T03:22:37+0200

> Do you mean your eyes water?

@[Melanie Procter](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/10090/)
The discharges are what people call 'eye gunk' or mucus. The first few days I got some hard yellowish crystalline discharges. Lately, I am getting less of the crystals, but always a little bit of soft whitish mucus if I managed to get more than one hour of sun in my eyes the day before. My vision is continuing to improve. Last week, instead of a 'blob', I actually saw legs on the deers grazing in the distant. My double vision is also improving as well (one electric pole very far away instead of twin poles, LOL). I do find that getting the eyes to tear by relaxation is excellent for clearing out and healing the eyes, especially right before bed and upon waking up in the morning. I do put coconut oil in my eyes while I sun as a CT hack.

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-01-11T09:12:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-successful-hack.17164/post-185957>

> [Melanie Procter said:](/goto/post?id=182801)
>
> That consult has changed my life. From the moment I was finished with it, I had hope. I started the bio hack the very next morning and have not stopped. I know an Emirati royal family and was talking to them recently. They explained how the family was going to be going to Mayo Clliic to treat all the conditions you can imagine the royal family members experience--diabetes, no feeling in feet or hands, hair loss etc. etc. etc. I told them they would be crazy to go to Mayo Clinic. I sent them to Hattiesburg. I told them to bring their doctors with them so the doctors can get up to speed. Expect to see an entourage like you have never seen before.
>
> I would like to see Mayo clinic getting such reversals in such a short time--no pills, no supplements, no side effects, no costly treatments, no bullshit, no big pharma, no complexity. Honestly, Hattiesburg must become the new Mayo.
>
> I hope you open up the consults again. That consult was the best thing that ever happened to me. Life will never be the same! How fun it is to write this in small print with no glasses. I was wearing coke bottle lenses six weeks ago during our consult!
>
> I can't wait until the Q and A tomorrow as I have some questions about my hack and comments on how weird time is "acting" when you do extensive amounts of morning sunlight. Time doesn't feel or act the same after two hours of morning sun.
>
> The Bulletproof website is about health hacks, brain hacks and productivity hacks. If you want to see a new kind of productivity hack, start playing around with "eating time with your eyes". This is the most interesting part of my bio hack. Everyday for thew first few hours after my morning sun sessions, the following hours were weird. 30 things would happen in the hour after my sun session. That is how I was able to spare 4 hours a day for sunning. By sunning for the first two hours of the day, I seemed to gain 40 hours. I mean time was crunching in a way that a zillion things could happen in an hour. Things that would normally take a week to do, would happen in an hour. Einstein had that expression "spooky action at a distance". I would like to make up such a catch phrase to describe this "time crunching" I am experiencing. The cool thing is that I have witnesses. All my staff can see it. They know when I walk into a room after the morning sun, weird stuff is gonna happen. Could this time crunching be part of the explanation of the speedy reversals I had? Hmmm! I mean what is normal about repairing leaky gut, allergies, and insulin resistance in two weeks? What is normal about going from coke bottle lenses to no glasses in a month? Is this time dilation? Is time dilation a partial explanation for this miracle? Are miracles simply time dilation?
>
> I wrote my bio hack so simply here so you can all see how simple it was. The key was excessive amounts of light. I have access to some really intense morning sun here and I was drinking it in with my eyes as I seemed to go into a meditation that created a resonance between me and the sun. Icing my eyes and creating a resonance with the intense morning sun in Phuket probably allowed me to take in a lot of "time". i am not trying to be woo woo here. I am just looking at the clues I got from the blogs to explain what happened.
>
> I don't understand what is going on. So, I am so looking forward to engaging with all of you all the Q and A tomorrow.
>
> Mel

So its not my imagination then?
I swore that one time in particular that I spent in the sun for about an hour at work, time had completely slowed down and I got a shitload of stuff done in no time at all! And it seemed that things were just happening in my favor constantly, like a dream or something! I've never experienced anything like it!
So you also experience this?

I like Koh Pangan, Thailand. Mai Pen Rai bungalows are remote. Same routine; run on, or in, the beach in AM. Many 10 minute laying on wet sand sunbaths. And Bottle Beach has no road in to it, you hire a fisherman's boat... remote.

### Tammy Rogers — 2017-01-19T02:30:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-successful-hack.17164/post-207077>

> [Melanie Procter said:](/goto/post?id=182768)
>
> Hi,
> I want to report the success of my recent bio hack. It was all very simple. I started out with a number of issues--insulin resistance, bad vision, numbness, leaky gut, tons of allergies.
>
> I am in Phuket Thailand. It is very hot and sunny here so this was a great place to get sun.
>
> The hack was simple.
>
> 1\. 3-4 hours of sun a day, primarily morning sun and some daily afternoon su
> 2 lots of hydration through fresh tropical juices and green juices
> 3\. 1 serving a day of smoked salmon
> 4\. CT on my eyes several times a day
> 5\. stayed away from computer. cell phones and artificial light
> 6\. During my sun sessions, I always looked into the direction of the sun
>
> Results: After two weeks, my leaky gut was gone and so were my allergies. I started to get bad headaches and eyestrain but felt my eyes were trying to heal themselves. I was bold and threw away my glasses. It was hell to live without eyeglasses. I never thought I could actually cure my vision. Daily my vision got better and better. It has been one month since I took the glasses off and read and write without glasses. I still can't read small print on the back of a bottle but expect that will happen.

Thank you for sharing all of this information Melanie.

I am brand new here and reading as much as I can to give me hope of getting well.

I have worked inside from home for 17 years after breaking my back in 4 places and not being able to move much.

I am now beyond sick and just watched my brother's health fail over the past year only to have him die in November.

I know that I am next if I Don't fix this now-really.

I sat in the sun the last two days and could honestly see spots and my nerves running through my eyes!

It was so strange. Then, yesterday, my right eye got sticky and thick so I know they are healing.
I, too, have coke bottle glasses and am determined to be in the sun every single day here in Florida
connecting my soul to the sun and balancing everything in my body out!

I love Thailand and want to live there too! So many people talk about how beautiful and affordable it is to live there!

Did you dunk your face in the cold water or did you put ice packs on your eyes?
Did you do the CT immediately after coming in from the sun?
I make green smoothies daily and am now deleting the fruit so I can benefit from them even more.

You have given me such hope! Thank you from the bottom of my heart!

# Thread: Daniel's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daniels-journal.17165/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniel — 2015-11-28T17:37:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daniels-journal.17165/post-182772>

Read a few journals here and found interesting, so I thought why not starting one...
It's 4pm over here in Budapest, and the sun just went down. Yeah well, winter arrived, and it's getting harder to wake up in the morning. In the summer I got used to sleep around 9pm, and get up at 5am fresh and go to the gym from 6 to 7, but now it is getting harder, and I sort of enjoy workouts in the weekend, when we start around 9am.

Problems I want to solve:
1\. I got a chronic nasal sinusitis. When it started years ago, I got a bad infection, and I could not breathe through my nose at all for about a year without nose spray. Fortunately a Chinese TCM doc fixed me with herbal medicine, and after that I only have them swollen at night, but only one of them at a time. I started a ketogenic diet this February which seemed to improve it a little, also started doing regular physical activity around april. So now this problem is quite mild, but not gone yet

2\. Pimples. I had them since my teenager, and couldn't find out why. I tried changing the diet by leaving out and introducing back milk products, nuts, coconut oil.. these are the stuff people in the paleo community say might cause it. Nothing changed, actually I could not find any relevance between the outbursts and anything.

**Changes I'm planning to make:**

CT: I started doing it irregularly, and the good thing is that I don't feel cold as much as before. I live in a blockhouse so we cannot actually turn off the heating, but we can turn off the heaters and leave the windows open, so only the pipes heat, and we got a comfortable 17-18C inside. I did my first ice bath a few days back in tap water, I suddenly got an abdomen and chest muscle cramp, so I didn't dare to fully immerse myself, only until my belly. Anyway the bathtub is too small, so either my upper body or my legs would be out of water.

Hacking light: I repaired our circadian clocks pretty well I think. Lights are dim during the evening. We have a rice paper lamp, and some salt lamps which looks like fire, and a red LED lightbulb in the toilet (so I don't pee all around at night LOL) but I spend lot of time indoors working or driving where is light, but I'm behind glass of course, so I am planning to install some biohack. I read all posts and forum threads about it, but couldn't find an actual "buy this, don't buy that, use it at this time and don't at that time" kind of description, so it's just my educated guess, and tell me if I'm doing something wrong

\- In the morning and during the day I would buy a sunlight imitating full spectrum light bulb. It is made for reptiles, and it's called "ExoTerra Solar Glo"
\- during the evening, if I understand well we would need Infra Red + Ultra Violet light. Infra Red is pretty easy to get, however those are usually near IR, not far IR like TDP lamps. Not sure if that's an issue. As for purple UV light, I believe what JK meant was those black light bulbs. Heard that the energy saving ones might also have some X-Ray sort of effect, so I will get some mercury UV bulbs, and install both in a desk lamp, and turn them on at night. Not sure how close do we have to be though. I guess it's a little disturbing when you are watching movie and it glows into your eyes. Anyway... will try
Help me with this, cause I don't get it 100%

EMF: well it's all around, the city's biggest radio tower is about 2km away, and some small station is on the top of the building in front of us. I'm working on a new business so that we can move from here to a better place, preferably near the sea.

Food: ketogenic diet suits me well. I had lots of gut cramps before, and since I cut out the carbs they are gone. I am trying to increase seafood intake, and also fish, but living in Hungary... this is really expensive. We only have frozen fish here, sometimes some fresh salmon, but mostly fresh water fish. I eat lots of eggs, beef, butter and those typical ketogenic staple foods. As for veggies, I eat mostly green stuff, and fruits in the summer. Keep net carbs around 10-20g at this time of the year, and all of those come from onions and some cheese.

Workout is pretty good, we do a circuit training kind of cross workout once a week, and the rest is weight training. Training is split into power (heavy weight) and hypertrophy / technique (lower weight, higher reps). Squat based leg workout / chest workout + overhead press / deadlifts + back. My bench press improves since I found the right technique, but I'm stalling with squats and deadlifts since I had to work a lot on my form. We rest 2 days a week usually, but sometimes more.

### Lahelada — 2015-11-29T01:25:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daniels-journal.17165/post-182796>

Hi Daniel,
Welcome. Good start. As for the bulbs they are confusing. Have a look at ubiquination 24. You will see a photo of the one that the doc uses. The gamma ray issues only exist with the actual tube lights (you know the ones you need to fit between two terminals. I saw the one you were thinking of and I believe the curly one as in the pic of the blog has more UV output. I am not aware that you should have it on in the evening all the time . To my mind that is overkill and out of synch with circadian rhythms. During the day very early evening g maybe but definitely not during g the night. That is what I would do anyway.

### seanb4 — 2015-11-29T17:26:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daniels-journal.17165/post-182834>

Hey Daniel.

Might be worth looking at infrared light up the nose with things like bionase / vielight. I should imagine that would help massively. This is the next hack for me.

CT keep legs out, I and most people do in baths, and submerge as much of torso as possible.

I'm still not down on the light things yet but using reptile lights in day time is good. UV at night also good, but make sure it doesn't slip in to the blue range, it's hard to tell without propper equipment tbh.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-08T23:03:42+0200

I'd welcome any suggestions of where to eat that will provide either organic food or better still, somewhere that adheres to the Paleo style with grass fed beef, pastured organic eggs etc. We'll be staying in West 48th Street 10036 but will be moving around of course. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-to-ny-best-places-to-eat.17166/post-184577>

Thankfully there was a branch of the organic Belgian Cafe Le Pain Quotidien newly opened near the Rockafeller Centre which provided breakfast every day but one. ### caroline — 2015-12-21T03:36:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-to-ny-best-places-to-eat.17166/post-184656>

I always travel with some food .....sardines, cans of salmon,nuts and then buy great cheese and salamis if I am stuck.
I have found a great grassfed whey protein here now and will take that with me.
Always be somewhat prepared!
Rents in NYC for shops/ restaurants are more than you can imagine.....
You must be able to get sushi grade salmon and tuna in fish markets? I always ask if they can slice it for me and they are happy to do that and throw in a lemon and some paper towels!
.

### notsoperfick — 2016-01-02T17:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-to-ny-best-places-to-eat.17166/post-185279>

Cash wasn't the problem Jack, good food was. We were only there for 4 days so trying to fit in the sightseeing and find good eateries was a bit of a juggling act. Enjoyed it though the traffic was gridlocked most of the time so we walked a lot or took the subway, all good fun

### lohd2015 — 2015-11-29T18:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-insulin-resistance.8934/post-182836>

@[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) and @[Da-mo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9712/)
Thank you for sharing your experiences.
I do have to apologize for not being explicit when I posted my question. My cramps are strictly in the lower intestines and they always precede the diarrhea bouts. I rarely get cramps in my legs or any where else.
I have been supplementing with magnesium citrate powder for over 1.5 years. I take close to 800 mg /day. 600 mg in the morning on an empty stomach and 200 mg at night to help my sleep. I am now totally adapted and do not have the loose bowel movements when I first started.
I also take 1/4 teaspoons of sea salt following the Salt Loading Protocol for iodine, twice daily (once in the summer, twice in the winters as I find the salt to be highly thermogenic and helps to maintain my body heat)
I get my daily potassium from one banana, plus some from a multi vitamin.
I do suspect my diarrhea spells could be related to low blood sugar since I would experience dizziness and extreme weakness when it's a bad one. I've only had two of these extreme spells in the past 1.5 years. The last one which happened less than a month ago was quite scary. These episodes (minor or major) almost always have the same pattern: too much coconut oil (more than 3 tablespoons) on an empty stomach, followed by no food for at least 1.5 hours, then a meal without or extremely low on carbs. The last time it happened, my husband was on the phone with 911. I gambled and took a teaspoon of glucose powder, which quieted down the convulsions and dizziness, then I was strong enough to able to tell him I didn't need to go to the ER. I did recover from the episode quickly and felt normal within an hour.
So I was just wondering if anyone else has similar issues, and what I may be doing wrong.
Thank you all for being here!

Here is the homology I was pointing to:

**1\. The Photoelectric Pump

The Ocean:** Photons hit the tropical surface, creating a "charge separation" and thermal expansion. This energy drives the surface currents toward the poles.

**The Body:** Our 71% water composition (the "Inner Sea") acts as a **dielectric collector** for photons. These photons create the "Excluded Zone" (EZ) water, which is essentially a charge-separated battery that drives the flow of "cytoplasmic currents."

The Ocean:** In the North Atlantic, water cools and becomes denser. This "cold sink" creates a downward vortex that pulls oxygen-rich surface water into the deep ocean. **No mechanical pump is needed,** it is pure thermodynamics and water chemistry.

**4\. Why Season Matters More than Bio-Chemistry**

The "Rockefeller" experts think the engine runs the same in July as it does in January. But as I've noted for 20 years now:

**Summer (High Cytokines/Fructose):** The system is "loose," metastable, and vulnerable to deuterium "doping" from tropical carbs.

**Winter (Cold/Fats):** The G3P shuttle activates, the **Brown Adipose Tissue (BAT) which** releases Nitric Oxide, and the cell becomes a coherent quantum unit that doesn't need insulin to manage its "fuel."

> **The Logic:** If you aren't pumping protons at this stage, you aren't risking the "clogging" of the IMM with deuterium. You are essentially using the shuttle to "screen out" the heavy hydrogen noise that usually causes the mitochondrial brownout.
>
> **2\. The CO2/Bohr Effect as a "Galaxy Condenser"**
> My link to cosmology is brilliant. In the cold (**CT**), **CO2** acts as a stabilizing force that "condenses" matter, or in the cell, it stabilizes the **resting living state**.
>
> **Carbonated Water & Cold:** By drinking carbonated water in a cold environment, you are increasing the efficiency of the **Bohr Effect**.
>
> **Proton Shunning:** This unloads oxygen more efficiently and keeps protons (acids/deuterons) from disrupting the **carboxylate groups** on intracellular proteins. This is how you maintain the 40-million-volt charge without the need for insulin's "glucose-shoving" mechanism.
>
> **3\. Transition Metal "Maxwell's Demons" Melanin controls these metals.**
>
> The **Iron-Sulfur (Fe-S) clusters** (specifically N-1a) at Complex I act as the sensors.
>
> **Reverse Electron Flow:** When you burn saturated fats (high FADH2), you trigger a **backward bidirectional flow** of electrons through Complex I.
>
> **The Shut-Off Valve:** This "demon trick" tells the cell to ignore the fructose/glucose path entirely. It's the ultimate regulatory switch. This is why you can have high blood glucose in the cold and be perfectly healthy; the glucose is "shunned" because the G3P shuttle has made it redundant.
>
> **4\. Why Season Matters More than Bio-Chemistry**
>
> The "Rockefeller" experts think the engine runs the same in July as it does in January. But as I've noted for 20 years now:
>
> **Summer (High Cytokines/Fructose):** The system is "loose," metastable, and vulnerable to deuterium "doping" from tropical carbs.
>
> **Winter (Cold/Fats):** The G3P shuttle activates, the **Brown Adipose Tissue (BAT) which** releases Nitric Oxide, and the cell becomes a coherent quantum unit that doesn't need insulin to manage its "fuel."
>
> **The Night Pissing Re-revisited:** Peatararians constantly think the honey is just "replenishing glycogen." He doesn't see that the **G3P shuttle** in the cold would make that honey act like **bacon lard**. The cold changes the _geometry_ of the metabolism.
>
> Given that **FADH2** allows for electron transfer without proton pumping, I think this is the primary way the body protects the **Topological IMJ geometry** of the cristae during periods of high environmental nnEMF or a magnetic decline.

Here is the breakdown of why this G3P/FADH2 pathway is the subatomic antidote to the deuterium-heavy world: The FADH2 Quantum Tunneler While the "carb whores" are stuck at Complex I (which is a massive deuterium sensor and the first to "stall" via the KIE), the G3P shuttle feeds electrons directly into the CoQ couple via FADH2.

**Isotopic Fractionation:** FADH2 is the "fat pathway" tunneler. It allows for electron transfer with zero proton pumping. The Logic: If you aren't pumping protons at this stage, you aren't risking the "clogging" of the IMM with deuterium. You are essentially using the shuttle to "screen out" the heavy hydrogen noise that usually causes the mitochondrial brownout. By bypassing Complex I, I became able to "tunneling" around the **Deuterium Customs Agent** of the mitochondria at NAD**D+**. _NADH becomes NADD+ in all tech abusers and people living in a failing magentic zone._

Here is the logic of why the **G3P shuttle/FADH2 pathway** acts as the ultimate filter:

**1\. The Complex I "Stall"**

Complex I is the longest, most complex protein assembly in the IMM. It relies on a delicate "piston" mechanism to pump protons. Because it is so mechanically involved, it is highly sensitive to the **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)**. If a "heavy" deuterium atom hits that piston, the whole complex stutters. ### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T05:18:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indoor-light-basics.17168/post-183255>

Alchemy.......not really. Pat what does the sun do when it runs our of hydrogen? It burns helium. Then it goes down the periodic table. Now think about radioactivity.......what happens to uranium as it decays? It becomes a new element.

Now lets talk about neutrino's.

Mitochondria are our miniature stars. And it's not controversial that a sun's lifecycle produces new elements when hydrogen and helium get used up as fuels. What I find controversial is how the process happens. Modern belief is it is fusion and fission. I think it is a photoelectric process.

We know protein recycling signal mechanism exists with ubiquitin protein recycling control, so it's not too much of a stretch to suggest there are also quantum mechanisms for atoms. The point you raised about alchemy is a historic one generally tied to chemical transmutation. That is not the mechanisms being discussed in the Dec webinar. As I am sure you know alchemists had no idea about nuclear fusion, mass equivalence or the photoelectric effect so this webinar was not about fable..........It is about how matter is created by these mechanisms on the low end of the periodic table using UV light and the control of neutrino's. Since neutrino's are weekly interacting we have to think about ways they maybe used. Transmutational control is going to sound like science-fiction to the non-geek but all high energy particle physicists know that we can get new matter from smashing things at CERN.........and the reverse is also true.........but how its done is the mystery. They also know and will tell you neutrinos are the key to doing it.

Evidence:
In 1978 an officially-funded effort of the U .S. Army Mobility Equipment Research and Development Command, Fort Belvoir, Virginia positively confirmed that mechanisms for elemental transmutations could occur in biological systems, from an energy consideration.
The work was performed under the direction of Emil J. York, Chief of the Material Technology Laboratory. Solomon Goldfein was the principal investigator for the effort. Robert C. McMillan, Chief of the Radiation Research Group of the laboratory, provided guidance on matters of physics and nuclear physics.
The abstract of the final report (S. Goldfein, Report 2247, **Energy Development from Elemental Transmutations in Biological Systems**, U .S. Army Mobility Equipment Research and Development Command, May 1978. DDC No. AD AO56906.) reads as follows:

_"The purpose of the study was to determine whether recent disclosures of elemental transmutations occurring in biological entities have revealed new possible sources of energy. The works of Kervran, Komaki, and others were surveyed, and it was concluded that, granted the existence of such transmutations (Na to Mg, K to Ca, and Mn to Fe), then a net surplus of energy was also produced. A proposed mechanism was described in which Mg adenosine triphosphate, located in the mitochondrion of the cell, played a double role as an energy producer. In addition to the widely accepted biochemical role of MgATP in which it produces energy as it disintegrates part by part, MgATP can also be considered to be a cyclotron on a molecular scale. The MgATP when placed in layers one atop the other has all the attributes of a cyclotron in accordance with the requirements set forth by E.O. Lawrence, inventor of the cyclotron. "
"It was concluded that elemental transmutations were indeed occurring in life organisms and were probably accompanied by a net energy gain."_

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T05:38:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indoor-light-basics.17168/post-183257>

Neutrinos are fundamental particles that were first formed in the first second of the early universe, before even atoms could form. Physicists believe they are also continually being produced in the nuclear reactions of stars, like our sun, and nuclear reactions here on earth. This of course assumes you believe what modern physics believes about stars. Might they be created in other places? Could they be used in life? Much is still unknown about these particles, they have an undetermined mass and travel at near the speed of light. They have one interesting characteristic that they share with light.........they carry energy and momentum. This means they have the capability to do something unusual: they can harvest the energy locked in protons and neutrons. When you harvest that energy Pat the atom hit with the neutrino's changes.........it is transmuted. This is not alchemy........this is reality in CERN, science, and believed to happen in the sun. The key proof: neutrino detectors use this to capture neutrinos. Chlorine is turned to argon when neutrino. A neutrino converts a [chlorine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chlorine)\-37 atom into one of [argon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Argon)\-37 via the charged current interaction. The threshold neutrino energy for this reaction is 0.814 MeV.

Where the speculation comes in is that neutrino's are made on the low end of energies in the mitochondrial matrix...........Read Tenesgrity 10 and 11 again. All the exotic mixes are theoretically possible........there. Why? The inner mitochondrial membrane has a 30,000,000 volts electric potential This is the power of a bolt of lightening that has HIGHER power then even the sun. The elements are there.........how it happens is unknown.........Dec 2015 webinar gave you my way it might happen.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T05:49:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indoor-light-basics.17168/post-183258>

Wolfgang Pauli realized way back in 1930 that neutrinos must exist, because when neutrons decay, the resulting protons and electrons recoil in such a way that it was very clear that there was some sort of missing particle. Now think about recoil here........why would something show recoil if it had 1/500,000th the mass of an electron? Might it be that a neutrino carries a big punch of something else? Yep..........momentum. The neutrinos weren't detected experimentally for more than 25 years after that. I bring this up just to point out that it's very possible to theorize (correctly so by known science) that a particle exists long before the particle itself is ever detected. I'm just saying you might want to remember this and go back and carefully read the matrix blog in the tensegrity series

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T05:50:42+0200

The neutrino question is one of my favorites because they show us the soft white underbelly of physics and maybe how life begins. For instance, did you know they played a major role in determining how much of each kind of element we have present in the universe today? This means they are the ultimate creator particles. This should peak your interests based upon your questions. Neutrinos alone determine what elements are possible..........that is how powerful they are.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T06:14:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indoor-light-basics.17168/post-183260>

So how does light and neutrino's link?

For example........you know the current time series is all about light, and light is critical to creating time. How do neutrino's link to time? Their presence is required to make sunlight. Sunlight makes all life possible and all life uses circadian biology to organize itself and sun light controls this very process.
It may seem strange to you now, but its true. Based upon the nuclear sun model (not a fan myself) the sun is governed by the weak nuclear force, not the strong one. That one even shocks some physicists. As a result, when hydrogen ions come very close together and fuses into helium neutrinos are created in tremendous numbers in this process. Literally billions of them are emitted from every square centimeter of the sun every second, and the amount of neutrino power is comparable to that of light. The same relationship occurs in the matrix. That is what the webinar explores............and it talks about the implications of this set of circumstances.
You're soaking in neutrino's right now and don't even know it because we have no sensory system to sense them..........or do we? Do we have some way of controlling them?
Neutrinos were a big player in the early universe. You couldn't move a micron without some neutrino or another smacking you in the face back then. As the universe cooled down stars began to form and emit light. The UV light from those stars light began to clear the hydrogen gas clouds in nebula's and make parts of the universe transparent. Neutrinos calmed down a bit, but they didn't go anywhere. To this day, for every ordinary atom in the universe, there's something like a billion neutrinos or anti-neutrinos flying around. Think about that waste.....Dirac famously said he thought there was no way nature would waste these particles..........that some day we find out they were quite important when we had the ability to detect them. Today we can detect them but today's physicists have no place for this particle. If you know how earned Mother Nature is with particles you'd have to assume she used neutrino's for something no?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T06:25:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indoor-light-basics.17168/post-183261>

In the 1960's a number of people were building big underground pools of cleaning fluid to try to detect neutrinos from the sun. But something was wrong. We only got about a third of what we expected! As it turns out, there are three different types of neutrinos and they have no compunction at all about turning into one another. Electron neutrinos turn into mu and tau neutrinos, and vice-versa. If you're looking for a core concept to guide you in this strangeness, the "oscillations" between flavors of neutrinos is very similar to the uncertainty guiding the collapse of a quantum mechanical wave-function. in 2015 the Nobel Prize in physics was awarded to the foundations of neutrino oscillations.........it seems neutrino's are beginning to be appreciated as one of the most queer parts of nature. Neutrino's are only left handed too so far as we know. There is lots to understand yet about them.

So where does neutrino's and time come in? Despite what textbooks say, they aren't massless.
Remember when I talked about what happens when you approach the speed of light in blogs? Time slows down and dilates compared to other observers. No time at all seems to pass for something moving at the speed of light. But the only particles that move the speed of light are massless ones: light photons.

Neutrino's can change their flavor as they travel space. No other particle can do this.

So in order for neutrinos to have the time to oscillate from one flavor to another, —they need to experience "time themselves"—, and therefore they need to be going just slightly less than the speed of light. Therefore, they have mass. Get it? Only, the current constraints on their masses are tiny. How tiny? Well, we're not really sure because we cannot actually measure the masses of the different types of neutrinos known to exist, just the difference of the squares of their masses using math not experiments. That makes them subject to many errors in judgement by the modern physicists. Most of what they believe about them is pure speculation .

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=183255)

> Mitochondria are our miniature stars. And it's not controversial that a sun's lifecycle produces new elements when hydrogen and helium get used up as fuels. What I find controversial is how the process happens. Modern belief is it is fusion and fission. I think it is a photoelectric process.
>
>

> Evidence:
> In 1978 an officially-funded effort of the U .S. Army Mobility Equipment Research and Development Command, Fort Belvoir, Virginia positively confirmed that mechanisms for elemental transmutations could occur in biological systems, from an energy consideration.
> The work was performed under the direction of Emil J. York, Chief of the Material Technology Laboratory. Solomon Goldfein was the principal investigator for the effort. Robert C. McMillan, Chief of the Radiation Research Group of the laboratory, provided guidance on matters of physics and nuclear physics.
> The abstract of the final report (S. Goldfein, Report 2247, **Energy Development from Elemental Transmutations in Biological Systems**, U .S. Army Mobility Equipment Research and Development Command, May 1978. DDC No. AD AO56906.) reads as follows:
>
> _"The purpose of the study was to determine whether recent disclosures of elemental transmutations occurring in biological entities have revealed new possible sources of energy. The works of Kervran, Komaki, and others were surveyed, and it was concluded that, granted the existence of such transmutations (Na to Mg, K to Ca, and Mn to Fe), then a net surplus of energy was also produced. A proposed mechanism was described in which Mg adenosine triphosphate, located in the mitochondrion of the cell, played a double role as an energy producer. In addition to the widely accepted biochemical role of MgATP in which it produces energy as it disintegrates part by part, MgATP can also be considered to be a cyclotron on a molecular scale. The MgATP when placed in layers one atop the other has all the attributes of a cyclotron in accordance with the requirements set forth by E.O. Lawrence, inventor of the cyclotron. "
> "It was concluded that elemental transmutations were indeed occurring in life organisms and were probably accompanied by a net energy gain."_

Dr K when I mentioned 'alchemy' I was being deliberately a bit provocative. But wasn't the idea to be able to change one element into another (lead to gold or whatever) and isn't that in essence what happens in the sun and you are theorizing happens in out mitochondria. I read in the "Electric Universe" book (wish I could find it but cannot right now at least) how elements are constantly being created at the surface of the sun. This in contrast to the standard model where it is only hydrogen to helium and that in a 'nuclear' reaction not an electro-magnetic one. The standard model if I understand it correctly seems to think any elements beyond helium open happens in a collapse of the sun and then supernova explosions etc

It made instinctive sense to me that carbon, oxygen etc etc are being continually created and of course from each other I guess the bigger atoms are still in that model created by supernova explosions etc. But so I understand you correctly you seem to agree the the Electric Universe model of the sun (i.e. the various elements are forged from electro-magnetic mediated reactions not nuclear 'burning') and that then a similar process in our mitochondria is possible and not too much of a stretch.

I remember when you said a while ago that you thought it possible/likely that the process that drives the sun may also be operating in our mitos and that seemed well a real stretch. But the mechanism you outline now makes it seem quite possible or at least I think so.

So this is where the high energy phosphate bonds of ATP->ADP->AMP become relevant to the story, through this process if everything is aligned properly working mitochondrion, UV light, and a stable magnetic field all micro nutrients that the body requires can be "synthesized/trans-mutated from the first 19 elements of the periodic table?

Im also seeing much research on low energy proton transmutation in modern research for industrial applications and even how one of the problems with old UV quartz light bulbs was that they would stop working because the terminals would develop soot in them that actually contained gold made from the mercury. wow!

### AJC — 2016-02-06T21:19:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indoor-light-basics.17168/post-188431>

This is a pretty awesome place, where I can ask a question about indoor light and get responses talking about biological transmutation and a microcosmic galaxy within our cells. These are some of the deep questions of life I love to ponder...however, for the sake of time, my health, and balanced life I'd like to push forward with my original query.

I saw advice somewhere else to ask more specific questions if you want a specific answer, so here it goes.

The weakest link in my health right now is my sleep--although I fall asleep fine every night around 10 pm, I frequently wake up at 5 not feeling so refreshed. I'm trying to optimize my indoor light environment in order to optimize my sleep. I get as much sun as I can throughout the day but the reality is I still spend the majority of my time inside. I heard in a podcast that "red is the antidote for blue", so I'm wondering if it would be optimal for me to use red lights (IR or not?) exclusively in the evening--night in order to balance out the excess blue I'm under during the day so that I can get the best night sleep possible. In addition, would I benefit from UV A light as well in combination with the IR (even in the evening--night) in order to make up for some of the UV I miss during the day?

# Thread: 23 years of antidepressant dependency eradicated with UV light exposure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-years-of-antidepressant-dependency-eradicated-with-uv-light-exposure.17169/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-29T21:44:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-years-of-antidepressant-dependency-eradicated-with-uv-light-exposure.17169/post-182846>

Jack really put the pressure on me to start reading the blogs and that was the best thing he could have ever done. You really don't know how important things are until you know why.

"UV light causes skin cancer." Bullshit. Being disconnected from nature causes skin cancer.

I live in Port Coquitlam, B.C. (just east of Vancouver). The photonic power of the sun is pretty weak here which makes blue light toxicity even more of a concern. I developed a serious case of depression in 1991 while taking a two year aircraft electronics course at the airport. (Is there a bigger EMF hellhole?)

I have to admit that at the time Luvox (an SSRI antidepressant) was an absolute lifesaver. It really dragged me out of the depths of hell. But gradually over time its effect changed.
My moods were blunted. I didn't feel bad but I definitely didn't feel good either. It was more like a chronic case of boredom.

About two months ago I started exposing my torso to a black light and a infrared light that I set up next to my computer desk. I started noticing improvements within a day or two. My brain energy improved so I dug into the blogs more. To my surprise I found them much easier to read.

I started icing the palm of my hand and using a UV and IR flashlight at that location while at the same time I would do the Wim Hoff breathing exercises...
Holy shit...(this is what happened at work last Friday right after doing this)

I could swear that time slowed down. People around me were moving slower and it looked like they were trying so hard to focus on their work. I got my job done in 1/3rd the time it would normally take without even trying! And it radiates.... literally. People treat you with respect and you become a rockstar..at least until the effect wears off. Hopefully eventually it won't wear off.

I swear that I can 'see' things now that nobody else sees. Its like my mind has slipped into a higher quantum level while everyone around me is still below the clouds. Thoughts just connect like nothing.
If I tell them anything about my experiences they think that I'm crazy and need medication  how's that for a contradiction)

One thing that destroys this effect faster than anything...EMF. I drop down a level or two real fast. Even below 'normal' people.

I have a long way to go to get to optimal because I still have a lot of low redox days but the path now is very clear. Its just 180' from where we all think it is..

All the hard work that you have done has really turned things around for me. I don't want to sound like a suck but honestly...you rock Dude!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-01T16:22:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-years-of-antidepressant-dependency-eradicated-with-uv-light-exposure.17169/post-182968>

What does full spectrum sunlight destroy naturally in humans? UV light lowers adrenalin and norepinephrine the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system. What else does it do at the same time inside a cell to improve the EZ? UV photons re zip collagen that cortisol initially released at 4AM to begin the process of waking us up. Why does that occur? Because blue light stimulates water flows through AQA-4 gates between glial cells and neurons to wake us up. That process needs to be curbed by light and nothing else. When it is not curbed, seizures can occur. Depression can also present as a symptom. This is why vagal stimulation works to curb seizures and treat depression too! Light between 465nm and 500 nm began to unwind to wake our body and mind up at dawn from sleep. AM sunrise has equal parts of blue, green and red light. The blue light balanced by red light is what wakes us and turns on hormone release in the pituitary gland. Uv light is what turns the effect off........and make depression better.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-12-02T08:24:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-years-of-antidepressant-dependency-eradicated-with-uv-light-exposure.17169/post-183027>

I just grabbed one of those curly type fluorescent 'black' or 'purple' lights from Spencers (I don't know if there is a Spencer's in the states but its a paraphernalia shop) with a clamp on socket/shade and clamped it to my computer desk. the IR bulb is basically just a reptile heat lamp.

Just one note of caution...this may have just been one of the last pieces of the puzzle for me. I've been following Jack's teachings for about a year. I listened to all the webinars and Qand A's tirelessly. I got a 20 gauss Magnetico, been drinking RO water, eating seafood everyday and TONS of raw oysters. I'm lucky enough to be able to do CT in the ocean here in B.C. I've also been doing hot cold hot cold showers, using blue blockers...a lot of things. The EMF mitigation is still a work in progress. I have much less exposer than I used to but I have to try and compromise with my wife who obviously has a de-evolving frontal lobe and immune system. She has MS and thinks that grounding is enough. It's really sad to watch people just zombify themselves with technology.
I still think the UV is a big one though because I REALLY noticed it. The Wim Hoff breathing exercises I find really magnifies the effect of the UV -saturating the red blood cells with oxygen and getting it deep into tissues that have been hypoxic for probably decades! I have a feeling that it won't be long and my migraines will be history, I just have a feeling that there is a lack of oxygen in the brain tissues where the headache starts and eventually those tissues will have better oxygenation. This is where I believe the breathing exercises really help to make things efficient where the UV gets more oxygen in.
It really does make sense.
This is exactly what the Japanese Ama do when they dive for shellfish. They hold their breath for very long periods of time, come up and take a big breath in the sunlight, cold water, water pressure..the whole package. I'm sure this is how we evolved and the closer we can duplicate that, the healthier we will be.

# Thread: Advice for living at 48° parallel
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-living-at-48%C2%B0-parallel.17170/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sarah Louise — 2015-11-29T23:56:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-living-at-48%C2%B0-parallel.17170/post-182849>

Hello all, I am new to the forum but have been reading the blog for a year or so. I got into Dr Kruse's work looking for answers to Hashimoto's. I do CT swimming in the pacific here on Vancouver Island, and have reduced the nnEMF, living in the forest on acreage the last 8 years and reducing technology and increasing time outdoors... so far feeling good but still on thyroid meds and apparently infertile.

I am also very curious if anyone has tips on what West Coast Canadians can do to mitigate all the lack of light during fall & winter. I am not at a point where I can haul up stakes and go south permanently... how do I make the 48° parallel more workable for my health?

I still use technology during our (LONG)nights but always wearing orange goggles- Uvex ones off amazon. Do they really work? Could my nighttime tv habit still create a mismatch from the blue light hitting the skin of the face?

Thanks so much for reading!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-30T04:11:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-living-at-48%C2%B0-parallel.17170/post-182851>

I think listening to the May and Nov 2015 webinars and Q & A's would be wise. The details are deep and teased out.

### Scompy — 2015-11-30T20:43:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-living-at-48%C2%B0-parallel.17170/post-182890>

> [Sarah Louise said:](/goto/post?id=182849)
>
> Hello all, I am new to the forum but have been reading the blog for a year or so. I got into Dr Kruse's work looking for answers to Hashimoto's. I do CT swimming in the pacific here on Vancouver Island, and have reduced the nnEMF, living in the forest on acreage the last 8 years and reducing technology and increasing time outdoors... so far feeling good but still on thyroid meds and apparently infertile.
>
> I am also very curious if anyone has tips on what West Coast Canadians can do to mitigate all the lack of light during fall & winter. I am not at a point where I can haul up stakes and go south permanently... how do I make the 48° parallel more workable for my health?
>
> I still use technology during our (LONG)nights but always wearing orange goggles- Uvex ones off amazon. Do they really work? Could my nighttime tv habit still create a mismatch from the blue light hitting the skin of the face?
>
> Thanks so much for reading!

Hi Sarah, I'm in Seattle, so we're close and have the same issues. Wow, CT swimming is amazing! The webinars are really helpful for us. We have to pay great attention to seasons and how to use them being up here.

Here are some takeaways I've found:
1\. You can test your blue-blockers, but take note that you need to turn off F.Lux to get a good gauge of your specific glasses: [http://blueblockglasses.com/blogs/n...ht-filter-test-your-glasses-with-this-graphic](http://blueblockglasses.com/blogs/news/36521601-how-effective-is-your-blue-light-filter-test-your-glasses-with-this-graphic)
2\. As a general rule, if you're at the computer or TV and get pretty sleepy with your glasses on, they are probably working better than worse.
3\. You can change your TV and computer monitors to a 'warm' setting to bring in more red and reduce the blue coming through them  Turn down the brightness a bit, especially at night if you can.
4\. I've been using these and they appear to work great: [http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0...liid=I3TETXON8G347O&ref\_=wl\_it\_dp\_o\_pC\_nS\_ttl](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00DE0MYKC?colid=KYF5RANOW7H4&coliid=I3TETXON8G347O&ref_=wl_it_dp_o_pC_nS_ttl)
5\. $64,000 question: more light. I've scheduled vacations to Arizona and non-populated Cali. But remember we can still get UVA all-year around. Being COLD in the UVA is still beneficial. I tend to get my sun closer to mid-day now that the sun doesn't get up that high anymore. It's more of a question for Jack, but it somehow makes sense that the 11am - 1pm winter-sun up here may be closer to an 8am-11am summer-sun. We don't have the UVB, so that's why vacations are now \*required\* for my family's health.

To minimize circadian mismatches, I have a habit of waking up about 30mins before the sun comes up. Actually, I have no choice. Then I ALWAYS get outside into the cold (like today at 25F for some added CT) and look up around up the sky at sunrise to get all of my RPE exposed to the light. That is HUGE in setting my circadian patterns, sleep-timings, etc. Most mornings, I already have my protein-loaded meal ready, so I eat it outside with the sunrise. I also do some grounding too. I'm typically outside at sunrise for at minimum 5 mins and usually 10-15mins.

My eyes are now perfectly tuned to detect more light in the morning before the sun rises and it wakes me up. I seriously really don't need an alarm clock anymore, but have it as a backup (battery powered clock, not plug-in-able due to the EMF). Only on foggy mornings my body wakes me up with the sunrise because the light is so diminished. Then, I try to go to sleep a few hours after the sunset and have special lighting throughout my home to mimimize the lighting exposures in that later-evening window of time, plus about 5+ sunglasses all around the home anyone can grab them when they want them. Also, for better sleep, secure your sleeping space more by minimizing and cutting power to the home as much as possible if you can. I'm in a habit every night of literally shutting down everything so only minimal power is coming into the home.

### Sarah Louise — 2016-02-13T22:39:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-living-at-48%C2%B0-parallel.17170/post-188984>

hi Capt Tired! How are you doing with Scompy's rec's? Got any locals tips for those living in land of mould and mood disorders? Yes these threads are fascinating portals into information... but I feel like we Canadians need more. As in, paid Sunlight Leave as part of our health premiums? ? That'd be some real healthcare coverage! When looked at from a distance, seems obvious. But really it's like we've drunk the Koolaid and now are trying to make sense of madness within an unacknowledging society.

I flew back to BC from Montreal a few weeks ago in the most insane nnEMF/Wifi soup - must have been hundreds on their laptops and devices in that metal tube, for hours. Felt terrible. Digressing... but anyways, my pre-dawn wakeups/watching dawn break outdoors are going well, and yeah they helped sleep in general. Seafood here is delicious and easy to get, barefoot walking here on the farm and swimming in the ocean (with or without wetsuit) is happening, but not enough probably. Just. Can't. Face. Boring. Long. Cold. Baths. But really my takeaway is that I need to leave the 48° parallel, at least for Nov-March, and I am still figuring out how to do that. Still or likely always will have more viewing/learning to do - those May and Nov -15 webinars. Will try and get on it soonish.

# Thread: If you have cancer patients pay attention to their ANS.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-have-cancer-patients-pay-attention-to-their-ans.17172/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-30T04:42:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-have-cancer-patients-pay-attention-to-their-ans.17172/post-182856>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25001652](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25001652)

The vagus nerve connects the digital and analogue systems together in the brain. It also is the protector of the blood brain barrier, the gut barrier and the pneumo-environmental barrier in humans. The vagus nerve connects the entire gut down to the transverse meso-colon of the hind gut to the fourth ventricle of the brainstem. This ventricle is filled with CSF and the vagus nerve is covered with myelin in connection with this CSF. This is how the “central digital” circadian system of the SCN and the “analogue circadian system” of the gut are linked to light and the timing of food growth in the environment, so that the signals can be yoked via the area postrema and the median eminence in the brain. These are two areas in the brain do not have a blood brain barrier so the electron density in the CSF is accurately tied to the local environment. The area postrema and median eminence are called the circumventricular organs. Both of them are bathed in CSF that surrounds the brain. The digital circadian signals project visible light photons from the retina to the median eminence to affect hormone release in the pituitary. The analogue system from the gut’s photoelectric code for food projects directly to the area postrema. This unifies how the analogue and digital circadian signals in the brain work in unison to tell an organism what their energy balance is at anytime of the year by counting electron density in the CSF. With in CSF layers there also is a stratification of water by density as well. This is another example of fractal design of how water stratification occurs in the oceans to create oxygen. This is also how the central clock and peripheral clocks yoke the photoelectric effect from the different sources in our environment. It should be intuitive now why environmental mismatches in the brain are perceived as a mixed message by our leptin receptor in the hypothalamus now. Your leptin receptor’s job is to count the electron density in CSF that surrounds it to send this information to different areas of the body. When we have any mismatch in the photoelectric signaling in the hypothalamus, the result in a cell with the leptin receptor is molecular crowding. Molecular crowding is a synonym for inflammation. Inflammation is a synonym for leptin resistance. Fat provides more electrons than protein, but protein is more thermogenic and it is also energy efficient because it lowers the cost of protein ubiquination when autophagy is broken or inefficient.

# Thread: The dynamics of the atmosphere
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-dynamics-of-the-atmosphere.17173/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-30T05:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-dynamics-of-the-atmosphere.17173/post-182861>

Earth’s atmosphere is a mixture of gases that surrounds our home planet. Besides providing us with something to breathe, the atmosphere helps make life on Earth possible in several ways. It shields us from most of the harmful ultraviolet (UVC) radiation coming from the Sun, warms the surface of our planet by about 33° C (59° F) via the greenhouse effect, and largely prevents extreme differences between daytime and nighttime temperatures.

The atmosphere grows thinner (less dense and lower in pressure) as one moves upward from Earth’s surface. It gradually gives way to the vacuum of outer space. There is no precise “top” of the atmosphere. Air becomes so thin at altitudes between 100 and 120 km (62-75 miles) up that for many purposes that range of heights can be considered the boundary between the atmosphere and space. However, there are very thin but measurable traces of atmospheric gases hundreds of kilometers (miles) above Earth’s surface.

There are several different regions or layers in the atmosphere. Each has characteristic temperatures, pressures, and phenomena. We live in the tropo- sphere, the lowest layer. Most of the mass (about 75-80%) of the atmosphere is in the troposphere. Clouds, too, are found in the troposphere, and almost all weather occurs within this layer. It extends upward to about 10 km (6.2 miles or about 33,000 feet) above sea level.

Some jet aircraft fly in the next higher layer, the stratosphere, which contains the jet stream and the ozone layer. A rare type of electrical discharge, some what akin to lightning, occurs in the stratosphere. These blue jets appear above thunderstorms, and extend from the bottom of the stratosphere up to altitudes of 40 or 50 km (25 to 31 miles).

Higher still is the mesosphere. This layer is directly above the stratosphere. It extends from about 50 to 85 km (31 to 53 miles) above our planet. Odd electrical discharges akin to lightning called sprites and elves, occasionally appear in the mesosphere dozens of kilometers (miles) above thunderclouds in the troposphere below. The stratosphere and mesosphere together are sometimes referred to as the middle atmosphere.

The thermosphere is directly above the mesosphere. It extends from about 90 km (56 miles) to between 500 and 1,000 km (311 to 621 miles) above our planet. Temperatures climb sharply in the lower thermosphere (below 200 to 300 km altitude or 124 to 186 miles), then level off and hold fairly steady with increasing altitude above that height.

**Solar activity strongly influences temperature in the thermosphere.** It is typically about 200° C (360° F) hotter in the daytime than at night, and roughly 500° C (900° F) hotter when the Sun is very active than at other times. Temperatures in the upper thermosphere can range from about 500° C (932° F) to 2,000° C (3,632° F) or higher. This is especially true during a period called the solar maximum, the period of greatest solar activity in its approximately 11-year solar cycle.

Although the thermosphere is considered part of Earth’s atmosphere, the air density is so low in this layer that most of the thermosphere is what we normally think of as outer space. In fact, the most common definition says that space begins at an altitude of 100 km (62 miles). The space shuttle and the International Space Station both orbit Earth within the thermosphere.

High-energy solar photons also tear electrons away from gas particles in the thermosphere, creating electrically charged ions of atoms and molecules. Earth’s ionosphere, composed of several regions of such ionized particles in the atmosphere, overlaps with and shares the same space with the electrically neutral thermosphere. Moving ions, dragged along by collisions with the electrically neutral gases, produce powerful electrical currents in some parts of the thermosphere.

Finally, the aurora (the southern and northern lights) primarily occur in the thermosphere. Charged particles (electrons, protons, and other ions) from space collide with atoms and molecules at high latitudes, exciting them into higher energy states. Those atoms and molecules shed this excess energy by emitting photons of light, which we see as colorful auroral displays.

Earth’s atmosphere gradually fades into the vacuum of space. The outer most limit of the atmosphere’s uppermost edge occurs in the region around 190,000 km (120,000 miles), about halfway to the Moon. There, in the vastness, is space.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-30T05:18:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-dynamics-of-the-atmosphere.17173/post-182862>

The depth to which particles penetrate the atmosphere depends on their energy and momentum; the more energetic there are, the deeper they travel.

Cosmic rays can reach the troposphere or even the surface of the Earth; solar energetic particles and radiation belt particles can reach the strato- sphere and mesosphere; and auroral particles reach the lower thermosphere, usually to around 62 miles or 100 kilometers. Auroral particles entering the atmosphere are far more numerous than the others, which is why auroral displays are seen.

Its believed that many nocturnal programs in biology can be affected by these energy systems.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-30T05:33:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-dynamics-of-the-atmosphere.17173/post-182864>

**The Ionosphere**\---- Earth’s atmosphere contains a series of regions that Earth’s have a relatively large number of electrically charged atoms and molecules. As a group, these regions are collectively called the ionosphere and they extend from about 56 to more than 1000 km above Earth’s surface (35 to 620 miles) from the middle of the mesosphere through the entire thermosphere and further.

Interestingly, the existence of an electrically conducting region of the atmosphere was not confirmed until the first decade of the 20th Century. Its discovery ushered in the long-distance transmission of radio waves by the ionosphere’s reflective properties, which acts like a mirror in the sky making the transmission of radio waves possible.

The ionosphere is made up of three horizontal regions commonly referred
to as regions D, E, and F. Region D extends from approximately 40 to 60 miles or 64 to 96 km; Region E extends from 60 to 80 miles or 96 to 129 km; and Region F extends from about 100 to 1,000 miles or 160 to 1610 km or more above Earth’s surface. The ionosphere reaches its maximum density of charged particles in this last region, Region F, from 125 to 375 miles or 201 to 603 km altitude. It then decreases in density with height.

It is in constant flux over time and place in both its charged particle density and its structure. It can be affected by man made nnEMF transmissions. It also can create interference patterns for other forms of electromagnetic waves. Its variability is due to the variability of the Sun. The variability may also have to do with manufactured technologies.

Not only does the Sun’s energy change throughout a 24-hour period, the Sun’s x-ray and ultraviolet emissions also change and impact the ionosphere due to the Sun’s highly variable 11-year sunspot cycle, changes in the Sun’s rotation, solar flares, and more. The ionosphere not only varies greatly with time, but also by geographical location (polar, auroral zones, mid-latitudes, and equatorial regions), and with certain solar-related ionospheric disturbances.

**Interestingly, Region F is also an exporter of ions and electrons.** Ions are neutral atoms that have become electrically charged by the addition or loss of one or more electrons. The more electrons that are lost the less light waves can propagate. Electrons are subatomic particles within atoms that carry a negative charge. An atom that gains an electron, therefore, is negative, while an atom that loses an electron is positive. Protons are the corresponding positively charged particles within all atoms.

As the Sun’s energy reaches the ionosphere in the day, **_the ionosphere expands and carries ions upward into an area surrounding our planet called the magnetosphere._** As the Sun sets and night arrives, the ionosphere cools and contracts, which draws lost ions and electrons back to the ionosphere until the Sun’s return each dawn. The key question remains what happens to the ionosphere over densely populated areas where non native EMF is present. Does this affect the ionosphere above this city and alter the quantum yield of sunlight? Here, at the poles, the atmosphere crackles with electric current, and dances with curtains of light over the poles due to Earthward solar storms’ solar plasma.

_The presence of these charged particles makes the upper atmosphere an electrical conductor, which supports electric currents and affects radio waves_. This ionization depends primarily on the Sun and its activity. Ionospheric structures and peak densities in the ionosphere vary greatly with time as already noted.

The major part of the ionization is produced **by solar x-ray and ultraviolet radiation,** and by radiation from the solar wind of the Sun and space rays. The most noticeable effect is seen as the Earth rotates with respect to the Sun; **_ionization increases in the sunlit atmosphere and decreases on the shadowed side._** Although the Sun is the largest contributor toward the ionization, cosmic rays make a small contribution, as do auroras caused by solar storms. There are some that believe man made technologies also can alters the electrical balance in our ionosphere too. Any atmospheric disturbance affects the distribution of the ionization globally. This can have a huge effect on the sun light penetration to the surface tectonic plates.

The ionosphere is a dynamic ever changing system controlled by many things including acoustic motions of the atmosphere, electromagnetic emissions, and variations in the geomagnetic field. Because of its extreme sensitivity to atmospheric changes, the ionosphere is a very sensitive monitor of atmospheric events. The most accurate way of measuring the ionosphere is with a ground-based ionosonde, which records data as ionograms.

### Da-mo — 2015-11-30T12:49:46+0200

Here, at the poles, the atmosphere crackles with electric current, and dances with curtains of light over the poles due to Earthward solar storms’ solar plasma. . . . . .
>

DARPA and HAARP?

> **United States to close HAARP military facility after final DARPA project**
>
> . . . . The US government claims that the site is used to study natural processes in the ionosphere — a region of the upper atmosphere — in the hope of building better radio communications technology. It also looks to develop technology using those processes, it said.
>
> **The site has been used to create artificial northern lights (aurora borealis).**
>
> HAARP will shut down after a final research project in mid-June. That will be conducted by the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) . . . . . .

[http://www.belfasttelegraph.co.uk/n...ility-after-final-darpa-project-30306963.html](http://www.belfasttelegraph.co.uk/news/world-news/united-states-to-close-haarp-military-facility-after-final-darpa-project-30306963.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-01T03:33:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gold-club-access.17175/post-182932>

Tomorrow you get to sample a new particle at the buffet........neutrino's. Neutrinos are everywhere and very few people have a clue what makes them special. They permeate the very space all around us. They can be found throughout our galaxy, in our sun and every second tens of thousands of neutrinos are passing through your body. But there is no need to become alarmed for these tiny particles barely interact with anything unless they ate directed to do so. In fact, they can even pass through the entire Earth without being affected. Only about 100,000 per day hit the Earth.
Neutrinos are fundamental particles that were first formed in the first second of the early universe, before even atoms could form. Physicists believe they are also continually being produced in the nuclear reactions of stars, like our sun, and nuclear reactions here on earth. This of course assumes you believe what modern physics believes about stars. Might they be created in other places? Could they be used in life? Much is still unknown about these particles, they have an undetermined mass and travel at near the speed of light. They have one interesting characteristic that they share with light.........they carry energy and momentum. This means they have the capability to do something unusual...........find out what that is tomorrow when the December 2015 webinar goes live.

I have been taking three minutes cold showers in the morning's and evenings. I seem to be developing very mild Raynaud's syndrome and not sure if it's related or not. Many times after a cold shower, a couple of my fingers are totally white and feel ice cold and the other fingers are red. Not sure if that's something I should be concerned about or not.

Also, my skin in general is quite a bit drier and wrinkled. Again, I'm not sure if this is some negative effect from the cold showers or something different that's going on with me altogether.

Thanks so much.

# Thread: When to sungaze how it works. Questions

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
