# Thread: Tara's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tara_Tuck — 2015-12-02T06:53:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/post-183025>

Hi there! I'm Tara. I'm a 27 year old female living in Wisconsin. I’ve been waiting to write here until I could get everything organized, list all of my symptoms, labs, supplements, environment, diet, lifestyle and things that I’ve tried so that a female my age with the same symptoms would stumble upon this journal and it potentially could help her, but I’m running out of time. Today is the day! My 2015 theme was the “Year of No Fear”. It’s December 1st and I’ve got to keep going.

SO, I was diagnosed with Hashimoto's in January 2015. That was quite the journey getting that diagnosis. Every time I googled my symptoms, I either had menopause or a thyroid problem, but per the doctors, my TSH wasn’t “out of range enough”. OK, so we’ve all seen this before, right? So I kept digging, and discovered a Chiropractor with a functional medicine background and she diagnosed me with Hashi’s. The best advice she gave me was “Don’t underestimate Hashimoto’s”.

After the Hashi’s diagnosis, I met with an endocrinologist. The appointment didn’t go well. I felt unheard by the endo. Being too brain-fogged, unable to focus, and exhausted most days to read about Hashimoto’s and hormones, I turned to an ND with experience in treating patients for hormone problems.

In October 2015 I did full hormone testing at two points in my cycle. We determined my hormones are low, particularly progesterone, estrogen and thyroid. Per the ND, progesterone was at 1/3 of where it should be and estrogen at 1/5. I was relieved! No wonder I could relate to menopausal women! I also tested poor detox and pre-diabetic.

He offered two treatment options for the low hormones: (1) BHRT for 3-6 months and then my body would adapt back to normal, or (2) use supplements and herbs to adapt. I opted for BHRT. **Turns out it’s the Wiley Protocol. I’m concerned! Why am I concerned?**

1\. I’m in my child-bearing years and have yet to have kids. It's a risky time. I also recall reading the words “childbearing years” and “concern” in Jack’s book/somewhere. That has not left my mind.

2\. I cannot find information online about any women similar to me that have completed the protocol. It seems most women that have been on the Wiley Protocol are menopausal or have had a hysterectomy and are older than me. They were on the protocol greater than 6 months. I’m not menopausal, haven’t had a hysterectomy, and the plan is for me to be on the protocol for 3-6 months.Is there anyone out there like me that completed the protocol and had success? Failure?

3\. It’s controversial amongst doctors and patients. Examples**:**

-   **Rhythmic Living** – Women’s stories with the adverse effects of the high doses of hormones [http://rhythmicliving.org/?page\_id=3](http://rhythmicliving.org/?page_id=3)

-   **The Wiley Protocol: an analysis of ethical issues as of October 2008:** [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/18551081/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/18551081/)

-   **Discussion between Dr.** Kara Fitzgerald and Dr. Sara Gottfried as of November 2015:

**_Dr. Gottfried:_** Sometimes, these small tweaks can really swing big doors. And then I like to move on to herbal therapies. And if those aren’t resolving symptoms, I’ll do some more testing and then we’ll do some bioidentical hormone therapy. But I agree with you, in conventional medicine, their approach has always been one dose for everyone and it’s a pretty whopping dose. And I find that the human body, especially the female body does a lot better with the gentle coaxing and nudging, especially of herbs and the more tonifying approach rather than a sledgehammer of big dose of the Wiley protocol for estrogen and progesterone for instance.
**_Dr. Fitzgerald:_** Yeah, absolutely. Absolutely. I’ve inherited former Wiley people into my practice and that’s a whole other podcast, but when you’ve been mega-dosing…

**_Dr. Gottfried:_** _The Wiley protocol refugees, we will have a separate conversation._

**_Dr. Fitzgerald:_** _Right. I mean there are some varying important cleanup work with such hormone overload and just definitely detoxification and attention._ ​

[http://www.drkarafitzgerald.com/201...ns-hormone-management-with-sara-gottfried-md/](http://www.drkarafitzgerald.com/2015/11/16/episode-7-from-cell-to-soul-a-functional-approach-to-womens-hormone-management-with-sara-gottfried-md/)​

-   **Letter to Suzan Sommers**

[http://drerika.typepad.com/notepad/2006/10/letter\_to\_suzan.html](http://drerika.typepad.com/notepad/2006/10/letter_to_suzan.html)

Day 1 of my cycle starts sometime this week, and that is when I would start the Wiley Protocol. I was hoping to gain some vitality and joy as a result of the BHRT this month. I am afraid of messing up my hormones more without precaution, but part of me wants to just jump in and do it. I want my brain back so I can start taking classes this January for pre-health. Time is running out to live my life. Longevity would be awesome, but how about quality? Would love a quality day where I felt like myself again. I know this will happen. At one point this year, I tried progesterone cream for a few months, despite my estrogen being low. The first two days were AMAZING! I felt so focused and full of life! Even though that wasn’t the solution, I am so grateful I had that experience to give me hope.​

It’s past my bedtime. Next Actions: Go to bed, compile all of my BHRT questions and contact my ND tomorrow, practice patience, and keep on inquiring!

### caroline — 2015-12-02T10:37:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/post-183030>

Welcome to journal land Tara.....

### fitness@home — 2015-12-02T13:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/post-183038>

Welcome Tara!

@[Shijin13](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/969/), can you give some feedback on the Wiley protocol? If I recall correctly, you have experience with it.

### Shijin13 — 2015-12-03T01:19:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/post-183102>

Hi Tara

I looked at the Wiley protocol, and ended up going with Dr Dzugan, to manage my migraines. I've been on compounded Progesterone with success for almost 4 years now, its taken a while to get there. I had kids before starting progesterone hormone therapy. Initially I started on Tri-Est - but it wasn't working for me..

Ask questions, and make sure you're questions are heard. Also, considering finding a BHRT doc locally.

I like Dr Gotfried. however If you have read Dr Uzzi Ressi's book its worth a read as well. he treats women of all ages with BHRT. its a good place to start. [http://amzn.to/1NpMcnz](http://amzn.to/1NpMcnz)

I ended up partnering with my PCP and Dr Dzugan to manage my migraines. I take progesterone across my entire cycle. w/o issues. But I do not take any estrogens, or Testosterone, thanks to be PCOS...

sometimes herbals supplements can help, but as Jack says, you can't out supplement a bad environment and you can't get better in the environment you got sick in. So focus on fixing your environment. Some of us need compounded hormones regardless where we're at in our lifecycles. you want to leverage the BHRT To help you achieve optimal, while you fix the environment that got you here.

I'm not sure this helps... but it at least gives you a place to start.

### fitness@home — 2015-12-03T02:28:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/post-183110>

Thanks Gretchen. My memory failed me...it was Dr. Dzugan you were working with!

### JMO — 2015-12-03T02:29:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/post-183111>

Hi Tara,
Welcome! I've had a hysterectomy over 10 years ago due to endometriosis. I went on Wiley Protocol Dec 2013. My doc tested my hormones every 3 months and adjusted my doses. At first she was doubling my doses because my hormones were so low but all of a sudden they went sky high and it took a few months to get them back down. At first, they seemed to help but then I questioned whether they were really helping and decided to go off them and try DHEA and progrestrone. I was on them for about 18 months. My doc said DHEA/progestrone was the protocol she used previously and didn't have a problem with me trying it. I wish I would have tried that to treat my endo but didn't know about it at the time. I actually have not even been taking my hormones lately because the UV lights have made a tremendous difference in my energy and having hormones work. I know my situation is different than yours but wanted to tell you of my experience.

### JanSz — 2015-12-03T02:56:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/post-183118>

27 year old female living in Wisconsin
diagnosed with Hashimoto's in January 2015
in my child-bearing years and have yet to have kids
brain-fogged, unable to focus, and exhausted most days
at two points in my cycle. We determined my hormones are low, particularly progesterone, estrogen and thyroid
tested poor detox and pre-diabetic

sledgehammer of big dose of the Wiley protocol for estrogen and progesterone for instance
\===================================================================
Hi Tara,

Wisconsin 44.5000° N
That is way up North, insufficient sunlight year round.
Consider emphasis on Circadian Rhythm
Supplement with full spectrum light, such as Exo-Terra Solar Glo 160W, few of them.
To monitor your Circadian Rhythm do blood test:
pregnenolone
cortisol AM/PM (7;30AM/noon)(preferably close to the upper range)
DHEAs, aim at Women 275-400 μg/dL
May need to supplement with pregnenolone and DHEA
in heavy case for a time may need hydrocortisone or Medrol if flat cortisol
\--------------
Follow dr Kruse diet
but
check
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
you want at the minimum
AA and DHA in mid range on that test
preferably all individual results should be in mid range
Adjust dieet after you got test results.

pre-diabetic
no high glycemic index carbohydrates.
[http://modernsurvivalblog.com/health/high-orac-value-antioxidant-foods-top-100/](http://modernsurvivalblog.com/health/high-orac-value-antioxidant-foods-top-100/)

cloves
cinnamon
black pepper
turmeric
oregano
cocoa
cumin
parsley

\--------------
Willey protocol or dr Dzugan or your local doctor should be just fine as long as the idea is to aim at desired hormone blood levels.
Preferably measure blood hormone levels (not saliva).
Preferably for estradiol use LabCorp
Estradiol, Sensitive Test Number: 140244 CPT Code: 82670
Aim at average levels for given day on cycle as shown on chart post #4:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)

Major brain fog or headdache you should get adjusting progesterone.
\---------------
Get this test (cash)
[http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com/](http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com/)
Micronutrient Test
Use suggestion from test report to adjust defficiencies.
\---------------
1/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day (start with selenium for 2 weeks, then real slow with Lugol's to see how you will react. When all is well, increase dose till you get full dropper daily)
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00)

4/day Biotic Research Bio-D-Mulsion Forte, 1drop=2000iu Vit D
\---------------
After you addressed all above
or
at least
you have cortisol-AM in mid range and decent DHEAs
start working on
FreeT3
you want to have FreeT3=(3.5-4.2) pg/mL
if supplementation need, Natural Thyroid Hormone is usually better than synthetic
\---------------
Good idea to check
IGF-1
IGFBP-3
\----------------

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-06T06:27:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taras-journal.17185/post-185544>

Two things I carry with me daily: Death is certain, but life is not.
You pay the price of both, with your choices. choices = dopamine and dopamine controls the pituitary. It has an assistant who likes darkness, its name is prolactin. The pituitary is the compound pharmacy within.........very few understand how to unlock its fruits.........because the photoelectric effect is that key. Doubt you'll find that in any of their books........FYI

The surge of prolactin is normally quite large in normal darkness but is significantly diminished in artificially lit environments after sunset. This was shown in the [CT 2 video](http://www.ted.com/talks/jessa_gamble_how_to_sleep.html). This has big implications for modern humans.

This may begin to make you why peri menopausal or PM women gain weight no matter what they do with their diet or exercise programs who who's book on hormones they buy. This is where paleo diet fails many people. Their Rx is to exercise more at the crossfit and consider carbing up diet wise to support T3, and this only worsens the plight. They never tell you how light build dopamine sans food or exercise. Even worse they never mention how cold can do it too. This is where I begin on my blog. I focus on those of us who are broken and not working well because our environmental choices have destroyed these cycles. It is all driven by a massive change in their light environment that controls dopamine and prolactin which controls their hormones. Once they lose their period, or in men, when their androgens and vitamin D levels fall apart for many reasons. This is often why older women sleep badly and gain weight they can not seem to lose in the gym even with a good dietary template and good exercise habits. This is another reason I am a big advocate for bio-identical hormone optimization in women and men when it is required when their light environment is destroyed by singlet free radicals. This need is greatest in women who are warm adapted (not cooled), because they have the worse PG/E2 ratios due to too many protons. The need is lower in the cold adapted females because their leptin levels are already low due to the cold protocols, and this implies that their hormone panels are not too far off the optimal path. Everyone’s mileage varies because our Ferrari engines are all different and at different points in our life cycle because of the light choices we make.

_Making dopamine begins with the interaction of UV light and tyrosine for humans._ As we age, we lose dopamine because the lens of the human eye does not allow UV free passage to all light frequencies as it did in youth. This can happen due to surgical changes to the lens or due to optical changes due to lensed on the eyes or contacts. When this occurs, less dopamine is made. This closes down the compound pharmacy from the sun control............. A lack of UV light exposure via the eye causes severe cyclic changes in these hormones (leptin, dopamine, prolactin, and ghrelin) simultaneously. This all leads to destroyed hormone panels in very novel ways.............photoelectrically. Light sculpts your life even when you cannot fathom her cosmic wand.........soon enough you'll get her deft touch with a kiss from some blogs.

### PaulG — 2015-12-02T12:41:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2015-webinar-pions-neutrinos-and-the-creation-of-matter-from-light.17186/post-183033>

This is a pretty amazing webinar - highly recommend everyone watch it - need to brush up on the Photoelectric effect and probably high school chemistry...

This may be completely unrelated but I do believe in [synchronicity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Synchronicity).

This morning as I sat shirt-off drinking from a green plastic bottle of Santa Vitoria Sparkling Italian mineral water I thought what would happen if I held the half-full bottle up to my eye and angled it toward the AM sun. Well, what a revelation! Looking inside this new world I noticed that the water was shimmering and vibrating, initially I thought my hand was shaky but no it was steady and water drops were popping, fizzing and jumping in chaotic fashion. Next, I observed that water bubbles were flying from the surface of the water and adhering to the inside of the bottle closest to the light.

This of course can be explained by [effervescence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Effervescence) and the fact that the carbon dioxide gas bubbles are lighter than surrounding water but I think this is also a demonstration of charge separation as explained by Pollack in his book "The Fourth Phase of Water". Now, having watched the Dec 2015 webinar I am thinking this is also tied into Jack's explanation of how nature uses light to transform matter from one energy level to another. The water contains bicarbonate, calcium, magnesium, chloride, sodium and potassium all in the top 19 of elements by atomic weight apart from calcium.. The plastic bottle which may be made of [BPA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bisphenol_A) or some similar polymer has a molar mass of 228.29 g/mol and perhaps due to its complexity collapses any quantum coherence.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-13T15:05:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2015-webinar-pions-neutrinos-and-the-creation-of-matter-from-light.17186/post-183998>

**Missing Neutrinos:**
**Sudbury Neutrino Observatory Looks for the solar neutrino's**
**CURRENT BELIEFS BY MODERN SCIENTISTS:** A thermonuclear reaction of the type assumed to be powering the Sun must emit a flood of electron-neutrinos. Nowhere near the requisite number of these neutrinos have been found after thirty years of searching for them. A series of grandly expensive experiments have failed to find the necessary neutrino flux.

Some solar neutrinos have indeed been observed - but **only one-third the number required** if the fusion reaction really is the main source of the Sun's energy production. These negative results from the neutrino experiments have resulted not in any re-examination of solar models. This is problematic for me and for biology when you carefully listen to the December 2015 webinar.
If you cannot account for all the particles it means we do not know where they come from or how they are used. It also may be the main reason we have not been able to see a magnetic monopole in nature.

A rather, an intense theoretical effort to discover new properties that solar neutrinos 'must have' has occurred. As a result of this effort, it was announced (June 2001) by the Sudbury Neutrino Observatory (SNO) in Canada that neutrinos _have mass_ and can change 'flavor'. This supposedly accounts for why they have not been fully observed previously. However, several important questions remain to be answered about the methodology that was used by the SNO researchers in arriving at their conclusions.

Of course, whether neutrinos actually do change type or not has no bearing whatever on the validity of the Electric Sun model. The neutrino problem is a hurdle only for the standard fusion model. In the Electric Sun model there is no energy produced in the core - **_radiant energy is released at the surface by electric arc discharge._** This implies that the same type of discharges might be in operation in the mitochondrial membranes. They contain 30 million volts of charge within them because at their surface they also act like a capacitor by harnessing electrons from foods and spitting out protons from its core to create an environment that would favor electrical discharge at its surface. It also would favor light liberation in this process. When you consider an electric model for the sun there is no 'missing neutrino' problem for the electric Sun model. The electron-nuetrinos that are observed are probably produced by fusion taking place at the solar surface that produces heavy elements (other than hydrogen and helium). Might the same thing be happening in the cytoplasm of cell at the mitochondrial surface? To control weakly interacting neutrino's we would need something extraordinary. We know surface defects on topologic insulators are capable of magnetic monopole ability. This is the most plausible and ideal way to control neutrino's to allow them to act like a surgeons' scalpel on atoms in our cell to carve out the elements they need to make atomic substrates to drive the biochemical pathways we see in a cell.

For decades the measured deficiency of electron-neutrinos has been a continuing embarrassment for those who want to believe that the accepted H-He fusion model of how the Sun produces its energy is correct. This failure to observe the predicted neutrino flux in the sun clearly constitutes falsification of the solar fusion model. There has been a great effort to explain away the observed deficit. The issue for us in biology is that when and if the sun is found to be electric sun then we will have proof that neutrino's emant from massive electric arc discharges with light at the surface.

It will be at this time that biology will have to be reformulated

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-13T15:07:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2015-webinar-pions-neutrinos-and-the-creation-of-matter-from-light.17186/post-183999>

[http://www.sno.phy.queensu.ca/sno/first\_results/page00.html](http://www.sno.phy.queensu.ca/sno/first_results/page00.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-13T15:09:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2015-webinar-pions-neutrinos-and-the-creation-of-matter-from-light.17186/post-184000>

They believe the loss of neutrino's is explained by a "change in flavor" of neutrino's.

There are thought to be three flavors of neutrino: electron-neutrinos, muon-neutrinos, and tau-neutrinos. Some of these flavors were not measurable by the previous experiments that were looking for them. SNO researchers claim, on the basis of their experiment, that the measurable neutrinos turn into previously non-measurable ones enroute from the Sun's core. That 'oscillation', they say, explains the previously measured shortage.

This idea has never sat too well with me.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-13T15:15:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2015-webinar-pions-neutrinos-and-the-creation-of-matter-from-light.17186/post-184001>

The SNO observations were only made here on Earth and not close to the sun nor in or around the Earth. No satellite observations were made anywhere along the path, certainly not at its beginning where the neutrinos start their journey (inside the Sun).

QUESTION TO PONDER GIVEN THE ABOVE: Consider a freight train that goes from New York to Chicago. We live in Chicago and are only able to observe the train as it arrives in Chicago. It arrives with 4 freight cars, 2 tank cars, and 1 flat car. How is it possible, no matter how sophisticated our method of observation, for us to make any conclusions about whether freight cars, tank cars, or flat cars have been added to or subtracted from the train at, say, Cleveland? Moreover, how is it possible to say that freight cars have mysteriously turned into tank cars or flat cars along the route somewhere? Moreover, do it with “99% confidence” that was the number give in their press releases trying to explain away the missing neutrino's?
The answer must be that they are assuming they know the value of the neutrino flux leaving the Sun. If so, this is an exercise in circular reasoning. If they know what the solar neutrino flux leaving the Sun is, there is no need for the experiment. The experiment adds nothing in the way of verification of the assumption. It certainly does not explain the low value of neutrino flux observed here on Earth, it only confirms it. This is circular logic.

What happens if the neutrino's were controlled by a force not known in the sun like a magnetic monopoles on the surface that use the neutrino's from the core. harness them to cut atoms present in the sun's core to make the elements we see in the sun spectrum?

These are the basis of the Fraunhofer lines critical in making the sun's light. Fraunhofer lines, in astronomical spectroscopy, any of the dark (absorption) lines in the spectrum of the Sun, caused by selective absorption of the Sun's or star's radiation at specific wavelengths by the various elements existing as gases in its atmosphere. Anything that alters sunlight generation becomes critical for biology on Earth. The basis of all food webs and biology is tied to the creation of the sun's light.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-13T15:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2015-webinar-pions-neutrinos-and-the-creation-of-matter-from-light.17186/post-184002>

The final issue to consider from the report, in the conclusion of the Sudbury:
"Comparison of the (neutrino) flux deduced from the ES reaction assuming no neutrino oscillations, to that measured by the CC reaction can provide clear evidence of flavor transformation without reference to solar model flux calculations. If neutrinos from the Sun change into other active flavors, then CC flux < ES flux."

This is clearly a simple math problem.............let us examine really what this means here.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-13T15:24:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2015-webinar-pions-neutrinos-and-the-creation-of-matter-from-light.17186/post-184004>

MATH PROBLEM TIME:

Let:
(a) = Neutrinos from the Sun change into other active flavors.
(b) = Electron-neutrino flux measurement is less than the measurement that includes electron-neutrinos and some of the other two types as well.
The sentence says: IF (a) is true, THEN (b) is true. No one can disagree with that.

But they are implying: IF (b) is true, THEN (a) is true. If the measurement of the flux of electron-type neutrinos is less than the more inclusive measurement that includes some of the other types, then neutrinos from the Sun change flavor on their way to Earth.

That is a logical non-sequitor. If the Sun is emitting all three types of neutrinos, e+u+t, then any Earthbound experiment that measures only e will always have a lower output than one that measures (for example) e + 0.1u + 0.3t. Moreover, the report states that the CC measured value (e type only) is "significantly smaller than the measurements by \[S. Fukuda in an earlier experiment\]". So the electron neutrino flux just measured by SNO is _**even lower than previously reported levels**_. And it is possible that muon-neutrinos oscillate into electron-neutrinos. And that presents a further complication to the SNO conclusions because of the already extremely low value of measured electron-neutrino flux.

This sounds like the neutrino's might be being consumed at the surface of the sun in atomic creation. This is my insight here.

There have been other neutrino experiments that have resulted in unclear answers about whether neutrinos 'oscillate' into different types. The final report of the Liquid Scintillator Neutrino Detector (LSND) experiment in 2001 said their results strengthened previously published, but controversial LSND results that provided evidence of neutrino oscillation and mass. The LSND data, collected from 1993 to 1998, _suggested_ that muon anti-neutrinos oscillate into electron anti-neutrinos. However the MiniBooNE project results of 2007 reported _**no**_ mu-neutrino to electron-neutrino oscillations of the sort that would explain the LSND result. MiniBooNE was designed specifically to look for this, and has successfully ruled it out at 98% confidence level. So it is now exceedingly doubtful that the long sought excuse for the solar neutrino flux deficit has been found.

This is why neutrino's for me are the final piece of the puzzle.................I believe we need to ask better question in our physics experiments to really nail down biology's main problems...............

The problem in science today is we all stand on the shoulders of the past generation, This becomes problematic when the generation has been wrong about many things. To live an optimal life we must first lose the fear of being wrong. The NIH hates to be wrong, and therefore how they fund research makes sure they never appear incorrect. Our problems today are built around their myopic focus. Doing the wrong thing daily in science, compounds over time to destroy wellness.

# Thread: 2 probably very simple minded questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eilee — 2015-12-02T19:04:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183068>

I am new to all of this and haven't yet had the time to do a lot of reading. Plan on becomigna member and listening to some Webinars over the holidays.

I haven't yet gotten new lenses, but wondering if, in the meantime, I should be wearing my blue blockers (amber from lowbluelights.com) during the day when I'm inside?

Also, when I'm outside, are there certain times of day that I don't want to be looking directly at the sun?

Thanks!

### thomas — 2015-12-03T18:58:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183188>

\-Wear blueblockers in the evening when it's dark outside, and maybe late afternoon when the sun gets lower. During the day they usually aren't necessary (except if your in a place filled with led/fluo shitty light, in that case remove them or gtfo). Be outside it's even better.
\-You don't want to look directly at the sun if it the sun is too high (which is 90%+ of the day). When on the horizon and it's orange/red it's ok. (even pretty good).

### lilreddgirl — 2015-12-03T19:47:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183198>

> [thomas said:](/goto/post?id=183188)
>
> \-Wear blueblockers in the evening when it's dark outside, and maybe late afternoon when the sun gets lower. During the day they usually aren't necessary (except if your in a place filled with led/fluo shitty light, in that case remove them or gtfo). Be outside it's even better.
> \-You don't want to look directly at the sun if it the sun is too high (which is 90%+ of the day). When on the horizon and it's orange/red it's ok. (even pretty good).

But you do want to look close to it up to 10 am right? 15\* East? and blink a lot...

### Eilee — 2015-12-03T20:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183208>

During the day when i'm taking a walk, do i not even want to look up at the sky or should i look up, just not right at the sun?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T00:21:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183222>

You wear blue blockers during day only indoors when artificial light is present.

### Penny — 2015-12-04T00:48:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183229>

> [Eilee said:](/goto/post?id=183208)
>
> During the day when i'm taking a walk, do i not even want to look up at the sky or should i look up, just not right at the sun?

definitely not at the sun - you need 3 things: cool surface on your eyes (face dunk in ice water), DHA so the light will be absorbed, to be hydrated and around 400nm of light - but the amplitude of the wave will dictate its power - the amplitude is higher mid day and longer waves in the morning - so less \*toasty\* shall we say? Dr. K said to get in the morning sun and look 10/15 degrees in relation to the sun - and that probably means it's ok to look in the direction of the sun if you don't look right at it - in fact I believe the point is that all blue light is a signal that the sun has risen and if you constantly give yourself that signal, you age faster through ubiquitination - i.e. rapid aging and the diseases thereof...

### Eilee — 2015-12-04T02:01:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183241>

I'm a bit confused. You seem to be making a case for sunglasses if out in the sun during the day when there is lots of blue light??

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T02:12:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183243>

no sunglasses ever......just for pics or on your head or tucked in your shirt front

### Penny — 2015-12-04T19:54:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-probably-very-simple-minded-questions.17190/post-183292>

The blue light at sunrise is mitigated by the UV light and IR light that go with it - when you use an artificial light like an led, you get not only a higher amplitude of blue light than the sun ever actually even provides plus there is no UV or IR... I really don't get how I made a case for sunglasses when in fact, the only time you should block the light from your eyes is when it is artificially blue - like when I'm looking at the screen...
this is Melanie Procter's biohack on her eyes - it pretty well sums it up - do this:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/very-successful-hack.17164/#post-182810](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/very-successful-hack.17164/#post-182810)

# Thread: Transcendental Meditation and lifestyle modification increase telomerase, new study finds
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transcendental-meditation-and-lifestyle-modification-increase-telomerase-new-study-finds.17191/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2015-12-03T03:40:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transcendental-meditation-and-lifestyle-modification-increase-telomerase-new-study-finds.17191/post-183123>

"Increased telomerase associated with decreased hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and cellular aging

MAHARISHI UNIVERSITY OF MANAGEMENT

A new study published in [_PLOS ONE_](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article%3Fid=10.1371/journal.pone.0142689) found that the Transcendental Meditation technique and lifestyle changes both appear to stimulate genes that produce telomerase, an enzyme that's associated with reduced blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, and mortality.

Specifically, these approaches were found to activate two genes that code for telomerase, which adds molecules to the ends of chromosomes, or telomeres, protecting them from deteriorating.

May explain mechanism for known health benefits

"The finding that telomerase gene expression is increased, and that this is associated with a reduction in blood pressure in a high-risk population, suggests that this may be a mechanism by which stress reduction improves cardiovascular health," said Robert Schneider, MD, FACC, coauthor of the study.

Earlier research on the Transcendental Meditation technique found lower rates of high blood pressure, heart attack, stroke, and death, as well as slowing of biological aging. The new study examined what was happening at the level of DNA, showing that the Transcendental Meditation technique increases telomerase gene expression and suggesting that this may contribute to the cardiovascular and aging benefits.

Both groups show improvement after 16 weeks

For this pilot trial, the subjects included 48 men and women with high blood pressure who were recruited and studied at Howard University Medical Center. Half were assigned to a group that learned the Transcendental Meditation technique and received a basic health education course. The other half were assigned to a group that focused on achieving significant lifestyle modifications such as weight reduction, reducing salt intake, engaging in regular physical activity, and moderating alcohol. They also participated in support groups and group exercises.

After 16 weeks, both groups showed significant increases in telomerase gene expression and reductions in blood pressure. There was no significant difference between the changes in the two groups. The results also showed that the lifestyle modification group made a larger number of changes in their lifestyle behaviors.

"These findings are very encouraging for prevention," said Dr. Schneider, director of the Institute for Natural Medicine and Prevention at MUM. "They show that both the Transcendental Meditation technique and active lifestyle modification can contribute to heart health."

Coauthor Otelio Randall, MD at Howard University College of Medicine concluded, "This pilot study in African Americans suggests stress reduction and lifestyle modifications may reduce blood pressure with an increase in telomerase."

Encouraging findings for heart health

"The result is valuable new information, relevant both to cardiovascular disease and to the molecular mechanisms involved in Transcendental Meditation," said John Fagan, professor of molecular biology at Maharishi University of Management and senior author on the study.

###

This research was part of a larger clinical trial funded by the National Institutes of Health--National Heart Lung and Blood Institute and by the Howard University College of Medicine General Clinical Research Center.

Collaborating on the study were the Howard University College of Medicine, the Center for Natural Medicine and Prevention at Maharishi University of Management Research Institute, and the Department of Physiology and Health at Maharishi University of Management.

Duraimani S, Schneider RH, Randall OS, Nidich SI, Xu S, Ketete M, et al. (2015) Effects of Lifestyle Modification on Telomerase Gene Expression in Hypertensive Patients: A Pilot Trial of Stress Reduction and Health Education Programs in African Americans. _PLOS ONE_ 10(11): e0142689. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0142689"

[http://www.eurekalert.org/pub\_releases/2015-12/muom-tma120215.php](http://www.eurekalert.org/pub_releases/2015-12/muom-tma120215.php)

# Thread: CoolSwede's Thoughts, Ideas and Reflections...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 51
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CoolSwede — 2015-12-03T18:33:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-183185>

After being around here since I first heard about Dr Kruse (Oprah and Leptin blog posts) I have finally decided to start a journal...or rather a living document where I will try to write down current
thoughts, ideas and reflections. It this a record in lurking around? It could be!

Who am I? Well I'm a Swedish guy in my mid 30's who is trying to reach optimal. What are the goals? Many, I like improvments! One is to improve my hayfever, another is to improve my dopamine (I used to be
very active starting things but now I have a tendency towards procrastination, especially in the afternoons). Another is to get rid of my life long dandruff. And so on...and so forth...

One turning point in my life was the early discovery of LCHF and Ketosis, made popular by the now well known Dr Eenfeldt (then just a forum poster named "Doc" in one of the LCHF forums). This made me
realize that I was able to make changes in my life to lessen the risks of future illnesses. This was really the lightbulb moment for me. Ever since, I have been devoted to improve myself in many areas.
This also made me devoted to LCHF (until now).

It has been quite a journey following Dr Kruse from the beginning. At times I was annoyed by his ambiguity saying that "ketosis is king" one day and "food macros are unimportant" the other day. Food is the most important thing for health, how can he say such things?! Well...now I know better.

For me, the most disturbing part was that even though I was strictly LCHF, borderline ketotic, I gained weight periodically. I was under the impression that LCHF + IF (Thank you Berkhan) would make me
ripped, but that just didn't happen. Rather the contrary. My light environment then? Horrible. I was working in front of two computer screens (then set at 6500K) with 5500K fluorescent tubes above me, A big LED TV at home and lots of watching the smartphone. Blue light toxicity galore!

Then I struggled to find a doc to help me doing blood tests upon my request and that was not easy. At first I tried the governmently funded doctors and they were completely uninterested in preventative
measures. For instance, when I asked about testosterone, the response was "Unless you suffer from erectile dysfunction, forget it!". Luckily, I found one practician who has been really good and actually listens to my requests and most importantly, she is interested in hearing what I have learnt recently!

The introduction of CT was really intresting for me. Living in a country where cold is "readily available" during Winter months I thought that this might be the missing piece of the puzzle. Having no
access to a tub, I started to take cold showers every day, Wore less clothing outside and got used to the attention you get when you walk the streets in a t-shirt when everybody else wears a heavy coat,
scarf and a hat. Now, some years later, my cold tolerance is very good but I am not a scandinavian Wim Hof yet. I still get ice-cream headaches from the shower at times. DHA I know...

I have also been deeply into the supplementation camp ranging from vitamin D3 to methylation supplements coming from lab results and the 23andme analysis. I could write an essay here...and make my wallet weep when I look at what I have payed...

Enter Ubiquitination series and the importance of light:

I installed f.lux, bought a pair of blueblockers and turned off the lights above my desk at work. Then Summer came and I decided to be in the sun as much as possible while at the same time eating more carbs than over the last years. The result was astounding. The weight just fell off! I can imagine the flood of newborn mitos after many (LCHF) years of increasing senesence. Needless to say, next year I will try to be even more in the sun, while eating carbs in proportion to UV exposure.

That's all for now. Don't expect me to write much about my daily life, but I am really interested in discussing Kruse's ideas and potential biohacks.

Meanwhile I will go and unpack my new 20W 365 nm blacklight armature...

### fitness@home — 2015-12-03T20:32:50+0200

### CoolSwede — 2015-12-07T13:33:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-183610>

Since starting getting my blood drawn regularly, there is one lab test that has been standing out: The OxLDL.

In a reference range of 20-170 ug/l my results have been (rougly, don't have exact numbers at hand). 550 - 180 - 1400 - 2200(!).

Today I received the latest number: 1400. Down from 2200 but still ridiculously high.

I have lost a bit of weight and that could of course be a contributor but I will continue to research about possible causes.

### CoolSwede — 2015-12-15T12:59:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-184162>

It's always interesting to look back. Three years ago:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/benefits-of-carbs-in-summer.1964/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/benefits-of-carbs-in-summer.1964/)

Me:

> Being VLC for 3 years now, and using CT since March, I wonder what are the gains from adding carbs while reducing cold during Summer? Why not living in "Winter" indefinately? I am a bit reluctant to add carbs since I don't really miss them...

JK:

> Well yesterday was the longest light day of the yr......I ate 375 grams of carbs.....how is that for an answer?

This was 2012 and I was so puzzled as to why JK chose to eat carbs when he so many times wrote about the benefits of ketosis. Now, I know that the benefit of carbs in summer is to renovate the body by getting rid of bad mitochondria. The missing piece of the puzzle was UV light. Carbs + UV light = magic!

So, three years later, I will also let Summer solstice be a carb feast, but until then, I will leverage cold and ketosis (while letting some UVA photons hit my retina of course)!

### CoolSwede — 2015-12-18T10:53:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-184426>

I have a tendency towards dandruff and mild seborrhea in my face. This Summer it was completely gone, but for some odd reason it has started to come back again (maybe bit of irony here ).

So yesterday I made the decision to visit a tanning salon for the first time in my life. They had no eyewear left (unmanned salon) so I ended up standing next to the tanning bed wide open with my arm in front of my eyes. There is a first time for everything, haha!

However, I would like to figure out a way to let some UV reach my retina without cooking my eyes.

### CoolSwede — 2016-01-13T10:31:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-186153>

The second time I tried the tanning salon, I stayed in the tanning bed for 5 minutes. Still not sure about the safety regarding radiation into the eyes, I used protective eyewear. However, I have a hunch that it would suffice to have the eyelids closed since the thickness of the eyelid probably is thicker than the penetration depth of the UV rays.

The day after I experienced that it was much easier go get things started so I guess that my dopamine levels had risen substantially from the skin exposure of UV...or placebo I need to test this further...

Another test I am planning to do is to try DIY NIR therapy using a 500W incandescent light.

### CoolSwede — 2016-02-03T10:32:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-188044>

Yesterday, I visited the tanning salon again for a 10 minute recharge. Damn, the power of UV is amazing. I feel sooo relaxed and I notice an impact on my mood (not necessarily from a low level, I just get even happier). Dopamine FTW!

I know it is not optimal like the real sun, but with with the current UV index of maybe 10^-9, it benefits me. I never got the opportunity to try the 500 W halogen when it was really cold here (-15C) which is sad because I think that the cold would have worked synergistically with the IR radiation (Quantlet alert). Need to find a solution for the NIR while waiting for the real sun, and for the Quantlet to appear in my mailbox!

### CoolSwede — 2016-03-17T13:12:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-191063>

### CoolSwede — 2016-07-13T16:58:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-198302>

Summer solstice has passed but the sun is still decent with UV indexes reaching 6-7 on sunny days. At work I have been out in the sun from 11.30 to 12.30 daily (skipping lunch on sunny days) which has given me a decent tan. Of course, when my vacation finally starts, I will try to be outside as much as possible.

My facial skin has really improved with no tendency towards seborrhea but I still have some dandruff. Annoying, but not a huge problem. What still bothers me, though, is low exercise tolerance meaning that I still perform nicely at the gym, but running is a challenge. It has always been my weakness and when I was younger, running in the Winter was impossible due to cold induced asthma.

Another challenge is my lowish stress tolerance together with motivational issues. I experimented with using nicotine which initially gave me a boost but needs a higher baseline dopamine level I guess.

Foodwise, I have been quite liberal with carbs, according to the season, and I seem to deal with them decently. Unfortunately, I have eaten them later in the evening which is suboptimal, I know.

Finally, I really hope that my OxLDL numbers will continue the trend of going down. Maybe the Quantlet can assist when it arrives!

### CoolSwede — 2016-10-20T15:11:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-203098>

Long time since I wrote here, time to write about what is up. Fall is here and temperatures have been dropping. I have not fully transitioned from sun/carbs to cold/fats but it is ongoing. Diet LCHF, albeit could be higher DHA. I have started to visit the tanning salon again due to the lack of sun and to give me a boost of UVA.

I have not taken any new labs regarding OxLDL but plan to do so soon. It will be interesting to see if the downward trend continues or not. Those high numbers are a bit scary...

I have ordered a number of 360 nm 3W LEDs which I plan to use as a biohack at work. I am behind a 3 glass window which effectively blocks UV from the sun which is sad.

Last visit at the dentist revealed that the cavity was ok, but the pockets around my molars had deepened and I was informed that wisdom teeth extraction could be necessary in the future due to this. Started countermeasures:

1\. Stopped using toothpaste. (Glycerin = bad)
2\. Started oil pulling using coconut oil 20 mins every night.
3\. Continue use of 15 mg Thorne K2 MK4 drops every morning.

Still no "proper" CT (no access to a tub), but I wear as little clothes as possible and always shower cold every night, albeit for only a few minutes.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-20T16:13:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-203112>

Larger pockets = poor light

[http://msystems.asm.org/content/1/5/e00105-16](http://msystems.asm.org/content/1/5/e00105-16)

Mitochondrial cytochrome c oxidase, a photoreceptor and the terminus protein of the electron transport chain, reduces oxygen to water and also reduces nitrite to nitric oxide. Nitric oxide production depends on specific wavelengths of light from the skin and the Q-cycle that interacts with this heme protein as well as oxygen concentrations present at cytochrome 4. So how does this link to oral health and to the bacteria in your mouth to cause headaches? Nitrates, such as cardiac therapeutics and food additives, are common headache triggers, with nitric oxide plays an important role. Facultative anaerobic bacteria in the oral cavity may contribute migraine-triggering levels of nitric oxide through the salivary nitrate-nitrite-nitric oxide pathway.

Red light preconditions the skin’s mitochondria in the morning to protect itself from UVA and UVB light that will come later in the day. What helps protect us? Red light improves mitochondrial function by shrinking the respiratory proteins by raising melatonin levels which act to regenerate and recycle mitochondrial DNA. This is how you build your solar callus with AM sunlight that is filled with red light and has the ideal color temperature fingerprint for our skin and eyes. Conversely, blue light has the exact opposite effects on the mitochondria in the skin and eye that lead to degeneration and the need for medical renovations which are not optimized to natural law. Blue light causes mitochondrial destruction, loss of DHA, lowering melatonin and leads to aging and illness everywhere we look. A lack or AM spectrum has massive biologic implications when we miss using visible range AM sunlight at 550, 605 and 655 nm and near-IR at 830 nm light. Even the oxidized species that can be present in the AM light and can be represented by the 655 nm band or light is bleached by the presence of molecular oxygen intermediates P and F in cytochrome c oxidase (cytochrome 3). P is characterized by an absorbance spectrum at 607 nm relative to the oxidized enzyme and F is characterized by an absorbance spectrum at 580 nm relative to the oxidized enzyme which are both present in AM sunlight. 635 nm LED light has even been shown in the literature to increase the scavenging of excessive superoxide anions made in mitochondria completely independent from the cellular enzymatic antioxidant system.

The steady-state behavior of isolated mammalian cytochrome c oxidase has been examined by increasing the rate of reduction of cytochrome c (ECT). Under these conditions the enzyme's 605 (heme a), 655 (heme a3/CuB) and 830 (CuA) nm spectral features behaved as if they were at near equilibrium with cytochrome c (550 nm). This has implications for non-invasive tissue measurements using visible (550, 605 and 655 nm) and near-IR (830 nm) light. The oxidized species represented by the 655 nm band is bleached by the presence of oxygen intermediates P and F (where P is characterized by an absorbance spectrum at 607 nm relative to the oxidized enzyme and F is characterized by an absorbance spectrum at 580 nm relative to the oxidized enzyme) or by reduction of heme a3 or CuB.

Nitric Oxide (NO) binds to cytochrome c oxidase to block ECT because when sunlight is present we don’t need a speedy ECT. This means that electrons from strong UV seasons need to be excited by UV light frequencies. If they are not, we will fatten and NO won’t be liberated from the skin. This proves all mammals have photosynthetic capability in our skin, eye, blood, and our mitochondria. Only short wavelength UV light liberates NO from the skin into the circulatory system. NO is a vasodilator that lowers BP and enhances circulation, while reducing energy production in mitochondria. There is no better proof that humans are photosynthetic when you understand how NO works to liberate oxygen in cytochrome c in the mitochondria under strong mid day sunlight. The complementary NIR light restores energy production when food electrons are not present. This is why intermittent fasting is much easier in full balanced solar environments. It also points out why artificial light leads to some sort of disease in all cases because energy declines from mitochondrial oscillation frequency change. NIR light increases O2 and ATP sans food electrons. This implies that NO helps slow ECT when we are in sunlight that contains UV light and UV light is capable of "de-gaussing" RBC's to reelase oxygen to our mitochondria. It is obvious sunlight can be used to power us when we need it too, but only if we allow our skin and eyes to assimilate the light signal in our tissues mitochondria the way nature designed the system. Man's world has altered that blue print.

### CoolSwede — 2016-11-03T11:24:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-203553>

I listened to @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)'s Vermont talk, and in the end he said something that struck me: He told about Jeremy and how his disease required him to eat carbs since the high flow of electrons induced by fat did not have enough electron acceptors to terminate the flow, ie oxygen. The result was a large increase in ROS.

Since my latest OxLDL labs indicates an extremely high, albeit decreasing, amount of ROS. I start to wonder if my case is similar. Why, one might ask, since I do not suffer cystic fibrosis.

Well, my whole life, I have been suffering low exercise tolerance and was asthmatic as a kid. Today I only feel the asthmatic effect when exercising in cold, ie cold induced asthma.

So, it seems like I also need to biohack my oxygen levels (hypoxia?) to be able to deal with a high fat induced electron current without creating ROS. Maybe red light?

BTW, as an electrical engineer, I have started to view electrons in terms of voltage and current. Summer carbs energized by UV -> energized electrons = higher voltage. Winter fats -> more electrons but not so energized = higher current. Hm...thinking out loud... impedance = voltage / current -> summer = high impedance and winter = low impedance?

### CoolSwede — 2016-12-07T17:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-204935>

Now I'm back home to the darkness and cold here in the North. Some observations from the journey:

\-During the stay I never burnt myself from the sun. I was at the beach, exposing myself to midday sun for at least 2h daily. Got a decent tan.
\-I was never hungry during the stay. Photoelectic effect FTW!
\-Despite the above, I ate lots of seafood (thanks for the Blue Lobster tip, @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) . The grilled lobster tail with butter sauce was delicious!).
\-Every day was a carb feast with lots of fruits, juices and nachos/quesadillas etc, weight gain ~0, judging by my belt!
\-Sleep was as deep as home but shorter
\-Most mestisos were overweight.
\-I hated the overuse of 6500K fluorescent tubes everywhere
\-Almost everybody used sunscreen in ridiculous amounts

It will be interesting to see if there will be any impact on the upcoming labs...

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-08T04:46:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-204968>

Tonight's factoid: Polar environments can teach those people above 50 latitude North some great things. The relevance of polar observations scales to other regions like the UK and Scandinavia where heart disease, autoimmunity, and cancer incidence grows with every decade since the 1880's. Polar regions are greater teachers of the 3 legged stool of how life is organized. Polar regions provide a useful environments for observing and optimizing the intensity and spectral composition of ambient light. Most of this light is sun light in the summer and artificial light in autumn and winter. Many circumstances in the high latitudes during winter with the short photoperiods allow for unique windows to see how humans react. When we add the complexity with an interior workplace, which are associated with a lack of bright natural light, the observations paint a clear picture of why shift work at night in low latitudes is deadly. In midwinter in the UK, for example, London at 52°N, travel to and from work will often be completed wholly in artiﬁcial light, since from 4 December to 7 January the sun rises after 08:00 h and sets before 16:00 h. Right now Londoner's are in this veyr window. Workstations may well have no windows, and outdoor activity at lunchtime or during daylight hours may be difficult, if not undesirable in poor weather conditions. Thus, data from polar regions are likely to have applicability in urban environments with winter daylengths shorter than the working day. And those observations have not been kind to those subjects. “Midwinter insomnia” has repeatedly been described for regions of northern Europe, particularly Norway, and is characterized by difficulties falling asleep. These people tend to lose Stage 4 sleep routinely. The available information suggests that internal desynchrony will be associated with metabolic abnormalities, particularly risk factors for heart disease, and possibly cancer. Similarly, cardiac function and, indeed, all physiology governed by circadian rhythms (Hastings et al., 2003) is likely to be affected. Subjects free-run infrequently in a structured polar environment in winter, but this does happen, especially after a period of night shift. Most readily adapt to the night shift, but the readaptation to day work is often very slow in winter. All of these problems can be addressed by maintaining a consolidated and suitable circadian phase. The treatment of choice, both in winter and summer, is carefully timed light exposure and avoidance as a function of endogenous phase, with or without carefully timed melatonin treatment.

### CoolSwede — 2016-12-13T11:51:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-205208>

I made this _gedankenexperiment_ yesterday:

1\. Two people are standing naked facing the sun.
2\. The first is standing barefoot on the ground, and the second one is standing on an ideal isolator (there is no such thing, but imagine standing on a big chunk of nylon).
3\. The first person experiences the photoelectric effect, where the excited electrons can flow to the ground. The charge build-up is 0, but the current flow is >0.
4\. The second person is isolated, but still experiencing the photoelectric effect. However, since the person is isolated, the current is 0 but charge is starting to build up.
5\. After some time _t_, the sun disappears (...or sets) and the photoelectric effect seizes in both persons.
6\. The second person, steps off of the insulator and becomes grounded. In this very instant, current flows until charge is balanced out.
7\. Now, both persons have experienced the same charge flow (_Q = I\*t), but at different time instances and different current levels._
8\. Since both persons have experienced the same charge flow, albeit at different time instances, the physiological effect should be the same.
_
Conclusion: Exposing yourself to the sun and grounding do not need to take place simultaneously.
_
Right or wrong?

### CoolSwede — 2017-03-21T15:04:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coolswedes-thoughts-ideas-and-reflections.17195/post-210835>

The quest to solve my scaringly high OxLDL is moving on. From the moment I realised that most ROS were generated by the ETC, I have been focusing on understanding the ETC in detail. I have read the following papers:

Murphy: How mitochondria produce reactive oxygen species \[2009\]
Schönfeld et al: Mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation and oxidative stress: Lack of reverse electron transfer-associated production of reactive oxygen species \[2010\]
Selivanov et al: Reactive Oxygen Species Production by Forward and Reverse Electron Fluxes in the Mitochondrial Respiratory Chain \[2011\]
Quinlan et al: Sites of reactive oxygen species generation by mitochondria oxidizing different substrates \[2013\]
Turrens: Mitochondrial formation of reactive oxygen species \[2003\]
Chen et al: Production of Reactive Oxygen Species by Mitochondria \[2003\]

Using them (and Jack's excellent blog posts!) I have made this virtual interview with myself.

Q: Why are my mitochondria producing excessive ROS?
A: Environmental stress or defects in the ETC. Probably not from external sources.

Q: Is there any special environmental stress?
A: No, not compared to others. I work in an office where I have managed to mitigate most of the blue light using f.lux /turning off the lights in the ceiling etc.

Q: Are there any defects in my ETC?
A1: My mitochondria could be enlarged, but how to measure that? I take a cold shower every other day and dress lightly (enough to make people ask if "Summer is here")
A2: I have yet to find out if there are any SNPs that could be related to this.

Q: Are there any other manifestations of high ROS other than OxLDL?
A1: Yes, I have always had bad exercise tolerance and asthma tendencies.
A2: I have deepened tooth pockets and since many people say that that could be improved by Q10 supplementation, the root cause might be bad mitochondria.
A2: My hair started turning grey at 32.

Q: Does Q10 supplementation make any difference?
A: I have tried 1000 mg Ubiquinone daily for a number of days. No significant difference, maybe improved mood.

Q: Any other supplements?
A: I have tried them all. I know that sunlight would be the ultimate supplement...

Q: According to the articles above, ROS are mostly generated by complex I, but LCHF/Keto diets direct electrons to complex II. How can so many ROS be emitted?
A: Good question. High fat diets increase the number of electrons, but if there is no oxygen to accept those electrons...

Q: Hypoxia?
A1: Maybe, I have exercise induced asthma.
A2: After a large high-fat meal, I start to yawn a lot and my head becomes "woozy" with brainfog. Reduced cognitive abilities also.

(to be continued)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-03T21:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet-indiegogo-campaign-is-live.17196/post-183211>

GREAT NEWS EVERYONE!

We have been gearing up for the launch of our Indiegogo Crowdfunding campaign over the last few weeks. The mechanics have been quite involved but we are finally able to say that we are finally going LIVE TOMORROW December 3rd, 2014 at 12PM GMT.

That is 7AM EST, 6AM CST and 4AM PST and 11PM in Sydney. To find your exact local time, please use this tool.

Please bear in mind that there are a limited number of BETA units in this crowdfunding campaign. Our manufacturing capacity for BETA units is limited by several factors. Full production units will not be limited by these factors, however.

We have been working behind the scenes to raise that number and we have been able to do so, but not my much. This is because we insist on keeping our costs low and our price point below $600 USD.

The difference between BETA and full production units will be mainly with delivery timing. We anticipate BETAs will be delivered around the April 2016 time frame. Full production by late summer. So, if you are keen on getting a BETA unit, please make sure that you act quickly once the campaign goes LIVE.

We don't have a limit on full production units, but they are about $150 more expensive.

So, if you want the Quantlet faster and cheaper, then be part of the first 150 people to act tomorrow! There are about 5,000 people on our email list, so the likelihood of these BETA units going quickly is HIGH!

We will be sending an email with the Indiegogo campaign page link, as well as posting it on our site at [TheQuantlet.com](http://thequantlet.com/), and on our social media pages including Facebook and Twitter.

GOOD LUCK!

GREAT NEWS EVERYONE!

We have been gearing up for the launch of our Indiegogo Crowdfunding campaign over the last few weeks. The mechanics have been quite involved but we are finally able to say that we are finally going LIVE TOMORROW December 4th 2015 at 12PM GMT.

That is 7AM EST, 6AM CST and 4AM PST and 11PM in Sydney. To find your exact local time, please use this tool.

Please bear in mind that there are a limited number of BETA units in this crowdfunding campaign. Our manufacturing capacity for BETA units is limited by several factors. Full production units will not be limited by these factors, however.
