> a high fruit diet is really no bueno for someone living north...huh In fact it is so easy to understand!

I traveled a lot for my previous career, but I was based in Seoul for 17 years. Imagine that! Sadly, Koreans are addicted to artificial light. When I arrived there in 92, I was disgusted by the lighting. It was actually my major issue there because Koreans are addicted to it. If you put on a lamp ore lower lighting, they get really upset. They have to have their places lit up with the brightest light you've ever seen until late hours of the evening. They can't understand how we use lamps in our houses. They ALL say that when they don't have the bright lights on they feel uncomfortable. Most Koreans have myopia. However, their health is not as bad as you would expect considering their work conditions, hatred of sunlight etc. I mean Koreans really worship blue light and absolutely are scared to death of the sun, a recipe for the perfect storm. The one serious health condition Koreans have tends to be gut issues and gut related cancers. Koreans were the first people in the world got addicted to cells, texting, and even their own Facebook. Facebook was actually invented in Korea, but under a different name. It was called Cy World and was around long before Facebook. The whole country was addicted long before Facebook came out in the US. Another thing, even in the earliest days of wi fi, it became a national policy to make certain cities "ubiquitous in wi fi". It was the campaign promises when people were running for election. Again, this is when the world barely knew what wi fi was. Korea was the most connected place in the world for years.

However, I must say that Koreans are waaaaaaaaaaay healthier than they should be. These people should be dropping like flies. Well, if you know the Korean diet, it IS the epi paleo RX, even more so than Japan. Yes, Koreans eat too much white rice. If it was not for the white rice, it would be the Epi paleo RX.

Korean diet and Korean herbal medicine is from a real ancient civilization that seemed to have high level insights. Here is the daily diet in Korea.

raw seafood--as much as possible
oysters in everything, at every meal. You can't get a meal without oysters in a Korean restaurant.
seaweed in everything
pork a few times a week, and ALWAYS when you drink alcohol
fermented veggies at every single meal--The kimchi is fermented cabbage with oysters and a ton of chili with ginger and garlic fermented in a pot buried under the ground
After a woman gives birth, she has to have seaweed soup at every meal for a long period of time

Lifetyle

a public bath house is on every block with a cold plunge pool--All the elderly Koreans use this as a regular ritual. These bath houses have saunas too, but the cold plunge pool is full of people in sitting in it for long periods doing CT.

Koreans love their hot springs and the elderly can all find spring water even in Seoul.

You could actually plan a healthy holiday on the coast of Korea, eating the local food and swimming in the ice cold waters, and you would probably do quite well. Raw fish and oysters are everywhere. It is the seaweed capital of the world. Actually, I would recommend some of the coastal towns of Korea if someone wants to do an epi paleo RX bio hack with some ocean CT;

### Sue-UK — 2015-12-21T14:08:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mels-optimal-journal.17244/post-184668>

> [Melanie Procter said:](/goto/post?id=184486)
>
> Wow. Thanks for the update. As you write this, I feel like I am reliving my hack. Many of the things you describe are like things I experienced. I am very impressed you can do this in the UK winter. R u in London?

No I'm rural south west. This morning saw me out to get the morning light on my eyes, and then I went out to CT shortly afterwards. Very strong winds, not my favourite but its gotta be done! My latest is I found a couple of ice cube trays which make ice sticks, like mini wands  More manoeuvrable over the eye lids than cubes, but colder than the jade roller. I wrap the end where I hold it with a bit of chamois leather so my fingers don't melt the ice so quickly, and if I'm indoors just put a hand towel round me to catch the drips. If I lay down or lean backwards I can also direct the cold water at the tear ducts like eye drops, and its sets the blinking off ... I've used them outside whilst CT ing as well. Last night after being in town all day I used one until it melted, switching between eyes, waited 15 minutes and then got another one ..... It took 6 before I felt it was enough .... Rock solid sleep and cornea seems a bit "cleaner" this morning ....

### Melanie Procter — 2015-12-22T03:50:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mels-optimal-journal.17244/post-184735>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=184682)
>
> I reckon I damaged my brain, my optic nerve, my RPE, the rods and cones, got blood in the cornea, had a misshaped eyeball (and who knows what else). I've been to the sun twice for a week since then (the second week I must have eaten 40 portions of seafood ......) and I improved each time. I'm going to Marrakesh for new year ..... but I got to thinking that between times there has to be something more I can do in the environment I am in. I'm trying to make sure my vortices are reversing at night properly .....

I am in Bangkok now and was really worried that being in the city for awhile like I have been would reverse my biohack. I got black light in my room to supplement my time in the sun here. I still do AM sunlight and get out in the afternoon daily. Because of the haze and EMF's, I felt I would not be getting good UVA/UVB light. Additionally, I am doing shopping for my resort for the holidays and am in the blue light palaces--shopping malls daily. I get sooooooo de dehydrated there. It almost feels like an illness. So, I am have black light in my hotel room. I have it on all the time and sleep with it, cutting out all blue light. The result? I actually feel better than I did in Phuket. Now, I know that the science does not support me here. Partially, I am in a high stress environment in my resort. There are no words I could say to describe how hard my work is there. If you ever come, you will see. My life is full on from the time I open my eyes until I close them with what feels to be zillions of people coming at me all the time--my staff and all my guests. My head spins daily. I feel like I am always in a heightened state of alert. Though I am in the city now, I don't have this stress. So, stress is like blue to me.

My point is that I feel you can improve your results with supplementing with black light, increasing hydration and adding in meditation or other elements in your life to reduce stress. I got great results in Phuket from light alone, but I am getting better results now from some lifestyle changes and I believe this black light. I would suggest you grab a black light and put it into your hotel room In Marakesh.

Jack told me about the black light in my educational consult, but he did not tell me how radical it was. For me the result is profound. I don't want to make a big deal about it as nothing can replace natural sunlight. So, I am gonna keep my enthusiasm about the black light under wraps. I will just say I have tried every supplement on the planet, I have never had a supplement work like the black light. When I say supplement, I mean supplement to the sun and seafood. I am not again talking about replacing the sun, but supplementing the sun.

The things I learned in that educational consult! Wow!

### Sue-UK — 2015-12-22T14:54:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mels-optimal-journal.17244/post-184748>

I'm wondering if the science may support you in that if stressed cells (from whatever stress) leak UV, if you don't restore the UV it can (amongst other things)lead to a relative excess of blue ..... the black light becomes the "supplement." I've not played with a black light at night, because I need to understand better about UV and melanopsin ... If I've understood it right, a photon of light will desensitise melanopsin, and the next photon resensitize it .... and I can't remember if that includes UV, or decide whether with my current context, it's a good thing. Its interesting you mention meditation, IIRC meditation can start the vortices reversing, so when I'm in the dark icing my eyes before sleep, I try and make it a meditative practice too, particularly at the moment as we approach full moon ..... (which I think can interfere with the vortices reversing?...). Because it was the shortest day, I went to bed at 9 last night, 20 minutes of icing my eyes meditatively in the dark, and then woke up naturally at 8 this morning, later than normal but it felt "right" as if waking from hibernation ... The morning light seemed more effective.....

I've been playing with hydration signalling, when I first have a drink in the morning on an empty stomach, usually when I go outside and look at the early morning sun, and when I start to prepare the evening meal, I drink just to a point where I can feel my stomach stretch receptors ...... My version of going to the watering hole at dawn and dusk, but maybe I should be looking at tide times... That's next  I also drink between times, but I'm interested in biohacking the signalling part of hydration, and also UV. I reckon even if theoretically the UV index is 0, there will be enough UV photons to signal, even if its a case of scavenging them for signalling, rather than harvesting them for energy. If I've been desperate to harvest I've used a vertical tanner in the morning, but as my stress levels have been coming down I'm drawn to it less. Hoping that's a good sign.

OMG. I am so excited that someone tried my biohack. OK. Well, I must be honest, the dose make the poison. I have had a fab time in Bangkok playing with black light in my hotel room I found a nearby store who sells black light in every which way. So, I have played with it in every which way. I should photograph my hotel room for u.

Anyway, all was fine with sleeping with black light until last night. I had this grand idea to create a purple light booth out of my bedroom and created all kinds of purple lights around the room. It could have been a night club.

The result? I could not sleep at all! It was awful. I had to take some melatonin to knock myself out. Now, to be fair, I was upset about something. There was a bunch of chaos in my head about some things and perhaps I amplified the chaos.

Today, I put on an old school far infrared bulb and black light bulb and meditated in the room. That was cool.

Yes, dreams are kind of wild when u sleep in purple light. I don't know what to think of it. It seems to be an amplifier of whatever is going on.

I wish Rubin Salinas would run a "light meditation retreat". We could experiment with his meditation practices and light at the same time. Wouldn't that be fun? Until then, it is just u and me inventing this stuff as we go!

Mel

PS Tomorrow morning, I go back to my house in Phuket where I will be the head chef at all the Xmas events. I will black light up the kitchen! My staff is Indian an Thai and won't go in the sun at all ever! So, I will supplement their sunlight. Let's see what happens. Of course, there will be my 90 minute morning sunlight to crunch some time to at first to make sure I can get everything done.

PPS I want to be clear, I am only supplementing with black light as I need it. I have been in Bangkok under extreme blue light conditions. I am putting black light in my kitchen because South East Asians refuse to see sunlight, as a tan causes social problems for them. They go home to the highest dose of blue light as they can get as they are addicted. They think I am a crazy hippy and won't listen to my warnings about artificial light. I thus put black light in our kitchen. I say this as black light would not be normally necessary in these equatorial zones. The sun is always my sun. Black light UVA bulbs and infrared bulbs are moons when needed. I achieved great results in my bio hack, but it was the sun that treated me. I am just starting to experiment with light bulbs as I am in a city.

# Thread: What does tinnitus suggest to the clinician?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-tinnitus-suggest-to-the-clinician.17245/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-15T05:57:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-tinnitus-suggest-to-the-clinician.17245/post-184155>

In biology, the lower energy systems required for autonomic or vegetative functions. The brainstem get energy loaded first, at the expense of the more energy intensive systems, like the brain's neocortex. If no energy is remaining after the basic systems are loaded with energy, the brain runs at an energy deficit, and it directly affects neocortical function. Moreover, the brain is built by evolution to "steal energy" naturally to preserve its own function in mammals, because it has the physiologic ability to auto-regulate its oxygen and blood flow requirements itself. This makes the brain quite unusual in this sense.

This means that the brain will preserve its function at the expense of other systems. This helps you understand where tinnitus and hypertension really come from. As energy is lost from our body systems back to the environment for any reason at all, the brain increases its mean arterial pressure to maintain its energy status at the expense of other systems. This is why BP rises in non-wellness states. When this happens in the brain, cognition suffers in the regions of the brain that are most energy dense. The next system in line is the immune system. It was the system that the primate tree innovated before neurogenesis occurred using the changes in the MHC1 gene.

In the archeo-cortex hearing sense is an energy hog. This is why tinnitus is a big clue to a clinician that a patient's environment is sub-optimal. Ammon’s horn, part of the hippocampus, is the most energy-inefficient part of the neocortex. It is one of the first areas affected in Alzheimer’s disease. This is why, in anoxia or pseudohypoxic states, we see this area of the cortex affected first. The areas most affected earlier by lowered blood flow are the parts of the cortex that cost us the most energy. This is how why tinnitus is a symptom showing us we are deficient in photon assimilation or electrons in our tissues.

Charge in the human body is built by the assimilation of UV photons that are being recycled through nucleons, atoms, and molecules. Light is carried by photons, and photons have both energy and momentum that travel in ambient electric and magnetic fields that we measure or sense in a variety of ways. Light gives a charge to our proteins and lipids below our perceptions. Sunlight recharges the batteries in us that make life possible. Charge is normally composed of smaller photons that have had their energy and momentum harvested by our tissues. Light devoid of energy and momentum is heat. Heat is red light. Red light augments ATP creation, and red light carries the least amount of power in all of light’s colors. Ponder that for a minute. People focus in on red light as a “healing frequency because they fundamentally have no idea of how light really works in tissues to regenerate us. Nothing is more regenerating to us than UV light. It contains the most charge because it has the most energy and momentum to change us and our lives. Charge can also be thought of the same as heat because heat is also a form of light used within mitochondria for signaling. Heat is caused by the motion of photons stirring within us looking to still have a usefulness to our bodies. We are built not to waste any light. When we waste it in our auditory relays tinnitus is the result.

# Thread: Adrenal Fatigue, Low T, Problems with CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-low-t-problems-with-ct.17252/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Toby King — 2015-12-16T01:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-low-t-problems-with-ct.17252/post-184231>

Hi everyone!

I am 16, and have had many issues with health for quite a while. After living in Southeast Asia for four years, I developed many problems with adrenal fatigue, nausea and headaches every day, and took around a year off of school. I started working with a naturopath and changing my diet/lifestyle, I have been strict paleo for 12 months, but even though I have had some success I still am struggling with quite a few things:

-   Adrenal fatigue - Cortisol levels of Morning 6.0, Midday 4.2, Afternoon 1.4, Night 0.3
-   Low Testosterone - 284 pmol/L
-   Estradiol at 72 pmol/L
-   Estrone 6.0 pg/ml
-   Lack of energy
-   Still a relatively high body fat

After scouring many of Dr. Kruse's blogs (Thank you so much by the way!) I have been working intently on changing my environment. I have been avoiding all blue light after sunset for the past few months. E.g. Blublockers/F.lux/Red LED strips throughout the house. Have been on the Leptin reset starting my day with the BAB for around a week now, quite a change from the Bulletproof coffee/intermittent fasting of the past year.

I have been experimenting with CT for more than 6 months, but am starting to have issues with worrying parents and my naturopath is very concerned that it is damaging to my adrenals. I love CT, but my parents seem to think that it has attributed to my low cortisol. I would love some advice for convincing them that it is a power for healing.

I have been following the Epi-paleo diet for a while now, unfortunately I can't stand the oysters... Any time I try to eat them I retch  Luckily here in Australia we have found a source of Canadian wild caught salmon that I consume as much of as possible.

After reading the Adrenal Rx I am starting my day with 5-20 minutes of sunshine for my eye-clock, and try to get as much sun throughout the day as I can.

Very happy to have found this community, and I hope that some of you have advice that I can implement now into moving forward. I can upload the rest of my biochemistry tests if needed.

Thank you in advance,

Toby

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-16T03:35:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-low-t-problems-with-ct.17252/post-184244>

You developed many problems: adrenal fatigue, nausea and headaches. All of these are tied to a dimming effect of parts of your brain. An acute dimming of the brain occurs in TBI cases..........so let us use this as an analogy of the quantum model of cell degeneration.
How does a coma presents in humans? A coma is a situation where neocortical function is lost and only the vegetative remnants of our brainstem remains. Photonic and electronic energy is acutely disrupted and often lost in a coma state from the neocortex. Simultaneously there is a shift in photonic energy stores in mitochondria as the brain’s neocortical function is browned out. Vegetative functions in the brainstem (PVN) remain intact initially while higher brain functions are "dimmed". This implies that how we treat coma presently should also be altered. Coma is an abrupt loss of photoelectric energy to the neocortex. Neolithic diseases of the brain are a chronic, slow, insidious loss of photoelectric energy in different parts of the neocortex. These cause regional brownouts on the neocortex. A diimmed brain = cognitive haze or chronic adrenal fatigue. It is as if since the loss of energy is slow and insidious, the same process found in a coma situation is unfolding via a different space/time continuum in our life. This implies the geometry of space is tied to light and so is time. Time 5 will give you insight why this might be the case. Since we perceive time based upon our basal metabolic rate, and this rate is linked to how we assimilate the most powerful parts of the spectrum of sunlight, modern humans never seem to perceive this relative change. Einstein’s mass equivalence equation says that we should consider it.

Once you understand this neurologic time parallax, it should also explain why we are seeing the current increases in neuro-degeneration. In chronic diseases, we now see diseases of the neocortex manifesting early because light is missing from the eye clock to lower dopamine. This is why depression, mental disease and neuro-degeneration shows up in younger and younger age groups (YOU) as the processes in our environment allow for the slow insidious loss of energy by the technologies we call new age or progressive.

Anyone know about issues concerning circadian mismatches by using CT at other times of the day?

Also, are pure red LEDs safe at night? I have many strips throughout the house so that we don't need to wear our blue blockers, just wondering if there is any issue with possible UV emission...

Thanks so much for your comments!

### Lahelada — 2015-12-22T20:00:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-low-t-problems-with-ct.17252/post-184765>

Not A
not B
But C....wake naturally around sunrise. That means your circadian rhythm is restored.
Having said that I would not force it with alarm clocks if you are new to this. If you have been doing this for a while then there might be some tweaks you need to make. On a hunch if you wake up much later than sunrise it maybe that you are eating too late.

### JanSz — 2016-03-29T18:12:07+0300

\[ quote="Toby King, post: 184231, member: 18139"\]Hi everyone!

I am 16, and have had many issues with health for quite a while. After living in Southeast Asia for four years, I developed many problems with adrenal fatigue, nausea and headaches every day, and took around a year off of school. I started working with a naturopath and changing my diet/lifestyle, I have been strict paleo for 12 months, but even though I have had some success I still am struggling with quite a few things:

-   Adrenal fatigue - Cortisol levels of Morning 6.0, Midday 4.2, Afternoon 1.4, Night 0.3
-   Low Testosterone - 284 pmol/L
-   Estradiol at 72 pmol/L
-   Estrone 6.0 pg/ml
-   Lack of energy
-   Still a relatively high body fat

Toby\[/quote\]
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------
1000 pico=1 nano

Low Testosterone - 284 pmol/L=0.284nmol/L ====>8.2ng/dl (double check test results)
that is very low, practically there is none, it should be (700-1000)ng/dL
have you injured your testicles (inborn deficiency, chemical use, physical injury)?
is your pituitary working properly (LH, FSH)?

Something looks fishy because your estradiol is just right (in presence of almost no testosterone).
Possibly you have mistyped testosterone results, and the test report shows different units (like nmol/L)
Estradiol is made from testosterone.
Estradiol at 72 pmol/L=20 pg/mL

.

It is always better to post test results as given by laboratory.

The test you posted on post #25 indicate that you have tolerable amounts of testosterone, but could use much more testosterone
and have way way too much estradiol.

Estradiol causes growth.
High estradiol, high growth (sometimes undesirable growth).
This may be the reason of your high body fat.
May cause also genecomastia (breast tissue growth).
Note if one or both of your breasts itch (check is car's seat-belt over the breast makes you uncomfortable)

Good idea to check (in blood)
pregnenolone
progesterone
DHEAs

you should have progesterone=1.4 ng/mL, little more may be acceptable, little less=ng

At least two reasons for this level of progesterone;
controls estradiol (may lower your very high E2)
converts to cortisol (may raise your low cortisol)

You want DHEAs=men (520-640)μg/dL
DHEAs is super important steroid hormone
its normal participation level by weight, in steroid hormone panel is in high 90%
it is even more important if pregnenolone and or progesterone are low
good DHEAs levels prevent pregnenolone steal

.........

### JanSz — 2016-03-31T21:56:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-low-t-problems-with-ct.17252/post-191792>

> [Toby King said:](/goto/post?id=191725)
>
> Hey Jansz, thanks for that, yeah I definitely do have a tendency towards the moobs...
>
> I will have Pregnenolone, progesterone and DHEAs tested in my next blood test.
>
> Any other ways/ideas for lowering E2?Thanks!
>
> T

Good progesterone level is major part of controlling E2.
Body fat have aromatase enzyme.
Aromatase converts testosterone to E1-estrone and E2-estradiol.
E3-estriol is made from (16-OH Estrone) (indirectly from E1)(men have small amounts).
More aromatase---> more estrogens.
Less fat ---> less aromatase ---->less estrogens (specially E1 & E2).

//
2/16ratio=(2-OH Estradiol)/(16-OH Estrone) ratio is important in preventing man-boobs and any other undesirable growths (men and ladies)

//

\==========================================================
AST=294(10-40) --->294/40=7.35
ALT=96(5-40)------>96/40=2.4

SGOT(AST)Aspartate aminotransferase
SGPT(ALT)Alanine aminotransferase
are liver indicators
both are too high
[https://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/ast/tab/test/](https://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/ast/tab/test/)
(AST)
How is it used?
The blood test for aspartate aminotransferase (AST) is usually used to detect liver damage.
It is often ordered in conjunction with another liver enzyme,
alanine aminotransferase (ALT), or as part of a liver panel to screen for and/or help diagnose liver disorders.

AST and ALT are considered to be two of the most important tests to detect liver injury,
although ALT is more specific for the liver than is AST and is more commonly increased than is AST.
Sometimes AST is compared directly to ALT and an AST/ALT ratio is calculated.
**This ratio may be used to distinguish between different causes of liver damage and to distinguish liver injury from damage to heart or muscle.**

AST levels are often compared with results of other tests, such as alkaline phosphatase (ALP),
total protein, and bilirubin to help determine which form of liver disease is present.

\================
What does the test result mean?
See link above.

### Rubicon — 2015-12-16T03:50:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronological-listing-of-blog-entries.17253/post-184246>

Is a complete chronological listing of all the blog entries available? I feel like I'm missing out when a post I'm reading refers to an older post I haven't gotten to yet.

To navigate through the blogs I've been using:

-   the [blog Index](https://www.jackkruse.com/blog-index/) of course
-   the [blog Table of Contents](https://www.jackkruse.com/table-of-contents/)

# Thread: Listening to Webinars
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/listening-to-webinars.17254/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bjclifto — 2015-12-16T05:37:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/listening-to-webinars.17254/post-184253>

Hey guys,

Long time lurker, first time poster....I've been listening to Jack's podcasts, read the book, part of FB group, need to read more of the blogs, but decided to jump in as a Gold member to learn as much as possible. I plan on taking a deep-dive into the webinars over the upcoming weeks. Do ya'll recommend starting with the webinars in 2012 and working up to the most recent webinars or would it make more sense to listen to the most current and work back to the older stuff? I know there's been much more emphasis on light so I'm wondering if listening to the most recent webinars will have the most impact on protocols/information. However, I'm also interested to see the transformation from webinars in 2012 to 2015 so I'm curious how more senior members here would approach the learning process.

So I still plan on listening to all of them, but was hoping for some feedback as far as how to attack learning all this information. I'm certain all of the information is still relevant, but I can see some of these shifts from earlier blogs to current content. I'm halfway through the first webinar on Deep Cold CT and loving it, but have heard a few things (ex: drinking BP coffee after CT) that I'm not sure Jack would still recommend. I could totally be wrong in saying that (thought I read somewhere recently where coffee was kind of a no-go), but was hoping to get some advice on how to attack the webinars.

I'm excited to join and can't wait to absorb this information to help myself and family become more optimal!

### Joe Gavin — 2015-12-16T15:39:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/listening-to-webinars.17254/post-184283>

Welcome! Great to read you're taking the leap into learning!

My take is this: read the following books to get a good grasp of where Jack takes us in the webinars. You've already read Epi-Paleo. He references these books and authors often and ties them together in the webinars:

The Body Electric by Robert O. Becker
Light In Shaping Life by Roland VanWijk
Life on the Edge by Jim Al-Khalili and Johnjoe McFadden
The Fourth Phase of Water by Gerald Pollack.
The Vital Question by Nick Lane

Personally, I've found the webinars and blog posts much more educational with this background reference.

This work is like eating an elephant: take it one bite at a time. It also depends on your current knowledge of things, and you own ability to apply the principles and see how you and your family respond to the suggestions.

The simple things are always the hardest to put into practice. Getting back to a natural way of living is challenging. Controlling your light environment, specifically fake blue light after sundown, sounds simple but can be tough in practice. It takes diligence and committment. My own experience: it's changed my body and mind for the better, so I'm not going back. Even my kid knows not to fuck with me after a certain time of night!

Jack leaves nuggets of information on health, wellness and life throughout his postings and webinars. It's important to listen and digest first. I was a long time lurker, but I found by reading what has been suggested has allowed me to be a better contributer to the tribe.

Enjoy the ride. It's worth the effort.

### Bjclifto — 2015-12-16T15:58:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/listening-to-webinars.17254/post-184286>

@[Joe Gavin](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/15415/) thanks for the insight!

I've read parts of Body Electric a long time ago, but could definitely use a refresher. I recently purchased Life on the Edge so will get to that soon as well. I've listened to Gerald Pollock on Joe Cohen's podcast some time ago as well, but I definitely agree that reading these books first will give me a better grasp on picking up more nuggets throughout the webinars.

Our family is definitely doing some things right (drinking RO/spring water, eating a good amount of seafood, try to limit nnEMF, where blue blockers at night, get outside as much as possible...), but our biggest issue is definitely environment. We live just north of Chicago so my biggest challenge is convincing the wife to move to a more optimal location.

Thanks for your feedback; I'm hungry to learn more and ready to put forth more effort

### Josh Rosenthal — 2015-12-16T19:04:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/listening-to-webinars.17254/post-184300>

Welcome.

Im currently reading blogs in chronological order as well as old webinars... listening to current banter and webinars, but haven't read current blogs. It is hard straddling the two sides, but I think it is worth it. You may see the hidden gold in older blogs as you follow with recent stuff. I do recommend the above books Joe mentioned. I'm 3 down and halfway into fourth. Its a lot of work, but very rewarding. You will become aware of the problems and solutions for the first time.

If you search you will find PDF word files of the blog. I recommend printing them out (less blue light). I had them bound by a printer and have the year's worth in a nice package. Finishing 2013 and we still need to put the 2014 into digital format so printing is easier. If you're any good at that, feel free to contribute!

Good luck.

# Thread: Breast screening. Is ultrasound a good way? Pls help with some ideas!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-screening-is-ultrasound-a-good-way-pls-help-with-some-ideas.17255/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniel — 2015-12-16T13:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-screening-is-ultrasound-a-good-way-pls-help-with-some-ideas.17255/post-184269>

My wife found a knot in her breast today which wasn't there before and we'd like to make sure what it is. We have an appointment to have ultrasound inspection on 8th January. Is there anything in the meantime which can help us figure out something more?

I was thinking to have a bloodwork, and check CEA, CA 15-3 and CA125 (unfortunately CA 27-29 is not available here).

Is there anything else we could check?

Since it's on the sides of her breast close to the bones and muscles, it feels like a muscle but not in the proper position. I don't know much about this, can you please help with some ideas?

Regardless of what it is, I got some ideas from here [https://www.jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with-new-cancer-diagnosis/](https://www.jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with-new-cancer-diagnosis/) to boost her immune system. A little background info. We eat a primal kind of diet, mostly beef, mutton, wild meat and trying to eat more and more fish/seafood, plus green vegetables, squash, avocadoes etc... Pretty much real food, very few processed stuff. I'm in ketosis, she sticks with low carb and eats a little rice here and there. We live in Budapest, winter time, no UV or any other light outside. EMF is 40-ish everywhere (radio towers, 3G-4G). Exercise 3-4 times a week (weight training, crossfit and yoga for her), and go hiking on weekends.

### Lahelada — 2015-12-16T13:38:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-screening-is-ultrasound-a-good-way-pls-help-with-some-ideas.17255/post-184271>

While it may well be nothing it might be good to ditch crossfit for a while. Loads of info why it is not good for you here but for now let's just sum it up as it would help her to conserve energy.
I googled for you and there are interesting results. You will have to look into the comments for some blogs but they are all listed. All the best !
[https://www.google.com.ar/webhp?cli...pHQCA#q=jack+kruse+breast+screening+mammogram](https://www.google.com.ar/webhp?client=ms-opera-mobile&gws_rd=cr&ei=6EtxVsWHNMKlwgSO0pHQCA#q=jack+kruse+breast+screening+mammogram)

### Daniel — 2015-12-16T14:04:04+0200

Thanks a lot Lahelada

Well, by crossfit I mean circut training. It's safe version of it with TRX, medicine balls. No funny stuff like barbell squats on yoga balls and stuff  now she sprained her ankle (running downstairs) so she won't do it for a while

### Daniel — 2015-12-16T14:07:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-screening-is-ultrasound-a-good-way-pls-help-with-some-ideas.17255/post-184273>

# Thread: Selenium is a light story too?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/selenium-is-a-light-story-too.17256/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-16T18:37:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/selenium-is-a-light-story-too.17256/post-184295>

Heard from Janel, "While helping my son study for his Chemisty exam, this caught my eye. "Because selenium can convert light into electricity, it is used in solar panels. Selenium's ability to conduct electricity increases as its exposure to light increases." Remember learning long ago in one of Jack's early blogs about the anti carcinogenic value of selenium and Vit D. Food with the highest selenium level...oysters, followed by tuna." Surprise! Light is the biggest story in life.

# Thread: Co Q10 - caps or liquid form? + Best natural sources...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-q10-caps-or-liquid-form-best-natural-sources.17258/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniel — 2015-12-17T18:14:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-q10-caps-or-liquid-form-best-natural-sources.17258/post-184342>

What is better form of Q10 supplementation, capsules or liquid? Any brand you suggest, you trust?

Seems like Chicken heart and Beef heart is the best source, but does cooking destroys it? Not sure I could convince my wife to eat it raw

### Inger — 2015-12-17T18:32:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-q10-caps-or-liquid-form-best-natural-sources.17258/post-184346>

It is pretty heat stable so your wife will still get Q10 even if she heats it  but many other benefits will diminish... like the B vitamins.... and bacteria..... etc etc
I would try to cook it as gentle as you can to save the most nutrients. I mostly eat lambs hearts raw but sometime I cut them up in slices and heat them gently in a little butter and heavily spiced with curcumin and black pepper and a little sea salt.. I just fry them on low heat for a minute or so.... totally delicious.. tastes like amazing sausage to me but just better...lol

She could cook them moist with cabbage too.... lamb with cabbage like a stew is delicious too

I mostly make lamb heart carpaccio

### Inger — 2015-12-17T18:34:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-q10-caps-or-liquid-form-best-natural-sources.17258/post-184347>

I think eating gently cooked hearts of grass fed animals wins over supplement any time
I personally like lamb hearts the most

>
> I think eating gently cooked hearts of grass fed animals wins over supplement any time
> I personally like lamb hearts the most

well unfortunately we don't have lamb heart over here... not sure what do they do with it. We have beef, pork, chicken hearts... so I guess beef is my best option. It has that hardcore cement-like fat deposits outside, so I doubt that it's 100% grass fed

### nicld — 2015-12-17T22:33:17+0200

> I would try to cook it as gentle as you can to save the most nutrients. I mostly eat lambs hearts raw but sometime I cut them up in slices and heat them gently in a little butter and heavily spiced with curcumin and black pepper and a little sea salt.. # Thread: Near infrared sauna and insomnia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-sauna-and-insomnia.17259/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2015-12-17T18:15:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-sauna-and-insomnia.17259/post-184344>

Hi!
Has anyone noticed having any negative side effects when using a NIR sauna? I added two black lights to mine and started using it again the other day and had significant insomnia after just using it once for 20 minutes (I used it during the day). I now realize that I also had insomnia when I used it several months ago without having the black lights....it seems to give me a bit too much energy. Any thoughts? Chrissy

### fitness@home — 2015-12-17T19:40:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-sauna-and-insomnia.17259/post-184352>

I have a FIR sauna and use it in the evenings. It hasn't caused any insomnia that I've noted.

I have noted an increase in my energy levels during the day though...

### Jonathin — 2015-12-17T19:50:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-sauna-and-insomnia.17259/post-184353>

nnEMF issues? Have you checked it out?

Chronobiologically effective blue light does not always mean healthy; in fact in most cases it means an ROS stimulus to the RPE. Light is a complex drug. Light provides biology a complex answer because of how complex light can be when it interacts with many different new proteins. I only tell folks to avoid mercury vapor fluorescent bulbs if they have documented mercury toxicity. Hg bulbs are a light source that can act as a sender of resonant energy. Hg in your body can act as the receiver of resonant energy if it is present. This is one of the reasons I am not in favor of amalgam removal. It likes to find harbor in fatty tissues like When Hg is charged by current is has a spectral array that give two lines in the violet at 404.66 And 407.79, one in the indigo range at 435.84, one in turquoise at 490.05, one in lemon at 546.08 and two in the yellow range at 576.92 and 579.03. It binds to sulfur enzymes to remove them from use and interferes with ECT directly by lowering energy production. This is akin to putting a match to gasoline with respect to Hg. This causes more complicated electric and magnetic fields and introduces flicker effect in the brain that is very dangerous for those with any neurodegenerative disease of age related macular degeneration.

Because they are DIRT CHEAP on the internet and their profits would crumble. I am about to enter my third trimester and astonishgly i have very little hunger.
i eat breakfast at 8.00am and lunch at around 1.00pm, usually shellfish and rice,since we don't get oysters here  and after that i just don't feel like eating anything.

# Thread: Time 5:  Where geometry and light meet is the birth of consciousness?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-5-where-geometry-and-light-meet-is-the-birth-of-consciousness.17266/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-18T16:10:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-5-where-geometry-and-light-meet-is-the-birth-of-consciousness.17266/post-184433>

**Biology gifts us with a brain, but light give us a mind through its wave particle duality.**
From this duality was a single point source of light. That light has the power to alter geometry of everything in the world by transferring its energy and information. Water happens to the the market maker in the rearrangement of the energy and momentum of lights power. It has the ability to organize the chaos of the environment to make sense of it for a cell.
**Time needs light to be moving in order for it to emerge and acquire a direction.** When it obtains a direction it can alter the atomic geometry within a cell. Stop and think about this for a minute. **Time emerges from this atomic interaction between light and electrons in atoms.** Time can and does change in us. Consciousness or wakefulness is what changes it. This implies that consciousness is a state of geometric change. And it is. What happens to water through an AQA4 gate between glial cells and neurons is how we awake and create reality. **Light is an electromagnetic disturbance in space that define’s time presence.
**
The type of matter that light strikes also reveals time to the observer in their retina. This makes our consciousness a unique experience. This is hard for most to fathom but it is true. No two lives are the same because of this but light is the unique point from which all change comes. Change comes from non change because of the duality of light. This makes time relative for all animals to the amount of DHA/electrons within that observer’s SCN. This also makes time relative to all observers who see the same thing. The light others see and the electrons it hits is unique to each observer. _There is no cause and effect here because cause and effect is a property of how light interacts with matter. This is why life is variable._

The atomic lattice in two SCN’s are never quite exactly the same. Some have more DHA and less carotenoids or vice versa. This creates many possibilities and this is a very quantum idea. All light hits all life and alters it in some way they rarely perceive.
After being hit by any light, a**ll life liberates heat after interacting with light to make Rayleigh Bénard convection in cells. This cell is what changes the geometry within our cells. Heat is another form of light that is of the lowest power. The shear amount of it changes the sizes and shapes within our cells to give us the life, health, or illness we observe.
**Time began in a great void of infinite space. Two atoms met because of a light collision and they proceed on a different path. That meeting of these two primordial things caused changes in energy and direction of the flow of light energy and momentum. The new direction created a new geometric relationship in space. **That geometric change embodies the information of the event.** When a collision occurs the information from the collision cannot be cancelled out. The new direction carries the memory of the collision. Any collision that improves the redox potential (charge) of the physical system, increased energy assimilation from the ground state. Life and cells, do the exact same thing with energy, as occurs in space. The difference is we have gravity on Earth so we use the Rayleigh Bénard convection cells to our energy advantage. Proteins and lipids in cells retains the memories of the collisions with light frequencies to build systems that overlap with one another to not allow light energy to escape. The assimilation of light from our surfaces to collect the sun’s energy gives cells the “energy currency” to make use of the Rayleigh Bénard convection in cells because mitochondria liberate heat (IR) release. Our surfaces absorb the more powerful part of the solar spectrum under the force of gravity while we release the least powerful part of the visible spectrum to form the energy saving Rayleigh Bénard convection mechanism.

Time 5 contains some of the deepest mysteries of life between its words. Have you thought about any of them yet? Have you just read the words I strung together like an alphabet soup? You might want to sit back and open a bottle of wine under the red sky and think about what I just said. The implications are immense for things or creatures that need light to live.

The Power of Vibration (words, chanting- Sanskrit is a powerfully vibrational languge upon which many many languages are based). Memory of Water. Duality of Light. Time Dilation. ### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-18T17:33:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-5-where-geometry-and-light-meet-is-the-birth-of-consciousness.17266/post-184445>

Here is an epilogue of my current blog series on TIME: If we were visualizing a civilizations recovery plan what did Feynman say was the key point to leave them?

Imagine a major planetary catastrophe that was leading to an extinction event. It could be a global nuclear war, a CME, a devastating global pandemic, or rapid climate change due to interruption of photosynthesis or the thermohaline current. It could be a viper computer virus, wiping out all digital memory banks on servers. In any of these scenarios, we would anticipate an economic and environmental collapse. The catastrophe would directly and indirectly involve a massive die-off of human populations. What did Feymann say was the key?

The interaction of light and atoms. The earth receives about one one billionth of the sun's energy, and that humans utilize about one millionth of that source. So we consume about one million billionth of the suns total energy. We have a lot to learn according to that basic math. There is nothing in the laws of physics which prevents building armies of molecular-sized machines that give us quantum control of most things we can experience. We can do that with something called a quantum dot. What is that? The Time series deals with them.

In the 1960's Feynman was asked to think about what might be the most important scientific “sound bite” to pass onto future civilizations faced with catastrophe. Feynman opted to give future peoples the idea that "all things are made of atoms" This shocked many. I think it was brilliant because 50 years later we still have not opened that box of Pandora to get the essence of this gift. Atoms are little particles that move around in perpetual motion, attracting each other when they are a little distance apart, but repelling upon being squeezed into one another. How light interacts with with matter is key...............And atoms contain electrons............light has a special affinity with for them.

In fact, when Feynman he saw atoms for the first time with a scanning tunneling microscope he called it a "religious experience".

My bet is he would have thought he died and went to heaven if he knew about atomic force microscopy. We can only guess today that atomic force microscopy which extends our ability to see beyond the scanning tunneling microscope. This method of observation allows us to probe the atomic forces between atoms and light that have been found so elemental to scientific understanding. That interaction is what RF said was most important so now 50 years later we are just getting the ability to fully assess that interaction.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-18T17:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-5-where-geometry-and-light-meet-is-the-birth-of-consciousness.17266/post-184446>

As an atheist even Feynmann was moved to speak of religion with respect to light and atoms............What links them both? Light's duality. It should be no mystery why all Holy books talk about there "letting their be light"

We were given the solution and not the recipe.

This site is about recovering the that recipe ingredient by ingredient and then recreating the proper order that recipe requires. Direction and order is why circadian signal is fundamental why food is in the third position.

### Joe Gavin — 2015-12-18T18:54:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-5-where-geometry-and-light-meet-is-the-birth-of-consciousness.17266/post-184466>

So I was googling around a bit on emmission, 1523, Mito and came across the attached doc. Most fascinating part is the Concluding Remarks. Also references VAnWijk, All about the eyes and light:

"There is no doubt that EEG waves are deeply involved with the basic functioning of the brain but the origin and the exact function of EEG has remained a mystery. The EEG waves associated with two distant neurons are strongly correlated and this supports the view that EEG waves are related to the properties of the brain as a coherent quantum system. It is not possible for a scalp EEG to determine the activity within a single dendrite or neuron. Rather, a surface EEG reading is the summation of the synchronous activity of thousands of neurons that have similar spatial orientation, radial to the scalp2 . Synaptic transmission and axonal transfer of nerve impulses are too slow to organize coordinated activity in large areas of the central nervous system. Numerous observations confirm this view \[73\]. The duration of a synaptic transmission is at least 0.5 ms, thus the transmission across thousands of synapses takes about hundreds or even thousands of milliseconds. The transmission speed of action potentials varies between 0.5 m/s and 120 m/s along an axon. More than 50% of the nerves fibers in the corpus callosum are without myelin, thus their speed is reduced to 0.5 m/s. How can these low velocities (i.e. classical signals) explain the fast processing in the nervous system? We believe that quantum theory is able to explain some of the above mysteries. As an example, recently it has been shown theoretically that the biological brain has the possibility to achieve large quantum bit computing at room temperature, superior when compared with the conventional processors \[60\]. 2 Updated from : [http://www.reference.com/browse/electro-encephalogram](http://www.reference.com/browse/electro-encephalogram), 13 November 2009. 17 **Neuroscientists or brain specialists record the EEG diagrams of their patients when their eyes are closed, because when their eyes are open the amplitudes of diagrams dramatically would be reduced and they cannot diagnose changes in the amplitudes. In Figure 7 we represent the EEG diagrams of a person with different situations as explained in the caption to the figure. When the eyes are opened, the number of interacting photons with the biomolecules becomes high and the amplitudes of EEG become low. It is clear when the intensity of incident light is high; it must produce more action potentials than low intensity light. In classical physics, it is argued that this situation is because the superposition of several waves tends to decrease the amplitude of the total wave. _This argument ignores the intensity of the incident light entering the eye._ This classical argument also cannot explain when and how the synchronization happens in order to decrease the amplitude of EEG.**

# Thread: Pescetarian Epi-Paleo questions

### Eugenia — 2015-12-19T19:18:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pescetarian-epi-paleo-questions.17269/post-184515>

So, I want to cut down land-meat completely. I'm currently eating land meat every Sunday only now, and I have bone broth 1-2 times a week (I used to be full Paleo meat-eater, but I just don't want to eat meat anymore). My plans is to completely phase the remaining land meat out in 3 months time, and only eat more seafood instead.

So my question to you is this: if I only eat seafood (lots of shellfish, low-mercury wild fish, and sea veggies), do I miss anything important nutritionally and epi-genetically from not eating land meat? Can my body have everything it needs to function (given the rest of the Epi-Paleo lifestyle) with seafood+eggs+kefir for animal protein? Before you jump on the dairy btw, I want to be eating goat kefir btw, it's the only dairy I eat and I won't cut it because it helps me with my destroyed gut flora. My questions is primarily on the seafood and if it's enough in the absence of land meat. Thank you.

### PaulG — 2015-12-20T01:51:50+0200

Probably should have lambs liver once a week. [Cystein](http://www.healthaliciousness.com/articles/high-cysteine-foods.php) which is important is high in pork, beef/lamb and seafood.

I am part-way doing an analysis at the moment (see attached pdf) but also take a look at [this article](https://optimisingnutrition.wordpress.com/2015/10/19/optimal-foods-for-different-goals/) for Nutritional Analysis of the USDA food database.

**Caveat: You can have too much 'nutrition' for example eating too much liver, bone broths etc will lead to overly high copper levels**

Paul: thank you in the info on cystein. But that also exists on seafood too in adequate quantity? Also, for the liver: what can it give me more than oysters, except too much copper (which I can get from mussels and squid anyway?). Thx!

The reason why we ask is that this forum has left food as a supplement of magnesium boron behind a long time ago. We eat, seafood but also pork for Glutathione for example. Food helps you along but the real base for optimal redox lies elsewhere.

### Eugenia — 2015-12-20T21:23:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pescetarian-epi-paleo-questions.17269/post-184637>

_**Dr Kruse:** Nothing is more important that light in the eye clock. To say otherwise means you are not understanding the story in the Quilt.......or the blogs._

I understand them alright. It's just that morning UV light is not always an option here. Sometimes I have to work late (I often work commissions for Europeans, and have to stay in some sync with them), so that means that I'd have to wake up late the next day too. That's why, I have to fall back into optimizing the secondary strategy: food. Which I KNOW that it won't get me 100% where I need to be, but at least, if I optimize it, it might provide just enough to get me through this until I don't need to work with Europe anymore, and be able to sleep and wake up at normal times, and see the sun more, and fall back into primary strategy: Light. So it's not that I don't understand you, it's just that "life happened" in the meantime.

_**Inger:** Eugenia, why would you like to opt some natural food sources out? Does not make sense to me.......
_
Because I don't want to eat animals anymore. I grew up in Greece, eating the whole animal head to toe: including stuff most of you Epi-Paleo guys don't even find to eat around here, like lungs and intestines, and spleen (my favorite). And even when I moved to the US, I was still hunting for liver and heart, and I've already been in this Paleo thing for years now. So I'm not "just some vegan crazie" browsing your forums. I've been following Jack (and Paleo) for years now. I know the benefits of good quality meat.

The thing is though, I just don't want to eat smart animals anymore. This includes all land meat, and birds, except most insects/larvae. And it also includes the smarter seafood, like octopi. I just don't want to eat them anymore. I don't have any qualms of eating oysters or small fish, but I do have against intelligent animals (at least, intelligence as we, humans understand it). It's a rather arbitrary line, but it's a line I have to draw. Currently, I still eat bone broth from grass-fed bison (for my gut, which is not in good condition due to many years of undiagnosed celiac disease), since I've bought 20 lbs of knuckle bones. But when that's done, I will stop that too.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-22T03:41:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-and-cold-thermogenisis.17270/post-184734>

yep Read brain gut 16 PVN = paraventricular nucleus........what does ventricle stand for? CSF. CSF is 99.9% water. Water cools and does amazing things as detailed in Pollack's book.

Ancestral health, alternative health, and Conventional Wisdom in healthcare knowledge is based firmly entrenched and operates in a Newtonian/observation world.....Life and nature however are quantized to light............life is built in a cell as a playground for light and cooling using the metastability of the second law of thermodynamics which is statistical and not absolute. Life works at this edge between thermodynamics and the subatomic world. This edge is literal and figurative. Below this edge is all the quantum mechanisms that hurt people's heads I speak about on my blog........Every last one of these processes are built into your mitochondria by evolutionary design, just as chlorophyll is built into the 39 steps of photosynthesis which are also quantized and well aceepted by science now. Trees are made from the thin air where carbon exists as a gas, carbon dioxide, and with the sun's ability to knock one atom of oxygen out of CO2. Water is the playing field this occurs because sunlight needs to use water's electrons in this magic trick. Hard to believe, but none the less it is reality. Photosynthesis is not controversial, but few in healthcare or ancestral health know the details as well as I do. The reality built into your mitochondria is more amazing.

The more disconnected your mind is from the natural environmental light and magnetic effects that are formalized in circadian signaling, the more altered your perceptions of reality become. If you're well connected to nature (sun and ground), your need for food is small, inflammation levels are naturally low, electrons and protons are closer together because low inflammation cools things within a cell. Moreover, your body acts in coherent fashion because the sea within it handles the fire that a mitochondria gives a cell. That fire comes from rays of light through cytochrome 1 at NADH. Electrons enter with a 340 nm wavelength. All mitochondria spit out IR light as a signal. They also spit our ROS signals. these signals are vastly misrepresented by modern science. Why? We look at damage as a path of destruction.

Growth is necessary in life, but it is always stimulated by damage (ROS) and limited by regeneration. This is the key...............

**Nature's disruption is dependable, because we cannot control her therefore ROS is not a bad thing. It must be controlled.

Failure is the pivot foot of change in this dance for biology. In physics things always fall to equilibrium. Life is built far from equilibrium. When things fall to equilibrium the decay to chaos. Physics, therefore has no regeneration built in to its story. Biology is based upon coupled feedback loops to take a damaging stimulus and use that damage as its stimulus to regenerate.**

Adrenal fatigue is a loss of such a signal and failure to ever get to the regeneration via autophagy. You lose the ability.

In the natural connected state, you are able to create thoughts and intuitions of what life looks like and feels like is acceptable for you. Why? The stimulus of light builds dopamine and dopamine builds the substates that create ROS in our eyes. Conversely in sickness, the opposite happens and dopamine and all pigmented proteins falls because we bury the sunlight. As a result our minds and thoughts become disconnected from the operations in your cells as dopamine and its cousin proteins fall. The feedback loops required to regenerate are lost. When this happens biology fails and physics begins to win.........the march to equilibrium begins in many systems. This is what adrenal fatigue is.

The irony most are unaware of is, that the body doesn’t give a hoot what you think about this process; it goes on about the business of survival while paying deep attention to light and your connection to the sun and Earth. All of our goal's in life should be to learn what we need to know to coherently link our thoughts to how our mitochondria fundamentally work in quantum fashion. When we do, we begin to realize that our body conforms to what our mind thinks is optimal based upon the conditions we allow. Once we learn who the master of our body really is, then and only then will we come to know how the quantum world manifests in everyday life. This is why many illnesses shows up when we disconnect from nature in these states.
Life organizes around the 3 legged stool, light, magnetism, and water chemistry both in your body and in your skull. Adrenal fatigue is an unraveling of that connection to nature.

### JanSz — 2016-01-19T19:50:13+0200

Adrenal fatigue ---->it is not that adrenals are fatigued and can't produce, it is that they are not getting proper signaling. They are not being asked to produce.

What you are describing is low cortisol and low DHEAs.
Cortisol and DHEAs are two of many steroid hormones.
\----
In healthy person when one would measure major steroid hormones by weight, in the blood
and take that as 100%
DHEAs participation is 95%
cortisol participation is 4.26%
all other 0.73%

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

So someone who is missing a lot on DHEAs and cortisol have a major problem.
There are other problems.
The very first steroid hormone is pregnenolone, and it is made from cholesterol.
When it is made, it is made in relatively small amounts.
Even if you would turn all the right switches and started producing pregnenolone in good amounts and all the rest of the system suddenly started working ok it would take a long time to fill up the deficiencies.
Think of 95gallon water tower being filled with syringe 0.04" thick.
Per my understanding, DHEAs reserves act similarly to water tower in town.

Cortisol is the only hormone that we cannot live without.
Body would steal any available upstream hormone to get material to make cortisol.
Most commonly known is called pregnenolone steal.

When desperate, with very low corisol levels, direct supplementation with hydrocortisone (natural) or Medrol (synthetic) may be need.

If one can wait little bit, it is better to supplement with pregnenolone.
Pregnenolone plus correct light at the right time, may allow for cortisol levels fluctuation according to right Circadian rhythm.

Supplementation with DHEA untill levels suitable for 20yo person should take care of the (Water Tower).

There is a reason why the system got miss-regulated.
For myself I like to deal with this aspect on wholesale basis.
I use **Spectracell Micronutrient analysis**.
Using results of that test I either supplement deficiencies with pills or change my diet.

There is more.

From time to time one may want to check his/her reaction to CT, at some point it may begin to feel beneficial.

> oxygen--->cortisol
> CT--->fan

Transcortin binds most of cortisol.

Also look up the PDF file in post above (#16)
\======================
You have used word:
**non-inflammatory**

That reminds me of DHA and AA (also EPA)
Two or rather three fatty acids. Unfortunately only one got attention.

AA promotes inflammation (therefore got bad press)
DHA controls amount of inflammation (suppresses AA)(it is anti-inflammatory)(hero)

Neither is excessively bad or excessively good.

When they are
1\. at good levels
2\. in balance

the part that they control works good

life is controlled inflammation

excess or deficiency of either one creates problem
\-----
The word anti-inflammatory sound so good that caught attention, highly easy to sell.
The word inflammatory had no chance in this context.
\-----
However super important role it plays, we (adults) need minute amounts of DHA in our body (and diet).
We need also minute amounts of EPA.
Instead we are sold on fish or krill oils or seafood, that not only supply us with tremendous amounts of DHA and EPA,
but it does that in highly reversed proportions of DHA/EPA.
We need a teaspoon, instead we are sold 1/4 of truck load of the stuff we actually need (DHA) and 3/4 truckload of stuff that messes up the first 1/4 truck (EPA).
Some people are able to tolerate that and are smiling, others are not smiling.
Able to tolerate=they are not being helped heath vise, just eat good tasting food.
The amounts of DHA that we actually need could be found in many other foods.
But if not we have about 2 years time to find it.
\-----

### Summer Day — 2019-08-26T18:16:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-and-cold-thermogenisis.17270/post-276676>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=186739)
>
> I think the issues appear when we leave stuff out.... our understanding is too narrow.. we push too hard in one area of life and do nothing in other areas.. and we forget to follow our intuition
> It is complex... and it is easy... a funny paradox
> Many ignore the CT protocol... it goes very slow
> If you are sick you need to do things different than if you are healthier. It is all about the concept

I completely agree. I have thyroid and adrenal issues, so I started out really slowly. One 2-minute cold shower per week. Then after reading an article on Wim Hof's site about a man who had great sleep when he did it right before going to bed, I tried it, but had the opposite reaction. I shivered in a cold bed with 2 layers of down comforter. Finally after 3 1/2 hours of using my husband's body heat to warm me up, I finally stopped shivering and was warm enough to fall asleep. Really bad for the adrenals to lose all that sleep and be in a panicked survival mode.

I am zealous, so I didn't let that discourage me for long, and tried again. Jack is right when he says to eat a full fat/ protein meal right before doing it. Now I am doing it almost every day, still only 2 minutes but will increase very gradually as I adapt. This 3.5 hours of shivering happened again, but now I know why- the meal right before is a MUST. Even a full fat meal at dinnertime but going to bed 2-3 hours later after a cold shower was a living nightmare for me.

Go slowly and build up. I am the most cold-fearing person on the planet probably and even I am doing it. So be encouraged- you can do it too.

couple thoughts for you [@Summer Day](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23915/)
My wife is just like you when it comes to cold - i couldn't pay her to get into an ice bath. In fact, she is always covered up in a blanket, sleeping with alot of clothes on, craving hot showers, sitting under out IR-A and red light lamp, or outside in the sun enjoying the warm. After rereading alot of jacks work it dawned on me that her body is craving IR and red light, and is significantly lacking this spectrum in her life & she can't create a battery well. she does eat seasonally, with alot of seafood, but can't give up the carbs. I suspect that the lack of IR/Red light in her life is to blame for her constant feelings of cold and craving heat aka IR light. So, perhaps the reason you are shivering for 3.5 hours after 2 minutes of a cold shower is that you are lacking IR/Red light spectrum. When your mito's burn fat they release IR light, and create water/C02. The EZ water created along with the IR light released builds the battery in the cell. But, if the circadian mechanism isn't timed up to your local environment this might be throwing off your mito's ability to burn fat well to create IR light and EZ water to build the battery. Jack says that AM sunlight is the most critical thing because this sets the circadian clock but it also serves as the off switch to our hormones! thyroid, adrenal, etc. Now, my wife and I watch the sunrise every morning, in fact, we changed our work times so that we could maximize AM sunlight. We've been doing this ever since our mexico trip last winter when everything changed for us (we literally watched our health come back in a matter of 24 hours down in cancun mexico in UV 12 sunlight!). My wife now will take ice baths (mainly outside in direct sunlight) and her body temp is the best its been, but she still likes her warm showers but not because she can't warm up. also, no complaints of her raynaud's either. Just personal experience here but are you seeing the AM sun?

My other thought - I have hashi's, i was dx 3 years ago and I am currently on meds. Over the past 3 painful years of experience this is what i've found to work. A - See the sunrise and get as much AM sunlight as possible. As i mentioned above, we changed our work schedules to spend 1-2 hours in the AM outside making like the sphinx (we are working on moving south...we are going to mexico 3x this winter). B - MAX and I truly mean MAX your Vitamin D via Sunlight. The higher i get my Vitamin D the better i feel and the better my thyroid labs are. My intuition with sunlight tells me that UV-B is really important for my health and my condition. C - We smash seafood and sea vegetables. 90% of our food is seafood now. oysters, salmon, sardines, roe, etc. D - CT. I have found that doing CT outside after a big meal is literally better than any drug i know of! I sat in my tub last night during sunset for 30 minutes and it was amazing and i slept so well. (I will mention that i've done CT for 5 years with varying intensities. before i broke my thyroid I used to melt ice with my body heat) I'd suggest starting at a warmer temp for longer versus a colder temp for shorter time. Water is a natural faraday cage and it gives your mito's a break! E- Defend your Eyes and skin from ALAN. I include my office and home for this one. When melatonin is lowered due to blue light exposure, it will royally mess up your circadian clock and impair your ability to recycle your cells. I actually supplement some IR-A and UV-A lights into my office and in the evening at home. Finally, CT shrinks the ETC, lowers inflammation, even stimulates peripheral thyroid hormone production but my opinion is that you can't handle the cold because you are missing the OFF switch to your hormones via AM sunlight. Cold should improve the fat burning, create more IR light and EZ water, building the battery but this may not be happening because your colony of mito's are not synched up with the master clock in the eye in the AM. Until my wife and I realized this and prioritized AM sunlight, CT didn't do for us what it does for others here on the forum. Now, my wife actually likes the cold! and for me it destroys inflammation and improves my mito's tremendously.

### Summer Day — 2019-09-05T20:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-and-cold-thermogenisis.17270/post-277242>

I really appreciate all of your advice. I wrote this mostly to encourage the guy who said that he didn't want to do CT due to adrenal issues. I am so glad to hear that your wife is doing well with CT even with such cold intolerance. I too crave the sun's warmth and 9 months of the year hover over our wood stoves (one of the joys of the cold season is cooking on a wood burning stove) for IR/heat.

I think what is going on is I am looking for results too fast. We just discovered Jack's YouTube videos in June, at the beginning of summer, so yes, even though I was immediately obedient to the AM sunrise and putting my feet on the ground and have been maximizing my time outside in the sun, it is naturally less effective being winter. The very few carbs I have been eating are non-starchy vegetables like broccoli, cabbage, cauliflower, swiss chard, so no issues there. I also drink or eat homemade beef broth on a daily basis, and eat and drink fermented vegetables for the vitamin C and probiotics.

The first CT I did was putting my feet on the cold or frosty ground while watching the sunrise. I remarked to my husband, "Jack Kruse better be right about this" because I do not even stick my toe in cold water if I can help it. Or touch stone or go anywhere without my down comforter, etc. Wind chills me to the bone and I have a really hard time recovering. But now I am CTing regularly, at first once a week for 2 minutes, now a few times a week.

Last week I decided to go get into the creek (remember it's winter so this is totally uncharacteristic of me) but felt so good after 2 1/2 minutes, drying off in the sun, that I decided to set the timer on count-up instead and get out when I wanted to, and got in again. Over six minutes later, I finally decided that I should get out. By the time I walked back to the house, I was shivering violently, but I recovered in front of the fire. The next day I had symptoms of severe adrenal fatigue (lightheadedness, feeling faint when standing up, shortness of breath, heart beat rapid, bad sleep), so my guess is that my zealousness has hurt me again and I overdid it, too soon. I should be more patient and build up more gradually.

I am looking forward to the summer when I can get plenty of sunshine, then my results will come faster. I have also made myself herbal tinctures too for improving brain function, adaptogens to stress, etc. I am hoping that by next winter, I will be much farther along after a whole year of change.

Thanks for the information and encouragement!

### Lady Eve — 2015-12-20T03:43:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/problems-in-the-gulf.17272/post-184545>

Hey!

I moved to the Santa Rosa Beach, FL area back in August. The Gulf is beautiful and there are lots of forests here. Lovely!

However, the chemical exposure here is crazy!

\- the county sprays for mosquitos with neurotoxic chemicals a few times a week
\- most of the homes have grass and they spray regularly for bugs, weeds, other pests

If you go out for a walk, you can literally smell the harsh chemicals!

We also have annual Red Tide exposure which are a natural algae that releases spores into the environment and they cause lots of issues from respiratory distress and are very harmful

Do you think this is too toxic to live in long-term? I wouldn't mind if I was just visiting a few times per week but to live here day after day and be exposed to all of these chemical sprays is not fun.

Would love your thoughts!
Eve

### MySecondFiftyYears — 2016-05-15T22:39:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/problems-in-the-gulf.17272/post-194841>

> [Lady Eve said:](/goto/post?id=185199)
>
> Actually, Destin was hit hard by the Red Tide. ([http://www.thedestinlog.com/article/20151028/NEWS/151029193](http://www.thedestinlog.com/article/20151028/NEWS/151029193))
>
> This is becoming a chronic issue. Because it's pretty toxic, I'm now wondering if I should head toward the east side of Florida along the Atlantic, where the bloom doesn't come. We could definitely feel it in our lungs and it's no fun.

We just spent 14 days on Navarre Beach, up before sunrise on the beach until sunset everyday, in the gulf for hours walking thigh deep, resting directly on the sand, no shoes, all day long. Nothing bothered me except a few tourists on their phones and a few screaming kids. We swam, snorkeled, kayaked, wave boarded, everything. Nothing bothered our skin or lungs. We also slept with the glass doors wide open every night and never used the AC. If not for the tourists (I'm a traveler, there's a difference) I could stay in Destin/Navarre/Pensacola area forever. I have spent entire winters there and still never any issues with water or air. The traffic didn't concern me as i only drove a total of 17 miles during our 14 days on island and crime didn't touch me as I always carry my trusty side kick. Our time was near perfection. We are strong and tanned, walked over 100 miles in water and sand.

# Thread: Philip OLED R&D dept closes down
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/philip-oled-r-d-dept-closes-down.17281/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2015-12-21T15:12:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/philip-oled-r-d-dept-closes-down.17281/post-184671>

Today i met a young German guy over lunch and as the conversation progressed he said he used to work in the Philips OLED R&D department.

He explained to me that his whole department has been closed down as Philips (major industry player) is shifting its industry focus and only buying its products from third party suppliers ie China and other asian countries.

His team was working on developing a fuller spectrum OLED and unfortunately they have all been let go.

I (himself as well) could not believe that such a time honoured industry leader was just shifting its focus to a 'distributer' of sorts.

When i began to talking about nM ranges, photoelectric effect , the atomic structure of porphrin rings and such he was baffled as to why a long haired surfer looking guy would know any such info......THanks to Jack and this group today I earned a wry smile and a laugh to myself.
