# Thread: Do you prescribe oral melatonin?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-prescribe-oral-melatonin.17286/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-22T04:17:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-prescribe-oral-melatonin.17286/post-184737>

Let me share the results of my biohack from 11 years ago if you do.

Biohacking tip #722: taking oral melatonin might be a real bad idea. Why? Oral melatonin is an inhibitor of retinal dopamine production. If you use it for sleep you might be lowering ocular dopamine and frontal lobe dopamine levels leading to circadian disorders may affect cone functioning. What else? Dopamine controls hormone release from the pituitary so you lose control of your hormones. How do we know this is true? Bio hack the impact of oral melatonin usage on your cone response and the regeneration of your retina using electroretinography (ERG). You might be shocked at the results.

It turns out the reason your retina has more DHA than the brain dose per gram is because of how ocular melatonin regenerates rods and cones. It is the hormone that controls the process.

Oxidative stress is involved in activating photoreceptor death in several retinal degeneration experimental set up.
**Growth is necessary in the eye for wellness, but it is always stimulated by damage (ROS) and limited by regeneration. So how does the eye accomplish this?
**
Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), the major polyunsaturated fatty acid in the retina. It protects cultured retina photoreceptors from apoptosis induced by oxidative stress and promotes photoreceptor differentiation. It works with dopamine to accomplish this task. So why does the retina have more DHA? It is the one part of the human that has ability to make DHA from EPA. Investigations have shown that eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) is the booster to making more DHA in a complex photochemical dance using the side chains of tyrosine and UV light. EPA is a metabolic precursor to DHA and it has been shown to have an effect on retinal neurons so that they could activate Δ6 desaturase (FADS2), which introduces the last double bond in DHA biosynthetic pathway to metabolize EPA to DHA. In the retina, because of melatonin and dopamine EPA is able to promote photoreceptor differentiation and rescued photoreceptors from oxidative stress-induced apoptosis from light through its elongation and desaturation to DHA. Experiments have now shown in humans for the first time, that isolated retinal neurons can synthesize DHA in culture.

This increase of DHA is important in running the SCN faster than all other circadian clocks.

Taking so many mg's of oral melatonin for so many years was literally sucking the life out of me. The supplement guru's won't tell you this though. One more reason of knowing better and doing better here. Looking forward to the next Blog coming out! Thanks, Jack!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T00:08:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-prescribe-oral-melatonin.17286/post-189132>

you're going to be an expect real soon..............

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T00:10:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-prescribe-oral-melatonin.17286/post-189134>

Eicosanoids are derived from either omega-3 (ω-3) or omega-6 (ω-6) fatty acids. In general, the ω-6 eicosanoids are pro-inflammatory; ω-3s are much less so. The amounts and balance of these fats in a person's diet will affect the body's eicosanoid-controlled functions, with effects on cardiovascular disease, triglycerides, blood pressure, and arthritis. A biogenic amine made by sunlight controls their formation and was my major discovery of this hack 11 yrs ago.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T00:30:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-prescribe-oral-melatonin.17286/post-189139>

The eicosanoids from AA generally promote inflammation. Those from [GLA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gamma-linolenic_acid) (_via_ DGLA) and from EPA are generally less inflammatory, or inactive, or even anti-inflammatory. Dietary ω-3 and GLA counter the inflammatory effects of AA's eicosanoids in three ways – displacement, [competitive inhibition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Competitive_inhibitor) and direct counteraction. Dietary ω-3 decreases tissue concentrations of AA. DGLA and EPA compete with AA for access to the cyclooxygenase and lipoxygenase enzymes.

The arachidonic acid cascade proceeds somewhat differently in the brain. Why? Light does not act the same in all tissues because mass and energy,while the same........act differently because of shapes and sizes of molecules. [Neurohormones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neurohormone), [neuromodulators](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neuromodulator) or [neurotransmitters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neurotransmitter) act as first messengers in the brain.

They activate phospholipidase to release AA from [neuron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neuron) cell membranes as a free fatty acid. During its short lifespan, free AA may affect the activity of the neuron's [ion channels](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ion_channel) and [protein kinases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protein_kinase). Or it may be metabolized to form eicosanoids, [epoxyeicosatrienoic acids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epoxyeicosatrienoic_acid) (EETs), [neuroprotectin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neuroprotectin) D or various [endocannabinoids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannabinoid#Endogenous_Cannabinoids) ([anandamide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anandamide) and its analogs.)

In humans, DHA is mostly obtained from the diet or very small amounts may be converted in small amounts from [eicosapentaenoic acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eicosapentaenoic_acid) (EPA, 20:5, ω-3) via [docosapentaenoic acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Docosapentaenoic_acid) (DPA, 22:5 ω-3) as an intermediate. Only 1-3% can be converted this way. This synthesis had been thought to occur through an elongation step followed by the action of Δ4-[desaturase](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Desaturase) enzyme. It is now considered more likely that DHA is biosynthesized via a C24 intermediate followed by [beta oxidation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beta_oxidation) in our [peroxisomes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peroxisome). Thus, EPA is twice elongated, yielding 24:5 ω-3, then desaturated to 24:6 ω-3, then shortened to DHA (22:6 ω-3) via [beta oxidation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beta_oxidation). This pathway is known as **Sprecher's shunt.**
**
It seems this shint works a lot better when our Vitamin D3 levels and melatonin levels are optimized. Funny.......both of those things require sun exposure and grounding to the Earth's magnetic field. Nature is queer like that.**

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T00:52:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-prescribe-oral-melatonin.17286/post-189141>

**Docosanoids** are like eicasanoids but they come from DHA specifically. They are [signaling molecules](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipid_signaling) made by [oxygenation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxygenation_\(medicine\)) of twenty-two-carbon essential fatty acids (EFAs), especially DHA. They include [resolvins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resolvins) of the D series and the [docosatrienes](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Docosatriene&action=edit&redlink=1) such as neuro[protectin D1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protectin_D1) and [maresin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maresin) 1. Maresins can turn a macrophage from inflammatory to anti-inflammatory almost immediately. Might light be the switch that turns this on? Docosatrienes contain conjugated triene structures generated from DHA as a defining feature. The [protectins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protectin) comprise docosatrienes and resolvins of the D series that are both neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory. [Neuroprotectin D1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neuroprotectin) is formed in r_etinal pigment epithelial_ cells when they are confronted with oxidative stress, in the brain during experimental stroke, and in the human brain from Alzheimer's disease patients as well as in human brain cells in culture. Neuroprotectin D1 displays potent anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective bioactivity. **Blue light destroys DHA and melatonin at the same time.**

These substances are extremely potent. Neuroprotectin D1 and the 17S series resolvins are potent into the picomolar range.

[Neurofurans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neurofuran) are docosanoids formed nonenzymatically by free radical mediated peroxidation of DHA

Bazan NG. Neuroprotectin D1 (NPD1): a DHA-derived mediator that protects brain and retina against cell injury-induced oxidative stress. _Brain Pathol._ 2005 Apr;15(2):159-66.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21452466](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21452466). Accessed August 9, 2012.

Bazan NG, Molina MF, Gordon WC. Docosahexaenoic acid signalolipidomics in nutrition: significance in aging, neuroinflammation, macular degeneration, Alzheimer's, and other neurodegenerative diseases. _Annu Rev Nutr._ 2011 Aug 21;31:321-51.

Lukiw WJ, Bazan NG. Inflammatory, apoptotic, and survival gene signaling in Alzheimer's disease. A review on the bioactivity of neuroprotectin D1 and apoptosis. _Mol Neurobiol._ 2010 Aug;42(1):10-6.

.

### TerrierMom — 2015-12-23T00:57:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-184777>

Deep breath and jumping into the Optimal Journal pool. Those of you who met me on the Kruse Cruise know that I was looking for a community when I chanced upon jackkruse.com - and boy howdy did I get that in spades here! I did a lot of posting early on in the forums (and was doing the best health wise the more connected I was) but never started a journal. I am a 49-year old female, married 24 years to the most wonderful man I could ever dream of, who joined me in my journey immediately. I'm the reader and researcher and he is happy to go along with my plans with respect to our health.

and a shout-out to all I met on the cruise - I went on it to reconnect and get re-inspired to resume my journey after some slight detours, and it had just that effect on me. It was truly amazing to meet JK in person, his wonderful wife, and all the members who joined us. I learned from absolutely each and every one of them and hope we have the chance to get members together again, often. I will be there if I can!

Reader of Jack's blog since 2012 and eating Paleo, then epi-paleo as the blog progressed. Definitely more DHA now than ever, though! A fair amount of it is raw as I adore sashimi and oysters. I eat seafood 1-3 times daily and crave it like I used to crave a juicy steak (which was A LOT). In a 2012 consult with Jack, he told me a lot of things I am only just now understanding and implementing. Thank God he can be patient if he sees you are not giving up on yourself......

In 2012 we started our journey, I dropped 70 lbs (down from 265) and hubby dropped 55 to a low of about 160, over about a 6- month period. This was by changing diet and improving sleep to begin with. Can't say I haven't had dietary indiscretions since then and have bumped up 20 lbs, hubby as well. Still, I can see my collarbone AND my feet, and can shop in the Misses' department instead of the "plus size" exile department - all major WINS in my book. I went from borderline high BP and T2D to low-ish BP and rock solid blood sugar within about 60 days. At first, I jumped into CT but never truly committed to it and so need to make it part of my life again. But more on my plans to change later.

**Current environment (light, water, magnetism) hacks**: Location -Southern CA (Orange County) EMF hellhole to be sure. We've had our 20G magnetico for about a year now. Bedroom was blacked out in 2012 with blackout shades, removal of most devices, and covering anything else (located far away from us) that had a little indicator light with black electrical tape - sleep improved dramatically, and immediately. Also amber bulbs in the bedroom - our retreat when the sun goes down. Blue blocking filters on iPhones (which I try to limit use of, getting better at that, and NEVER hold to my head). Blue blocking filter on the TV up here which is far from our bed. F.lux on computer screens (I am to the point where orange looks so natural I constantly check to be sure it is set on the nighttime setting!). Blue blockers at night after sunset - (this part is new, I have not always paid "tight attention to light" as I should have). Found some for hubby's office that have a "reader mode" that blocks most blue light, he uses that feature constantly.

Sun: For whatever reason I am only now clueing in to the fact that I need to get myself out in the sun and I do that. I work from home when I am not on the road, so I have a lot of control of my time and environment that many of you would kill for, so I owe it to you and to me to actually make the most of it. Bare minimum - I get the dogs outside for a walk of at least 20 min twice a day between 8-10 and 4-7. Sometimes more often, and longer. I have a great lounge chair in the back patio (townhome life) where I can get morning sun also, taking paper reading material out there. Working on correcting my vision so I do not need reading glasses - I know the sun needs to get to my retina so sometimes I just take a break out on the sun so I can get that. I'll wear a bikini top and shorts - I don't have the requisite privacy for nude sunbathing (though I am still considering it... heck, I can hear when my neighbors are home....) Note - was myopic since 2nd grade (convinced that reading books in bed with a flashlight from kindergarten was the culprit). Had lasik surgery about 15 years ago now and started needing reading glasses 3 years ago and depend on them now, though, am seeing some improvement.

So that's light, water, magnetism, sun. I drink Pellegrino like it's Diet Coke (had a HUGE addiction to that back in the day). I avoid drinking the fluoridated CA water which includes iced tea while eating out in restaurants..... I charge water on the magnetico or a magnet pad on the countertop for extra boost to structure.

HEALTH GOALS: Believe it or not, weight loss is not the main goal for me - it is getting my labs to look good. When they look good, I figure the rest will come. Struggling with a chronically elevated (gulp) hsCRP (I will post recent labs soon). I am scared as hell about what that means for me and I want it reversed. I have never been pregnant.... have apple shape..... live in EMF land....stress.... cancer history of various types in family..... so, I would like to do what I can to AVOID CANCER. That may include moving, (still wrapping my head around that and planning some Yucatan vacations in the meantime). I know I present as dehydrated on the cellular level, I have an upside down PG/E2 ratio and all that bad stuff that I would like to get better. Struggling with low thyroid, on 30 mg armour daily now. Hoping Mssr Soleil will fix that for me as I would dearly love to NOT have to take medication. The only supplements I take are Sun Chlorella in the morning, and Mg Malate 2 hours before bed (without which, I would be constipated in a day or two!).

Current health issues: Sometimes my heart beats fast, like 100-120 bpm, for no apparent reason (Ca efflux from crap environment?). On at least 2 occasions (over 2 years ago now) I woke with my heart pounding so hard you could hear it cutting out my breath when I breathed. Sleep study (wore monitor) showed no sleep apnea. My resting heart rate is 75 on a good day so it's always a little fast, but sometimes it is 100 bpm and "throbby" feeling where I can feel it anywhere my body is touching something (feet, hands, butt, legs). It causes a real anxious feeling but does not feel like a panic attack? It can last a couple of hours. It usually happens in the evening but can happen any time of day. Most prevalent last fall Sept-Nov (light change thing?). Now, I still sometimes feel unsettled and when I check my heart rate it may be "fast" 100-110 when sitting doing nothing but not "throbby" feeling so I can try to just ignore it, but it has an uneasy feeling that comes with it that is hard to ignore. No clue what THAT is all about. MD thought it was the compounded thyroid I was one, maybe I got a bad batch that spiked me? changed to another form and it went away but came back. Sooooo no clue what is happening. Saw a cardio and wore a Holter for a day and no arrhythmias noted or anything he was worried about or wanted to medicate me for.... So, would like that to go away and stay away. On the scale of life issues, people deal with lots worse.

Health background: I have all my organs! Nobody has taken my gallbladder or appendix or ovaries from me.... I still have cycles though the last 3 years they have varied from 21 to 40 days, norm of about 26-33 with a few outliers here and there (they used to be 28 days on the nose like clockwork), always heavy and horribly crampy and that's just something I live with, though, fixing that would be nice! and I understand it is possible.

Weight/health background: Always a bit on the pudgy side as a teen, like, 15 pounds overweight. PAcked it on at age 26 when I went to graduate school (40 lbs in a year then slow steady creep 10 lbs a year until I reached the mid-200s). Lost about 60 lbs Atkins-style (low carb, lots of shakes, bars, and fake food) in 1999. Smoker age 19-25. When I quit smoking, I slid right into a clinical depression and carbed my way out of it, also had a little help from an SSRI (forget which one!) that I endeavored to wean from within a year. Horrible case of pneumonia in 2000 which was only kicked with Levaquin (a quinone - oops, didn't know better). Inflamed sinuses and allergies since my mid-20s which meant at least every May and December, like clockwork, I'd get colds, sinus infections and bronchitis (sometimes) and antibiotics to cure them. Got off that treadmill when I changed my diet though - allergies are rare, and I think I've had 2 sinus infections in the last 3 years (instead of 7 or 8). Cortisol is low in the morning with a spike in the early evening, need to retest and see if I have fixed that (did the salivary 4-panel test).

Mental health: While I have not had a major depressive episode since 1999, I am a real Type A personality who wants it MY WAY OR THE HIGHWAY. Yeah I know I have work to do there and I have come a long way in letting go and chilling out. I am married to a very chill guy and have some great friends who have helped me change. My brain spins when left unchecked so meditation is torture - I have done the sensory deprivation chamber/epsom salts soak, and, while I really like it, it is torture for my brain because I have nothing to read or watch or listen to except what's whirling in my brain. Told that to my NeurOptimal feedback trainer and he was not surprised from looking at my brain wave patterns!!! I have had bouts of anxiety/panic attacks that almost made me agoraphobic as you just want to curl up somewhere you feel safe, and avoid the place it happened. But I power through it. Scary as hell though. Those have rarely occurred since I got my blood sugar under control.

**So - part of community is keeping accountable. So here is what I am working on for me, right now.**
1) I need to give up my wine glass. I did not drink any alcohol for the first 9 months of changing my way of eating and when I picked it back up, weight loss slid to a crawl and then a stop. It dehydrates me, which does not help my goals. But, wine is part of hanging out with friends and enjoying life! I enjoy wine and have a few wine club subscriptions, like to take trips to wine country with friends, but... need to kick it to the curb, until I am fixed and can handle it. (not an alcoholism issue, does not run in my family, and we are talking only a few glasses a week but it is NOT HELPING so it needs to just stop.)

2) I need to work more sunlight into my day on a more regular basis, and really crank it up on the weekends when I am not distracted by my work day responsibilities. I need to be VIGILANT and even MILITANT about avoiding blue light. So far, I just open all the windows and avoid turning on lights as much as I can, have replaced CFLs with incandescent amber bulbs from Lumesty.com, and looking at getting some HUE for my can lights in the main area of the house (but that's a lotta light bulbs.....), and the aforementioned amber bedroom retreat.

3) I need to get with the CT. Part of me is being a lazy ass and waiting for my QUANTLET to arrive instead of committing to CT. But here are the options I know that I could do (some more easily than others):

-   face dunks to start of course (I keep a covered bowl of cold water in the fridge for this now)
-   CT in my tub (but it is shallow and does not fully immerse)
-   Use CFB/GB (I have one! Have not locked and loaded it with its ice packs though, my freezer is full of seafood..... need to make a shelf for my ice packs). It would be easy to wear at my computer here at home, I have no excuse really except my own laziness.
-   Pool at my parent's house which is not heated. Even if only for a few minutes at first - it should be around 50 these days. She only lives 2 miles from me. Right? I can hear you now.
-   Pacific Ocean, 3 miles from me. Now, I know if you don't have a handy Pacific Ocean you are pissed at me for not getting into it. But.... PARKING. Right now, the hassle factor is very very high. So I want to start by doing the things that are easier and making a habit and then get into the ocean at least once a week (so I tell myself). Also, the beach I live by is also known as Surf City -hard to find a place to immerse and not get pounded by waves (which is most days) - there is a great spot 10 miles south but again, hassle factor....

4) I need to work on my brain. I did 6 sessions of NeuroOptimal feedback training (I think I heard Rub Sal mention it as worthwhile). It was like a big drink of water for my brain, bliss inducing, and I **loved** it and want to do it a LOT more. I feel like it really helped focus me and it was uber relaxing, which I could use help learning how to do. Cool old cat who is also a psychiatrist?cologist? was the Master Trainer. I was making some progress.... I need to be able to focus for work, and to destress..... I have the means to purchase a machine for myself through my consulting company, so I can run my own sessions and also for my family and friends. No plans to make a living out of it, just to share it. So.... checking with my accountant and will spring for that pretty soon, I think. Very excited about that. (if you live near me...or are in town.... I am willing to let you try it too)!

5) have a black light bulb in a downstairs lamp for UV. Need to source a red UFO. I know neither replace the sun but on a day like today where it is rainy.... nice to have. I have some serious healing to do to crank down my hsCRP. A little red light might be the ticket to help get me there faster.

6)last but not least, spend more time in the Forum and less time on FB. I love the Quantum Health group and like the ability to browse there and there are some truly funny and epic threads (banana lady! bee guy! salty jack x 1000!) but, connecting here more with people and staying accountable to my journal will take me farther. I was sooo happy on the cruise that I 'knew' people from this forum - made all the difference to know about their journey.

Ok. Now to go find labs to upload for your dissection pleasure. I promise I will not write 25-page posts every time (verbosity is a vice of mine, I admit).

### TerrierMom — 2015-12-31T01:10:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-185134>

So far.... My grades
1) wine - F. Holidays. There's only A or F possible. I don't get drunk (I like my wits about me) but like a glass during a celebration. Jan 2 I commit!

2) more sun : B. I am getting sun every day but not as long as I would like (but do get 20 min 2x daily between 8-11 and 4-7, sometimes longer). The B is because I am getting out there daily on purpose. Now to increase time to an A

3) CT- so far, D. The most I'm doing is being outside in tank and shorts in 45-50 weather. Face dunks start tomorrow morning!

4) brain/stress - C. I did start a mindfulness meditation thing through an app called Headspace. So am doing something daily now.... Maybe that should score higher as before I didn't really do anything. I need to use my emWave and work the HRV too.

5) black/red light. A. Got my UFO 660 nm and hitting it 15 min in the morning after my sun/dog walks and breakfast. Black light in living room where I hang out and read. Plus, sun.

6) forum time: C. Off to scare up my labs and post them and catch upon the folks I'm following!

And true confessions: I worry about things (bad trait) especially health related for me and others. If I read too much I can start thinking "hey I have that!". I think today they call it cyber-chondria!

I know I am LR and have low thyroid (no Hashis though).

My current worry- may it turn out to be nothing....

Probably 10 years ago I had a lower back molar root canal and the tooth next to it could not be saved. So I have a bridge across the hole, with a crown on both sides connecting it. On my dental X-ray last month (I go for 6-month cleanings all my life) they saw a white area of denser bone. Had me back for a panoramic X-ray. Sure enough there's a dense spot there, no real form/shape to it, in the jaw under the hole and towards the forward crown. And you can see it is pushing down on the trigeminal nerve there.

Dentist showed it to an oral surgeon and I see him 1/4 to hear the diagnosis. Yes my jaw pokes out a bit there but no real symptoms unless I'm imagining them Maybe. No pain anyway...

So if it were an infection it'd be a dark spot. Since its brighter and whiter it's denser. Hoping to hear, my bite is weird from the bridge and pressure of the bite making denser bone there? That's the least scary reason I can think of. Otherwise I'm not thinking anything good. The only thing that grows on its own is cancer....

Will post what dentist says. And my X-ray film too, when I get it from him.

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-07T01:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-185586>

Ok some things of interest. First off, my dental panoramic xray. Note the white mass below where I have the bridge. Dentist said this week it could be a few things, none of which are likely to be serious or have much they need to do. I got a referral to an oral surgeon who will take a 3D type of image to get a better view of what that white area is. Then, we go from there.

Second of all I have finally scanned some labs for folks to pick apart. I have some from March 2015 (did a VAP, actually did it in Feb but one of the tubes got compromised so they retested in March) and also a Cleveland Heart Lab workup.

I know I have high hsCRP (and it actually rises from March to April) but I am insulin resistant so.... maybe not surprising? But the thing that kind of freaks me out is the high B12. WTf? I know my BUN/Creatinine ratio and O6:O3 ratio don't look good. I will have to get restested since I upped my spring water and DHA uptake and started getting some sun. The D is lower than I would like (I have seen mine in the 70s since I started this journey, so not sure what caused them to crash). Anyway. Low Testosterone..... no surprise there.... PG/E2 not looking great either. None of it a surprise if you look at me! Hopefully my numbers that I will get this quarter, will show an improvement. And then... who knows what it will look like when I get my mitts on a Quantlet.

Off to upload my labs. Feel free to discuss if you have any insights.... I am here to learn.

Lasik: the imagery created by a laser edge allowing life to live in the shadows of light and darkness leading people to ignorance and apathy because altering the lens deftly destroys their ability to understand and communicate even on simple levels.

Thought is created by light……….it says hello to us from the outside.
Thought is absurd by what it seeks, but is great by what it is.
Light is life’s correlated novelty expanding the adjacent possible by touching everything deftly.

Light, alien light let in by a laser beam is a modern theme leading to man's inability to communicate with man. Does an ophthalmologist see the extent of this light communication as part of the camera or clock in your eye? Might it be done with a superficial knowledge of light? Lasik is planned only its most superficial and "commercial" level. This allows us to create more neon signs for corrupt natural thought. Surgical creation for the ultimate upscale by allowing someone else to usurp your mind? There is no serious understanding today in healthcare of the eye because there is no serious communication with nature and her light alphabet - This leads to people talking without speaking - hearing without listening. No one dares take the risk of reaching out to solve for X.

Words tell most people when meaningful communication fails, when the reality is that a lack of sunlight and a lot of blue light destroy your choices to think by robbing you of dopamine to leave you untuned to nature. The only sound your here is silence or the meme's of those who know exactly what they are doing to the eye clock.

When you dance a laser over your eye surface you making the biggest gamble of your life. Your dopamine gear hangs in the balance of the hand of the surgeon.........and no one knows until years alter how good he was. If you sense you are not willing to go beyond the superficial, but merely to accept the world around you then you know the surgeon had a bad day at your expense.

The grand illusion comes into play in the beginning, as the patient begins to have revelations, a feeling, a vision, that something is amiss. Hormones, fatigue, bad sleep...........and that awakes them to a new deeper reality from their superficial stupor, and shadowed world. A simple laser could creates such shadows for a life time that not even the best metronome can reverse. It is at this time they rise, awaken, and begin to walk alone into the light, to see how sun light really works in life. They begin to try to tempt others out of the cave of ignorance and look beyond the surface of things, but their "words like silent raindrops" fall. Why? The Quantlet is "a helper" bringing light to meet water and magnetism to retune you to nature. Hello darkness my old friend. Where is your partner, the sun? What happens when you bury the sun from your RPE? You get down with the sickness............You'll have some trouble living with these changes. You’ve got to work at living today when 99% of the globe works at dying daily; denial of the truth will lead you into blind alleys. Let go of your paradigm, accept what nature designed, and embrace your ultimate potential.

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-07T21:04:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-185687>

Jack says in Time 6 this morning (bolds and emphasis is mine, not his)
"**Not everybody can assimilate light in the optimal fashion**. We have various surfaces that react to a combination of light frequencies. Depending on the light spectrum light that penetrates into our surfaces between 0.1 mm to a great depth and the magic of light happens in the top layers. If your top layers cannot convert this energy well due to a badly composed cell membranes, lack of melanin, or faulty tunneling in your respiratory chain, bad photochemical reactions happen fast because of how the _photoelectric works fundamentally._ Most people forget that the gut is highly sensitive to light information because of how the microbiome releases light. **The gut takes its lead from the RPE in the eye. The connection to both is the vagus nerve between the brain and gut.** Bacteria release 5000 times more light than our cells do. They release this light when we eat. On a relative basis our gut sees massive amounts of light frequencies as soon as we eat something. In this way, with any problem that develops in the gut, your reaction to light will be different. There could be tons of reasons why alternative reactions to light occurs in this fashion. Light always plays a role and how light works and when is not well understood. When frequencies are altered for any reason, disruptions of clock genes can happen in no time by tiny things most people are not aware of. When bad reactions occur while living an outdoor life in perfect light, you know something is wrong in your environment for your particular cellular costume. That is your cells talking to you! The key is, do you pay attention to these trends properly? If you miss it, it is likely because your dopamine levels in your eye and brain are sub optimal for some reason and you need to be aware of it."

And that is what I am trying to understand. Part of understanding Jack is - here is how it is SUPPOSED to work. But if you are broken, some things are not working and maybe I can not assimilate UV light like an unbroken person. Finding the pieces to the puzzle. If this, then that. If this does not work, then you won't get that. So what to fix first? In the mean time as I struggle to understand I can make the changes to get the light, water, and magnetism right. And I am far from living an outdoor life in perfect light. So I have to think about all my reactions in the context of the environment I am allowing. And the non-light part of nnEMF is part of my environment I have not tried very hard to hack - yet. Meter time. Anyway back to Jack's blog.

"People who get strong UV light signals via the RPE also curtail their eating easily. The more connected people are to light and to magnetic flux of the Earth, the less food electrons they need and this controls hunger and appetite easily. " I say YES YES YES to this. So if I notice that more sun = less appetite, wouldn't that indicate that I AM able to assimilate UV light.... I hope that is a GOOD sign... I have so many strikes against me!

" Lack of UV/IR light with excessive blue light at night with high color temperatures causes a massive increase **in ocular ROS**. This lowers dopamine. [Methylene blue is an electron donor](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23806754) to cytochrome 3 that can help when your RPE is trashed. Many time my patients who have had eye surgery get told about this bio-hack. " I went to the link but it's abstract not full text. I bet Jason will post it though (he rocks). I am interested in this hack, as an eye surgery patient....

"_Taking exogenous glutathione when UV excitation is not present will lead to more apoptosis of dopaminergic damage."_ I do have a functional/integrative medicine doc who gets a lot of stuff, and who can do Meyer's cocktail IVs and glutathione IVs..... I just am not 100% sure they get the context of when it should happen or not. And if I am not sure myself....I don't want it in me. Besides, I have some sun to go get.

"Making cooling part of your life is elevating dopamine in your CNS so you can make meteoric choices in your life. Mediocrity limits the time we have on the planet. Dopamine reverses a Rx for mediocrity. Action allows us to step out of the crowd of average people and dopamine moves to action. Nothing changes a human brain faster. Purple light is the key to making dopamine and blue light destroys it quicker than most imagine." and if I was looking for something to kick me in the ass to get me to fully embrace CT, well, there it is, Jack's boot in my butt. I hear ya, I hear ya. I do recall, that when I was getting in the cold water, there were times I absolutely craved craved craved it. (of course, there were times I could not stand it. Maybe tied to shark week hormones as some of the ladies here have posited?).

Anyway. If I needed any motivation to continue with my resolutions to get more sun, more cold - well, there it is in black and white (or orange-white, from the f.lux on my monitor).

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-07T23:56:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-185700>

Today's alternate lyric inspiration - Roxanne (You don't have to put on the red light). Well, I beg to differ. My lyrics

Roxanne – please go put on the red light
Blue light is bogus, you should never let your eyes see it at night
Roxanne – you should really think about UV
Get that sun at morning – you really want to charge your RPE
Roxanne – please go get in the sun light
Roxanne – please go put out the blue light

(chorus) Roxanne (put on the red light) etc

Sun, I loved ya since I knew, ya
Were so important to the…
Opening of the pharmacy
That is located inside my brain
I know my mind is made up, light’s important in its make up
I told you once and I’ll tell you again, fake blue’s a bad way.

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-08T01:16:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-185704>

Twas the Night Before Quantlet -

(to save this here, since I just posted it in the Quantum Health FB group but I want to save it to my journal..... )

Twas the night before Quantlet and all through the net
Quantum people were checking if the thing’d funded yet.
Contributions were logged at the website with care
In the hopes that their Quantlets soon would be there.

We spent the nights nestled all snug in our beds
With nary a blue light to dance ‘round our heads
And me in my cryohelmet and DH in his tub
Felt like we’d started our own CT club
We focused on light, quantum physics, and matter
While sheeple watched blue lights, ate crap, and got fatter.

When what to our wondering eyes should appear
But a miniature sleigh and eight quantum reindeer
With a white irish driver, with Malbec in his sack
I knew in a moment, I’d just seen Salty Jack!

More rapid than eagles his authors they came
And he whistled, and shouted, and called them by name
“Now! Pollack, now! Becker, now! Al-Khalili and Ling!
On Van Wijk! On Marino! On Ott and Ho (or something)!
To the top of their reading lists! May your ripe wisdom fall!
Now read away, read away, read away, all!”

Then down to the sundeck our Salty Jack flew
With his pack full of Malbec, and good seafood too
As the sunlight beamed down, the feast he laid out
And it was no surprise what our Jack was about.
He stripped down to his skin to get sun on his junk
And sat eating oysters (now who woulda thunk?)

He shared all his wisdom for us to take in
Spending time out on Facebook, and his forum, and then
He got all entangled with those who had vision
To bring health to the willing – the man had a mission

To get out the word, to help us see the light
And the Quantlet can help bring us out of the night
With RubSal (co-inventor), and with scientists too
They thought and developed, using what each of them knew.

And all that development led to its creation:
A device using the power of photobiomodulation!
They knew it would work, there was no need to guess.
They could increase ATP, and lower oxidative stress!

They picked lights from the spectrum: Red, “Black”, blue, and green
To increase human performance like you’ve never seen.
With thermoelectric cooling (something called Peltier)
Extracting heat from skins’ surface – they knew THIS was the way
To combine light and cold, other folks would be jealous
Of the wondrous results Quantlets made on OUR wellness.

Off to Indiegogo, they launched their campaign
And response was amazing!! (from those with a brain).
So now we await as it draws to a close
Anxious to get all the perks that we chose
Waiting for April (and some for September)
We know 2016 is a year to remember.

And I heard Jack exclaim as he drove out of sight
Happy Quantlet to all – but go get real sunlight!

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-08T21:23:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-185789>

Not if you don't make medical claims for it though, right? I cannot wait for my Quantlet. Considering a perk upgrade to join the folks who will get to come meet with you and Rub and get some consults....

I read Time 6, methylene blue with UV/IR light, but, how exactly to implement? More DHA needed methinks. Or popping for the full text of the report to see the dosage they used. I see Methylene blue USP on amazon (nothing available) but this interesting description:

"Methylene blue is currently being investigated as a potential treatment for Alzheimer's by TauRx. But there is also robust evidence that methylene blue enhances mood and cognition in healthy individuals, too. Hundreds of peer-reviewed papers have been published about Methylene Blue's beneficial effects in the central nervous system. Methylene blue putatively enhances cognition through it's pleiotropic neuroprotective effects in the central nervous system, including MAO-A inhibition, amelioration of Tau burden, redox cycling and self-regenerating antioxidant properties. Methylene blue is a potent reduction-oxidation (redox) cycler which enhances mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation by mimicking endogenous electron acceptors like NADH/NADPH. moreover, Methylene blue is capable of auto-oxidation to regenerates itself as an electron acceptor. A deleterious consequence of other antioxidants (e.g., ascorbic acid) is that these species may go on to oxidize cellular components once they have undergone a redox reaction. Though methylene blue has a long track record of safety in humans, the FDA prohibits the human consumption of research chemicals."

Now, I think I heard this somewhere before!!!!!!

The product link is here [http://www.amazon.com/Pure-Methylen...BIBUC/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1452281191&sr=8-](http://www.amazon.com/Pure-Methylene-Pharmaceutical-Grade-Powder/dp/B00WGBIBUC/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1452281191&sr=8-)
1&keywords=USP+methylene+blue

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-13T02:41:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-186121>

Uploading my 23andme results - which I ran through a Genetic Genie program (geneticgenie.org) to get the attached info. I didn't see much to worry about. If my environment is right then this stuff shouldn't be expressing itself into issues, right?
The little notations in pencil in the margins are whether DH has the same SNPs as me or not (since I was looking them all up at one point, but I gave up). Anyone with insights into this stuff, let me know.

On the Detox profile, I am heterozygous for:
CYP1A2 164 A>C, CYP1B1 L432V, CYP1B1 R48G, CYP2D6 S468T and 100C >T; SOD2 A16V. and homozygous for NAT2 I114T. and NAT2 K268R.

On the Methylation profile, I am heterozygous for:
COMT V158M and COMT H62H; MAO-A R297R, MTRR A66G, MTRR A664A, and homozygous for VDR Bsm, and CBS A360A.

For what it's worth

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-13T19:49:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-186196>

Be very careful with labs and labels..........why?

No measurement is very accurate. you need a battery of them constantly to improve the probability that the story they tell you is really close to reality............

Few do that.

Measuring is never accurate says Heisenberg's uncertainty principle........only biology and their experiments says they need accuracy in a RCT. Feynman in his lectures makes the key point no experiments is reproducible.........everything is based upon probability in nature and the human brain is horrible at understanding trends but good at picking them up by design to take advantage of her design. This is the basis of Blink by Malcolm Gladwell
How deep is this? Human “brain time,” or quantum time as I put it, is intrinsically subjective. Try this exercise: Put a book down and go look in a mirror. Now move your eyes back and forth, so that you’re looking at your left eye, then at your right eye, then at your left eye again. When your eyes shift from one position to the other, they take a small amount of time to move and land on the other location don't they? You observed it in the mirror. But here’s the kicker in this experiment: you never see your eyes move do you? There’s no evidence of any gaps in your perception; there is no darkened stretches like bits of blank film. Why? Electrons can act at a spooky distance is the short answer. Their existence allows us to create reality with light. Much of what you see has been edited out by your brain circuits by altering photoelectric flows. Your brain has taken a complicated scene of eyes darting back and forth and recut it as a simple movie you call life or perception: your eyes stare straight ahead. Where did the missing moments of time go?

The question raises a fundamental issue about our conscious state: how much of what we perceive exists outside of us and how much is a product of our minds? So when you get those labs what do they really mean to you and to others................Time is created by light and electrons. Time is a dimension not like any other that you normally experience, fixed and defined down to its tiniest increments: millennia to microseconds, aeons to quartz oscillations. Why? No one experiences electrons and light frequencies in their fundamental form. Things on the smallest scale to not act or appears as things do on the larger scales that you live your life in. Yet the data in the experiment I gave you above rarely matches our reality as we watch ourselves in the mirror just as Feynman and Heisenberg predicts. The rapid eye movements in the mirror, known as saccades, aren’t the only things that get edited out of your perception of reality. How much more does your brain filter? That amount is tied to your dopamine level............and melatonin level and many other levels all fundamentally tied back to when light hist tyrosine electrons in the aromatic ring. That is what the Time series is all about.

It also turns out that is what your life boils down to no matter how hard or counterintuitive it seems to be.........nature is based upon the absurd principles of light and electrons.

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-14T20:14:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-186299>

So, time for an update on my goals for improvement
1) wine - I have to admit to a sip of some very nice wine DH ordered, but, just the taste was enough to savor and I did not indulge further (nor feel the need to).
2) Sun - consistently getting my sun morning and afternoon with the dogs on a leash in shorts/tank, and most days getting 30-45 min in less than that out on the back patio. I do this whether it is cloudy or not!
3) CT - why oh why can't I make myself do this. After this post I will go get my face-dunk setup going (Cold water in a covered bowl in the fridge makes for easy dunking and saves water....drought you know!)
4) brain/stress- have not practiced meditation lately. Another one that is hard to make myself quiet down and do. I will do that right after this and use my emwave too while I do it.
5)black/red light - I get about 15 min every other day or so in the mornint with my red UFO. Black in the evening when I'm downstairs hanging out on the sofa.
6) forum time - I am getting here every day to check in on people and go down new rabbit holes

On the sun part - just ordered my tan-through swimsuit (naked is not my bag, baby, at least, not right now!) - and if it is too small then I can always just wear the sarong in the back yard and tan through that. Will be interested in seeing how getting sun where the sun normally don't shine will affect me.

Things that caught my interest in the forum of late: Wim Hof breathing techniques discussed quite a bit - but I thought hyperventilation was bad??? Reading about him though does stimulate my interest in CT. Will spend some time in the CT part of the forum for inspiration .... immersing myself in any topic puts it top of mind and motivates me. So, off to marinate in CT threads.

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-15T22:45:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-186403>

Dog issues take up some of my health-related mental bandwidth. I have a little terrier mix Merida (supposedly Porteguese Podengo pequeno) who has a regurgitation issue. Not vomiting - vomiting is the major peristalsis and heaving from the gut. This is just regurgitation - she opens her mouth and out it comes. Sometimes with a little force! When we first got her a few years ago now she did this CONSTANTLY and it seemed to be a "little dog excitement" thing. Which it partially is, for sure. Going to a new place, excitement of new people coming into the house, high energy. We thought it was her food and when we got her on a grain free kibble she pretty much stopped doing it. But at some point (not sure when or what we were feeding her) she started up again. Doc put her on metaclopramide, twice a day, 2 hours prior to eating. This is supposed to help gut motility. One vet listened to her belly and said her pyloric sphincter did not open and close as often as it should, causing delay in empyting her stomach contents.

So if she took her meds she was pretty much OK with an occasional "glurp". Usually she does a little belch in her mouth after finishing her food. Its when she regurgitates a puddle of mucous, or foamy bile, that I get really concerned. Or if she brings up food HOURS after feeding.

Sometimes, a well-meaning dog-sitter might decide she wasn't throwing up so she did not need her meds. Last November this is what happened when we were out of town for a week. Well that stuff takes a few days to build up in your system from what I have been told, so it takes a few days to remove itself - and sure enough when we got her back she was throwing up daily until a couple days in, she regurgitated 5-6 times in a few hours and I took her to the vet. We have been recovering from that SINCE THEN. She got a shot of Cerenia (anti-emetic, anti-nausea) though she still regurgitated later that night but it seemed to stop. She also got some Carafate to soothe her throat. So now it was - pill 2 hrs before eating, carafate liquid (to coat her system irritated from the throwing up) 1 hr before eating, then food. Twice a day. Quite a schedule but we kept it religiously to help our furry baby. It does hamper the social life a bit!

Put her on "chicken and rice" bland diet (she does not usually get rice). Regurgitation largely stopped but then she had an infected toe where the nail got all funky and impacted. Doc cleared that infection out and then she had oral abx which REALLY upset her tummy. So we added back the carafate again (which we usually administer for 7-10-14 days post a major regurgitation frequency), and so forth. And, vet gave her an abx shot instead, easier on the stomach. Of COURSE I know her gut is in a bad way at this point!

One vet I go to is Dr Coghlan, author of the PaleoPet book, advocating a raw and unstarchy diet for dogs. I went to see him (he originally diagnosed her pyloric sphincter and his office took care of her paw). He explained sometimes the regurgitation is a reaction from a bad gut - starchy stuff making a lot of gas in the bowel which delays the stomach emptying and even forces contents back up. So I decided it was finally time to get her transitioned to a raw diet. But then, she got a bout of bacterial colitis, which we traced back to the salmon "pill pockets" she got (vet also said there had been a rash of this with salmon-related treats). Gotta love bloody diarrhea right?!

So, after that, feeding her the purina gastroenteritis vet wet food for a week or so, mixed with probiotic from the vet, and lightly cooked veggies (broccoli slaw, cauliflower, brassicas, squash, etc). Slowly transitioning to a 95% meat wet food (EVO beef), before we graduate to raw. Colitis cleared up in about 36 hours yay. But regurgitation is still an issue.

For the most part she makes it through the morning but 1-2 hours after dinner she will grace us with a little pile of food. (and I have detailed notes going back to November so I can spot trends and keep track to speak to the vet). I can tell when she is about to hoik it up because she will sit up and sit very still, then up it comes silently, her mouth opens and there it is. (which of course she tries to lick up). SIGH. I am really really hoping that the diet change will cure this once and for all. In the last few days, she has regurgitation both after breakfast AND dinner. Vet appt next Thursday to discuss....... or earlier if I don't mind sitting in the waiting room for hours to be worked in.

Then, my other dog, Odie started to have an issue last night!! Also a tiny terrier (scotty/yorkie mix we think or maybe cockapoo). Not a single health problem with him normally, but last night oh how restless he was (destroying my sleep too of course) as he was up and downstairs off and on between 3 and 6 am. Not normal for him to poop in the night but he did and man was his belly making LOUD gurgly sounds. Sure enough in the morning I could see him poop barely-digested veggies after a normal firm poop, which I had to hose off the patio.... but he wanted his food, so that's good at least. I think he got too many veggies last night or they were not cooked enough for him to digest. He also is getting the probiotic the vet recommends.

Why am I writing all this here. Well heck, it's my journal. My dogs are a source of great joy in my life, every single day we walk and play with toys and snuggle. Normally, they sleep through the night like little rocks (yes they share our bed). Unless they are sick. In which case DH sleeps blissfully through the night while Mama sleeps with one ear open for the furbabies and tends to their needs. Needless to say sleep last night was horrendous. Maybe I am delirious!

I am writing this because, despite the great joy they bring me, they also bring stress. (or, I allow it to, I know stress is self-induced reaction to circumstances!) I have SUCH a hard time when they do not feel well! I cannot imagine being a parent to a human child with health issues, so my hat is off to all the moms and dads out there. I have such a hard time when my human loved ones have health challenges too. I wish I had my Zengar Neuroptimal Feedback trainer so I could meditate and just chill out. So this is the kind of STRESS I am working on, worrying about things that I cannot do anything about. (of course I can change my dogs diet, exercise, and medication, and take them to the vet, but I can't make them feel any better faster). I have a huge empathy for my furry babies when they are sick to the point of me feeling unwell too. And of course, we all know, we cannot change our humans who won't take good advice on light, water, and magnetism.

SO. I need to get better at self-talking my way through dog illness, and human illness. I have a friend who is really good at talking me down. Her mom has Alzheimers and her inlaws have health challenges also (colon cancer in recovery now, and perhaps the beginnings of dementia). Really, my problems with dogs pale in comparison with the health issues she deals with daily but yet she patiently listens and says reassuring things that I need to learn to tell myself (God bless her!). Like, "you are doing everything you can for them, let nature take its course, they will feel better soon). The bad voice in my head insists on going down the rabbit hole of BUT WHAT IF THEY DON'T? WHAT IF THEY GET WORSE OR DIE?! (shouting out the bad voice is something I work at for sure. It sure is mouthy at times!) I have already lost 2 dogs in my life. I have gotten through it. I will get through it again. Still.... I need to be able to chill out after I have taken all the action I can take, and refocus on work. I guess that is really why I am writing this, because it's been a rough morning with the pups and I am feeling too distracted to work. And oddly sad. Partial hangover from all the too-young cancer deaths in the news. I don't think I can watch anything with Alan Rickman in it for a lonnnnng time, I think I would cry.

I just checked and it is day 14 of my cycle so THAT is not the reason for feeling irrationally sad (then again if my cycle starts tomorrow - and it could - that would explain a LOT of how this week has gone for me mood wise). It is also VERY OVERCAST today and I could REALLY USE SOME FUCKING SUNLIGHT ABOUT NOW. I go out anyway to get whatever UV manages to make it through the clouds. Anyway. I feel better having written it down. For what it's worth, doggies still are playful with their toys and they are not moaning about. They have slept peacefully all morning. I am more worked up than they are.

And with that, I take myself back to my work screen and get some work done. Thanks for listening. It felt good to write it down and acknowledge that this is an area where I can use some improvement. More dopamine, please, stat!

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-16T00:04:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-186420>

Update on the dental issue. Oral surgeon looked at my xray and has sent me off for a Cone CT of #18 and #19 where the anomaly is. It is smooth and regular and unlikely to be cancer, also because it is denser rather than eating away at my bone. It is a "fibrous osseoma". He explained about dentin, enamel, and cementum (the last I had not heard of) and thinks perhaps it's some runaway cementum blastoma that is leftover from my tooth extraction (5 years ago now) and maybe is growing and maybe needs to be dealt with (or not). Whatever the diagnosis, I will get a second opinion! Blastoma....reading about those in Van Wijk. I know what those are.

I go for the Cone CT next Thursday.... and will get films and post them. If you want to see the panoramic Xray scroll on up a few posts, I uploaded the photo of the xray.

Wikipedia says " The cementum is light yellow and slightly lighter in color than [dentin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dentin). It has the highest fluoride content of all mineralized tissue. " Fluoride!

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-18T18:45:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-186653>

Listening to a boatload of podcasts Jack has done lately.... just googled Jack Kruse in the itunes podcast search window and BOOM (as it were). Starting to understand some stuff that eluded me before. PLanning to get a ton of sun today but right now it is GRAY and overcast  ..... will go get some UV through the clouds with the puppies anyway. Hoping for the clouds to burn off and get some D in at solar noon though. Ironic that just when I finally get the message I need to get sun, that is when CA decides to cloud up and rain. Only makes me realize how much I miss it when I cannot get any!!

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-01-19T15:24:06+0200

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-29T01:35:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-187585>

Tan-through suit arrived earlier this week. Sun where it didn't shine before? With the little time I have spent out there in my new suit in the backyard, I already feel things shifting. And with winter waning, the sun will only get better. WOO HOO. Dogs diets are finally under control and their GI issues settled, but it has been a tense week or so (months, for my little girl, but a change in her diet stopped her regurgitation INSTANTLY. ). My vet is Dr Coghlan who wrote the book PaleoPet. Since my little miss was losing weight, she has rice in her diet for now, because, the starch will help her gain weight. Then we will back off the grain. Wonderful that the babies feel better, but I love walking outside with them and snuggling with them on my lap as I work, good good vibes. Also when I walk my dogs, I am 100% present in that moment, delighting in how they sniff everywhere, mark, check their pee-mail (and sometimes reply-all), and just frolic around in the outdoors as we ground and get our sunlight. So my pets remind me, I should be doing that in EVERY moment. Why is it so easy to do with them? when I am down on the floor playing wrassle with them and their toys and fetch, it's just so fun and engaging! Good mental therapy, a destresser, and a good reminder to try to feel more present. I should hook up my emWave HRV monitor during my dog time to see what that looks like....

I have found myself looking up at the sun during our walks and saying (in the voice of the Austin Powers villain Fat Bastard) "GET IN ME RETINA" to the sun, lol. GET IN ME RETINA!!

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-29T18:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-187635>

Cmon Quantlet!! We have to make this thing happen!!

It's going to happen alright! 87% funded. Share, share, share! I have a vision of mine arriving at my front door soon.

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-29T22:19:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-187647>

Ok now off to see if I can remember someone else (female) who wrote about feeling a whisper in her ear, like there was trapped fluid - I think JK even weighed in on it but WHERE I read it, I can not remember. FB? Forum? I have that going in my left ear and it is making me craaaaaaazy. Gonna ice my head!

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-04T21:01:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-188219>

Icing my head made the ear whisper disappear in like 3 minutes and it has not come back. Also - SO FREAKING EXCITED ABOUT THE QUANTLET FUNDING! I will let any poetry on the subject marinate for a while until it is ready to gel into something.

My dogs are super happy with their diet change after a rough transition. You can SEE the energy radiating off of them - everyone remarks on it who has not seen them in a while. DHA for the win, there..... they are loving their rare salmon. And, if mama goes out in the sun, puppies follow. Trying to ensure they get THEIR sunlight and grounding too, for their health as well as mine.

As for my goals and CT. I don't know why but other than cold showers (that start out hot) I just can't seem to get into CT. Maybe I am lazy and waiting for the Quantlet. I would certainly be a CT-less subject to use as a baseline for any improvements!

I am doing GREAT on getting out in the sun. Right now GREAT consists of consistently getting out in the sun during the magic time every morning and evening, and 20-45 minutes during D-making time near solar noon, in the tan-through suit. If I am at home, I am getting my sun in. If I am travelling (like I will be next Mon-Thurs) it will be challenging as I will be in downtown Houston and tough to find sun between the high-rises..... but I will sit outside and get sun in my retina, at least! So I am giving myself an A for the sun, because it is a priority and a habit already, I crave it. Now I will just work on upping the amount of time spent in the sun. A lot more things printed out to read outside, I think, as I read for a living (essentially).

Wine - still avoiding. I will publicly admit to a mouthful of a particularly great pinot that hubby opened for a dinner party where our guest really was not someone who would enjoy that VERY nice wine. I had to have a mouthful, which I savored....but, I am totally fine being wine-free. And getting lots more mineral water instead.

Meditation - Need to work on stress, I have some guided meditations that I should really take advantage of my sun time and listen to them and get 2 birds with one stone - sun, and stress relief. Just need to bake in that little habit to take those mini-breaks so that I can be more productive the rest of the day. I am pretty good at getting into "coherence" with my emWave HRV monitor that attaches to my phone (set away from me of course).

Phone in general - am using less and less and less and less.

Blue light - have religiously worn my blue blockers after 6:30 pm at home and even out. I really really love the warm, cozy feeling of the amber light at night and strong white light ANNOYS the heck out of me. So it's candlelight and amber glasses in the house, if we are not upstairs in our amber-lighted bedroom. I am going to a friend's SUperbowl party in Houston when I land next Sunday, and will totally bring them along.....I NEED the amber glasses to reset my sleep when I travel east anyway, so I would not be caught without them. (of course I wear them on the airplane too).

Sleep - lately has been interesting. I have a UV light near the sofa I sit under at night while we are in the living room, maybe that has been helping, or the regular sun exposure has been helping. My sleep was iffy, I would wake up multiple times a night (every 2 hours or so) but fall immediately back asleep. In the last week, I have only awoken once or maybe twice a night and then immediately fall back asleep. Sometimes it has been because a dog jumped off the bed and sneezed or some other reason, not me waking myself up for no reason. I have felt more rested. And my dreams have been INTENSE and WEIRD and interesting. I sit and think about them after I awake and they are soooooo vivid and detailed. I'll just take all that as a good sign.

### Denise Moore — 2016-02-20T18:42:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-189455>

> [TerrierMom said:](/goto/post?id=186299)
>
> So, time for an update on my goals for improvement
> 1) wine - I have to admit to a sip of some very nice wine DH ordered, but, just the taste was enough to savor and I did not indulge further (nor feel the need to).
> 2) Sun - consistently getting my sun morning and afternoon with the dogs on a leash in shorts/tank, and most days getting 30-45 min in less than that out on the back patio. I do this whether it is cloudy or not!
> 3) CT - why oh why can't I make myself do this. After this post I will go get my face-dunk setup going (Cold water in a covered bowl in the fridge makes for easy dunking and saves water....drought you know!)
> 4) brain/stress- have not practiced meditation lately. Another one that is hard to make myself quiet down and do. I will do that right after this and use my emwave too while I do it.
> 5)black/red light - I get about 15 min every other day or so in the mornint with my red UFO. Black in the evening when I'm downstairs hanging out on the sofa.
> 6) forum time - I am getting here every day to check in on people and go down new rabbit holes
>
> On the sun part - just ordered my tan-through swimsuit (naked is not my bag, baby, at least, not right now!) - and if it is too small then I can always just wear the sarong in the back yard and tan through that. Will be interested in seeing how getting sun where the sun normally don't shine will affect me.
>
> Things that caught my interest in the forum of late: Wim Hof breathing techniques discussed quite a bit - but I thought hyperventilation was bad??? Reading about him though does stimulate my interest in CT. Will spend some time in the CT part of the forum for inspiration .... immersing myself in any topic puts it top of mind and motivates me. So, off to marinate in CT threads.

What company did you get your tan-through suit from? I'm been shopping for weeks and had a hard time deciding and ordered through Amazon the Kiniki. I was surprised at how non see through they are when on the body. Although I haven't gone out in the sun yet. I ordered way to small. I have no idea why I ordered a small... I'm a 32D, not a small. So, I have to send them all back. Now that I'm shopping again, I can't decide. What is your review of the brand you ordered?
Thanks in advance.
