### Jenelle — 2016-02-20T19:12:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-189460>

Hello, TerrierMom ~ I just wanted to say I hope your pets are feeling better!

We got our first puppy on November 1. So thankful that I learned about raw feeding before we got him, and that our vet is 100% supportive. (She says this is how she's fed her dogs for the past ~16 years or so.)

This is the book I have, and I love it:

[http://smile.amazon.com/Raw-Natural...UTF8&qid=1455987669&sr=8-1&keywords=lew+olson](http://smile.amazon.com/Raw-Natural-Nutrition-Dogs-Revised/dp/158394947X/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1455987669&sr=8-1&keywords=lew+olson)

It really gave me the confidence I needed to dive in to feeding this way ~ when no one else in my real life is doing so. Surprise, surprise... I am the one who feeds my family in a very different way... why should it be any different with our dog?

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-24T03:22:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-189687>

> [Denise Moore said:](/goto/post?id=189455)
>
> What company did you get your tan-through suit from? I'm been shopping for weeks and had a hard time deciding and ordered through Amazon the Kiniki. I was surprised at how non see through they are when on the body. Although I haven't gone out in the sun yet. I ordered way to small. I have no idea why I ordered a small... I'm a 32D, not a small. So, I have to send them all back. Now that I'm shopping again, I can't decide. What is your review of the brand you ordered?
> Thanks in advance.

I also bought a Kiniki. I'm a 38E and a size 16 so I got the largest size. I will order the next size down in the hopes that that girls have a little more support! Where the fabric is against your skin, it is not see-through. It lets through 80% of the light so you will still have some tan lines (going to order a bikini now for the back yard!). Where the fabric is AWAY from your skin, a little more see through (one reviewer noted she could see her 'posterior divide' through the fabric!!). As I am not sensitive about my ASS CRACK, that does not bother me, but, for that it is worth, there you go. I like my Kiniki!!

if you are getting one for the man in your life, they all seem so brief compared to trunks. Most look like a hipster or speedo, and even though I got DH the ones that said swim shorts, they are pretty darned snug. Not that it is a problem in the yard but I doubt he would wear it to the beach. No problem also at some Yucatan resort on our morning-sun-facing balcony on our next vacation......

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-24T03:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-189688>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=189460)
>
> Hello, TerrierMom ~ I just wanted to say I hope your pets are feeling better!
>
> We got our first puppy on November 1. So thankful that I learned about raw feeding before we got him, and that our vet is 100% supportive. (She says this is how she's fed her dogs for the past ~16 years or so.)
>
> This is the book I have, and I love it:
>
> [http://smile.amazon.com/Raw-Natural-Nutrition-Dogs-Revised/dp/158394947X/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1455987669&sr=8-1&keywords=lew olson](http://smile.amazon.com/Raw-Natural-Nutrition-Dogs-Revised/dp/158394947X/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1455987669&sr=8-1&keywords=lew+olson)
>
> It really gave me the confidence I needed to dive in to feeding this way ~ when no one else in my real life is doing so. Surprise, surprise... I am the one who feeds my family in a very different way... why should it be any different with our dog?

I know, I took grains out of my dog's food before I subtracted it from my own!! Vet still has me feeding them rice for the time being since they need to GAIN WEIGHT.... but my little boy threw up veggies at 4 this morning (not the salmon though!). I think he still has something brewing that may need addressing. Here's hoping it settles down! I am looking forward to transitioning them to ground pork or beef or bison or some other ground meat protein so I have better control of portions. 1/2 lb Salmon fillet will feed them both for a day but if I want to up the fish, it is hard because the next one I thaw is ALSO a half-pound, and I won't use it all at once. I'm a little anxious about having it thawed for a day before I use it up!!

YAY for vets who understand raw feeding!! It is really not that much trouble to fix them their food and boy oh BOY do they ever love it.

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-24T03:49:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-189689>

SO, just checking in. I went to a THOR Laser seminar a week or so ago now, and got to meet James Carroll who worked with Rub and Jack on the light module of the Quantlet. I have access to all sorts of neato LLLT research papers now, so there is a rabbit hole I have been spending time in lately! I would love to have my own THOR but, there are other financial priorities at the moment, so I have found a clinic near me. Taking mom (type 2 diabetes) Dad (high bp and atherosclerosis) and myself down there tomorrow for a consult and to book some treatments. They also have the NOVO THOR pod that looks like a red tanning bed, I will get 5 minutes in that to check it out. I am pretty sure the answer will be YES PLEASE.

I have been feeling about 75% of late. Sinus congestion, ear pain, a little cough. I am kicking it though, with lots of SUN and saline nasal spray 4x/day with some Argentyn (silver) spray in my nose also. Totally stopped the progression dead in its tracks but it lingers. I know others have REAL medical issues like cancer and MS, who am I to whine about some sinus/ear pain, but, it just dulls my edge and makes me foggy.

The real thing lately is I have just felt BLAH (low dopamine anyone? ZERO motivation). The only thing I WANT to do is play with my dogs, spend time with DH, walk with them in the sun, and lay out in the sun and listen to podcasts and read. I do NOT want to balance my books for my business or hubby's, I do NOT feel like reading for work, I do NOT feel like cleaning my house. And I am sleepy. My sleep the other night was SUPER SOLID. Though in the last month or two it has been a case of waking up a few times in the night (I look at the time through an orange screen) and I know that I fall RIGHT back asleep, no laying awake for hours. My dreams are very vivid so in a sense I feel like I am watching a movie or TV or living my life, and not really RESTING, if that makes sense. I know dreams are supposed to mean better redox but I would like them to not take up my entire night and let me have a little downtime where my brain is NOT thinking thinking thinking! I am yawning a lot during the day and it is easy to nap in the afternoon. It is NOT blood sugar crashing from carbs, I am eating seafood daily and ketogenic.... so not sure why I am feeling run down. Then again it is the run-up to Shark Week so perhaps that has something to do with it.

I googled on iTunes podcasts and downloaded every one that I could find with Jack, or Jack and Rub, or Pollack, etc. I have been listening to those and re-reading blogs. I finally MADE it to a members Q&A too, yay me. The more I immerse myself the more things jump out at me and connections start to be made. I know I am doing a lot of things right. I am eager to try some biohacks -

One that Jack mentioned on the Q&A was to ask one's optometrist if myopia has increased in their practice since they have been a doctor - if they have, then we know the quantum yield of sunlight is SUCKY where we live. Well, I live in Southern CA so I am pretty sure it's suck central. I am interested in other biohacks like nicotine and methylene blue and have been reading other people's journals about them to get clues for what I might try and what materials I need. But to a certain extent I suffer from the BLAHS about that too, in figuring out what I need, where to get it, and how to measure how it works other than how I feel.

Current environment:
Light: Amber EVERYTHING in my bedroom, phone screens. UFO 660nm red light. UV bulb (party light, will have to look at my AMazon orders to get the nm, but it is specific). Thinking of getting the red and UV together up here in my office. FLUX on my monitor. However I cannot load it on my work PC, so, I am going to donate this monitor to my church and get a monitor that has "reader mode" that blocks 55% blue light.

HACK TO COME: At my annual visit this year, my reading glass prescription stayed the same. (would like to reverse that so I can ditch reading glasses!). BUT I got new lenses which will be BlueTechs. ALSO, I got my OLD frames out and ordered 550 BPI tint on those, so I will have Gretchen-style amber lenses to rock after dark, or when I have to go into the alien sun world at HQ in Houston when I travel. I will still wear my fitover blue blockers at night if I am not in my bedroom where it's amber. I have come to REALLY enjoy the amber light and be offended by bright lights at night. I even rock my blue blockers at the Happiest Place on Earth (where I can frequently be found).

SLeep - blacked out room on a 20Gauss Magnetico. May ditch my sleep number matress (it is 15 years old now I think anyway!) and get a Casper or other mattress so it will not be so thick!! I just ordered some SHEEX sheets to put on it, to help regulate temperature. With DH and 2 terriers, it gets HOT at night even with just a sheet. Can't wait for them to arrive (someone on the FB group mentioned them).

Sun - I am religious now about getting 15-20 minutes in the early AM when I wake up (feed and walk the dogs without my glasses and BOOM. Even better when I go barefoot). And most days I get an hour around solar noon in my tan-through suit.

Water - I drink Pellegrino or spring water, though may have an iced tea when I am out - though since I live in CA, that is fluoridated water. I started giving my dogs spring water. I have RO water at the house, and they get that, but, my vet said he has seen great changes when people switch to non-FL water for their pets, and since mine still aren't 100%, time to up their game too.

so that's light, water, and magnetism. Of course my QUANTLET will arrive in a couple of months - cannot WAIT to get that thing on me. I will be doing some red light therapy to help my neck and shoulders (remnants of a couple old auto accidents and sitting at a computer all the time) and getting in the NOVOTHOR bed in the hopes of helping to diminish systemic inflammation I have been carrying around for the better part of 3 decades. WHen I read stuff on the blog about decades of inflammation and the things it leads to, it is very very scary. Too many around me with cancer and MS and yukky diseases that I do not want. I hope that I am reversing my path to these diseases with what I am doing so far. I cannot go back to eating donuts and pasta and living indoors 24/7! I am craving the outdoors, so I guess that is something anyway.

Anyway. Hope the BLAHs go away soon.

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-24T20:44:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-189717>

Time 9 and 10. So much to digest. I know I am not alone in that digestion though many more will get the AHA before I do. That's ok though, I will get there. Just have to keep on the path and keep eating DHA and getting sun and good water and my brain will catch up eventually, of that I am positive.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-24T21:03:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-189719>

> [TerrierMom said:](/goto/post?id=189688)
>
> I know, I took grains out of my dog's food before I subtracted it from my own!! Vet still has me feeding them rice for the time being since they need to GAIN WEIGHT.... but my little boy threw up veggies at 4 this morning (not the salmon though!). I think he still has something brewing that may need addressing. Here's hoping it settles down! I am looking forward to transitioning them to ground pork or beef or bison or some other ground meat protein so I have better control of portions. 1/2 lb Salmon fillet will feed them both for a day but if I want to up the fish, it is hard because the next one I thaw is ALSO a half-pound, and I won't use it all at once. I'm a little anxious about having it thawed for a day before I use it up!!
>
> YAY for vets who understand raw feeding!! It is really not that much trouble to fix them their food and boy oh BOY do they ever love it.

I just ordered 19 lbs of ground, whole animals from [www.mypetcarnivore.com](http://www.mypetcarnivore.com). Not sure where you are located ~ but they deliver to many states. Check out their site if you're ever interested... I am really impressed with their photos of small farms, the information on their entire site. You can tell they really do care.

The variety is amazing, as well. They have a lot of more expensive animals that I did not order (like muskrat and llama) ~ but for today I got a variety of turkey, beef, trout, whiting, all sorts of tripe, and pork.

Our guy LOVES frozen chicken wings -- and they keep him busy for quite awhile. Yep, bones and all. The bones are only dangerous when they are _cooked._ (If you're 100% raw-feeding, they need the bones for calcium, phosphorus, and fiber.) He's also a big fan of sardines, raw eggs, frozen beef patties, and any sort of meat that we cook in the instant pot.

If you feel like the veggies are upsetting anyone's stomach... just know that they really are **not** necessary. You'll find different opinions on this, I know ~ but the book I recommended is a good resource if you'd like info and support for just feeding a truly raw, carnivorous diet.

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-25T19:02:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-189777>

Got 5 minutes in the NOVOThor pod (660 and 810nm red light pod) yesterday afternoon as a test. Slept like a ROCK last night. I will be going the next 6 Mondays. Then it will be April and sometime then I will have a Quantlet or 3.... woo hoo!! My dad is going to get the THOR LX2 used on him and so is my mom for their various physical complaints. Maybe THIS will convince them light is a drug. My mom is great about getting her morning sun and she grounds in the garden while she does it. Dad not so much. Baby steps.

Jason Coates posted a great and simple story of de-synchrony of the master and organ clocks in the Quantum FB group today, which I love. I have a deep dark desire to adapt the SchoolHouse Rock songs (and YES I have ALL The DVDs) to Quantum Health principles and rewrite the lyrics. Then I just need someone to animate them, and, avoid copyright issues with the music . In any case..... it will require greater understanding on my part. But that's motivation for me to learn in a backwards kind of way- I want to make something creative out of it which gives me a spark that puts me in a flow state (like when I was writing the Night Before Quantlet, it was literally all I could think about so I had to stop what I was doing and write it all down and noodle around with it -and time both seemed to stand still and pass quickly - I lost all sense of time). I like a good flow state now and then but when the creative urge strikes everything else has to get out of the way because if I don't get it out, it will distract me from anything else until I BIRTH it from my brain. Then it is back to business as usual.

it makes me re-read things with a new eye though.... to find the narrative thread of a story like Jason just did. Genius. I love this group SO MUCH. Y'all are wonderful.

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-29T23:36:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-190000>

A weekend with my college BFFs and my little sister, driving up to the Central Coast for a wine tasting weekend, helped drive the BLAHs away. The scenery north of Santa Barbara is SO GORGEOUS with all the rain (comparatively speaking) we have gotten, the hills were so green and gorgeous, I drank it all in and lived in each moment thankful for the beauty around me. Had a room with a mineral spring hot tub full of water piped up from the geothermal spring (high sulfur content) and had a nice soak in that on a chilly night, then let the water run out and sat in the cold a while.... went to bed early, ate great food, enjoyed the company of friends, and yes had a little wine (maybe a bottle over the whole weekend). This was a planned event though, with the wine, so I am back to NO WINE as I try to reach my goals. Got my sun in daily. As soon as we were done tasting and I'd decided my purchases I was out on some gorgeous patios in the sun, soaking it up. More clothes than I would like but hey! Got the chance to worship at a Mission built in 1797.... up with the sun for 7 am Mass. Locals were very welcoming and it was so gorgeous in the morning. Anyway, I feel much more energized and ready to face all the stuff I don't "feel like" doing. IN fact this morning I knocked out a bunch of crap I'd been putting off forever, and then as SOON as the fog burned off, got an hour of sun outside on my patio.

Sleep was sadly sucky this weekend as the folks upstairs from us got in at 1 am and stomped around until 4 am and were really loud. I did fall right back asleep after waking up, but sis and I had some VERY VIVID dreams, in which we were both doing violence to the upstairs neighbors in various ways.....lol.... or just other really bizarre dreams. Really bizarre last night too but I awoke feeling rested despite all that. My dreams are freaky these days. I need to pay attention and try to do some lucid dreaming!!

VERY inspired by the guy on the FB forum who just posted that he doubled his testosterone after a few months of 100 min daily sun exposure (paraphrasing here but that was my takeaway). Mine is low, DH"s is low too. D was low for me last time I was tested (though even without dedicated sun time like I have now, I have seen my D up around 80 on diet alone... not sure how that happened). I will get blood work in March from the doc, and again in April through the Quantlet service, then we will see. Headed for another session with the THOR laser today, the first of 6 weekly sessions. Hoping to sleep like a ROCK tonight.

### TerrierMom — 2016-05-06T20:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-194300>

So it's been a while since I posted. I've been doing well on getting grounding, sun in my retina, sun on my body, etc etc - and even starting a meditation practice and using my emWave HRV dealie and working on that end of things. AND I finally got my BPI 550 tinted prescription glasses for night time. I have been stricter than EVER about light at night for the last couple of months but for some damned reason my sleep is fragmented lately. I wake up every 2-2.5 hours or so, and usually fall back asleep within 5 minutes. It may be a dog getting up to go outside (and while one of my dogs was having gut issues, I would get up too, to follow him out and see if he barfed or had bad poop issues). But I would put on my orange glasses etc and not turn on lights. He is fine now (for the moment anyway) so maybe I am just in hypervigilant mommy mode listening for things? Occasionally I will wake up about 3 or 4 am and not be able to get back to sleep for an hour. I do not eat after 7 pm and I do not expose myself to blue light after sundown (and I wear bluetechs during the day and use flux or orange screens on monitors and phone etc). I can't think what changed to mess up my sleep. I have had a sleep study done before so no sleep apnea issues.

Anyway I can count on 1 hand the number of times in the past month that I have slept all night long!

I did have a trip back east for a week (but sleep was fragmented before then). So it was easy for me to wake up at 6 am since 3-4 am Pacific was one of my awakening times. I am a total morning person and would get out and ground and get sun in the mornings while I was there, so I adjusted immediately and, putting on my orange glasses, had no issues getting to bed at 9 or 10 pm while I was there. I was TOTALLY drained when I got home though, felt like I was out of batteries that Saturday, and went to bed early that weekend and recovered (wish I had my Quantlet to help with jet lag!).

I am just stumped. Going back to the 3-legged stool - getting high quality spring water and mineral water, sleeping on my MAgentico, avoiding blue light at night and getting my morning UV and sun on my body daily for at least an hour if not more. OUr bedroom is a blackout at night, has been since about 3 months after I found Jack's site.

I will be interested to see where my Vitamin D is with all the recent sun. At one point I had a 70 Vit D without supplements and without consioucly making an effort to get sun or avoid blue light....last I checked it was like 30, ugh, need more labs as I have not had any in about a year now. Next step is to look at nnEMF in my environment so I just need to get the meters and start measuring.

I would like my rock-like sleep back!

Remember sleep links to UV light by way of aromatic amino acids that make melatonin.

Now for the rest:
Sun light on our gonads/mammary glands is capable of slowing down our peripheral clock gene's in these tissues relative to our SCN, hence lowering our chance of oncogenesis. This is move an optimal human male/female makes. Why? It's a "relative testosterone" booster by indirectly raising our RBC's mass while simultaneously lowering estrogen levels in these tissues. For men this increases testosterone and lowers estrogen in all body parts, but in women it is a boon for the breasts. Too much estrogen unopposed drives mammary gland growth. The porphyrins and hemoglobin allowed the capability of absorbing UV light to fuel the transformation of cholesterol to 7-Dehydrocholesterol to stabilize our epithelial surfaces over over our gonads/mammary glands. 7-Dehydrocholesterol is a semiconducting protein in our skin and epithelial tissues that takes the native EMF solar signal of light (with melanin and sulfated cholesterol and water) and changes it to a chemical signal that the brain samples to control peripheral and central circadian timing in our clock genes. How does this occur?

The photoelectric effect has been extensively studied in plants. It has been found to be massively energy efficient. The rate of energy capture by the photoelectric effect in plants is immense. It is approximately 100 trillion watts (1 trillion watts = 1 terawatt). This is ten times the current the current power consumption of the human species today. It is clear the photoelectric effect is very powerful for plant life that they could evolve just using water, CO2, and light to make nutrients. Animals are not connected into the ground 100% of the time, nor do they have their semiconductors (canopy of leaves) in the sunlight 24/7. So animal life had to come up with a new way to use the photoelectric effect to power its version of life. It did. It used DHA and water to build a powerful battery that was constantly recharged by solar power. Sulfated Vitamin D3 in our skin is a semiconductor LED designed to transfer the sun’s power to our hypothalamus by way of our blood plasma. The blood plasma then distributes the chemical message to our gonads/mammary glands. The brain uses this signal to OPTIMIZE SLEEP. The sun can increase the electric charge present in our plasma and our neuroepithelium simultaneously in this way.

Using real world experiments and the laws of nature that Einstein uncovered and showing you how Vitamin D3 use them as well. I have told you the leptin receptor is the accountant in the brain that measures photons and electrons. The epithelium and brain both come from the same material in an embryo. This tissue is called neuroectoderm. This shows you another quantum principle at play. At one time in all life the skin and brain were connected. Here is another twist you might not know. In the embryo, neuroectoderm receives a light signal from bone morphogenetic protein which absorbs UV light. This is an inhibiting signals from a protein called noggin. Noggin leads to the development of the nervous system from this tissue. Here we see how bone tissue can directs neuroepithelial and brain formation. Robert Becker's life's work established that bone formation was quantum because it used the photoelectric effect by rectifying its current. Now we see bone helps form the brain indirectly. This is how Vitamin D3 is important in both tissues. This implies the brain, gonads, and breast tissue is also quantized. Everything is connected in a quantized system. This is the key principle behind coherence in the living matrix of all biology. The key is realizing the system is quantized to begin with. Are there more connections you should be aware of with the skin? Yes. The integumentary system covers the surface of the embryo and its specialized skin structures including hair, nails, sweat glands, MAMMARY GLANDS and teeth. As a system it has contributions from all embryonic layers. This means that every system at some level is tied to sulfated Vitamin D3 in some way, and it is why the sulfation of Vitamin D3 by the sun is important to understand.

Ladies reading this should stop........and now realize why they need their breasts in the sun for wellness too. A woman's gonads, ovaries, are deep hidden from the sun, but not from the Vitamin D3 signal, but their breasts are out in the open awaiting the sun signal. What happens when women bury the sun from her life? READ THE LINK ENCLOSED.

In our adult form, the skin and our brain are no longer are ‘physically‘ connected. What binds their function together physiologically? Sulfated Vitamin D3 levels is the short answer. ### nicld — 2016-05-30T23:42:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-195664>

> [TerrierMom said:](/goto/post?id=189689)
>
> SO, just checking in. I went to a THOR Laser seminar a week or so ago now, and got to meet James Carroll who worked with Rub and Jack on the light module of the Quantlet. I have access to all sorts of neato LLLT research papers now, so there is a rabbit hole I have been spending time in lately! I would love to have my own THOR but, there are other financial priorities at the moment, so I have found a clinic near me. Taking mom (type 2 diabetes) Dad (high bp and atherosclerosis) and myself down there tomorrow for a consult and to book some treatments. They also have the NOVO THOR pod that looks like a red tanning bed, I will get 5 minutes in that to check it out. I am pretty sure the answer will be YES PLEASE.
>
> I have been feeling about 75% of late. Sinus congestion, ear pain, a little cough. I am kicking it though, with lots of SUN and saline nasal spray 4x/day with some Argentyn (silver) spray in my nose also. Totally stopped the progression dead in its tracks but it lingers. I know others have REAL medical issues like cancer and MS, who am I to whine about some sinus/ear pain, but, it just dulls my edge and makes me foggy.
>
> The real thing lately is I have just felt BLAH (low dopamine anyone? ZERO motivation). The only thing I WANT to do is play with my dogs, spend time with DH, walk with them in the sun, and lay out in the sun and listen to podcasts and read. I do NOT want to balance my books for my business or hubby's, I do NOT feel like reading for work, I do NOT feel like cleaning my house. And I am sleepy. My sleep the other night was SUPER SOLID. Though in the last month or two it has been a case of waking up a few times in the night (I look at the time through an orange screen) and I know that I fall RIGHT back asleep, no laying awake for hours. My dreams are very vivid so in a sense I feel like I am watching a movie or TV or living my life, and not really RESTING, if that makes sense. I know dreams are supposed to mean better redox but I would like them to not take up my entire night and let me have a little downtime where my brain is NOT thinking thinking thinking! I am yawning a lot during the day and it is easy to nap in the afternoon. It is NOT blood sugar crashing from carbs, I am eating seafood daily and ketogenic.... so not sure why I am feeling run down. Then again it is the run-up to Shark Week so perhaps that has something to do with it.
>
> I googled on iTunes podcasts and downloaded every one that I could find with Jack, or Jack and Rub, or Pollack, etc. I have been listening to those and re-reading blogs. I finally MADE it to a members Q&A too, yay me. The more I immerse myself the more things jump out at me and connections start to be made. I know I am doing a lot of things right. I am eager to try some biohacks -
>
> One that Jack mentioned on the Q&A was to ask one's optometrist if myopia has increased in their practice since they have been a doctor - if they have, then we know the quantum yield of sunlight is SUCKY where we live. Well, I live in Southern CA so I am pretty sure it's suck central. I am interested in other biohacks like nicotine and methylene blue and have been reading other people's journals about them to get clues for what I might try and what materials I need. But to a certain extent I suffer from the BLAHS about that too, in figuring out what I need, where to get it, and how to measure how it works other than how I feel.
>
> Current environment:
> Light: Amber EVERYTHING in my bedroom, phone screens. UFO 660nm red light. UV bulb (party light, will have to look at my AMazon orders to get the nm, but it is specific). Thinking of getting the red and UV together up here in my office. FLUX on my monitor. However I cannot load it on my work PC, so, I am going to donate this monitor to my church and get a monitor that has "reader mode" that blocks 55% blue light.
>
> HACK TO COME: At my annual visit this year, my reading glass prescription stayed the same. (would like to reverse that so I can ditch reading glasses!). BUT I got new lenses which will be BlueTechs. ALSO, I got my OLD frames out and ordered 550 BPI tint on those, so I will have Gretchen-style amber lenses to rock after dark, or when I have to go into the alien sun world at HQ in Houston when I travel. I will still wear my fitover blue blockers at night if I am not in my bedroom where it's amber. I have come to REALLY enjoy the amber light and be offended by bright lights at night. I even rock my blue blockers at the Happiest Place on Earth (where I can frequently be found).
>
> SLeep - blacked out room on a 20Gauss Magnetico. May ditch my sleep number matress (it is 15 years old now I think anyway!) and get a Casper or other mattress so it will not be so thick!! I just ordered some SHEEX sheets to put on it, to help regulate temperature. With DH and 2 terriers, it gets HOT at night even with just a sheet. Can't wait for them to arrive (someone on the FB group mentioned them).
>
> Sun - I am religious now about getting 15-20 minutes in the early AM when I wake up (feed and walk the dogs without my glasses and BOOM. Even better when I go barefoot). And most days I get an hour around solar noon in my tan-through suit.
>
> Water - I drink Pellegrino or spring water, though may have an iced tea when I am out - though since I live in CA, that is fluoridated water. I started giving my dogs spring water. I have RO water at the house, and they get that, but, my vet said he has seen great changes when people switch to non-FL water for their pets, and since mine still aren't 100%, time to up their game too.
>
> so that's light, water, and magnetism. Of course my QUANTLET will arrive in a couple of months - cannot WAIT to get that thing on me. I will be doing some red light therapy to help my neck and shoulders (remnants of a couple old auto accidents and sitting at a computer all the time) and getting in the NOVOTHOR bed in the hopes of helping to diminish systemic inflammation I have been carrying around for the better part of 3 decades. WHen I read stuff on the blog about decades of inflammation and the things it leads to, it is very very scary. Too many around me with cancer and MS and yukky diseases that I do not want. I hope that I am reversing my path to these diseases with what I am doing so far. I cannot go back to eating donuts and pasta and living indoors 24/7! I am craving the outdoors, so I guess that is something anyway.
>
> Anyway. Hope the BLAHs go away soon.

How do you like the Sheex?

### TerrierMom — 2016-06-09T21:52:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-196265>

I love having my Kiniki since my sunning area at my house is not private - but honestly it's so rare anyone is home during the day that completely topless may be the ticket, I peel the bikini down as far as it will go anyway. Morning overcast here has made for cool temps in the "sun", I know I get UV anyway.

The majority of my attention in the last few months has been with the guts of my dogs, trying to switch them over to a raw diet and some bouts of colitis ensured and a merry go round of abx I would not want for myself much less for them. But now they get probiotics and digestive enzymes and I have successfully switched them over and their guts are healing. Honestly not paying much attention to my own health other than to ground an hour daily (on my walks with them either barefoot or in grounding sandals), get my morning UV for at least 15 min in my eye first thing and then an hour in the sun between 8-11 at least 5 days a week (longer if the UV index permits) - eating properly, drinking non-fluoridated mineral water, sleeping on my Magnetico, using blue blockers at sunset and while looking at screens ...... all the usual stuff. I keep saying I need to hack the nnEMF and get some meters, so that's the next step. I am sure I will be horrified, maybe that is why I am putting it off. Reluctant to confront the data head on. So doing my usual stuff that is now routine for me..... need to measure my environment and consider MB or nicotine hacks (one at a time of course.....).....

As to the Sheex - I find they really do regulate temperature well, but I don't particularly care for the feel of them, they are a kind of stretchy poly fabric which will really catch on any rough skin spots (the way my heels are making calluses I know I am a long way away from leptin sensitivity yet.) I like the feel crisp sateen-style sheets better. When it gets hotter the Sheex will go back on though.

Sorry I have not been updating as I have not really been making changes (other than continuing with increased sunlight in the last couple of months). Not much to journal about when I am not doing a current biohack.

Going to PLAYA DEL CARMEN for about a week soon though..... call it a 25th anniversary celebration (actual anniversary in August but too much work travel for a vacation then). Planning to soak up major sun and swim in cenotes! Poor DH still white as a ghost, he gets his sun in his eye daily but not so much on the rest of him except on weekends where he has actually gone outside in HIS kiniki suit without me prompting him!!! What?!! Maybe my sun obession is rubbing off on him.

This is a great community of supportive people and I can't be more grateful for the time that Jack spends with us and all the moderators and frequent posters who weigh in on journals. So much to get through and listen to, reviewing the notes from the webinars that I have taken and seeing things that pop out now that did not before, so much to re-listen to. Maybe I will ask an intelligent question in the next Q&A. Still have lots of reading to do.... I am one of those people who like to know the WHY but there is a lot to digest. In the meantime I am doing the WHAT...

### TerrierMom — 2020-08-17T02:05:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-291561>

Back in the forum to continue my education where I left off and document my journey as I have been diagnosed with Stage 2 breast cancer. My surgery results revealed a 1.1 cm tumor in my right breast and a 5 mm tumor in 1 of 3 lymph nodes that were removed. I have more scans in the future plus some gene typing of the tumor to tell the docs whether I would benefit from chemo. So in 2 weeks I will know what the path forward is for me. I know I will get 6 weeks of radiation to my right breast and armpit - chemo is yet to be seen. And after that, hormone therapy since my tumor was estrogen and progesterone positive (but Her2 negative). Although Jack tells me - the sun is an aromatase inhibitor between 10 am and 3 pm so if I can avoid the hormone therapy side effects and just get cozy with Mr. Soleil, that suits me just fine.

So here is my current environment:

-   Moved from Southern CA to Rockwall TX (outside of Dallas in a low-density suburb/ag area) in 2018. Work from home since 2014 so decided it was time to move away from CA and the very dense and high EMF area I had been living in.
-   Had **Shielded Healing** out to the TX house to take measurements a year ago. My takeaway was turning off my bedroom circuits at night (and not right away either, but eventually). But a closer inspection is needed, since I have developed breast cancer, it seems there is something I should be doing about my EMF exposure.
-   Sleep - Bedroom is **BLACKED OUT** and circuits are off - day and night. I sleep on a **Magnetico** on a Nest bed (no metal springs or anything like that). I also recently got an **inclined bed wedge**, I have been wanting to do inclined sleeping to address frequent sinusitus and now I can! Just put it under the mattress today and it goes from 7" to 1" at the foot. I have a Pro-Somnus oral device since Feb, apparently I had ridiculous sleep apnea. SEE JACK's NOTES ABOVE and the linked study about poor sleep and breast cancer. Anyway since getting it I sleep GREAT. I wake up in the night briefly and then go back to sleep. Sometimes I sleep all night without consciously waking. I use the SLEEPTALK app to see if I am snoring or stopping breathing in my sleep from time to time, that is how I found the right setting on the appliance to use. (the right tray). So now my sleep is pretty darned solid. My husband ALSO got a device so HE no longer snores or has sleep apnea (and it was AWFUL for a while there - I could never sleep!)
-   LIght: **Blue blockers** in the house if I am looking at a screen - day or night. Regular glasses outside (if reading) or no glasses (if sunning). Amber bulb in master bath lamp, no lights in house at night. Amber filter on bedroom TV (which actually has not been on in almost 2 years) - amber filter on old iphone I use in airplane mode at night to listen to podcasts. I generally avoid turning on lights unless I need it. About to get a fresh pair of orange glasses (I wear prescription progressives) from **Lucia Eyes** (I see my eye doc on Weds and will get normal ones from them, and get my prescription to send to Lucia Eyes).
-   Work setup - monitor and 2 laptops - Home laptop has **f.lux** installed, monitor has "reader" setting that limits blue light even when my work laptop is being projected. I have a **ExoTerra reptile bulb** shining on me when my monitor is on. My study has french doors which I open a crack to get full-spectrum light in the house. My study faces the rising sun and I can go through the french doors to my front porch where there's a bistro table and can take my laptop and work outside. I face the street though so this is with clothes on (Kiniki dress works!)
-   **SUN** \- If there is not rain and lightning outside -
    -   I get out into the morning sun at least 30 minutes, often more, in my kiniki bikini (if I am at the patio table with my laptop) or naked (if I am facing away from the neighbors in a sun chair. We do not have privacy fencing in this neighborhood!)
    -   I get out into the sun as close to solar noon as I can to try and make at least 5000 IU of D.
    -   In the evening (especially in the summer) we eat around 5 pm and then I float around in the pool soaking up evening sun. We sit out there talking, sometimes working (I have a VERY long cord to connect us to the internet without using wifi), and just chilling. Maybe will watch TV on a tablet. One day will get an outdoor TV wired so I can safely watch TV out in the full-spectrum sunlight. SUnset around 8:30
-   FOOD - **Epi-Paleo RX**. I say ketogenic but honestly I do not count macros. I eat shrimp weekly, salmon, scallops, sashimi at least once a week with sea veggies. I eat a variety of non-starchy veggies and greens, I sprout broccoli sprouts and have some daily (sulforphanes good anti-cancer ingredient). I drink a liter of green tea a day (sencha I brew and then ice!) - I am heavy on the cancer fighters like garlic, any kind of onion, mushroom, spinach, all leafy greens, colorful peppers, cabbage, cauliflower/broccoli/brussel sprouts. Some squash for variety (zucchini, etc). I use ghee or coconut oil or avocado oil to cook in - Sugar has not been part of my life for quite a while. Trying to keep things mixed up and get as much variety as possible. Dark chocolate (85% or more) is my only indulgence.
-   WEIGHT - I am 5'6" and as of this morning down to 219 lbs (almost 5 lbs lost since I visited Jack at Longevity Farm last weekend). In the last 180 days I have lost about 35 lbs. I would like to get down to around 150 but have not seen that weight since 2000! My most recent low weight was around 180 and that was in 2014 when I started my new job. Gain came from sliding off the bandwagon, picking up my wine glass, and letting "special occasion carbs" creep into my life more often than not. 5-10 lbs a year crept back on. And I am sure, massive blue light exposure that was unchecked, had the diabetes effect on me also. I am smarter about that now. I think we all want to NOT believe the effects of light on us, but let me tell you something is off metabolically with me - plus, you know, CANCER. So I am here to tell you - light matters. You ignore it at your peril!
-   STRESS - besides getting tighter on light, I have to say STRESS is a focus for me now, and the mind-body connection. Needing to meditate, pray, whatever - but need to handle my stress. I needed to before this - and now I need it even more. I am a fairly tightly-wound type A personality.
-   Other hacks at my disposal:
    -   **Squatty Potty**. I love it. No strain, no pain - for one who tends towards constipation (and I take Mg Malate before bed at night because of that!) - it is a Godsend
    -   PBM - I have a **Quantlet**, of course - and a **810 Vielight**. Gathering dust, but getting dusted off now!
    -   **HeartMath HRV t**raining device - to get me into coherence. Looking at the effects your mind has on your body, specifically stress, is a priority for me. So I try to log some time with this device every day so that I can train my heart to be in coherence.
    -   **Jeremy's Entangled Red Light** - this is on when the TV is on in the house. Also used it on my surgery scars to stop pain and itching (it works!)
    -   **COOL Fat Burner/Cool Gut Buster** - and 2 sets of ice packs for it. I have been very inconsistent about CT so likely that will be changing!
-   Hacks I am considering:
    -   Zengar Neuroptimal Feedback Training - When I was in CA, I did about 6 sessions of this with a local psychotherapist - and I ABSOLUTELY love this decice. I have wanted the PRO model forever but it is pricey. But there is a local company that will rent one to me for a month. If I have chemo and radiation ahead of me, I need my head in the right place. This training helps you train your brain to be in the zen space - which I could use more of. Will be looking into renting one at least for a few months. [https://neuroptimal.com/store/](https://neuroptimal.com/store/) I need to look around their "find a trainer" page to find someone who will ship me a unit at the least monthly cost..... I can see a few months of this would be good.

Ok that is it for now. Still processing my diagnosis and working with Jack to see what else needs to change in my life.

### TerrierMom — 2020-09-18T20:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-292202>

When you have cancer you go on a major learning journey!! I am no exception. I have read at least a dozen books in the last month and dived down the rabbithole in multiple websites. Many are so-called alternative therapies. I read them all, digest, run them through a quantum bullshit meter, and decide.

I had my surgery August 5. I INSISTED on getting the Mammaprint gene typing test for my tumor before I would even consider chemo. Luckily for me, my score came back VERY low (a good thing) - meaning - no meaningful benefit from chemo! Hurray!

The mother of a friend of ours has a business as a patient advocate, specifically for cancer. She spent a dozen years running an oncology hemotology clinic before starting her business 10 years ago, and has a ton of cancer in her family of various kinds - so she has seen it from insurance and billing, doctor and nurse, and patient sides, and knows all the ins and outs.

So I talked to Tina and found out some disturbing (yet sadly unsurprising) news. The big Oncology center I am going to - is owned by McKesson - the USA's largest pharmaceutical company. I also found out that they entered into an agreement a few years ago with various medical insurers (including mine) to NOT recommend certain testing for their patients - in fact - they are incentiveized to NOT recommend testing! So - when I have asked my chemo doc, my radiologist, and my breast surgeon, at a total of 4 appointments, just HOW they were going to CLINICALLY track my progress before/during/after therapy - and wasn't there some blood testing we should be taking as baseline before surgery???? I was told, on every occasion, there was 'no good test' to measure for cancer in the blood, and we will just test to make sure you are ready for surgery with a CBC, etc.

Did they want to test my vitamin D? Not a whisper. Did they want to know, or even care, if I was a good surgical candiate based on my labs? All they cared about was whether I would survive aneasthesia. Luckily Jack taught me I better get my D up ASAP before I went under the knife. I sunned like crazy and I actually even took supplements this time - I need to get D up there and KEEP it up there post-op. (Of COURSE I am sunning too).

Anyway - knowing what Tina told me - I was no longer surprised to have gotten such pushback from the Drs on any kind of testing. They DID want to do a breast MRI before surgery (I assented after initially putting it off) - and after surgery they wanted me to have CT scan of pelvis, abdomen, chest, and a bone scan - since I had a positive lymph node I suppose they were looking elsewhere in my body. I actually asked them all about a PET scan - but was told each time "your insurance won't pay for it at your early stage" and was dismissed. (Now, PET scans are MAD amounts of radiation, so, I am happy to skip it - but, Tina told me, as a patient advocate she had certainly talked more than a few insurance companies into testing for her patients). Yet again I am being fed a line of BS. It gets tiring.

Next up, radiology. I am called to make an appointment and they say I need to allow X hours because they will set me up in the simulator so that I will be all ready to start radiation. I put on the brakes. I was NOT ready to start radiation. I had met with a radiologist briefly prior to surgery just to understand what it MIGHT entail depending on the outcome of my surgery. But I had not researched yet and did not have many questions ready. It was another story this time - I had a ZILLION questions.

The truth is - radiation, chemo, etc work on the microscopic, fast-dividing tumor cells. They really do NOT touch cancer stem cells. I asked them all if this proposed treatment would treat cancer stem cells and was essentially told, you probably don't have any because you are such an early stage. (BS. We all have cancer in our bodies, but whether or not our immune system takes it out with the trash, or whether it latches on and grows itself a little tumor, is up to your TERRAIN. The environment you allow yourself. Your stress level - the crap you eat - toxins you allow - and of course that includes nnEMF and especiallly blue light.

So armed with a shitload of research my radiologist and I had a very nice amicable chat. She went over the NCCN guidelines which meant based on my tumor, node positive status, size of tumor, I would get treatment X for my tier of disease. Nothing more personalized than that. I reminded her of my Mammaprint test, a 70 gene microassay which indicated I had a very low risk of recurrence. She said that did not apply because it only applied to Chemo (I guess my tumor knows chemo from radiation?). Radiation, my friends, is all about reducing risk in the ipsilateral (Dr-speak for same) breast. What they do NOT tell you - and we only discussed in my appointment because I brought STUDIES about it - is that radiation carries an INCREASED risk of lung, esophageal, and sarcoma type cancers in the areas irradiated. Oh and sometimes irreversible skin/tissue damage (which I have heard plenty about on FB Cancer support group message boards.). When I showed her the studies, which was a pooled analysis of over 750K patients - she really did not have much to say.

So I asked myself - Self, why would I want to reduce the risk of another breast cancer in the same breast, (which is VERY treatable) and INCREASE my risk of a secondary cancer that is not as easy to take care of? I think I would rather roll the dice, thanks.

I wondered about the Mammaprint and its usefulness as a prognostic tool for whether or not I could avoid radiation. I consulted google to look for studies where someone looked at Mammaprint scores of women who declined conventional therapy to see if it was still predictive - and I found a 2018 paper presented at a cancer conference about that VERY subject - and the conclusion was, for women over 50 in certain low clinical risk and low Mammaprint risk categories, we might be able to avoid radiation also. Well, that was enough for me! a big HELL NO to radiation.

Last but not least - hormone therapy. Well I know I have been low progesterone for decades - likely my reason for infertility, as back when I was charting my cycles (back when I had them!) I noticed a luteal phase defect (second 1/2 of cycle too short and cycle starts before egg has a chance to implant and grow) - which is due to low progesterone. And I know I am estrogen dominant - just by my obesity and body type as an apple rather than a pear. And guess what, my tumor is ER+ and PR+ so it responds to both.

There are natural aromatase inhibitors. The SUN, for one. (not a minor one either). And I am searching the literature to see if any natural substances have been tested alongside the poisons they want me to take (I get the post-menopausal poison instead of Tamoxifen). The side effects of Arimidex are pretty horrific and can linger for a lifetime. They can make you die from something OTHER than cancer. NOT my idea of a cure.

So - right now - my plan is - Deuterium Depleted Water for 6 months - 2 month break - repeat - eat everything grassfed and organic (which I mostly do anyway, but need to stray from that less!) - work on stress relief - get SUN in the early am, late pm and of course at solar Noon so I can make some D - regular thermograms to see how I am doing. And I found a local doc that WILL run the cancer marker tests for me so I can track those too. AND - I informed him about the Greece test by RGCC-group.com (originally started in Greece) - there is one that tests blood against natural substances to see which is best for my circulating tumor cells (you know, the ones the oncologists say I do not have, even though I had cancer in a lymph node, it must have magically appeared) - and will also count them, so I can keep track if that number is rising or falling. He will get those for me too (though THOSE will be out of pocket, they are VALUABLE information).

Just irritating to be flat-out lied to, misled, and misdirected. They do NOT want to empower the patient. They just want to give you your pink bag full of ads for compression sleeves, wigs, and lotions and potions to use during radiation, your pink ribbbon, welcome to the cancer tribe my sister! Come be cut, poisoned and burned with your sisters who have gone before you! Like a bizarro-world hazing in the world's fastest-growing sorority. Not one I want to be a member of!

### caroline — 2020-09-20T05:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terriermoms-journey.17290/post-292246>

I applaud your thoroughness and your bravery and courage in doing so much research to get some answers.

It seems there is always a conspiracy and cover up when we need to and try to take charge of our own situation and get answers.

Why is it so hard to find/get the truth?

Big pharma has a lot to answer for but how is that ever going to change?

Are we strong enough collectively to get answers? To make changes?

Good luck and please keep us posted ...sending you a big hug X

# Thread: All pigmented proteins use aromatic amino acids and UV light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-pigmented-proteins-use-aromatic-amino-acids-and-uv-light.17292/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T04:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-pigmented-proteins-use-aromatic-amino-acids-and-uv-light.17292/post-184791>

Tyrosine is the base AA for all pigmented proteins.

Another aromatic residue with non-negligible absorption in the near-UV region is tyrosine (Tyr-OH). At neutral pH tyrosine has absorption maxima at 220nm (є~9000 M-1cm-1) and 275nm (є~1400 M-1cm-1) (Creed, 1984a). At alkaline pH the OH group of tyrosine side chain deprotonates. The resulting tyrosinate (Tyr-O●−) has a slightly red-shifted absorption compared to tyrosine, with maxima at 240nm (є~11000 M-1cm-1) and 290nm (є~2300 M-1cm-1) (Creed, 1984a). Photoexcited tyrosine can fluoresce, decay non-radiatively, or undergo intersystem crossing to the triplet state, from which most of the photochemistry proceeds. Alternatively, at neutral pH, tyrosine can be photoionized through a biphotonic process that involves absorption of a second photon from the triplet state. This results in a solvated electron (e-aq) and a radical cation (Tyr-OH●+) that will rapidly deprotonate to create the neutral radical (Tyr-OH●). Photoionization of tyrosinate at high pH is monophotonic and results in a neutral radical (Tyr-O●) and a solvated electron (e-aq).

Triplet state Tyr - OH +hv yields try-OH singlet +electron (e-eq)

The triplet state tyrosine is rapidly quenched by molecular oxygen or nearby residues like tryptophan or disulphide bridges (Bent & Hayon, 1975b):

An important photochemical mechanism in proteins involves reduction of disulfide bridges (SS) upon UV excitation of Trp and Tyr side chains (Kerwin & Rammele, 2007, Neves-Petersen et al., 2002 & 2009a). As shown above, UV-excitation of tryptophan or tyrosine can result in their photoionization and to the generation of solvated electrons (Bent & Hayon, 1975a & 1975b; Creed, 1984b, Kerwin & Rammele, 2007, Neves-Petersen et al., 2009a). The generated solvated electrons can subsequently undergo fast geminate recombination with their parent molecule, or they can be captured by electrophillic species like molecular oxygen, H3O+ (at low pH), and cystines (EE series of blogs on cysteine/cystine

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T04:28:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-pigmented-proteins-use-aromatic-amino-acids-and-uv-light.17292/post-184792>

In the case where the electron is captured by the cystine, the result can also be the breakage of the disulfide bridge in proteins like glutathione. (Hoffman & Hayon, 1972)

Here you begin to see how UV light and the aromatic AA begin to work to create all the pigmented proteins in our body. Activated electrons from UV is where the process begins.

The resultant free thiol radicals/groups can then subsequently react with other free thiol groups to create a new disulfide bridge. Reduction of SS upon UV excitation of aromatic residues has been shown for proteins such as cutinase and lysozyme (Neves-Petersen et al., 2009a, 2006 & 2002), bovine serum albumin (Skovsen et al., 2009a; Parracino et al., 2011) prostate specific antigen (Parracino et al., 2010), and antibody Fab fragments (Duroux et al., 2007). As mentioned in the introduction, this phenomenon has led to a new technology for protein immobilization (LAMI, light assisted molecular immobilization) since the created thiol groups can bind thiol reactive surfaces leading to oriented covalent protein immobilization

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T04:48:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-pigmented-proteins-use-aromatic-amino-acids-and-uv-light.17292/post-184795>

In the case where the electron is captured by the cystine, the result can also be the breakage of the disulfide bridge in proteins like glutathione. (Hoffman & Hayon, 1972)

Here you begin to see how UV light and the aromatic AA begin to work to create all the pigmented proteins in our body. Activated electrons from UV is where the process begins.

The resultant free thiol radicals/groups can then subsequently react with other free thiol groups to create a new disulfide bridge. Reduction of SS upon UV excitation of aromatic residues has been shown for proteins such as cutinase and lysozyme (Neves-Petersen et al., 2009a, 2006 & 2002), bovine serum albumin (Skovsen et al., 2009a; Parracino et al., 2011) prostate specific antigen (Parracino et al., 2010), and antibody Fab fragments (Duroux et al., 2007). As mentioned in the introduction, this phenomenon has led to a new technology for protein immobilization (LAMI, light assisted molecular immobilization) since the created thiol groups can bind thiol reactive surfaces leading to oriented covalent protein immobilization

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T04:52:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-pigmented-proteins-use-aromatic-amino-acids-and-uv-light.17292/post-184796>

Pulsed UV illumination and the interaction of aromatic amino acids can halt activation of cancer cell membrane receptors (HER and EDGF) and thereby all downstream reactions that would lead to cancer, shutting down the cells’ biological functions. Moreover, there is new treatments based upon the ELF-UV release that cells use to activate the cell’s own cell death program. This has been documented on two human epidermal cancer cell lines (Olsen B.B. et al., 2007). The photonic dosage necessary for therapeutical results has additionally been determined. It turns out it is pretty low frequency as we saw in the Russian experiments and in Roeland van wijk's book.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T05:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-pigmented-proteins-use-aromatic-amino-acids-and-uv-light.17292/post-184798>

This pathway is how cells use UV light to activate apoptosis pathways and prevents all cancers by deactivating disulfide bridges in CM proteins that use optical signaling. HOW?

Throughout 4.5 billion year of molecular evolution, proteins have evolved in order to maintain the spatial proximity between aromatic residues (Trp, Tyr and Phe) and disulfide bridges (SS) (Petersen et al, 1999). There is a very special spatial geometry relationship that exists because of the interaction with the most powerful part of the solar spectrum of light (UV) measures the collisions. The aromatic amino acids become the first step in determining where the position and geometry of residues to act as nanosized antennas in the protein world that can capture UV light (from ~250-298nm). Once excited by UV light they can enter photochemical pathways likely to have harmful or beneficial effects on protein structures by affecting specific bonds like disulfide bonds in cysteine/cystine which are the most rare AA. It turns out cysteine/cystine disulfide bridges in proteins are excellent quenchers of the excited state of aromatic residues. This means they decrease the power present in UV light in excited aromatic amino acids. In this way they contributing this way to protein stability and activity, thereby affecting ubiquitin rates. UV light excitation of the aromatic residues is known to trigger electron ejection from their side chains (Bent & Hayon, 1975a; Bent & Hayon, 1975b; Bent & Hayon, 1975c; Creed, 1984a; Creed, 1984b; Kerwin & Rammele, 2007, Neves-Petersen et al., 2009a). These electrons can be captured by disulfide bridges in things like glutathione, leading to the formation of a transient disulfide electron adduct radical, which will dissociate leading to the formation of free thiol groups in the protein. This photochemical change leads to non optical signaling. Once disulfide bonds are broken we can inactive detrimental cell membrane receptors that cause epidermal cancers. The irony in all this.............UV prevents and doe snot cause cancer by these mechanisms. More irony for the skin and eye docs: This mechanism is now being used to develop drugs using nanotechnology and the ability of cells to make ELF-UV light.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T05:43:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-pigmented-proteins-use-aromatic-amino-acids-and-uv-light.17292/post-184805>

Popularity is usually the path of idiocy. If 150 million oncologists say a foolish thing, it is still a foolish thing.

# Thread: Getting Back To Me - DaLeak

### DaLeak — 2015-12-23T04:52:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-184797>

Long time reader, first time engager. I was a bit intimidated by everyone's knowledge here so I wanted to get somewhat up to speed before starting my journal. Here are the basics. Been overweight for as long as I can remember. Have lost most weight and gained it back than I care to mention. Nothing seems to get me past a certain point. About 2 years ago, I found this site and started reading, re reading, re reading again until some of the science started sinking in. I feel as though I have a pretty good grasp of what is happening in my body, at least a 101. On Jan. 4th of 2016, I am starting the Leptin Prescription. I have put my entire family on notice, sent them Dr. Kruse's Quick Start Leptin Rx and asked them to hold me accountable. I am currently on HRT with Testosterone so I do labs every 12 weeks anyway to keep an eye on things. I have started CT, experimented with eating fish in the morning, and working on stretching currently. I have a long way to go to optimal but it starts with the first step so here we go.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T05:16:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-184799>

Hold yourself accountable by realizing this: who are you really good for if you are not good enough for yourself. People spend a lifetime trying relive parts of their life instead of spending the present moment just living it.

### caroline — 2015-12-25T08:54:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-184919>

Welcome?

Give Dr. K. More health history ..... Your Moms and grandmas and siblings health?

Where do you live? Work? NnEMF?

How is your sleep? How much time do you spend outside?

Please remember it is all about the three legged stool. ...light, water, magnetism

It isn't a calories in/ calories out story .....

### DaLeak — 2015-12-27T04:33:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-184987>

38 Male, 298 lbs, Caucasian. I live in Nashville, in the suburbs, I work from home. My house has WIFI and a few more around the cul de sac. I am working on turning mine off at night and trying to wire my office upstairs. That is going to take me a beat though.

My parents are still alive and do pretty well for themselves. They are outside a lot, eat lots of fish, and exercise. My sister has had food allergies since I can remember and has really been an inspiration to me, she did a lot of early 90's research around cave man diet, then Mercola, now I have turned her on to this world. The rest goes like this:

Paternal Grandfather - Dead / Brain Cancer
Paternal Grandmother - 94 / had heart attack, quad bypass, still ticking quite well actually
Maternal Grandfather - Dead / Heart attack - was told he had sickness as a kid that damaged his heart
Maternal Grandmother - Dead / Stroke / Old age - she was in her 90's, had a great run

I have been really focused on sleep / started seeing a HRT specialist when I saw my Testosterone was in the toilet. Cortisol was a bit out of whack as well. High highs low lows. I finally got that under control and sleep has been going very well. I wear the uvex blue blockers at night after I put the kids down...around 8 pm. Then in bed around 9 pm. I usually rise around 5:30 - 6.

In the past 2 months, I have been trying to get outside as much as I can. Just today I sat in the sun with no glasses on for around 30 minutes. bare feet in the ground. Felt really good, wish I could do it every day.

The thing that i cannot get under control is how fast i gain weight. If I take my eye of the ball for a couple of days I gain 5 lbs just like that. When I read the blog about why Oprah is still fat, it clicked for me. I have NEVER understood why I eat so little and gain weight so fast while people eat twice as much as me and don't gain anything. There had to be more to the story. I have read the Leptin Rx and going to give it a go. I just want to set myself up for success. I am heading down to the Gulf for NYE and going to buy some fish, shrimp and Oysters at Joe Patti's. That should get me through January. I am fine with eating a lot of seafood, I love it. I am also fine with eating it for breakfast. Just need to do it, consistently. I have been reading about how to lose weight for 18 years, tried a ton of plans, lost tons of weight, gain it all back, again and again. I have to break this cycle, it isn't working for me anymore.

Also, I have been doing CT now for abour 3 weeks, started with face dunks, then moved to tub. I am down to 59 degree water now for 45 minutes. It keeps getting better and better.

I have all my bloodwork from 2009 on in a Google sheet if it is needed.

Let me know anything else that might help. Also, if there are any other current or former "BIG GUYS" on the blog that might be able to mentor me. I am happy to return the favor when I am down the road.

### caroline — 2015-12-27T04:52:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-184988>

I am guessing that you know Jack is a former big guy???

The lepton reset is about timing ....getting the Rolex in your head working correctly!

Please forget about calories .....and guys do really well on the reset!

Timing is so important!

Upon rising ... Go outside with few clothes on and barefoot and look at the sun ( even if it is a cloudy day)

Have a BAB within 30 minutes of rising .....so prepare the nite before if you want to. throw a leg of pork in a crock pot before you go to bed.

Leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed.

Make sure you load up on protein/ seafood for your BAB .....at least 50 g of protein. I needed quite a bit more.

Forget about the scale for now ...... You want your health back!

You are in a high nnEMF area ..not good.. Dr. K. Used to live not far from you.

What water are you drinking?

You are doing so much right! If you can manage it .become a gold member and you will have access to JK's monthly webinars and a live Q&A where you can ask Jack anything.

Can you work from outside your house? At least somewhat?

### caroline — 2015-12-27T04:58:34+0200

I forgot to ask you ...... How is your libido? That is a great health indicator.....

### DaLeak — 2015-12-27T18:11:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185006>

Thank you so much Caroline for your wisdom. I have made an instruction sheet for my days and adding all your comments until they become habit (so I don't forget). I am mitigating all the nnEMF that I can control, other than that it is what it is right now. I am in a lower density part of town so it could be a lot worse.

In terms of water, we have a rain soft softener with a extra filter for drinking water. It is pretty clean water as I have had it tested. If there is something else I need let me know.

Libido was totally tanked...that and the lack of energy, brain function is what led me to the BHRT doctor. I listened to Dr. Kruse on his Hormone webinar, selected a doc based on that, and feeling a lot better. Even though the testosterone has me feeling good, I am well aware that is is covering a larger issue which is obesity. It is a bit of a catch 22 which is what led me to taking my health into my own hands. All the docs had for me was eat less and exercise more. After 8 years of trying that, it doesn't work. Or at least doesn't work considering my current state. I am trying to rebuild my health one brick at a time. There are a lot of things going right and a few things going wrong. That is what I am working on.

I do not have a smart meter. I know they have been talking about it, but it isn't a reality yet.

Thanks for all your responses, it makes it feel less alone. Ryan

### caroline — 2015-12-28T06:17:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185030>

Ryan ..... Have a read of Mr. Pinkies journal.

This is Kate's journal and her and her husband Chris have turned their life around in huge ways.....especially their

son Ryan.

Kate has lost a huge amount of weight ...maybe 150 pounds. I have forgotten the exact number!

Your water ..... I am not sure what you have but I am pretty confidant that Dr. K. Would recommend bottles of spring water from Costco or somewhere like that.

You are definitely not alone ..... a lot of us try to limit computer time and it is the silly season!

Just jump into the reset and don't look back! Stick to it ..... Your optimal life depends on it!

If you fall off the wagon you reset yourself back to the beginning ..... And D. K. Has said if you do that you need a kick in the butt!!

It is actually very simple .....

A BAB within 30 minutes of rising... Lots of seafood and GF protein and GF butter

Leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed

NO SNACKING!

Drinks lots of spring water

Get outside as much as possible , no clothes and barefoot! And hug a tree!

Observe/ listen to mother nature

C/T

No artificial lites after sundown .....when the sun sets - so should you!

### DaLeak — 2016-01-04T15:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185398>

Day 1 of Leptin Reset - Hit the sack last night at 9:20pm up at 5:50 AM BAB within 30 minutes of waking up. Salmon salad, 2 eggs, greens, and fermented cabbage. No sun this morning, but going to get full spectrum outside anyway. Sun Glow bulb on the way from Amazon, should have by Wednesday. Grounding outside as well. I may hit CT today, but did CT for past 4 days. Just depends how I feel. Going to see how long I go until I am hungry, then I will eat some flounder I got from the Gulf. I also have some Kale I am going to try and cook up. Still not hungry at 2 PM. I ended up making it to about 4PM and then I was looking through the kitchen for food to eat. I decided it was time to cook dinner. Baked Flounder and kale cooked in bacon grease. It was an awesome meal. However, I found myself pretty hungry at 7:30 PM, but just drank a ton of water and hit the sack at 8:45pm. The no caffeine headaches started early that evening and I just wanted to sleep them off instead of live through them. Wore blueblockers after sundown.

### JanSz — 2016-01-04T16:10:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185399>

No sun this morning, but going to get full spectrum outside anyway. Sun Glow bulb on the way from Amazon, should have by Wednesday. Grounding outside as well. I may hit CT today, but did CT for past 4 days. Just depends how I feel.

I consider two aspects of benefiting from sunlight.
1\. getting up and getting 2-5 or more minutes of sun on the eyes for benefit of circadian Rhythm
depressing melatonin in the blood and increasing cortisol
2\. general benefit of tropical light

#1 is mostly about eyes only
#2 is about eyes and max skin area

On cloudy days you may use Exo Terra Sun Glow 160 W both ways, #1 and #2.
For #2 you may need more than one of these bulbs.
To find out if you have enough of overall light exposure you judge it by:
measuring vit D and finding it to be over 100 (365 days/year).
measuring cortisol (4 up to 8 times over 24 hr) and finding that it have desirable profile
desirable ups and downs

When having overall low cortisol (blood and saliva), it is good idea to do 24hr urine cortisol check

Always remember:
when measuring in healthy body,
most blood steroid hormones in the morning
and assuming the sum of it as 100%
DHEAs participation is about 95%
cortisol participation is about 4.25%
the rest .....
it is good idea to pay attention to major players first
.................

### DaLeak — 2016-01-05T15:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185472>

Day 2 of Leptin Reset - Up at 550am, caffeine headache almost gone. didn't get till breakfast until 6:45, took care of the kids first. Cooked up Shrimp with red onions in bacon grease and 2 pastured eggs. Also, had leftover salmon salad and a ton of water. Going to do my Physical therapy excercises now and stretch, then hit the sun. DID I MENTION THE SUN IS OUT! Got in sun this morning no glasses, bare feet on soil, for about 5 minutes. I'm sure the neighbors wonder what the hell is that boy doing, it's freezing. Had to eat lunch today, pretty hungry. Had a grassfed beef patty and salad. Dinner was GF Beef and cauliflower. Went to bed hungry again. Caffeine headache and very sleepy. Hit the sack at 8:45pm again up at 6 AM Pee'd twice in the night. Must be dropping some water.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-06T17:28:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185564>

Day 3 of Leptin Reset - Up at 6:00 AM, tuna salad, 1 pastured egg, 3 slices of bacon and a ton of water. Got 3 minutes of sun in my bare eyes. Starting to go around my house today and identify WIFI using things that need to go. Scale was first. So i stepped on it before taking out the batteries and was 298, down from 303 on day 1. I will be trying to find an old timey scale that doesn't use batteries and push out wifi. Caffeine headache is gone, now my head just feels a little hungover. Yesterday you could feel the sugar fed bacteria in the gut trying their best to persuade me around 3pm. Fought it. I believe it gets better from here on out now that the worst is over. Had lunch at 10pm, it was mahi and salad. Dinner was 8 Paleo fried oysters and salad. Starting to feel better even if it is ever so small. Good news, my Exoterra light came! Just in time because it looks like cloudy skies and rain the next few days. In bed at 900

### DaLeak — 2016-01-07T15:33:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185637>

Day 4 of Leptin Reset - Up at 6AM. Was difficult to get up today and get moving, not sure why, but I'll give my plan the benefit of the doubt. Takes more than 4 days to heal what I got going on. Breakfast was 45 minutes after waking up, had shrimp with onions, 2 eggs, and half an avocado. Sun is up so heading out there. Question for the group, how long should I sit in front of my new 160watt exoterra light and how close? Hit dinner with Paleo fish sticks (cod), asparagus, and beets. No food after dinner, still a little hungry at night but getting better. In bed at 9PM The whole day I had pretty low energy so if I had to take guess, the glycogen stores must be running out.
