Hey @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) - I had time to go through all your comments and questions, thanks for the help and support.
1) I have all limbs and both eyes - fully functional human just for from optimal
2) how long should I look at the Exo Terra Light on cloudy days in the morning, also how close should I be to it.
3) You recommended Androgel rub in on testes, but everything I have heard Jack say is to not rub TST on the skin and to do bio identical.
4) Agreed on fasting - once I get my body working on Ketones and off the sugar addiction, that will be a lot easier. I have been there before and could go quite some time without eating.
5) you wrote Get pregnenolone, progesterone, is that something my doc helps me dial in or should I self explore? Is there reading I can do on this?
6) Get Spectacell - Will do when I can save up some money again
7) I don't use 5AR inhibitors
8) I don't take statins
9) I quit fish oil since I am eating real fish
10) I am supplementing Vita D this winter and it has kept me from getting sick. my thought is once I can get lights and sun light dialed in I will stop

### JanSz — 2016-01-07T17:46:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185648>

\[ quote="DaLeak, post: 185640, member: 17979"\]Hey @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) - I had time to go through all your comments and questions, thanks for the help and support.
1) I have all limbs and both eyes - fully functional human just for from optimal

\--------------------------------------------------------
2) how long should I look at the Exo Terra Light on cloudy days in the morning, also how close should I be to it.
\----
Ideally one would live on equator, got enough of (strong) sunlight, and his vit D level would be above 100.
He would regulate sun exposure seeking shade.
Current theory is that human species (present form) originated from around Lake Victoria.
Note that vit D made by sunlight is vit D,sulfate (storage form of vit D).
Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W is full spectrum light (from UV to IR)
Box from the lamp contains information on UVB density at the distance.
Look up my posts on Low Cortisol thread.
Say info from this post #568:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-29](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-29)
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-29#post-183869](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-29#post-183869)
Exo-Terra-Solar-Glo-160W
at the distance of 36"
UVB 25 uW/cm^2
On strong sunny day in middle of USA one may get 150 μW/cm^2 UVB
How Much is Too Much?
50-250 uW/cm2 mimics the natural environment enough to keep your UV-dependent BASKING reptiles healthy,

I figured 36" as optimum distance and have two of this bulbs. Likely more would be better.
Distance of 36" gives room for movement, also at shorter distance temperature (IR light) is high and uncomfortable.

\------------------------------------------------------------
3) You recommended Androgel rub in on testes, but everything I have heard Jack say is to not rub TST on the skin and to do bio identical.
\---
Androgel, test Cypionate or Enantate, Nebido are bio-identical.
Post your blood test results and describe your experience/difficulties for any further discussion.

\-----------------------------------------------------------
4) Agreed on fasting - once I get my body working on Ketones and off the sugar addiction, that will be a lot easier. I have been there before and could go quite some time without eating.

\------------------------------------------------------------
5) you wrote Get pregnenolone, progesterone, is that something my doc helps me dial in or should I self explore? Is there reading I can do on this?
\----
The idea is to keep Steroid Hormone Panel at desirable level.
There is a lot of different steroid hormones.
Some of them we can test and just look at results, manipulate indirectly.
Some of them we can test and manipulate directly.

Only some doctors would go into this, they would call it deep, but it is not really.
More doctors would want to please you, write you script but do not want to discuss results.
You may want to look at list of tests I posted here:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/#post-81508](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/#post-81508)
there is also two charts that show some steroid hormones.

If you are able, lots of blood tests you can buy directly via internet and draw blood at LabCorp.
There is info about on that thread.

\-----------------------------------------------------------
6) Get Spectacell - Will do when I can save up some money again
\----
This is super important test.
But it does not test for iodine/potassium iodide.
For that everybody is always low.
I use one dropperfull/day of Lugol's solution.
Before you would do that you should know your selenium level (from Spectracell)
or eat Brazillian nuts, or eat selenium pills 200mcg/day (no more)

\-----------------------------------------------------------
7) I don't use 5AR inhibitors
8) I don't take statins

\------------------------------------------------------------
9) I quit fish oil since I am eating real fish
\----
Fish or fish oils contain EPA in huge amounts.
When someone have excess EPA(omega3) that is highly suppressive to AA(omega6)
I looked over fatty acids blood tests that some people post (while on fish diet)
It looks like about half have good EPA, the other half have it very high.
I am in the high half, so I eat seafood less frequently.
Measure your Fatty acids, post results here.

\------------------------------------------------------------
10) I am supplementing Vita D this winter and it has kept me from getting sick. my thought is once I can get lights and sun light dialed in I will stop\[ /quote\]
\----
Good idea, do it daily.
See also #2 above

### JanSz — 2016-01-09T16:40:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185830>

> [DaLeak said:](/goto/post?id=185764)
>
> Day 5 of Leptin Reset - Up at 5 AM, felt pretty good. Took me an hour to get to breakfast from waking up, but had my salmon / tuna salad with celery / onions / fermented cabbage. 2 pastured eggs. no sun today and figured out I need a special socket for 160W EXO bulb. So will get that today. Going on 1 PM and still not hungry today. 1pm had a salad with chicken apple sausage. I know it wasn't ideal, but it was fast.

The socket is of normal size, same as in household lamps.
But household lamps are meant for about 100W so you need arrangement able to handle more power.
In the thread Low Cortisol, I looked up two kind of setups.

.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-10T23:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185937>

Day 6 of Leptin Reset - Breakfast was bacon, eggs, and leftover salmon salad. Lunch was a salad with GF Ground Beef and salsa, and dinner was a salmon salad with egg, bacon and avacado. In bed at 9:30pm

Day 7 of Leptin Reset - Up at 6 AM - Breakfast was 4oz of smoked salmon, eggs, bacon and kale. Totally full at 3:20pm, no lunch but a ton of water. Dinner is gumbo sans rice and flour. Shrimp and oysters are in it as well. I made a roux with olive oil and tapioca flour and coconut flour. It was OK, but nothing like NOLA.

### caroline — 2016-01-11T04:29:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185945>

Are you eating enuf?

### DaLeak — 2016-01-11T15:48:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185981>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=185945)
>
> Are you eating enuf?

I mean, i feel full after breakfast and don't really get hungry much anymore. Keep in mind I probably have 100lbs of reserve tank fat so maybe my body is going to work on that. I make sure I eat a ton of protein and fat for breakfast, epi paleo based and try to get as much sunlight as I can. When it is rainy, I now have my Exo Terra light.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-11T15:52:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-185984>

Day 8 of Leptin Reset - Finally woke up feeling good, like hopeful. My sleep is starting to become something amazing. When I get up to go to the bathroom, I am able to go back to sleep (like 3 - 4 am) till 5 or 6 am. Something I thought would never come back. Woke up, got in front of my ET light for 5 minutes, then off to the kitchen to make breakfast. This morning was 1/3 lb of shrimp with two Pastured eggs and breakfast sausage. I felt full after it and then chugged a liter of water. I sold my WIFI scale so have no idea how much weight has left me, but clothes are just feeling tight instead of deathly tight. Wasn't hungry at lunch, drank a lot of water and urinated a ton. Then at dinner had a filet of mahi and the rest of the gumbo. In bed at 9PM.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-12T17:40:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-186084>

Day 9 of Leptin Reset - Up at 6 AM - 2 shrimps, 4 oz of smoked Makrel, sausage patty, 3 strips of WF bacon, 2 eggs. Hit the light right out of bed and feeling pretty good. I went to my first restuarant since starting this plan and got the Salmon Cesar. I had forgotten they put parmesean cheese in the dressing, so don't know what that means. Ate a light dinner since I wasn't terribly hungry. In bed at 9 PM

### DaLeak — 2016-01-14T04:56:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-186249>

Day 10 of LR - Breakfast / eggs sausage and mahi. Very good. Got a slight headache today, but wasn't that hungry. Ate at 3pm chicken and salad. Didn't really have any dinner, not that hungry. Which is quite amazing. I didn't do that good on drinking enough water. Tomorrow will be better.

Day 11 of LR - woke up at 4:30, a bit odd for me lately, tried to go back to sleep no dice. Hit light then started in on breakfast, mackerel eggs and bacon. I think fat is coming off but don't have proof. Then for lunch had bison beef on a salad. Wasn't hungry for dinner. Finally hungry at 8:30 but going to bed at 9 instead.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-15T22:49:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-186405>

Day 12 of LR - Got some good sleep, slept till 6. Put on a shirt that hadn't fit in a while so that was a good feeling. Had Salmon / Tuna salad and eggs for breakfast. Didn't really get hungry until 2:30pm, so I am just going to eat an early dinner. Shrimp tonight. It is rainy today so keeping my exo terra light on. At tons of shrimp in butter garlic sauce and TONS of kale fried in bacon grease. Feel full and content. Did good with the water today and even got a walk in.

Day 13 of LR - up at 5:30am, eating fish, shrimp by 6. I didn't eat enough, I was hungry at 10. Waited for lunch, salmon salad with roast beef roll ups. Dinner is chicken soup. Walked in between rain falls and water is going well. I did end up having some New Zealand grass fed cheddar last night. I remember jack saying something about that being ok, but I'm not sure 100%. In bed at 9 pm

### DaLeak — 2016-01-18T02:26:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-186605>

Day 14 of LR- up at 5 today, felt pretty good. Had to go out for breakfast so it was a bit tricky ordering. It wasn't optimal, but it was paleo. Lunch was on point. Salad with smacked salmon and roast beef. Dinner was Vietnamese. Seafood curry sans rice. There was squid in there, shrimp crab, octopus, things I'd never seen before. I'm pretty sure the sauce wasn't cool, but I did my best. I seem to be more positive lately, less agitated. In bed by 9PM

Day 15 of LR - Up at 5:30 and breakfast by 6:10 AM. Getting better. Had 3 ozs or smoked salmon, 2 Italian sausage links, and 2 eggs. I should be good to go and make it all the way to dinner. Mind and body are improving, but it is small gains every day. Going to try and focus on water today...lots of it. Looks like we might be a little sun today as well. 12:30...Not Hungry. I know I am over 60 oz. of water by 1pm. Trying harder to get in the water. Made it to 4:30PM and was pretty hungry by dinner. Had 2 GF Burgers (1/4 lb each) broiled beets, pickled green beans, a little bit of sweet potato and brocolli. Felt really full after dinner which felt good. Drank a ton of water. I got out in the sun for 5 minutes today. Going to hit the sack at 9pm.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-20T17:01:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-186896>

Day 16 of LR - BAB or Mahi, eggs and bacon. Lunch was a chicken salad. I noticed they snuck in beans and corn on the salad so I tried to pick out most of it, but I know I ate some. Dinner was my chicken tortilla soup, just had a little as it was a little late to be eating. planned on skipping lunch but a business lunch was bought for me so I was grateful. in bed at 9 pm again.

Day 17 of LR - My breakfast was a little short of protein this morning I am sure. Lots of eggs, onions, and green peppers. Also Chicken sausage. Going to have to eat some fish for lunch or dinner. I have this new old school scale but the scale seems off. I don't know what to do about that so I have no idea of progress. But I think I can safely say I am somewhere between 10-15lbs down. I did have a small bowl of chicken soup. I didn't really need to eat, i just wanted to. I do miss it so that is something I am dealing with. Dinner was flounder with almonds crust cooked in butter and lemon with spinach and bacon salad, and some peas. In bed by 9pm. It has been snowy and cloudy and I need the sun.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-21T19:50:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-187004>

Day 18 of LR - Work up at 5:15 AM, felt pretty solid. Started in with 2 poached eggs, leftover fish, and my now classic salmon / tuna salad. It is almost noon and I am not even close to wanting to eat lunch. I think I did it right today. Still staying on the straight and narrow. Making sure I get at least one serving of seafood a day. Sometimes 2. If it isn't seafood it is GF beef . I try to keep chicken in the bottom slot because JK say Fowl is foul. Also, I have on a sweater that fit last year and it feels pretty good, not too tight. Something must be happening. I need to get back into CT, it is just been so cold here I have a hard time not being frozen all the time.

Hi DaLeak!
Just wanted to say Hi and welcome! I live near you in Franklin, Tn so I feel your pain with the weather right now. I also work from home and I force myself to get out of the house and take at least one long walk every day. It's easy to do when it's sunny but these dreary days I have to force myself out the door. As you know, there's still some sun even when it's cloudy and I always am glad that I got out! Also, right now it's cold so you can get some natural CT by just going outside. Keep up the good work! You can do this!
Jeanne

### DaLeak — 2016-01-22T17:28:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-187085>

Day 19 of LR - Woke up at 6:30 AM, slept A LOT. I did where my blueblockers the majority of the night last night. Has the rest of my salmon tuna salad, eggs and sausage. It is snowing today so going to get out in the snow in a little bit with the kids. Had a little lunch today, GF burger on a bed of greens. Also, made it out in the snow for an hour for some CT. Piece of mahi for dinner and some peas. In bed at nine tonight glasses on since sundown. Woke up at 3:30 AM and couldn't get back to sleep. Just laid there till 5 then got up.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-25T02:53:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-187312>

Day 20 of LR - Woke up at 5, didn't sleep as long as I would have liked. Made bacon, smoked salmon and eggs. Lunch was a salad. Dinner was chicken wings and salad. Not the best meal choices but I got that salmon in there.

Day 21 of LR - Great nights sleep till 5:30 AM. Woke up, fixed smoked salmon, found a new brand that is delicious. Made sausage, onion, and egg omelet. For lunch had the leftover chicken wings. Got outside and GOT SOME SUN today. Took the glasses off, probably 20-30 minutes worth. Dinner was a bit of a F##K up. I had a cheeseburger without the bun, which isn't bad BUT i had about 12 french fries. AND not the sweet potatoes variety. The real deal. They were awesome. Yes, I know you aren't supposed to have potatoes, but I did. Moving on..Tomorrow is going to be a killer day. Also, i do think it is important to note that I have not had a drop of alcohol since I started. That means 21 days...my record is 46. Yeah, I keep track of that sh!t cause it is hard.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-25T20:42:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-187352>

Day 22 of LR - Feel really good today and was up at 5:15 AM. I noticed something lying in bed this morning - my fat is becoming a bit more liquidy and not so hard. Not really sure what that means, but i'll chalk it up to the "that's good" category. Had to start wearing a belt now a days. I also found this smoked salmon I really like at Costco. Seems pretty legit. So I had about 2 ounces of that this AM with 2 eggs and 3 bacons. It is almost 1 pm and I am not hungry. Also I got outside and got some sun with out the glasses. Bonus treat as they said it would be cloudy all day. It is 3:30PM and I haven't had one hunger wave. So awesome. Got home from a meeting at 6 PM, cooked up two pork chops and a salad. Full. My wife and I listed to some Jack Kruse Podcast then hit the bed around 9:30 PM. She is finally getting all of her hormone testing back and meeting with the doc tomorrow, so we were prepping.

Day 23 of LR - Woke up at 5 AM, made 2 eggs with ham, kale, onion, and green pepper. Also took another big hunk out of my smoked salmon. Good news was I fit in a shirt that I bought a while back (and it never fit). Well it did today. That put a smile on my face. Grabbed some lunch today, salad with boudin sausage. Then for dinner cod loin with coconut, Brussel sprouts and salad. Feeling tied today. Bed at 9:10

### DaLeak — 2016-01-27T13:13:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-187451>

Day 24 of LR - up at 5:00 am. Lots of the smoked salmon for breakfast, 2 eggs, and bacon. Feeling very positive today, good nights sleep. It is 4pm and I am just now starting to get a little hungry. I think I just miss eating. Don't get me wrong, I don't need to eat, just miss the little breaks it provides you throughout the day. I am headed to the cess pool of nnEMF tomorrow, NYC. Trying to eat lots of DHA before the trip. Tonight is Shrimp. I made stir fry with lots of veggies and shrimp, lots o shrimp. It was great. Got my eggs and salmon ready for tomorrow morning road breakfast. In bed early tonight because I will be up early.

Day 25 of LR - woke up at 4 am for flight. Smoked salmon bacon and eggs. Hit flight, lots of water. Skipped lunch, wasn't hungry. Big salad for dinner. Made a mistake when ordering and chicken was breaded. Had half and then half of my wife's salmon. Mushrooms. Full. In bed at 10pm

Day 26 of LR - breakfast at bathlazar. Smoked salmon egg bacon and potatoes. I know I shouldn't eat potatoes but at $19 a plate you eat everything. I skipped the English muffin. Lunch at lure, oysters! Salmon tartar, shrimp and chicken wings. Picture coming! Dinner was at a French restaurant. I was good, but did try a sip of the wine, was amazing, and then had salad and seafood stew. Tried one bite of wife's desert. Overall screwed up a little, but hands down the healthiest eating I've ever done here and this is probably my 20th time in the city. Head back home tomorrow.

### DaLeak — 2016-01-31T02:17:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-187709>

Day 27 of LR - woke up and headed to a diner for last NYC meal. Had onions, green pepper and ham 3 egg omelette. Also had tomatoes. Lunch was a chicken apple sausage and some walnuts/cashews. Tonight was Mexican salmon with pickled pico zucchini and corn. It was amazing. Had a slight head ache, probably from all the nnEMF on the flight. Got about 30 minutes of sun in my direct eye today as well as skin. Now that I am back in Nashville, I can recover from that trip.

Day 28 of LR - First day back home. Made an egg and chicken apple sausages. Not ideal, but that is all we have in the house until I get to the store. Up at 5:00 AM, was in bed last night at 8:30 PM. Skipped lunch, was really hungry though. My little mess ups seem to be coming back to haunt me. Dinner was pulled pork, cole slaw, salad, potato, kielabasa. In bed by 8:30 PM.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-01T20:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-187873>

Day 29 of LR - Up at 6AM, had a hard time getting out of bed. Had about 6 oz. of smoked salmon and eggs. It is noon now and I am getting slightly hungry. Had a fish salad for lunch, mahi on it. Then for dinner had gf meatloaf kale and some sweet potato. In bed by 830. Really tired

Day 30 of LR - Up at 5 AM, feel a lot better today. Lots of smoked salmon and eggs this morning. Going to make it to dinner i think. Didn't really get hungry today, although I am starting to get the first waves of hunger at 4:30 PM. I would say that is a pretty good breakfast amount for me. Also, got in a walk AND 20 minutes of sitting in the sun with my shirt off. Dinner is going to be pulled pork, cucumbers, and broccoli.

I found a bump under my skin in the right side of my abdomen. When I push on it it kind of hurts and feel like a squishy marble. I am assuming I should schedule an appointment with a doctor, just wondering if anyone else has heard of this. I just found it tonight, and I feel like I would have noticed it if it were here for some time. In other words, it has to be recent. Now that I have messed with it a couple of times and had my wife confirm I'm not crazy, I can feel every so often with the slightest pain. Going to book an appt with John Scott major tomorrow. After obsessing over this lump, I think it may be related to my TST injections. Seems very close to my last injection. I will email my other doctor.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-03T23:56:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-188125>

Day 31 of LR - Up at 5:30 AM. Had a bunch of salmon, veggie omelette and a salad. Not hungry and it is 4:00 PM at time of writing. Had 10 wings for dinner and pork chili. Totally full.

Day 32 of LR - I have about three very hard and late work days starting today. Just one of those things you have to do as part of business. It is going to mess with my circadian rhythm that I have tried hard to establish. This morning I had scallops, salmon, and an egg with salad. Feel full and ready to tackle the day. Here we go. Had a bison burger on top of greens for lunch with egg and avocado. That was at 1 PM. I spent the rest of the night running up and down stairs in a theater, moving things, etc. So energy output was high. Due to the nature of my business, we didn't break for dinner till 9pm. Everyone ordered MASSIVE meals. I knew I shouldn't have eaten that late, but i decided to keep it small. I had a salad with bacon and cucumber and 5 chilled shrimps. Got to bed around midnight and woke up at 6AM. Not ideal, but I have 3 days of this then I can get back to my circadian cycle better.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-05T16:55:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-188338>

Day 33 of LR - Woke up at 6 AM, made bacon, salmon and an egg. Not hungry, didn't get enough sleep. When I don't sleep enough, I am not hungry. More running around a theater today. Was stuck downtown so grabbed a gyro salad. Not ideal, but on the plan. Also met a homeless kid who asked me to buy him lunch. I did. I was talking to him about greek food as he had never had it. Worked my ass off the rest of the day and into the night. Finally we broke for dinner around 9:30 PM, had 6 baked oysters and 5 gulf shrimp. TONS of water, in bed by 1 AM.

Day 34 of LR - Up at 6 AM. Definitely not enough sleep, but I have one more day of this then I can SLEEP again. Breakfast was onion, avocado, and jalepeno omelette with bacon. Also, I believe I crossed over to 20 lbs lost today, but can't be sure. This scale I bought sucks. Lunch was tuna / salmon salad, tons of veggies and a little piece of sweet potato. I didn't get to eat again until 8PM and all everyone wanted to order was pizza. So i got a salad, but it didn't have any protein and SO...I took a piece of pizza, scraped the toppings off and put them on my salad. Again not ideal, but it worked. In bed at 1 AM after we cleaned out. MY hell week is over.

Day of 35 of LR - Up at 7 AM, feeling tired but glad that that week is over. Actually got in the sun almost everyday this week, but today was 45 minutes in 45 degrees without a shirt and shorts. SO CT and sun. Felt wonderful. Someone commented at the show that it looks like I went to the beach, I must look like I have a tan. Breakfast was eggs, tuna salads, bacon and kale. Totally full at 12:18 PM. Never ate lunch. Headed into Super Bowl Party with a game plan. Chicken wings, veggies, and La Croix. Didn't drink anything but water and had a meatball and sausages. All in all it was a nice effort. It IS possible to go to a party and not eat EVERYTHING in sight.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-09T02:54:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-188587>

Day 36 of LR - Got up at 5:30 AM after going to be at 9PM. Trying to catch back up on sleep now. Breakfast was Tuna salad and eggs. Lunch was fajita salad with steak and chicken. I didn't need any dinner. I know this sounds silly, but I feel like my brain is working at a higher level now. Able to think of better ideas. Also, I am headed to New Orleans in the morning for a week meeting with one of my clients. Should be able to get some GREAT seafood down there.

Day 37 of LR - Breakfast was a ton shrimp with an egg. Lunch was crawfish in New Orleans with corn and a salad. Dinner was sausage and green beans.

Day 38 of LR - Breakfast was some sausages, not enough, but was fine till lunch. Lunch was 6 oysters RAW and 1lb of boiled shrimp. Dinner was some more sausages, salad, corn on the cob.

Day 39 of LR - Eggs and Sausage, then had 12 oysters for lunch with a side salad. Was AWESOME. Wasn't hungry for dinner, but had celery and PB.

Day 40 of LR - Had to skip breakfast and get on the road back to home. Lunch was a burger with no bun and french fries. I know, not the best. Now I am back home. Dinner was a salad and chicken. Again, just trying to get home and back on my plan. I crushed seafood down in New Orleans, so hopefully that will carry through my non seafood day.

Day 41 of LR - Bacon, sausage, ham and egg with Gouda. Lunch was lunch meat roll ups with lettuce and a few chips. Trying to get back on a ton of water. Dinner was brocolli, chicken, shrimp and a little bit of rice. Also had a tiny bit of frozen yogurt. A little treat to celebrate 6 weeks.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-14T13:10:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-189008>

Day 42 of LR - Had some smoked salmon, bacon, and eggs. Trying to get back on track after some mishaps like FROZEN Yogurt. I clearly wasn't thinking. Lunch was a salad with chicken on it and some chips. Dinner was steak, lobster, mashed potatoes and asparagus that my FIL made. It was awesome, even had some shrimp. All this slight cheating is going to catch up with me.

Day 43 of LR - Good sleep, had some sausage and eggs. Had a couple sips of the new stevia / cane sugar coke as I was at Coke Headquarters. Lunch was at Chik fil a, we were traveling. Had grilled chicken nuggets with some waffle fries. AND dinner is where I blew it BIG. I had 2 margaritas and corn chips / guac. All of this cheating took over my brain and I made terrible choices. I have to get back on track. I will chalk this up to 6 week celebration point, now back on the plan.

Day 44 of LR - Listened to more podcasts about CT and I really need to do a better job. Recommitting myself to the process. Got great sleep, really working on circadian rhythm. Had my tuna fish and salmon salad with two eggs this morning. We are back home and getting into my routine again. Too much travel really screwed me up. Skipped lunch, was hungry though and made it to dinner. Stuck at a comedy club, best choice was fried shrimp. Bad planning day.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-18T16:13:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-189307>

Day 45 of LR - Things back up to speed. Breakfast was eggs and tuna. lunch was celery and PB and dinner was founder with chicken wings, beets, peas, and cauliflower. It was an awesome meal. I poached the fish in butter, onion and lemon. Had a square of dark chocolate. In bed by 9PM.

Day 46 of LR - Up and at em at 5:15 AM. Had 2 eggs, bacon and avocado with grilled onions and jalepeno. Amazing breakfast. I can't remember the rest of this day.

Day 47 o LR - Up at 5:00 AM, was able to grab some sunlight yesterday as it was out. Also, pulling my focus off of food but still want to keep track of it. Also, tried to be outside a lot, it was pretty and windy. Also tried to work on my laptop out side. I need to get some EMF blocker for when I do that. Food was salad for dinner and lunch was half a turkey sandwich. Breakfast was mahi and eggs.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-18T18:12:45+0200

### Jeanne — 2016-02-20T23:04:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-189473>

> [DaLeak said:](/goto/post?id=189324)
>
> My first goal was getting the behavior set, food, self reflecting daily, but you are correct. I need to take it to the next level. I have been focused on light, just not chronicling it. I will start including that as well as I need to get better about ct frequency.

I used to be a food/supplement focused person. Now, I just try to get seafood regularly and don't really think about food other than that. I focus on drinking a lot of water and getting my light as optimum as possible. When I changed my focus from food to LIGHT I saw the largest benefits! Amazing, really. AM sunlight as soon as the sun comes up will rock your world .....and eliminating blue light at night of course.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-20T23:43:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-189476>

Day 48 - Up at 6 AM, got a full nights sleep. It felt fantastic. Cooked up some bacon, eggs and avacado this morning. Then tried to get outside for a few sun breaks that came through the clouds. I also took my glasses off and got sunlight in my eyes...for the brief moment it popped through. Lunch was a greek salad with chicken and I had a few bites of chocolate cake. Dinner was chicken, noodles and drinks.

I have been slowly slipping off the wagon and not happy about that. I am still trying to stay focused on seafood and light cycles and in that regard I give myself a C. I can do much better. The night time routine is going pretty well, put the kids to bed than slip on the glasses and try to keep the light down. I don't watch TV anymore at night and I try to limit computer usage as well. I have been diving into the blog reading. I need to get better about morning sun. I keep thinking the sun needs to be out, but it is more just morning light in the eye. Something to work towards. The other thing I need to do is lower my nnEMF. I know I have stated that in the past, but I haven't done some simple things I could be doing. I am leaving for LA soon and when I get back that is a focus.

Day 49 of LR - Bacon, egg, L - chicken, broccoli, D pizza salad, wine. I made sure I got outside today and I stayed out, bare feet in the grass for the majority of the day. Yes, i had some adult beverages, yes I had pizza, one slice, but nailed my light yesterday. Up early, outside, no glasses and grounded for hours. Lots of spring water. In bed at 8:30 pm for early morning flight to LA.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-22T16:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-189568>

Day 50 of LR - woke up at 5 AM, fully rested. Showered, got in the car to airport and ate about 20 shrimp and mineral water on the way. Hoping that holds me over for quite some time. First thing I did when I got to LA was walk outside, take off my glasses and stand in the sun for 10 minutes. Then driving around all day taking meetings. Lunch was pizza and salad. Dinner was shrimp fajitas no tortilla. Margaritas and beer tonight, sadly it is part of the job. Bed was 11:30pm

Day 51 of LR - Up at 5 AM, when the sun came up, I was outside with glasses off. Ate breakfast in the sun. Chorizo burrito with avocado. Out in the LA sun a lot today. It is hot. Burger and fries for lunch. My night light wasn't great as I had to go see a show, but did get in bed by 11 PM.

Day 52 of LR - Up by 6 AM, showered and went for a walk down to the Santa Monica beach. At some point sat on a bench with my glasses off getting as much sun as possible. Then went to breakfast and had salmon and eggs with a salad, out side in the sun. Lunch was fish and chips. Then hit a plane back home and in bed by 11PM.

### DaLeak — 2016-02-26T00:01:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-189790>

Day 53 of LR - Very tired this morning at 6 AM. My trip caught up with me, plus I started getting a cold. It is raining today so I sat in front of my light this morning. Going to hit the sack early tonight. Not much sun of UV to be had today. Breakfast was tuna and eggs with bacon. Lunch was sushi and salmon salad. Doing solid on seafood lately. Going to get the glasses on early tonight and try to get in bed around 8:30pm. We are supposed to have sun tomorrow.

Day 54 of LR - This sickness is coming on. It is like a bad cold now.

Day 55 of LR - Totally out for the count. 1o0.7 fever.

Day 56 of LR - Slept a ton, got outside and laid in the sun for an hour and suprisingly, a lot of the sickness feeling went away. Had oysters and crabcakes for dinner. Glasses on by 7:30pm and hit the sack at 8 PM

Day 57 - Up at 5 AM, feel about 80% today. Looking at morning light 5 minutes. eggs, crab cakes, little bit of potato. Lunch was Bison Burger on top of Salad. In the afternoon I was able to take my shirt off and glasses and sit in the sun for 45 minutes. Had soup for dinner. Really focused on water again. I am putting water in the sunlight all day in clear glass bottles. I am not sure it does anything, still learning about structured water, but I feel it can't hurt. Glasses on at night and in bed by 9 PM. Woke up at 2:30 AM, awake till 4 am, then back to sleep till 6 am.

### DaLeak — 2016-03-01T17:11:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-190022>

Day 58 - Tired this morning, but had a piece of mahi, bacon, and egg. Out for a walk in the morning mist. If that sun peaks out again, I will try and get in it.

Day 61 - So I missed a few days, but damn if this sickness wouldn't leave me. I finally feel pretty decent on Friday and got some work done. Was outside a lot today. Been cranking on Seafood even though I haven't felt good. Been focused on water as well.

Day 64 - Monday - been getting 9 hours, focusing on getting outside and getting sun. Then hit the glasses at night. They make me super tired by 8:30pm. So I usually hit the sack around then. Then up at 5:30. Eating was not great over the weekend, but had a piece of salmon, egg and bacon this morning. Going to try and get ketogenic again this week. Got outside a little bit today, but not enough. I DID however manage to put the wifi unit on a timed outlet and we have been sleeping through the night. Lunch was jumbalaya and dinner was pork chops, salad and corn. We did eat dinner outside on the deck. In bed by 9 PM

### DaLeak — 2016-03-08T16:52:41+0200

Day 65 - Up at 5:45 AM sleeping awesome. I also managed to take both WIFI nest thermostats down over the weekend and put in the old school ones with no nnEMF. Making slow changes. I have been putting my water in clear glass bottles outside in the sun. I have no idea if this does ANYTHING of value, but doing it anyway. Breakfast was pork chop and eggs. Going to make sure I get some sun today, because it is supposed to come out today.

Day 66 - Was inside most of the day, but got the glasses on at kids bedtime. Tried to eat less than normal. Water was OK. Need to get more sun.

Day 67 - Got enough sleep last night. Up and at em at 5AM. Smoked salmon, egg and potato. Sat outside with glasses off for 10 minutes looking at sun. Listend to the latest podcast with Stephanie Seneff and Calyx Performance...mind was blown again.

Day 70 - We had a little sun break today at 4pm so I say down in it and had a Moscow mule. Feet in the wet grass.

Day 71 - Headed in to get my yearly physical with Scott Major. Rainy and gross, but great conversation.

Day 72 - The sun is back! I was out in it. The last of this cold is hanging on but almost out the door. Hoping the sun light kicks it ass out the door.

### DaLeak — 2016-03-18T14:14:53+0200

Day 74 - Major Sun advancements today! waking my eyes up in the day, and protecting them at night.

75,76,77 - Very focused on sun because we actually had a lot of it. I get up, get outside with the glasses off, drink a ton of water and ground for 5 minutes. Working on getting back into the seafood train and keeping with the glasses. My sleep has been awesome lately so I am hoping with that comes some health advancements.

Month 3 - Things are going well, I am not losing weight, but I am not gaining weight and I am able to really eat what I want. I try and focus on sunlight, grounding, seafood, and limiting WIFI.

Been getting outside so much in the sun that people ask me all the time if I just got back from Florida. I guess I am tanning a lot. My Vitamin D went from 46 to 55 last check up, so we are getting there. Staying with the seafood, staying with light. I think the part I am failing at the most is the WIFI / nnEMF. Things to work on. The interesting part in all of this is I lost 25 pounds and haven't really gained it back at all. I haven't lost more weight, but i haven't put any back on and that is a first. 13 pounds down from the holidays in what amounts to a little over a week. Keeping up with the good light, good seafood, and trying to my best to limit nnEMF. Here is what amazes me is my sleep has gotten even better. 8.5 hours last night! I'm usually a 7 to 7.5 hour guy. Going to keep pushing forward. it is warmer today here so the tub is heating up a bit. Probably do 60 degrees today. Breakfast was fish burger, 2 eggs. 1 L of water (RO).

### DaLeak — 2017-01-19T03:20:35+0200

### DaLeak — 2017-01-27T18:10:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-207440>

Day 27 of epi-Paleo round 2 and doing great. Around 24 lbs down. THe CT combined with the epi Paleo is the secret for me. It KRUSE's through the fat and I wake up almost 1 pound lighter almost every morning. So the question is, how does one stay in this state through out the year. This is my crux over and over again. Trying to put things in place so I can stay on the wagon. It isn't about falling off the wagon, i'm ok with that every once in a while, its getting back on it!

### DaLeak — 2017-02-14T16:43:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-me-daleak.17293/post-208568>

Haven't been back in a couple weeks, but things are going well. I have stabilized around 30 lbs. lost. My body doesn't want to let go of more weight yet. I'm ok with it, I feel better in a day eating this way and minding my light. I don't necessarily crave the things I used to as hard. Going to see the doc tomorrow and get blood work drawn up. Will be interesting to see what the changes look like.

### Melanie Procter — 2015-12-23T06:44:15+0200

I don't mean to be silly. I am just synthesizing all the material of the last blogs in a playful way to really get a grasp of the mechanisms we have been discussing and also to make context even more clear.

If I was to add a few bells and whistles to this "Magnetic Thought Boat", I would pipe in some brainwave entrainment binaural beats in delta or theta. I say this to incorporate what Rubin and and Dr. Kruse discussed in the May webinar and Q and A. If you guys remember, they ended up the conversation talking about meditation.

In the field of education, we always end a module with requiring students to do a task based project such as building or inventing something to synthesize the information. Invention is a powerful form of learning.

So the Magnetic Thought Boat is my synthesis of the material from the last 7 months.

Anybody else have an invention? I would love to hear your thoughts?

I do this little invention exercise not to make light of the material, but instead to build up our understanding of it. We are all surrounded by people who are very sick. It is obvious that our understanding of the material is critical as we are ALL starfish. The more we play and invent with the material, the more we can help friends and family tailor this information to their personal context.

# Thread: What frequencies of uv does the body use?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-frequencies-of-uv-does-the-body-use.17295/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RyanMSauce — 2015-12-23T07:44:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-frequencies-of-uv-does-the-body-use.17295/post-184809>

I have been thinking about this for a while and it is time for me to formulate some questions. I wish to dig deeper into this and I would like people to add their thoughts.

In the reading I have done I have seen the frequency of 380nm being mentioned as a healing frequency of light. Popp showed that 380nm was the frequency that cancer causing compounds would absorb and scramble.

I read somewhere online that for vitamin d production .. "irradiation between 275 - 310 nm (nanometers: 1 nm equals 1 millionth of a millimeter); however, Vitamin D is susceptible to degradation from UV irradiation between 260 - 290 nm, so the optimal wavelengths for Vitamin D production are between 290 - 300 nm."

I have quoted in a post by Jack saying that 290nm to 320nm UV light is damaging. 290 - 300 nm seems to be the optimal range for vitamin d production so I guess we only need so much of it? I have read that UVB is only strong at peak hours during the day.

Are there any more specific frequencies of UV light that benefit us or harm us?
If I were to get a lot of UV lights for my house would the 380nm frequency be the single best one to use if I could?

"tyrosine absorbs UV light 260-289 nm" I read this from jacks post but that is in the UVC range. I thought that UVC light is almost all blocked out so I don't see how the body would use that.

"In blood, hemoglobin’s peak light absorption peaks at 280 mm 420nm 540nm and 580nm and has sharp cutoff at 600 nm." Jack talking about some the frequencies in the quantlet. So here are some other frequencies that we absorb well.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T14:38:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-frequencies-of-uv-does-the-body-use.17295/post-184818>

Your post assumes 290-320 nm is damaging in a bad way......what if I told you it is damage with a good cononation? Because it is.
In this case I do not mind repeating myself many times.............normal growth requires the initial stimulus of damage to activate the regeneration programs in life. Uncontrolled growth results from a loss of the negative feedback control. This drives ubiquitination rates of proteins and leads to things like cancer.

260-289 is not UVC.............and you should also remember that 270 nm is the key frequency Pollack mentions in his book..............nobody seems to have caught that. Why is that important over the normal IR bands that water is the ideal chromophore? IR bands build the ideal batter from light and 270 nm is where water build amazing nano molecular motors in EZ capable of doing physiologic work. Aquaphotoomics...........google it.

**Repetition is ideal for learning or setting a metronome. It's not beating a dead horse; it's watering a live seed until it changes or see the patterns that life uses to organize nature's chaotic play ground.**

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T14:43:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-frequencies-of-uv-does-the-body-use.17295/post-184820>

Growth is necessary in the eye for wellness (dopamine and melatonin), but it is always stimulated by damage (ROS) and limited by regeneration.

Dopamine and melatonin contain aromatic amino acids that absorb UV light 260-289nm.

When the sun rises it always has equal parts blue green and red in its spectrum.

Blue makes ROS. Red balances that effect...............

What regenerates all three photoreceptors in the eye? Melatonin...............which is made from what frequency again? UV.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T14:57:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-frequencies-of-uv-does-the-body-use.17295/post-184822>

To beat this dead horse further to get you to understand better consider the following:

isothiocyanates in food sources have been shown in many nutrition studies to be helpful in preventing cancer. Why? Moreover, these compounds are now being used to develop new drugs that use ELF-UV. Why? They are now the basis of a drug in development that replaces the sulfur bonds with selenium, with far stronger potency against melanoma and many skin cancers..............

Wait a minute.........did Dr. Kruse just say we are using UV light to prevent cancer using electrons from things? YES. HOW?

Selnium is just behind sulfur on the periodic table so is the drug industry taking a page out of semiconducting engineers in assuming the noted increase in potency is a quantum effect as the outer shell of electrons of selenium are more loosely held than those of sulfur – right?

My response: Yes they are. Replacing sulfur with selenium makes a tissue more likely to refill itself with UV light. This is why selenoproteins have a huge anticancer effect. Why? Because selenium can convert light into electricity. The DC electric current is the key to biologic regeneration. Selenium is used in industry for solar panels because of this ability. It is a wide band semiconductor. Selenium’s ability to conduct electricity increases as its exposure to light increases. This means it produces more DC electric current when it absorb UV light. Remember learning long ago in one of my early blogs about the anti carcinogenic value of selenium and Vit D3?

What are foods with the highest selenium level…....oysters, followed by tuna. How can you help your own cancer diagnosis with this information? Stop allowing your cells to leak light to become cancer cells. Acute blue light from AM sun is the damaging stimulus we need to begin to repair the melanopsin and rods receptors we used at night. If your daytime environment however is loaded with massive amounts of blue this will destroy ocular melatonin and no regeneration can occur. Its even worse when you also subtract UV/IR to this mix. This is what fake light does in our retina daily. Make sense now?

Chronic blue light ROS without healing UV and IR = epi-oncogenesis. WHY? from leaking excessive amounts of ELF-UV light from cells. When you stop the leak and add back UV/IR you begin to engage the regeneration programs in biology designed to refill cells with UV. Its pretty simple. Pseudohypoxia is a sign of ELF-UV release. Read Roeland van Wick book for all the details. Cancer growth is uncontrolled. Normal growth is controlled damage and chaos. What controls it? UV light. Growth is necessary in biology but uncontrolled growth is not; but any kind of growth is always stimulated by damage (ROS), but it is normally under feedback control of UV/IR light to stimulate regeneration.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T15:06:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-frequencies-of-uv-does-the-body-use.17295/post-184823>

Food is just data on sunlight’s photonic power that it packs into food as it travels around our star. So can food hurt us if we do not have enough electrons in our cell membranes or mitochondria? Yes. Why? Highly powered electrons (carbs) can short out our cell membranes if they have no charge in them. Charge is built best by UV light via the eye and skin. We need to assimilate it. We need an adequate balanced diet of light in the game of life to deal with food electrons with high power................

Life organizes chaos with a deft recipe. What we call chaos are just patterns we haven't recognized. Our mind is built for pattern recognition but nature's trends are not fashionable. What we call random are just patterns we can't decipher. What we can't conceive of we call nonsense. What we can't visualize we call gibberish, but chaos is what we've lost touch with. Chaos is the law of nature; where understanding the order within it, is the dream of science's ego.

### Eugenia — 2015-12-23T23:07:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eskimo-vs-okinawan.17298/post-184845>

Serious, no-troll question, I just have curiosity about this:

Why traditional Okinawan people lived longer than the Eskimo people?

Eskimos ate way more seafood, while Okinawan traditional diet had fish 2-3 times a week, and pork only occasionally (on festive times). On top of that, 75% of the Okinawan diet was sweet potatoes, quite some soy, in other words, they were loaded with carbs year-round (not necessarily seasonally).

Finally, Jack had said that the best place to live is near the North Pole for CT and other reasons, so that would give the advantage to the Eskimos, but instead, the Okinawan lived longer. Anyone can explain this?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-23T23:09:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eskimo-vs-okinawan.17298/post-184847>

Jack never said living near the pole is best...........In fact Jack's biohack from the Dopamine Rx brought me to the Gulf and not the 59th parallel. So your understanding of my work is your perception and not my reality.

### Eugenia — 2015-12-24T00:02:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eskimo-vs-okinawan.17298/post-184849>

Now, maybe you've meant that "if humans were adapted to be a different kind of species, that place would be best", and not "where current humans should be living", so I apologize if that's the confusion about the heart of the matter. But if that's the case, and Eskimos never actually adapted to the point you think they should have (given their non-prolonged lifespans), then the rest of us have no chance of adapting more than they did, in which case, it renders the North pointless (as per your forum post). In that case, indeed, places with more UV/sun are best.

# Thread: Bio-Physics theory #1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-theory-1.17302/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-24T16:01:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-theory-1.17302/post-184865>

New tradition for this Xmas eve: Every so often I will post some kind of fun problem to consider that has a deep biophysical implication for your consideration.

In the 1978 film, [_Superman_](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0078346/), the Man of Steel, grief-stricken by the death of Lois Lane, travels backwards in time by flying at speeds that cause the Earth to spin backwards. Of course, time travel using this method is science fiction, but could the Last Son of Krypton reverse the rotation of an entire planet?

Believe it or not it is theoretically possible. Here is the Cite:
[https://physics.le.ac.uk/journals/index.php/pst/article/view/809/587](https://physics.le.ac.uk/journals/index.php/pst/article/view/809/587)

Why is it possible? According to Einstein’s General Theory of Relativity, a moving object increases in mass as it approaches the speed of light. If Superman were to fly fast enough, his relativistic mass would be 13.7 million times his normal mass. So the lesson here is that the faster a particle or wave travels the more mass that particle or wave acquires. Doing so would generate a gravitational field sufficient to change the polarity of the Earth’s spin.

Unfortunately, that gravitational field would also attract nearby asteroids because energy is being turned into mass and mass increases gravitational force. This also means that anything with mass or momentum would fall to it. The end game for superman would be Lois would be alive but Earth would likely be extinct. His action to save a life would pummel Superman’s adopted planet into extinction by making it a target. So spread the word: Do not try this bio hack at home.

Theoretical considerations:

1\. Do you think all the orbiting garbage above our head have an effect on our planet in some way because of this science?
2\. If the reverse spinning of Earth at high speeds can do this to time what would the effect of a changing or flipping magnetic field have on the very same process? Is this why magnetic flips happen every 4-6 thousand years?
3\. Since light has no mass but does have momentum and is clearly affected by this situation what happens to light frequencies in and around our mitochondria on ECT as it increases its speed? Does this affect the underlying mitochondrial distance to the cell's nucleus in some way biology is missing? How would the effect manifest?
4\. Could this be the mechanism that a cell uses to make neutrino's controllable? Think Dec 2015 webinar for a moment. Since neutrino's have momentum and a small bit of mass anytime things are sped up they would be greatly affected wouldn't they? To measure these effects we would need a quantum imprinting device.........could this be one of unknown functions of EZ water that Montagnier's water memory experiments allowed us to the pull the veil back upon?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-24T17:44:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-theory-1.17302/post-184869>

traveling at light speed changes many things.....In the protein and lipid rafts in us. Proteins undergo aberration due to length contraction and colors change and we begin to sense time dilation. Clocks running at the speed of light have their clocks slow...............Light speed also slows in different forms of matter.............and as it slows its frequency must shift to longer wavelengths........and as this happens light color changes by definition........so purple light that hits our surfaces is never purple at deeper levels on our surface...........do you think this shifting has cellular implications?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-24T17:48:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-theory-1.17302/post-184870>

Light slows down when it goes thru glass allowing us to concentrate light to see galaxies...........what happens to light that hits out lens then before it strikes our retina. Does that have a time implication?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-24T17:52:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-theory-1.17302/post-184871>

What happens when we slow light? Do we create a new regime of nature? As we slow light we lose mass..............Stop and think

If we absorb 186,000 MPH UV light.............on our skin and eye...........and slow it.......does what ever slows it lose mass?

Is this why people really gain and lose weight? Is it due to their ability to slow light better than an obese person? So it raises the point.............how does biology slow light?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-24T19:00:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-theory-1.17302/post-184874>

Does space continually change time on Earth?

Yes, I believe it does and we remain ignorant of it. How? Comets and asteroids.
1\. The great flood on earth is in many myths in many far different cultures.........how do we make global floods? Asteroids hitting into the ocean completely. It is followed by massive rains and hurricane like conditions which eventually block sunlight from hitting the Earth surface. This blocks photosynthesis and generally cools things on the surface..........that cooling will stress life because of the simultaneous absence of sunlight. When sunlight is a constant presence cooling at the same time allows for more sun light to be absorbed especially in the UV range.......due to gravitational lensing. Cooling condenses matter making it have more mass per surface area. This increases gravity. So if this great flood happens........where would we see evidence? Burckle crater? Indian ocean at the depth of 13,500 feet. So if this happened we should see chevrons on coastline close to the crater. We should also see fossils from the ocean bottom on land today. On Madagascar both have been shown. This contradicts many astronomers beliefs. But shocked crystal will clinch it. On problem......the crates is 13,500 ft below the oceans surface............where else should we look? How about on monolithic rocks on the surface of Earth where no water has been for many thousands of years. We should see evidence of water erosion at the foundation and not wind erosion..............Do we have evidence for that anywhere east of Madagascar on Africa?
2\. Yep..........the Sphinx is in the Sahara desert and there has been no rain there for 10,000 years, yet at the base of the sphinx there is significant evidence for water erosion............from flooding but at the top of the Sphinx by its head we see no water erosion.
3\. Implications? Is there a cycle on Earth that is associated with cooling surface temperatures that disrupt life on its surface? Does it happen enough times that it would be coded for in nucleic acids of living things or in ice core samples?
4\. Does this imply the Sphinx is way older than we all think? And a different group of older humans made it cause it could not have been the Egyptians. Might they have stumbled in the Sphinx themselves and this became the focus of their culture obsession with he sun? The Sphinx looks eastward at the sunrise daily.
5\. 536 AD the sun got dim for 18 months. It shined for only 4 hours a day. Tree ring evidence show there was a global cooling. Theory blames volcano ash clouds.............but could it have been asteroid in the late yet again? Gulf of Carpentaria has two craters with shock crystals........and there are chevrons associated with it. What about the Earth's frozen filing cabinet? What do Ice core samples in greenland say? They has magnetized spherules that match the gulf of Carpenteria in Australia. These things do not have be asteroids. Comets can do it. in 1992 Comet Shoemaker Levy 9 did this on Jupiter. On Earth, we also have evidence of tropical diatoms in Greenland.............might this be more evidence that asteroids/comets hit us more than we think to cool the surface?
6\. All impacts are not equivalent. High and low velocity impact craters do different things. anything the puts particulate matter in the atmosphere slows sunlight travels to the surface and cools the planet below...........
7\. 11,000 years ago the clovis people went MIA in North America. The mega fauna also went extinct at the same time This is also the date when it appears water surrounded the Sphinx base......................so what happened? Approximately 13,000 yrs ago the planet got cold again We know this from ice cores.........Younger Dryas is the name........another ice age came out of no where when the earth was warming. Why would it change on a dime? Could it have been another asteroid or comet immediately changing the quantum yield of sunlight? The Laurentide ice sheet is made of water that was frozen........By melting at impact it could have caused fresh water mixing with sea water to alter the thermohaline currents in the oceans to cool North America and cause a flood.............The Sheridan cave nano diamonds showed impact diamonds and meteor fragments in North America...........These are powerful evidence for a cosmic impact. Science does not believe there was a Clovis asteroid because there is no crater but would you expect a crater to be left if it hit a massive ice sheet? Wouldn't the ice sheet have melted ? Or could it have been an air burst to melt the water which would not leave a crater. This happened in 1908 in Siberia.
8\. what is not arguable is that climate changed on a dime...........you think life has a program built in for this kind of thing? Think CT 6.
9\. June 2002 a small meteorite created an air burst............at this time India and Pakistan were fighting over Kashmir...........many people thought one of them might have launched a weapon. The meteor happened close to the right latitude...........it is know as the East Mediterranean event.........most of these are completely unexpected...........and most of these are small velocity events..........and they can change climate and alter human history.
10\. 312 AD..........Romans are in civil war. Constantine was at war and at this time he and his soldiers were all pagans..........what made him convert to a Catholic? He signed the Edit of Milan because during battle he saw a flash of light in the atmosphere that likely was a meteor flashing in the atmosphere right before the definitive battle......................After seeing it, he instructed all his soldiers to paint this light vision on their shields and they all went on to win the civil war...............So do you think things from from Earth cannot change the course of human evolution on this planet in ways that seem far fetched? None of these examples seem connected.........Without this one event what would the modern world view of humans have become? It still is considered one of the most pivotal events in human history that is documented............
**_Human logic dictates the cosmos makes sense. But nature is always one step beyond logic, is it? Will you remember this the next time you hear something that just appears unfathomable?_**

# Thread: Has Jack written about the color blind?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-jack-written-about-the-color-blind.17304/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty — 2015-12-24T19:48:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-jack-written-about-the-color-blind.17304/post-184882>

I was told as a kid I am "red/green deficient", which is a mild version of color blind. Uncles, cousin & grandpa were totally color blind. I am just really curious if this has any affect on light absorbtion as it could make me rethink my "normal" ways.

Any links to relevant info would be welcome. I have been following Jack's blog for a long time and cNtnrecall this coming up, my search has yielded nothing, but once upon a time I asked Jack if he' d touch on "color blind" and he said he'd touch on a lot of things! Lol

I have learned so much since that question, yet I'm no closer to the color blind answer. I am turning to smarter minds here, as I might have missed it or not put two and two together somewhere.

Cheers!

"And this ends Krusty's non-denominational holiday fun fest. So have a Merry Christmas, a Happy Chanukah, a Krazy Kwanzaa, a Tip Top Tet, and a solemn, eventful Ramadan. Now, over to my god, our sponsors." [Krusty the Clown](http://www.imdb.com/name/nm0144657/?ref_=tt_trv_qu)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-24T21:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-jack-written-about-the-color-blind.17304/post-184886>

Green means cones are not great for color vision or camera function..........Red deficit is not good.......red increase ATP and the motor portions of water molecules and networks to offset the ROS of blue. So missing red means you cannot regenerate as well and you need to really dial down the chronic blue exposure.........and add back UV. This puts you at risk for AMD glaucoma, cataracts, mental disorders, Adrenal fatigue, depression, and AI's and lots of gut issues.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-24T22:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-jack-written-about-the-color-blind.17304/post-184887>

The most common types of hereditary color blindness are due to the loss or limited function of red cone (known as protan) or green cone (deutran) photopigments. This kind of color blindness is commonly referred to as red-green color blindness. This is usually tied to the X chromosome

-   **Protanomaly:** In males with protanomaly, the red cone photopigment is abnormal. Red, orange, and yellow appear greener and colors are not as bright. This condition is mild and doesn’t usually interfere with daily living. Protanomaly is an X-linked disorder estimated to affect 1 percent of males.
-   **Protanopia:** In males with protanopia, there are no working red cone cells. Red appears as black. Certain shades of orange, yellow, and green all appear as yellow. Protanopia is an X-linked disorder that is estimated to affect 1 percent of males.
-   **Deuteranomaly:** In males with deuteranomaly, the green cone photopigment is abnormal. Yellow and green appear redder and it is difficult to tell violet from blue. This condition is mild and doesn’t interfere with daily living. Deuteranomaly is the most common form of color blindness and is an X-linked disorder affecting 5 percent of males.
-   **Deuteranopia:** In males with deuteranopia, there are no working green cone cells. They tend to see reds as brownish-yellow and greens as beige. Deuteranopia is an X-linked disorder that affects about 1 percent of males.

So not all color blindness is the same..........and neither will their symptoms............it will lead to specific risk factors.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-24T22:14:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-jack-written-about-the-color-blind.17304/post-184888>

and specific disease based upon the subtracted frequencies........

### Matty — 2015-12-24T22:52:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-jack-written-about-the-color-blind.17304/post-184889>

Dang. Worse than I thought.
Thank you Jack, more to consider I suppose. Mine is mild, maybe the first one. I already avoid/block most blue light and add UV, but I need more time in the sun. Thanks again Jack, and Merry Christmas, Happy holidays!

# Thread: NO and cytochrome 3: COx
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-and-cytochrome-3-cox.17305/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-25T02:14:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-and-cytochrome-3-cox.17305/post-184900>

NO is a Janus molecule, since depending on its intracellular concentration, it may act as a physiological signaling molecule or as a toxic agent. This is also applicable to its reactions with the respiratory chain, which may be physiologically modulated at relatively low NO mitochondrial fluxes, but severely affected by higher, toxic concentrations. It is known that NO inhibition of mitochondrial respiration has a role in cell death, by either necrotic or apoptotic mechanisms. Moreover, NO toxicity in the extreme is mediated by the reactive peroxinitrite species (ONOO−), formed by the reaction of NO with the superoxide anion; unlike NO, peroxinitrite is indeed causing an irreversible inactivation of respiration. A strict control of intracellular NO may therefore be welcome by the cell.

NO is induced on surface with UV light exposure..........UV light is only present certain times of the day and it is also released by cells under some stress. Since NO slows ECT it can limit apoptotic damage that leads to tissue atrophy.......if we have no NO we see diseases where tissue atrophy occurs: AMD, Alzheimers, Parkinson's disease are some examples of diseases with losses of tissue density in places.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-25T02:15:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-and-cytochrome-3-cox.17305/post-184902>

-   S. Moncada, J.D. Erusalimsky
-   Does nitric oxide modulate mitochondrial energy generation and apoptosis?

-   Nat. Rev., Mol. Cell Biol., 3 (2002), pp. 214–220

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-25T02:16:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-and-cytochrome-3-cox.17305/post-184903>

-   G.C. Brown, V. Borutaité
-   Nitric oxide inhibition of mitochondrial respiration and its role in cell death

-   Free Radic. Biol. Med., 33 (2002), pp. 1440–1450

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-25T02:27:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-and-cytochrome-3-cox.17305/post-184905>

Regardless of the detailed NO• binding mechanism to cytochrome C heme protein, science cannot explain why in CBS, CO preferentially binds to the cysteine side, whereas NO• seems to bind to the histidine side. My answer is tied to how UV light interacts with aromatic amino acids. tyrosine has side chains that are photoelectrically excited by UV light to separate S-S bonds. This makes H2S free radical which likely guides the reaction sites. Despite the lack of an unequivocal explanation for this, it should be pointed out that, at equilibrium, binding of NO• and CO on opposite sides of the heme is not unprecedented, having been reported for bacterial cytochrome _c. This would make sense because bacterial do not use UV light the same way eukaryotes do because of a lack of DHA in their cell membranes_

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-25T02:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-and-cytochrome-3-cox.17305/post-184906>

Why might things be built this way? Sunlight normally stimulates NO release. This occurs on short timescales to vasodilate surface vessels to get UV light to bind to porphyrins and hemoglobin bonds oxygen. It turns out oxy Hb turns off NO flux. It limits NO affect on cytochrome c.

So why might we be built this way? To deliver oxygen to tissues that dont have a ton of mitochondria and/or blood flow/ and or light exposure. Consider under normal metabolic conditions, a considerable fraction of Cox oxidase (cyto C) is inhibited.....what would this do? It would have the effect of extending O2 availability either to cells at different distances from capillaries, or even within the mitochondrion, allowing O2 utilization by as many as possible respiratory enzyme complexes, differently distributed in space within the organelle. This would limit pseudohypoxia............Remember that oxygen is drawn to things naturally that are magnetic because O2 is paramagnetic.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-25T02:49:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-and-cytochrome-3-cox.17305/post-184907>

This helps explain how UV light exposure can stimulate venous O2 increase within cells.........heretofore medicine does not know this is possible........but this is the detailed mechanism of how it can happen.

# Thread: Dry Mouth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2015-12-28T23:13:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185053>

This problem has plagued me for years, I assumed if my health / gastroparesis got better, so would it, however this hasn't been the case.

It doesn't get better with fluid intake but actually maybe worse making me think possible electrolyte issue. It means I have to speak in short sentences and avoid the letter "s" at most times. It also gets worse with CT (ADH issue?) and is present high carb and low carb...

I have recently started taking a total of 1.5 tbsp of Low Salt per day with meals to see if the increased potassium helps (roughly 3g elemental potassium and 1g sodium) and will keep this thread updated. Anyone know roughly how long it would take to get potassium back up? I know it's absorbed easy but it's how well it can get into cells...

Maybe my body is just not hydrating well. I have noticed great improvements in all areas since adding IR / UV except this one. What could be causing it?

### fitness@home — 2015-12-29T02:27:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185058>

Sean, are you on any prescription meds that may have dry mouth as a side effect?

Many moons ago I got dry mouth from Paxil and it was at a very low dosage.

Just a thought... If you are on anything prescription look up the side effects.

### seanb4 — 2015-12-29T10:53:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185072>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=185058)
>
> Sean, are you on any prescription meds that may have dry mouth as a side effect?
>
> Many moons ago I got dry mouth from Paxil and it was at a very low dosage.
>
> Just a thought... If you are on anything prescription look up the side effects.

No meds. I've looked at the causes of dry mouth as well, others are

-   **medication**
-   **a blocked nose**
-   [**diabetes**](http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/Diabetes/Pages/Diabetes.aspx)
-   **[radiotherapy](http://www.nhs.uk/conditions/Radiotherapy/Pages/Introduction.aspx) to the head and neck**
-   **[Sjögren's syndrome](http://www.nhs.uk/conditions/Sjogrens-syndrome/Pages/Introduction.aspx)**

Non of these are me. I know diabetics have hydration problems so that's my only link here...

### seanb4 — 2015-12-29T11:27:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185073>

Tagging on that I have aversion to salt as well. I put this down to too much salt in foods and not enough k+ but I have only just now looked into it and it appears aversion to salt could indicate salt is too low...

### Gracelind — 2015-12-30T06:20:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185103>

Have you been tested for Sjogren's?

Might consider adding antibodies SSA and SSB to your next blood panel, just for fun.

### seanb4 — 2015-12-30T09:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185110>

> [Gracelind said:](/goto/post?id=185103)
>
> Have you been tested for Sjogren's?
>
> Might consider adding antibodies SSA and SSB to your next blood panel, just for fun.

Hey, Grace.

No I have not. I have recently purchased a lotta lights so can't afford blood tests.

It could be antibodies have developed since I got ill (as dry mouth was not a problem before) but I should think as the rest of my health (immunity) gets better, that should get bettter yet this has not been the case...

### Gracelind — 2016-01-02T04:59:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185264>

Another thought--does your mouth open when you sleep?

Took me a while to figure out that this was contributing to dry mouth.

### seanb4 — 2016-01-02T10:53:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185272>

> [Gracelind said:](/goto/post?id=185264)
>
> Another thought--does your mouth open when you sleep?
>
> Took me a while to figure out that this was contributing to dry mouth.

I'm not sure but I don't think so. I'm considering that the ADH is responsible. I have to pee at night and I always have dry mouth next morning. My hair is also very dry but slightly improves in hydration/quality as the day progresses.

When I CT I pee a lot after and my dry mouth is notably worse. CT affects ADH but I thought this only happens for a short while due to rewiring.

To fix ADH I would need good water, iodine, dha, and UV in the eye. I get all these things yet it hasnt got any better...

Supplementing with low salt (potassium) over the last week has helped very slighty. Maybe it is the salt in it that is helping. I could be low on salt as I have aversion to salt and that is one of the symptoms, anyway I'm going to have a lot of salt today and see...

### seanb4 — 2016-01-03T20:41:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185369>

Supplementing with salt / potassium has not worked. I have to think of something else...

Will try carb loading with UV tomorrow, see if that helps...

### seanb4 — 2016-01-08T12:22:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185739>

Carb loading hasn't seemed to work.

Here is what I shall look into next:
\-Increased Metabolism. Could be using up certain things in saliva? CT increases metabolism...
\-Calcium. Too much calcium seems to cause dry mouth, calcium is in my magnesium supp but not a lot from diet, calcium efflux a problem?
\-Parathyroid. Similar to above.
\-Oral Thrush. It's caused by dry mouth but could it also cause it?
\-Autoimmune. Sjorgens? Would seem to only be affecting mouth and not eyes (although eyes do get blood shot easy...)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-08T16:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185758>

dry mouth is from a lack of parasympathetic drive........low UV and no dopamine.

### seanb4 — 2016-01-08T21:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-mouth.17309/post-185790>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=185758)
>
> dry mouth is from a lack of parasympathetic drive........low UV and no dopamine.

Excellent. Thanks!

Just checking in with this community again...
To catch everyone up I am a teenager (16), lived in southeast asia, and had a past of adrenal fatigue, inflamation and poor choices.

Loving the Adrenal Rx! The sun has become like a new friend that I check in with every morning ☀️☀️☀️

Continuing with Leptin reset (BAB, 3 meals max, epi-paleo diet). Monitoring and minding my eye-clock as much as possible... A couple of bad mistakes of going out too late and leaving my blueblockers at home.

A few days ago I tried out a friends Google Glass for half an hour or so in the evening. Ironically as I got home I turned on the podcast Jack did with Ben Greenfield where they specifically talked about the reason it has failed and the issues associated with pulsed electromagnetic fields. Turned out I had god-aweful sleep that night and a blinding headache the next morning. Seems my biology was telling me something!

On a disparate note of using technology for good I just purchased a double pack of beta Quantlets! So excited for the possibilities that this might have!

Merry Christmas and happy holidays to all of you!
