# Thread: quervain's tenosynovitis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quervains-tenosynovitis.17311/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sami — 2015-12-29T17:31:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quervains-tenosynovitis.17311/post-185077>

Hi Dr Jack,

Recently I was diagnosed with the quervain tenosynovitis, i went to seek a second opinion and came with a new diesease called as Carpal Tunnel syndrome,
however my problem was that i could not fold my thumb into the wrist and move the wrist towards down.
the pain is horrible i couldnt tolerate and then i read some of your threads and started CT, initially it did help, but whenever the arm temperature returned normal, acute pain used to start.
I got some blood tests also done, ESR is around 25 uric acid is high, but my diet does not include too much fruits, i eat mainly sea food , then i found that the ratio of BUN/creatinine was around 15, i had a history of kidney stones \[colic\], i increased my water intake, i did less work with my laptop\[ unfortunately backlog started\].
until one day I stumbled upon a friend who knew Sujok, i told him my problems, he took my hand that had pain, he took my index finger and in the first joint of the finger from the tip, did a kind of massage for around 40 seconds, and then he asked me if there is pain in the wrist? to my astonishment the pain was gone and i felt like there was some tenderness in the area where it was paining.
I noticed that as the hour was passing by, my pain slowly was returning, now it has come to a state of pain which can be managed 40% still remains, iam continuing with CT, loads of Bcomplex, curcumin, sea food, vitamin D3, .
will update once i become better.

### Sami — 2015-12-29T17:33:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quervains-tenosynovitis.17311/post-185078>

But what wonders me is how does Sujok work and where does it fit in the quilt?

### Sami — 2016-01-01T09:31:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quervains-tenosynovitis.17311/post-185219>

Hi Dr Jack,

any suggestions, any thoughts !!!

Regards

### thomas — 2015-12-31T22:00:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-vacation-spots.17315/post-185175>

I may be wrong but any tropical volcanic island is among the top for health! (on the areas less populated/developped of course!)

### Scompy — 2015-12-31T22:13:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-vacation-spots.17315/post-185176>

> [thomas said:](/goto/post?id=185175)
>
> I may be wrong but any tropical volcanic island is among the top for health! (on the areas less populated/developped of course!)

Yeah, back in 1998 for a 7-month gig, I was working in downtown Honolulu; in my profession, I'm not only around massive nnEMF stew, in the building I was working in at that time, I was 36 floors up. The windows were fully UV tinted and I was under full fluorescent lighting. At the time, I was the only blue-eyed person in the office and could see the fluorescent-flickering and erected a CARDBOARD TENT over my cubical to block as much light as I could. Of course, I had to still move and work all over the office. What's interesting was that everyone liked that weird-craziness in my personality to do something like--no one complained. Many other co-workers liked to come into my 'tent' and hang out with me. Before I left from Honolulu back to Los Angeles, a few people had seizures and horrible migraines. At the time, they blamed it on over-work-ethic staying up all night and not getting sleep. Of course, we all know what happens when you work until 4am under fluorescent lighting. The irony was that I could look out at the pacific from my 36th floor window watching everyone down at the beach soaking up the sun. I was working 6-days a week from 9am til midnight most days, and then using Sunday to recover back on the 8th floor apartment building by cleaning up and biking down into a fluorescent shopping center for supplies. When I returned from that work-gig, I was around 300 pounds and started to develop my calcium-effluxing bigtime. It's all about personal, local environments. I was in the best place in the world to heal myself in 1998, and never used it... got much worse in fact.

### Jenny S — 2015-12-31T22:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-vacation-spots.17315/post-185177>

We went to Port Vila, Vanuatu couple of years ago. At the time (before I found Jack) I was really battling with chronic fatigue & leaky gut - could tolerate very little except meat, eggs, fish & couple of veggies. DH & I were very worried about me going backwards even more, but was surprised to find I was quite good. Didn't know at time, but now realise why.
I was only eating the local produce, (which is close to organic, no chemicals used as volcanic soil is so lush & not over farmed.) Place we were staying was out of town. The wifi wasn't working, TV was broken, no mobile phone reception. The power was just 1 single wire coming into the whole resort which didn't seem to be enough to run anything most of the time. The lights in unit were so dull couldn't read. I was swimming & snorkelling every day & our unit was only about 5 meters to beach.
The breakfast was eggs & fresh local fruit. Meat was all local & really cheap. Fruit, veggies etc at local market were also so bargain price, most days would go & have coconut milk straight out of coconut with straw, opened on spot for about 30c. Their local speciality was Tuluk (healthy meat pie!) Pastry made of tapioca & coconut milk, filled with local beef
cooked underground in banana leaf. Yum - making my mouth water thinking about them.
Land there is cheap to buy. I would love to move there but so far haven't convinced any of my family.
There was no overweight children, as they were so poor they didn't even own pushbikes, so walked everywhere - tho lot have a mobile phone now. Most don't have TV or computers in house & only power used is for single light bulb. Food is still cooked in big pot on open fire or gas ring. Fruit & veggies grown behind their villages in what we would call community gardens. Their main protein is local caught tuna as cheapest. Unfortunately there was a Macca's being built while we were there.
Perhaps we need to get a special "Kruse" retreat built there!

[http://www.exploringtheoceans.com/exploring-port-vila-market-vanuatu/](http://www.exploringtheoceans.com/exploring-port-vila-market-vanuatu/)

### Rubicon — 2016-02-02T04:51:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-vacation-spots.17315/post-187906>

I've been in Cancun for a couple of weeks now. The city itself is not ideal, but I did find the nearby island of Isla Mujeres to be pretty comfortable.

The north end of the island is where the nice beaches and town are located, however the south part is relatively forested. I rented a main floor of a house o[n AirBNB](https://www.airbnb.com/s/Isla-Mujeres--Mexico?checkin=&checkout=&guests=&zoom=16&search_by_map=true&sw_lat=21.201196379918695&sw_lng=-86.72190859238515&ne_lat=21.213117372047805&ne_lng=-86.70903398911366&ss_id=cf9m7fli) near the south end. Cell phone service on the south end of the island was relatively weak and there was no wifi in the house. It was a short walk east where I could climb down a ~10m ledge of rocks and watch the sunrise from the waters edge. Similarly, in the evening it was another short walk to a lookout point where I could watch it set. Perfect for me, and after 6-nights I left feeling really good.

Every morning I listened to a lot of webinars on light, magnetism, and water with a view like this one.

Evening sun gazing

### Dean6789 — 2017-12-30T19:48:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-vacation-spots.17315/post-231305>

> [Rubicon said:](/goto/post?id=187906)
>
> I've been in Cancun for a couple of weeks now. The city itself is not ideal, but I did find the nearby island of Isla Mujeres to be pretty comfortable.
>
> The north end of the island is where the nice beaches and town are located, however the south part is relatively forested. I rented a main floor of a house o[n AirBNB](https://www.airbnb.com/s/Isla-Mujeres--Mexico?checkin=&checkout=&guests=&zoom=16&search_by_map=true&sw_lat=21.201196379918695&sw_lng=-86.72190859238515&ne_lat=21.213117372047805&ne_lng=-86.70903398911366&ss_id=cf9m7fli) near the south end. Cell phone service on the south end of the island was relatively weak and there was no wifi in the house. It was a short walk east where I could climb down a ~10m ledge of rocks and watch the sunrise from the waters edge. Similarly, in the evening it was another short walk to a lookout point where I could watch it set. Perfect for me, and after 6-nights I left feeling really good.
>
>
>
> Every morning I listened to a lot of webinars on light, magnetism, and water with a view like this one.
>
>
>
> Evening sun gazing

Great pics... Ahhh this is heaven.

# Thread: Getting off adrenal support
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-off-adrenal-support.17316/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LisaLearning — 2015-12-31T13:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-off-adrenal-support.17316/post-185153>

Hi all,

I've been on methylprednisolone for several years (3mg) due to having rock bottom morning cortisol figures and before that was on various adrenal support formulations, not really knowing why my cortisol figures were in the toilet until I found Jack's theories.

Every time I have tried to wean myself off the methylprednisolone I've managed to give myself some major cold. And I'm the kind of person who (for most of my life) never got sick - I would get a cold maybe once every 5-7 years, usually after some stressful event. But when I don't have enough cortisol or thyroid, I can get these long-lasting colds (I once had a single cold that didn't leave for a whole quarter of the year).

Two weeks ago I was down to less than 1 mg of methylpred each day (one corner of a 1mg section every three days) and then I stopped, and of course, now I'm sick.

I'm trying to take some pregnenolone (50mg) every other day, thinking maybe if I had some extra substrate I might make enough cortisol on my own, and I'm getting a good dose of light most mornings. I think it may be keeping the cold moderate, but I am still shooting myself in the foot adding the pregnenolone?

Any suggestions?

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-01T05:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-off-adrenal-support.17316/post-185211>

Hi sorry you're going through this... are you CTing? I think I remember understanding that CT is the part of the protocol that raises and normalizes morning cortisol big time? It also ups the immune system...

### LisaLearning — 2016-01-05T13:26:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-off-adrenal-support.17316/post-185456>

Hi, I am CTing, but sunlight is my priority, so that happens most days, and the CT happens maybe 2x a week. I was lucky and I started feeling better pretty quickly, but now I need to wean myself off pregnenolone!

### LisaLearning — 2016-01-10T20:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-off-adrenal-support.17316/post-185919>

Ugh, well, after being off the tiny bit of methypred for a few days, I started waking up with headaches each morning, and then started getting blemishes on my face. Then my lips felt very dry.

Normally I associate these symptoms in myself with low thyroid. I never otherwise get blemishes!

### LisaLearning — 2016-01-22T23:02:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-off-adrenal-support.17316/post-187119>

Has anyone gotten off a cortisol replacement, and/or transitioned to cycloset? I'm taking about a third of a 1mg pill of methylprednisolone, and when I try to stop I feel ill, and my face breaks out, and lips get dry. Is there any option other than CT first thing (which doesn't work with my schedule bc I get up between 6:00- 6:30 and leave about 7:15)?

### Alla_Ellis — 2016-05-31T18:51:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-off-adrenal-support.17316/post-195692>

Never took cortisone, but I have bottomed out a few times. For me that was either due to too much sugar in the diet or too much T3 thyroid medication. Himalayan or Real salt in water really helped manage some of the symptoms (dizziness upon getting up from sitting) while I was recovering my adrenals

### lohd2015 — 2016-06-02T17:43:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-off-adrenal-support.17316/post-195877>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=195827)
>
> Pat the US is not the only GMO agenda......you do realize that Bayer, the German drug company just bid 62 Billion dollars for Monsanto right? Makes perfect sense for a Drug company to own a company that can drive illness subclinically below obedient idiots perception

......and the deal gets even more bizarre when you consider HOW Bayer will fund this LBO:

from Zerohedge [http://www.zerohedge.com/news/2016-...cb-could-end-funding-bayers-purchase-monsanto](http://www.zerohedge.com/news/2016-05-25/monetary-lunacy-ecb-could-end-funding-bayers-purchase-monsanto)

" Today we find an even more striking example of just how broken the global bond market has become thanks to the ECB because as Reuters writes, **Bayer could receive financing from none other than the European Central Bank to help fund its takeover of the world's largest seed company, US-based Monsanto**, according to the terms of the ECB's bond-buying program.

As reported yesterday, Monsanto turned down Bayer's $62 billion bid on Tuesday, but said it was open to further negotiations. Bayer, which many were surprised by its eagerness to pursue a quasi-hostile offer, promptly agreed to get more actively involved in the negotiation process. Now we know why: the cost of debt would be funded by none other than the European Central Bank.

As we documented back in March when describing the terms of the ECB's CSPP, or corporate bond buying program, the ECB can buy bonds issued by companies that are based in the euro area, have an investment-grade rating and are not banks, provided that they are denominated in euros and meet certain technical requirements. **The purpose for which the bonds are issued is not among the criteria set by the ECB, which will start buying corporate bonds on the market and directly from issuers next month**.

It now appears the "use of funds" may be M&A, and even LBOs.

This means that, in theory, the ECB could buy debt issued by Bayer, which said on Monday it would finance its cash bid for Monsanto with a combination of debt and equity. And since the ECB will buy a substantial amount of debt Bayer has to offer and will likely provide it with preferential terms, it explains why Bayer has been so surprisingly price indiscriminate to confuse even Bayer's own shareholders. It also explains why if the Bayer deal goes through, the world will likely see a surge of ECB-funded M&A deals unleashed by European corporates eager to sell billions in debt to Mario Draghi.

"It will be interesting to observe how much of such a deal would be absorbed by the central bank," credit analysts at UniCredit wrote in a note.

The ECB is buying 80 billion euros ($90 billion) worth of assets every month in an effort to revive economic growth in the euro zone by lowering borrowing costs.

According to Reuters, central bank sources said it would not be the ECB's first choice if the money it spent ended up financing acquisitions, however they have no say.

Reuters adds that "even this would have a silver lining if consolidation made an industry or sector more efficient and if it gave fresh impetus to the stock market, the source added."

And if issuers ended up exchanging the euros raised through bond sales for dollars, that would also help the euro zone by weakening the euro against the greenback, the sources said.

Bayer has investment-grade ratings from S&P, Moody's and Fitch, but all three agencies said they were reviewing their ratings for possible downgrades following the offer for Monsanto. We doubt that any downgrade will push the pharmaceutical giant into junk status.

**And here is the punchline:** once Bayer acquires Monsanto, a substantial number of the company's highly-paid 22,500 workers will be "synergized" into unemployment. Only this time we will know who is directly to blame for thousands of _**American**_ workers losing their jobs: the _**European**_ Central Bank."

I'm going outside for a Dopamine fix.

# Thread: specific bands of UV best?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2016-01-01T00:15:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185185>

The sun being best is a given, but are there particular bands of UV that are most beneficial, such as particular wavelengths of red? I know black lights are a very specific wavelngth of UVA, are there better bands of UV to "supplement" with during times of less sun?

### twilightkid — 2016-01-01T01:17:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185187>

for supplementing there is no one "best" range of light. It comes down to context and what it is "best" for what your looking for.
UV-B 280-315nm is for making sulfated vitamin D
UV-A 315-400nm is good for thyroid
Water in the cell acts as both a battery and a motor (the work of biochemistry)
UV is what makes the motor in water – Red light makes the battery (ez).
It would be "best" to use both UVB,UVA, red light and infrared with correct timing in my opinion.

### Aerose91 — 2016-01-01T01:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185189>

I know theres no substitute for that, im just wondering which bands come to the top when used artificially; such as the quantlet or use of black lights

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-01T05:24:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185209>

> for supplementing there is no one "best" range of light. It comes down to context and what it is "best" for what your looking for.

> Water in the cell acts as both a battery and a motor (the work of biochemistry)
> UV is what makes the motor in water – Red light makes the battery (ez).
> It would be "best" to use both UVB,UVA, red light and infrared with correct timing in my opinion.

Thank you for your clarity! Could you please give an example of correct timing of use...

### SpiritGuide — 2016-01-04T19:54:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185407>

After researching this recently my wife and I just purchased the Duracell CFL Ultraviolet light bulb at Batteries Plus along with several full-spectrum bulbs to use throughout the house.

The guy at Batteries Plus was shocked we would spend so much on just a handful of bulbs, so we told him what we were doing... very interesting conversation indeed. Hopefully we opened his eyes to what is possible... good luck!

### thomas — 2016-01-05T21:30:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185490>

No better band, just get both uva and uvb consistently. consistency is king to me.

### JCDC — 2016-06-13T20:05:29+0300

My uncle spends a few months (jan-april), every year, for the past 14 years in playa del carmen. I recently talked w/ him for the first time in years. His trip has been delayed this year b/c of my grandparents ailing health. He takes care of them where he lives in NY. He says that come December/January of every year his body is calling for Mexico. He shared some great info on the rural areas and the city. When he first started going in 2002 he described it as Key West back in the 50's. He said the population was maybe 15,000... now about 150,000. He actually stayed at the mayor's house when he was first there... that's how small the town was. I can't wait to go!

Best wishes for a wonderful New year full of optimism and great energy and hope and love and quantum magic and quantum entanglement.

Let your smile burn thru the negative layers of people who are close to you.

Smile and they will come!

This has been my experience the last number of years.....a smile says it all.

Who can resist an honest to goodness smile that comes from the heart? It promises nothing - but gives everything.

Words seem to pass on thru ...... But a smile lingers and warms you up to the core and leaves a glow of love and kindness and hope and caring.......

Let's keep dancing and laughing and loving!

# Thread: Blacklights far away due to nnEMF... but does Solar Glo have similar nnEMF issues?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-02T21:37:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185296>

I've read on forum Dr. Kruse recommending the use of blacklights from across the room, as the desired effect is still going on, yet it avoids nnEMF generated by the bulbs...

here: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/uva-uvb-light.17231/#post-184585](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/uva-uvb-light.17231/#post-184585)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=184588)
>
> The distance from the light is immaterial for the effect.......but to get away from light for nnEMF reasons is get the light as far from you as possible. To get your specific answers Eilee you need a journal and likely to get on the member Q & A's.

A member mentioned that nnEMF is around 100mG AC magnetic I assume right close to the bulb...

Is this EMF an issue with blacklights in particular?

Is it ok to use the other bulbs such as Solar Glo 160's, Exo Terra's 200 (a mainly UVB bulb that one could try to hack at close distance for Vitamin D production) close up? Or is it just a trade-off between Vitamin-D production and EMF exposure ?

I assume there is not an EMF issue with non CFL bulbs like LED's...

### nonchalant — 2016-01-03T01:06:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185312>

LED's have transformers to change the voltage. They bother me, but I am able to tolerate an LED blacklight when it is on the far side of the room.

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-04T03:23:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185376>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=185312)
>
> LED's have transformers to change the voltage. They bother me, but I am able to tolerate an LED blacklight when it is on the far side of the room.

Thanks! Gosh complications... can you tell me more, I'm not very tech savvy on this. What are the implications of the LED's having transformers? Does that mean they put out more nnEMF?

How does that compare to a CFL bulb ?

In what way do they bother you?

Is this exclusive to the blacklights or are the Red LEDs also a problem? I think I read of some using the Red LED's directly on skin to heal...

### nonchalant — 2016-01-06T01:43:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185513>

They put out some electrical and magnetic EMF, like an appliance would. They cause ringing in my ears, but not as bad as the CFLs do. The CFLs cause huge spikes on the meter. I also use red LEDs, but only for a few minutes close up. The blacklight LEDs tend to stay on for hours.

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-06T05:16:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185540>

Thanks! So the LED's are still better for you than the CFL's as far as triggering symptoms...

I am noticing some symptoms (a body tension I have) from using the Solar Glo, I am EMF sensitive in this way... but it could also just be the strong light (the blue? or just strong light asking my body to create hormones and thus causing some 'stress' ? ) that triggers the symptoms. I'm still using it as I'm also noticing benefits...

I also noticed EMF symptoms from using the CFL blacklight bulb close up, no symptoms when I moved it over 3 feet away.

# Thread: Rubicon's Journal - A return to optimal?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 94
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2016-01-04T07:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-185383>

**Goals**
As I've been reading through Dr. Kruse's blog posts, I was very inspired by his description of how he optimized himself back to how he was at a much younger age, and then was able to do even better. That idea is very attractive to me. Up until my early 20's, I had high levels of motivation and energy, but since then I've seldom felt that way...and never for sustained periods.
Over the last few years I've developed some significant gastrointestinal issues. I would like to fix those if possible, and then attempt to achieve the kind of optimal that Dr. Kruse has achieved. Extending my health & lifespan is my top priority.

**Background**
Born in Winnipeg Manitoba. Weight 7lb, Length 19 inches
Currently: 37, 5'7", 129lbs, married, no kids

**Parents**
Father died in 1993 at the age of 42 from a heart attack that occurred in the morning while he was at work. He was working as a house painter at the time. My father was slim, did not smoke, drank only occasionally. He had no known health issues. I was 15 at the time.
Mother died in 1997 at the age of 49. She was slim when I was born, but struggled with weight gain later in life. She was diagnosed with a benign pituitary tumor around 1989 and underwent surgery & radiation to remove the tumor. She recovered and did well for 5-years at which time the tumor returned, and her health declined after a series of additional operations. I was 19 at the time.

**Body Composition**
I've been slim my whole life. In my childhood, I was generally healthy but from grade 1-9 I was always the shortest kid in class. As an adult, my weight has generally fluctuated between 140-145lbs. Over the last 20 months since April 2014 I've steadily been losing weight and am now struggling to stay above 130lbs. Body fat percentage has dropped from ~20% to ~15% recently.

**Diet**
Early childhood diet was not always ideal, but my mother cooked generally balanced meals for dinner in my early years. Cereal for breakfast, sandwiches for lunch, meat/potatoes/veg for dinner.
Teen years and 20's I've eaten a diet high in refined carbs, and typically fast food 2-5 times per week. Breakfast was pop tarts, corn flakes, toast. Sandwiches for lunch. Dinner was typically what I thought was healthy whole wheat pasta, brown rice, and a bit of protein. My understanding was that meat/fat was to be avoided, and I tried to eat 'healthy' whole wheat bread, brown rice, brown pasta, lentils, beans, etc.
Last 3 years - transitioned to a paleoish diet with mixed results. Cut out most carbs, all wheat, added grass fed meat, healthy fats, and for the last 3-6 months I've been increasing my seafood consumption and now eat 10-20 raw oysters per week, sardines, pickled herring, salmon, etc. In this time I developed abdominal pain, had my appendix removed, and suffered from bouts of constipation since transitioning away from carbs. Currently I'm trying to consume a good amount of carbs in the form of white rice & sweet potato daily due to recent weight loss & gallbladder/liver issues.

**Alcohol**
I've never been a heavy drinker, and I gave up beer/liquor 3 years ago since it was making me consistently feel unwell. I was consuming a glass of red wine a few times a week up to 2 years ago when it also began making me feel generally unwell. In the last 2-years I have avoided all alcohol.

**Water**
I source RO water from the supermarket and leave it on my Magnetico at all times. I try to drink it as cold as possible, but have very sensitive teeth that make the experience quite unpleasant. I drink a lot of water and typically also consume 1-3 cups of green tea per day.
The water supply where I live is fluoridated, and I was drinking it up to about 6 months ago when I started RO water only.

**Environment**
Up to age 9 I lived in a house in the countryside in central Alberta on a small acreage (2500ft). For a year at age 10 the family moved to a small town, and we resided in a neighbourhood within 1-mile from a coal fired power plant. Family moved to Edmonton (2200ft) for 5 years where we lived on the main floor of an apartment, and then to Calgary (3500ft) where I remained for ~15 years living mostly in suburban houses. 3 1/2 years ago I moved to a small city north of Edmonton (2200ft) where I lived on the second floor of an apartment for 3 years. I've now moved to a house.

**nnEMF**
Since 1998 I've spent a lot of time sitting in front of a computer at work and for leisure. Usually the computer was turned on and located in the room I slept. The power bar was often at my feet where I sat.
I did not own a cellphone until 2009 when I got my first blackberry, but since then have used smartphones heavily. About 6 months ago I started avoiding my smartphone and now leave it in airplane mode whenever possible.
There is currently a smart meter connected to the water line in my basement that appears to be self-powered. The water company refuses to remove it.
The apartment I lived in on the second floor from 2010-2014 was constantly exposed to 20+ wifi networks. For the last 8 months I've lived in a house with only one wifi network from the neighbour.
The house I currently reside in is located under a mile from a large broadcast tower. There is a transformer in front of the house roughly 30 meters (90ft) from where I sleep. I've been measuring magnetic & electric fields around the house with my Trimeter, trying to reduce EMF's in the house, but need to learn more and address this further.

**Exercise**
Long periods of being sedentary have been common over the last 20 years. I don't like sports, but sometimes cycle & occasionally lift weights. Over the last year I've been trying to walk a minimum of 30 min per day, but running is no longer possible due to abdominal/liver pain that is induced by any vigorous exercise.

**Light**
Days here are way too long in the summer (4AM-11PM daylight), winters are very dark (9AM-4PM daylight). The winter sun does not get higher than 12 degrees above the horizon at solstice. I've never paid attention to light in the past, but lately have been experimenting with infared and reptile lights with varied success. I've started visiting a tanning salon for limited 5-8 min sessions and find it quite energizing.
I started wearing glasses for myopia at age 10. Contacts were often worn 50-90% of the time from age 17-35. My eyes are currently at -2.75 diopters
I purchased a FIR sauna about 6 months ago and use it regularly. It makes me feel great and typically helps reduce my abdominal pain.

**Circadian Cycle/Sleep**
Over the last 10 years I've been aware of how much better I feel when I sleep ~8 hours and go to bed at the same time everyday. Generally I've tried to aim for that. For the last year I've been using amber glasses worn over my prescription glasses 2-4 hours before bed. However, I usually use the computer right up to bedtime. For the last 4-6 weeks I've been waking up after 6 hours of sleep and am unable to go back to sleep.
I do dream every night and typically recall some kind of dream when I wake up.

**Libido**
Good libido, but fertility tests have shown very low sperm count and motility. Small ejaculate size has been common for many years. My wife and I are currently having difficulty conceiving.

**Stress/Anxiety/Emotion**
I've had several stressful episodes throughout life. Death of family members, relationship issues, work, etc. ?The health issues I've run into in the last few years have caused me anxiety. Currently I have stress relatively under control and use the Inner Balance Heartmath regularly whenever I feel stressed. However, I find I'm sometimes prone to bursts of anger & anxiety that fade over time but are no fun.

**Supplements**
I have been experimenting with various supplements over the last year, but have cut back considerably on most everything. I find some supplements cause my abdominal / liver pain to worsen. I now regularly take vitamin D, K2, Curcumin, Resveratrol, Ubiquional, PQQ, Artichoke/Dandelion, but minimize most others

**Dental**
Most of my teeth have had cavities and have composite fillings (a number of 10+ year old amalgams were removed about 3-years ago). One molar on my lower right side was extracted over five years ago, and has now been replaced by a titanium implant/crown. Overall my teeth are very sensitive and I'm concerned about losing more teeth.
I'm currently visiting [Dr Jennings in San Francisco](https://www.bulletproofexec.com/dr-dwight-jennings-tmj-jaw-pain-substance-p-179/) who has created a specialize appliance for me to correct my bite.
I need to do a detailed journal entry on dental issues
Edit: [Here's my dental journal entry](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/page-2#post-192361)

**Genetic Testing**
My 23andMe results indicated:
Paternal haplogroup = J2 (Region = Southern Europe, Near East, Northern Africa, Example populations = Ashkenazi/ Sephardic Jews, Lebanese)
Maternal haplogroup = H1 (Region = Europe, Near East, Central Asia, Example populations = Norwegian, Spanish, Berbers)

**My Current symptoms**
Currently I have a number of gastrointesinal issues that concern me. My gallbladder area is constantly sore, especially when I'm stressed out or consume a high fat meal. If I sleep on my right side my mid abdomen becomes sore. The liver area under my right side ribcage has been somewhat sore for years but has been especially bad over the last 3-months. My lower abdomen gets migrating pains which I've determined to likely be constipation.
Tinnitus developed 1-year ago and is especially loud after a meal.
My right arm & hand are often cold and numb/tingling, as well as my right foot. Occasionally I've gone through brief periods where it's actually been difficult to walk on my right foot.
My weight has dropped from 146 to 129 and I'm finding it increasingly difficult to prevent my weight from falling.

**Testing Notes**

-   A Genova GI Effects Comprehensive Stool Profile in June 2015 revealed very low gut diversity. My family doctor used these results to prescribe digestive enzymes after finding Pancreatic Elastase was high in stool.
-   A WellnessFX cross the board blood testing in June 2015 found high Omega 3 levels, high FSH, and low vitamin D
-   A recent HIDA scan found Cystic Duct Syndrome
-   A recent abdominal MRI found a mildly enlarged liver

See here for all labs: [Rubicon's Labs](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rubicons-labs.17326/)

**Future Testing**
Salivary Melatonin/Cortisol is next

**Optimal Education**
\- I've read EpiPaleo RX
\- I've read 60% of the blogs
\- Currently making my way through the archived webinars and Q&A's now. Lots of good stuff there

I still feel like the more I'm reading, the more I realize I still don't know. More to come

### Rubicon — 2016-01-08T03:58:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-185708>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=185466)
>
> Mexico is a good compromise for you

Staying in Canada but spending a few months each winter in Mexico would be a good compromise?

Using the [USNO's Sun Altitude/Azimuth Calculator](http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php), I've found that the sun reaches an altitude of over 50 degrees at my location in Canada starting around May 1 and ending mid August. I was very surprised to discover that natural Vit D production is really only possible here about 3.5 months of the year. With this in mind, my "optimal compromise" schedule might be:

-   March 15 - Sept 30 - Canada (6.5 months in Canada, 6 weeks spring, 3.5 months of summer, 6 weeks fall)
-   Oct 1 - Oct 15 - Mexico (2 weeks optimal sunlight)
-   Oct 15 - Dec 1 - Canada (6 weeks Canadian winter)
-   Dec 1 - Jan 15 - Mexico (6 weeks optimal sunlight)
-   Jan 15 - March 1 - Canada (6 weeks Canadian winter)
-   March 1 - March 15 - Mexico (2 weeks optimal sunlight)

That protocol would give me all of the Canadian summer, and roughly 2.5 months throughout the winter in Mexico.

Ideally I won't have to apply this protocol for more than 1-year, because I aim to move south permanently.

### Rubicon — 2016-01-17T05:17:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-186552>

I've spent a few days in Cancun now.

The flight down was on a plane with a newly innovated entertainment system: no TV's in the seats....instead it's all wifi tablet access only. Over 100 tablets streaming video simultaneously over wifi on that flight. Scary to think the amount of EMF.
After arriving I made my way to a simple house in the city of Cancun that I found on AirBNB. Bedroom is on the main floor, concrete construction. 2 wifi networks detected in the house. I immediately went to a nearby park and grounded in the rain. Slept for 10 hours, very deep sleep. First time in a long time.

Been getting a bit of morning and mid day sun in between periods of rain. Sleep has been great overall. The first time visiting the beach I soaked up the sun for about 30 min until I started feeling crispy. Going to take it slow and try to work up to staying exposed for longer amounts of time. After the first good sun exposure I could feel a headache coming on and feeling generally unwell. This is a similar experience to what has happened at the beginning of many summers after the end of a long winter of zero UVB exposure.

I'm really impressed with how good I've been sleeping since changing my environment. It will be interesting to see it keeps up the whole time I'm here.

### Rubicon — 2016-01-25T05:03:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-187318>

After week 1 I moved from staying in the city of Cancun to Isla Mujeres, a small island a short ferry ride from the city. I found a nice main floor of a house on AirBNB in a converted estate on the south side of the island, away from the main development. There's no wifi in the house, and I'm really liking it. It feels like the most optimal place I've been in years.

I'm a short walk to the ocean on the east edge of the island where I've been watching the sun come up, and a short walk to the west edge where I can gaze as it goes down. Combined with lots of daytime sunlight, I'm hoping my Vit D levels are coming up.

Diet is mainly a big protein breakfast, a whole grilled fish for lunch, and a light dinner.

^^^ Lunch

### Rubicon — 2016-02-05T15:09:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-188330>

Vacation is over in Cancun. Now heading back to Canada with a 1-day stop in San Francisco to visit the dentist.

After the 2nd week of my vacation spent on Isla Mujeres, I reached a point where I felt I made good progress. I slept well (not perfect), but generally managed to stay in bed 7-8 hours. I hit the beach everyday to tan for 1-2 hours. Symptoms really got better and I was feeling great. The tingling in my right arm/foot that has been pretty steady for the last few months stopped, and gallbladder/liver pain noticeably decreased. One of the best effects is that I actually went from a Type 2 (Mildly Constipated) on the bristol stool scale to a perfect Type 4 smooth snake.  It's been months since that's happened.

The 3rd week I stayed in a main floor apartment just north of Cancun that unfortunately turned out to be right across the street from a big cell tower. Incredible views of sunrise in morning, and lots of sun tanning everyday. My sleep was terrible there and symptoms began to worsen. I regretted not staying at Isla Mujeres

**Permanent Move South**
One of the best things to happen on the trip is that I pitched the idea to my wife of moving south from Canada to a location with UV light year round. She was pretty shocked and resistant at first, but after a few days I think she's becoming interested in the idea. We're thinking perhaps the West coast of Florida or Hawaii could be viable options.

### Rubicon — 2016-02-22T01:31:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-189550>

**Back from Vacation**
It's been a couple of weeks since leaving Cancun.

On the way back I took a 1-day delay to Oakland to visit the dentist. While I was there I visited an integrative medicine doctor for my first Meyers Cocktail ($100 first time consultation + $150 for the IV) with the intention of counteracting some of the negative aspects of flying. I really felt good afterwards. I wish I had easy access to these regularly.

The first week back in Canada overall things were good in terms of pain in the gallbladder/liver/spleen area. However, my stool has quickly transitioned from Type 2 smooth snake back to being constipated and difficult to pass. I visited the tanning salon once for 12 minutes, stomach didn't like that.

The second week back after another visit to the tanning salon I really wasn't feeling good. Going to stop that for now. I'm really not feeling great overall. Acne that was non-existent on vacation has returned along with abdominal pain, low fever feeling, tiredness, and sore joints in fingers.

**LeptinRX & CT**
I'm continuing on the leptin reset protocol, eating a big high protein content breakfast within 30min of waking, and 3 meals per day on a set schedule. Unfortunately I haven't really been able to get motivated to get back into any kind of CT. I feel like 3-weeks in Cancun undid any progress I had previously made, and the idea of "the quantlet might replace CT" has reduced my motivation.

**Light**
I'm paying attention to the sun every day and loving seeing the days getting longer. Sunrise around 8AM, sets at 6:15PM and it's up to 24 degrees at noon. I try to plan my day to be able to watch the sun rise/set, and get out during the day as much as possible.
Due to potential mercury toxicity, I'm avoiding fluorescent lights including using any reptile lights or other CF UV lights....so any artificial light hacking opportunities are somewhat limited. I have ordered [this LED UV light](http://www.amazon.com/Sunlite-A19-LED-2W-BLB/dp/B013W6SJEM/ref=sr_1_15?ie=UTF8&qid=1456096849&sr=8-15&keywords=uv+led) and am looking forward to possibly giving it a try.

**Relocation**
Hawaii might be a potential place to move in a few years, and my wife and I have scheduled a visit there at the beginning of April.

### Rubicon — 2016-03-03T06:07:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-190120>

For over a week now I've tried upping my use of Betaine HCL. The first few days after starting this I experienced joint pain and considerable fatigue in the afternoons & evenings. However, I'm now experiencing big improvements in my abdominal pains. Energy levels are up, super high appetite is going down, and it seems I've been able to gain a few pounds. Hopefully this is the start of a new trend. It's nice to feel like I'm making some progress.

### Rubicon — 2016-03-06T22:47:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-190297>

At Jacks recommendation above, a few weeks ago I contacted Clayton for a consultation via Skype. Clayton is a Naturopath Doctor from Kamloops BC and [guest blogger from Oct 2015](https://www.jackkruse.com/guest-physician-blog-1-the-power-of-uv-light/) on JackKruse.com. Here are some notes from that conversation.

-   I mentioned to Clayton that there's only one strong wifi network that I pick up in my house from neighbors. He suggested an easy fix might be to buy a power timer and give it to my neighbor for him to add to his wifi router to turn off at night.
-   There is a smart meter on the water line in my basement, a Neptune E-Coder R900i ([here's a video about this meter](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=INDEHZzX-Rw)). I might want to consider smart meter shielding. However, in the past when I've tried to shield the meter with Argenmesh, I actually get a higher RF reading on my Trimeter. I'm wondering if the copper piping that the meter is connected in line with is acting as an antenna.
    I called the water company and asked if it could be removed...they just laughed. It's too bad I can't have an ethernet enabled smart meter installed to eliminate this RF source. Need to research this more.
-   Consider a faraday cage from RF Sensitive Solutions. [A canopy + grounding mat is $3,442.00](http://www.rfsensitivesolutions.com/canopies.php). This faraday cage can be grounded. It looks nice, but quite pricey. Clayton mentioned someone in the quantum health facebook group might be able to help advise me on what to look for here. I need to figure out how to get in there...
-   I talked about my mercury toxicity concerns combined with my magnetico use. Suggested to possibly try DMSA with the magnetico
-   f.lux has a darkroom mode that's useful for reading on the computer. I'm going to try this. After searching for more info about this on the forum, I found a useful note from @Cpt. Tired; f.lux can also control screen brightness via the keyboard shortcut (Alt + page up or page down)
-   Using amber glasses after dark is good. Try Dewalt laser enhancement glasses (blocks green in addition to blue)
-   Talked about my history using natural light. A few years ago before I read any of the Jack Kruse info, I became aware of the importance of vitamin D and that it was produced by exposure to UV light. Knowing this, it seemed logical that I should try to generate vitamin D using UV light. In Dec 2014 I traveled to Colombia for a few weeks and was intent on getting ~10min everyday in the mid-day sun. When I was there I was staying in an apartment on the 17th floor where the sun would shine through a window perfectly around 1:00PM. So for most days for 2-weeks, I would lay naked in the equatorial sun. I even took my glasses off (though I didn't know at the time that I should look in the direction of the sun).
    At the time I was experiencing mild abdominal symptoms, but after 2-weeks of this routine my symptoms completely resolved for a brief time.
-   Clayton mentioned his own experiences of being in New Orleans and in Greece where he noticed the difference of being exposed to strong sunlight. As a result, he intends to move south from Canada at some point in the future.
-   Suggested to try booking a vacation south and see what happens.
-   Regarding my liver/gallbladder issue, Clayton mentioned that the organs are probably having difficulty handling whatever is in body
-   Try tanning booth, hydrated with 500ml-1L of water, up to 15-20 min
-   For sleep difficulties, try Natural Factors phosphatidal serine 30min before bed to help drop cortisol level and maintain sleep
-   Going into a high UV environment might not fix liver right away...could take a few months.

### Rubicon — 2016-03-14T00:53:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-190786>

My current diet/light/supplementation/sleep routine.

Context: Attempting to address a disturbed circadian rhythm, leptin resistance, often difficulty getting quality sleep past 4-5 hours, intermittent pain in gallbladder/liver/spleen areas of abdomen, simplified gut flora, tinnitus, chronically sore tailbone, possible long-term chronic infection of some kind, telomeres of an 85 year old, etc

**6:30-7:30AM** - Wake and eat a BAB of avocado, canned sardines/herring, butter, sometimes eggs. Supplementing with HCL, digestive enzymes, ubiquinol, pqq, K2, astragalus. Typically wear blue blockers during this time. Try to fit in 15 min of meditation here.
**8:00** Workday begins, check email on computer
**8:15** - Sunrise. I try to take a break and go outside for 5-10 min to watch the sunrise & ground. However, usually I open a window and sit in the sun indoors.
**8:15-13:30** - Work in front of a computer with dual monitors. Flux is set to as red as it can go, and I often turn down the brightness of the screen. Try to go outside for 15min around 10:00 for some later morning sun.
**13:30-14:30** - Go outside and try to catch the sun as it's at the highest inclination of the day (currently 32 degrees) for grounded naked suntanning & lunch. I'm currently augmenting the seasonally weak sun with a mirror and sheet of aluminum foil to reflect sunlight to increase my exposure. Lunch is typically some steamed frozen cod/salmon, tin of herring, 1/2 cup of rice or a small sweet potato, veggies (a mix of either broccoli, brussel sprouts, kale, spinach, green beans, carrots, peas, etc), lots of ghee/butter. Supplement with HCL, digestive enzymes, NAC, zinc, quercetin
**14:30-17:30** \- Work at computer
**17:30-18:00** \- Exercise in the form of a short walk & sprint, pushups/situps, or far infrared sauna. The sun is close to setting at this time when I'm out for a walk.
**18:00-19:00 -** Dinner: Usually more fish, rice, veggies. Sometimes pork, lamb, or chicken. Supplement with HCL, digestive enzymes, methylfolate, molybdenum. Blue blocking glasses go on.
**19:00-21:00** - Relax by reading at computer, occasional CT with blocks of ice on chest, watch TV, sometimes stretching/meditation.
**21:30-22:00** \- Off to sleep, supplement with magnesium glycinate
**3:30-6:30** - I typically sleep well until around 3:30 when I find I often wake up and cannot return to a deep sleep.

### Rubicon — 2016-03-16T21:20:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-191007>

I've had 8 days in a row of good sunshine at solar noon. It's been excellent, but the sun still only gets as high as 33 degrees above the horizon. According to dminder, 1-hour of naked sun bathing only produces ~1000 IU of vitamin D. To attempt to hack that, I've been experimenting with reflectors.

First try: 2 mirrors

1 sun + 2 mirrors was really nice. The temperature is still right around the freezing point everyday, so the extra 'suns' allowed me to stay much warmer. Although the heat was good, it seemed something was missing. After reading that mirrors perhaps don't reflect the UV, I tried covering one mirror with aluminum foil instead:

The aluminum foil does not do as good of a job reflecting light as the mirror. I think the relatively rugged surface is scattering a lot of the light in other directions. For now I've continued using one extra mirror (for warmth) and the one aluminum foil covered one. It feels good, but I'm not sure if it's really a good idea to fool around with the mirror as it's probably not reflecting full spectrum sunlight.

My next step is to attempt to replace the mirrors with some aluminum plates that will hopefully reflect the sunlight more effectively. Possibly building something out of a bunch of [aluminum ceiling tiles](http://www.decorativeceilingtiles.net/aluminum/), or get a [aluminum sheet](http://www.russelmetals.com/en/Products/Aluminum/Pages/Sheet-and-Plate.aspx). I'm not sure yet which would be reflective and resistant to oxidation.

to be continued...

### Rubicon — 2016-03-27T18:01:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-191585>

The last couple of weeks I've been feeling better overall. BAB's, 3 meals per day, and increased exercise is causing weight gain. I feel like my energy levels are improving.

I've started to make progress on CT. I first started with face dunks 3 months ago, moved onto blocks of ice on my chest with a compression shirt for 1-hour, and yesterday I took the plunge into 52 degree water in my bathtub around 1:30PM. I lasted for 9 minutes with my entire torso submerged up to my neck, excluding only my feet, hands, and head.

Afterwards I went outside into the sun where I began to really shiver/shake for at least 10-20 min. The sun warming me up was great. Going to try for 15 minutes submerged today...hopefully the sun will be out this aft.

Next week I'm going for a 1-week vacation to Hawaii, staying on Oahu. I'll be trying to get lots of sun, check out the island, and maybe try to convince my wife to move there.

### Rubicon — 2016-04-08T21:12:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-192361>

**Dental Journal**

This is going to be a long post.

About 2 weeks ago I began getting a nagging pain in my right jaw that extends down my neck and into my shoulder. This is similar to a pain I had a couple of years ago before undergoing dental surgery to treat a wisdom tooth extraction site for a cavitation. The jaw pain that has now developed is similar to the past, but also more intense and with worse accompanying symptoms.

Now I'm contemplating exactly what to do about my current dental symptoms, how they fit into my overall health, and if perhaps my dental issues could be impacting other areas of my body.

**Dental History**

-   **Pre 1999** - I developed several cavities that required fillings. A few were filled with amalgams, and a few with composite. I didn't always have insurance coverage, so sometimes I went long periods of time between visiting the dentist. I remember one dentist scolded me for letting the cavities go so deep.
-   **1999** \- Wisdom teeth extracted (upper left, upper right, and lower right). There was no wisdom tooth at the lower left
-   **2002-2005** - More cavities & composite fillings
-   **2009** \- Developed a pain in first molar on the right side (tooth #30). I visited the dentist who found no evidence of infection and adjusted the bite over the course of several visits to try and calm down the nerve. When it didn't work, a root canal was performed. For 3-days after the root canal I was pain free and feeling very good. It felt like a weight was lifted from my shoulders. Then, a crown was placed on the tooth and the pain returned. Around this time I made the mistake of visiting another dentist for an opinion on my pain. That dentist proceeded to make a substantial adjustment to my overall bite, grinding down a bit of enamel from each of my teeth in an attempt to correct the bite. From this point forward my bite never really felt balanced.
-   **2010** \- Noticed my gums seemed to be receding somewhat as spaces opened between the teeth that were not previously there
-   **2011** \- Tooth #30 remained in pain and a CT scan showed a possible fracture underneath. Tooth was extracted by a conventional dentist that did not remove the periodontal ligment. After the procedure the pain did not fully go away, but gradually subsided to the point that it was bearable
-   **2012 November** \- A regular dentist removed my amalgams under blue light using only a dental dam. Before and after the procedure I had right neck/shoulder pain and was referred to a physiotherapist. Pain in the neck/shoulder reduced gradually.
-   **2013 Feb** - A titanium implant was placed at the extraction site. Everything felt normal after implantation.
-   **2013 Oct** - Pain in my neck/shoulder re-occurred and I returned to physiotherapy. After a few months the pain once again died down.
-   **2013 Nov** - I remember asking my dentist around this time why all my teeth had become very sensitive? He said there's nothing he could do about general sensitivity, and recommended using Sensodyne toothpaste. My teeth have been very sensitive to hot and especially cold ever since.
-   **2014 May** - Diagnosed with appendicitis, developed IBS symptoms, miscellaneous abdominal pains
-   **2014 Oct** - Crown placed on implant. Pain in neck immediately returned. I was very surprised and confused after going so long with no symptoms.I researched the condition of "cavitational necrosis" which was promoted by Weston Price and various biological/alternative dentists. There was information online that disputed the condition was real and I did not know what to believe. The typical solution to cavitations is a messy and risky surgery, and I was reluctant to go ahead with an invasive procedure that seemed questionable. After a few weeks the pain of the crown was too much and I had it temporarily removed by the dentist that placed it. When I asked my dentist about the possibility of a cavitation, he researched the topic and said he didn't believe cavitations were real. After removing the implant the pain went away a bit but not completely.
-   **2014 Dec** - I visited the nearest biological dentist (8 hour drive away) that could deal with cavitations and showed him my old x-rays, CT, etc. He didn't say much in the consultation until I asked him if I might have a cavitation. He sortof nodded his head and said yes, there was a cavitation at the bottom right wisdom tooth extraction site. No other examination or diagnosis was offered. I didn't know if I should trust him, but thought I better go ahead with some kind of treatment due to the pain. I had cavitation surgery at extraction sites consisting of the dentist drilling a small hole at the site, using a drill to clean out the cavitation area, and injecting ozone. A IV Vitamin C was administered to help fight infection. The procedure was not as extensive as the type of surgery that was discussed online. I was sent on my way and caught a flight home that evening. At the time I asked the dentist if I also had a cavitation at the implant site and he said I probably did. He said he could extract the implant and that would be a good opportunity to clean out the cavitation. I did not go ahead with the implant removal at that time
-   **2015 April** - The old cavitation site at the wisdom tooth was healed and I was feeling better. I had the crown re-installed crown on my titanium implant to see what would happen. Pain was minimal and gradually subsided.
-   **2015 June** - I learned of a procedure to re-align the bite so that the front teeth meet tip to tip via Dave Aspry's [Bulletproof Podcast with Dr Jennings](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-_CJZi9EEaw). Due to having had what I felt to be a suboptimal bite since the root canal procedure, I decided I should get diagnosed. I had difficulty finding similar dentists in Canada, so I traveled to San Francisco to visit Dr Jennings. He thought I would benefit from his treatment and I decided to go ahead. He created dental appliances for my teeth that will allow my back molars to grow, bring the lower jaw forward, and eventually allow the teeth to bite tip to tip. I currently wear these appliances 24/7 and expect the treatment to be complete in 2017
-   **2015-2016** - During this time my teeth have been 'ok', no new cavities, and I've been re-visiting Dr Jennings in San Francisco to adjust my appliances. My previous cavitations symptoms of neck/shoulder pain disappeared and I became focused on other health issues, hoping to finish the bite alignment before revisiting the idea of removing my implant. I was focused on lots of pain in gallbladder, liver, spleen, etc. Sofar I've been unable to really address these.
-   **2016 March** - Seemingly out of the blue, within a 1-week timeframe, the same old familiar pain in jaw/neck/shoulder returned worse than ever. Now I'm wondering, has the original cavitation at the wisdom tooth site come back? Or, has the potential cavitation at the implant site now become a problem? After researching more about cavitations, I'm wondering if perhaps these jaw infections have potentially been a cause of my health issues?

**Current Symptoms
**
\- Pain in my right jaw, neck, and shoulder. Some days pain is very mild, while others it's very intense
\- Occasional pain up my right face up to my eye
\- Sore eyes & headaches
\- Turning head to the left causes a lot of pain/inflammation in my right neck and shoulder, especially if I look left and hold my head still
\- Tinnitus has intensified and become very loud
\- Intermittent pain/numbness in my right forearm and right leg
\- Racing heart beat, heart palpitations, heart beats very 'hard' and seems to intensify when I'm laying down. Lately my sleep is disturbed after a few hours and I have a hard time going back to sleep
\- Increasingly sore gallbladder/liver/spleen
\- Coccyx pain started 4 months ago and has not gone away. I feel a lot of soreness particularly when I transition to/from sitting

**X-Rays of my Teeth**

2010-05 - Tooth #30 is in the middle. This X-ray was taken when the tooth was apparently healthy and I had phantom pain in the tooth that was present for no clear reason.

2010-09 - Tooth #30 after the root canal. Pain remained

2013-02 - After #30 was extracted. The outline of where tooth #30 was is clearly visible, which could indicate that the bone did not heal properly and has a cavitation

2013-02.2 - After Implant

2014 - A close-up of the implant, the outline of the old tooth is clearly visible

2016 - A recent x-ray showing the placed crown. Bone appears to be receding around implant. Outline of old tooth remains.

**Dental Meridians**

Here is [a chart of "Dental Meridians"](http://naturaldentistry.us/holistic-dentistry/meridian-tooth-chart-from-encinitas-dentist/). The idea is that every tooth is connected to an area of the body and a problem in a tooth could affect the corresponding part of the body. I'm not sure if this stuff is woo or not... Various meridian charts available online conflict with each other and the topic does not seem to be something that is embraced by general dentists.

In my case, tooth #30 was extracted, likely has a cavitation, and there is a titanium implant at this location. Also wisdom tooth #32 was extracted and previously treated for a deep cavitation.

Organs affected by these teeth are the heart, Terminal Ileum, Duodenum, lung, large intestine, Ileoceal Area. A lot of these organs are involved/suspected to be involved in the abdominal symptoms I'm experiencing the last few years

**Various Links about Diagnosing & Treating Cavitations**

-   Diagnosis could be via X-ray, Cone Beam CT, Cavitat, Applied Kinesiology Muscle Testing. I'd really like to get a thorough diagnosis before going ahead with any further procedures
-   Cavitation treatment options:- Surgical Scraping - Thoroughly removes the infection, but has the risk of damaging the trigeminal nerve or other potential complications. Sometimes multiple surgeries are required if re-infection occurs. Many sources say that surgical remove is really the only option that works- Ozone injections / Ultraviolet blood irradiation (UBI)
    Sook Hong recommended ozone or a combination of surgery & ozone to treat cavitations in [this Silicon Valley Health Institute video](https://youtu.be/z4C3HKvtpUA?t=44m9s). She uses kinesiology to diagnose cavitations- Light therapy - Dr Lawrence Wilson [talks about using near infared saunas to treat cavitations](http://drlwilson.com/articles/dentistry.htm). I have a far infared sauna, but I'm wondering if I should replace it with a Sunlighten full spectrum sauna...and maybe look into augmenting it with extra near infared. I've tried using a [250 watt heat lamp](http://www.amazon.com/Philips-415836-250-Watt-Flood-Light/dp/B0066L0YJE) for 5 minute sessions on my jaw and it caused quite a lot of pain. I've stopped this for now.- The Fullerton Dental Wellness Center [uses lasers to clean out cavitations and stimulate bone growth](http://www.cdchealth.com/healingdentalcavitations.html)
-   [Various forum posts talk about using light therapy devices such as the Lumen Phototherapy device to help support teeth, fight infection, and avoid extractions
    Lumen Photo Inc's website](http://lumenphoton.com/)
-   Wes Shankland is a dentist in Ohio that specializes in surgical treatment of cavitations. In [this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O6fatrZ96Do) he mentions use of near infrared light for early stage cavitations, but maintains that large cavitations will require surgery
-   In [this Curezone thread](http://www.curezone.org/forums/am.asp?i=846638), a person says they treated their cavitations with a [904nm 30watt Thera Laser](http://www.theralaser.com/design---specs.html)

**Current thoughts**

There seem to be quite a few diagnosis & treatment choices, with most being messy and expensive. I think I need to get properly diagnosed to determine if I have any cavitations and if so find out exactly where they are.

I suspect I should probably get the titanium implant removed. Many of my abdominal symptoms with appendix area pain, gallbladder/liver/spleen pain all started after I got the implant. Maybe it was coincidental, but it would be ideal to remove this variable. If it's determined that there is a cavitation around the implant, the only option may be to remove it.

If I do remove the implant, it could affect the jaw repositioning treatment I'm currently undergoing. I still have to determine how the current appliances I wear will be affected. I'm leaning towards getting a a partial denture if the implant is removed since the adjacent teeth likely have so too much decay that they could not support a bridge, and either way I don't want any further unnecessary tooth loss.

I contacted the biological dentist that performed the first cavitation surgery at my right wisdom tooth and asked about removing the implant. It will be $1500CDN for the extraction + $75 for IV Vitamin C and there is significant risk of complications. No discussion about diagnosing cavitations before or after the procedure.

A long-shot option might be to somehow find a protocol using light to heal any cavitations I have. IR LED or IR laser therapy is interesting. I wonder if I combine grounding + lots of sunlight + IR therapy if I could heal a jaw that has cavitations and heal them completely?

### lohd2015 — 2016-04-15T17:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-192756>

> IR LED or IR laser therapy is interesting.

@[Rubicon](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18225/), I would like to share a recent experience using Rubylux 640nm-660nm bulb with you. About 6 months ago, a very old symptom of shooting nerve pain on the left side of my face returned. I've had it twice in my life. Once when I was a teenager. That went away on its own after about a week or two. Once it was in my late twenties to early thirties. It would come on and off without notice, but was finally gone after I had a cavity filled in my upper left cuspid.

Fast forward about 30 years. Six months ago, this shooting nerve pain happened again (this was before I started working with the Doc's teachings). It was the worst I've ever experienced. Basically anytime I chew, there'd be shooting, pulsing sharp pains running from my upper left front teeth all the way up to my lower left eye. Brushing and massaging may have helped a little, but the pain would not go away.

Then I started to work with the IR bulb. I used it on my face to improve my skin texture, about 2 mins (max) on one area. Then a few days later, I realized the pain wasn't there anymore. This was about 5-6 weeks ago. The pain hasn't returned even after I stopped using the IR bulb about 3 weeks ago (for other reasons).

I don't know how you may react to the IR bulb treatment, as n=1. But for me, the IR bulb is extremely powerful, and one needs to be very careful with it. Go slow, and try to observe all the minute changes you feel. Good luck.

That's an interesting idea that I haven't heard before. I'm assuming you're referring to something like a [Mylar Emergency Blanket](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B01ATS601K?psc=1). I'll give that a shot and see if anything happens

### Rubicon — 2016-04-21T23:42:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-193192>

@[lohd2015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18338/), that's cool that the Rubylux near IR bulb helped you. Any reason that you limit use to 2 min max per area?

### lohd2015 — 2016-04-22T00:13:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-193194>

> [Rubicon said:](/goto/post?id=193192)
>
> @[lohd2015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18338/), that's cool that the Rubylux near IR bulb helped you. Any reason that you limit use to 2 min max per area?

Rubicon, I think the bulb, although small and not too hot to the touch, can be deceiving in the power it is able to deliver. I find that it has deep impact on many aspects of the mitochondria. Red light condenses EZ, shrinks proteins, and produces ATP. This article from Doc is most inspirational: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4701522/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4701522/)

I actually found that after using the bulb for long periods (total time about 90 mins in different areas, including spine/back), I get a huge surge of energy (without food). I felt great and loved the effects.
BUT, I think my mitochondria is unable to handle the excess IR, so I believe I ended up paying for it. I am still not sure if my recent bout of non-stop cold-sniffling is caused by overuse of the IR. I will confirm after I resume it's use. One thing I do notice also is that my husband also would sniffle a little after he uses the IR bulb on his elbows. Perhaps you can share your experiences with us also!

That is the reason why I recommend not to use the bulb for more than 2 minutes INITIALLY on any specific area, since n=1.

You can mentally divide skin surface into one inch squares and work on each 'square' for about 1-2 minutes. If you don't feel any effects after two or three sessions, you can try to increase the time. For new injuries, you can have 3-4 repeated sessions per day.

I tripped once and had to use my thigh muscles to break the fall. The muscles got really sore to the point I couldn't move without sharp pain the next day. One short session with the IR bulb took care of the sharp pain. I was back to normal within 48 hours doing no more than 2 short 3-4 minute sessions a day. The effects of the IR is not immediate. It takes an hour or two before you will notice any significant changes.

Good luck, Rubicon.

### Rubicon — 2016-11-14T00:15:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-203936>

**One Full Season of Sun Exposure**
It's been roughly one year since I really started getting the message of getting exposure to sunlight. It was fall of 2015, VitaminD winter was already in effect in Canada, and I had just spent most of summer 2015 indoors all day, rarely going into the sunlight. It was disappointing to say the least to know that I'd have to wait over 6 months for a time where there would be decent UVB light.

When summer 2016 rolled around I really made sun exposure a priority, including getting morning sun every day while grounding and mid-day sun in max UVB light. The result: this summer was the most sun I've ever consistently been exposed to in my life. I developed a dark tan and I felt that my circadian rhythm really established itself. Sleep became really excellent overall and I found a wave of intense tiredness hitting me every night around 10:00PM. I began consistently waking up without an alarm clock.

I tracked all my sun sessions with Dminder. One thing that struck me is that even at the height of summer, the sunlight in Canada is never really as intense as closer to the equator. On June 20th the days are about 16 hours long, but the sun does not get higher than around 60 degrees in the sky and Dminder told me the most vitamin D I could get was around 175 IU's per min. In comparison, I found when I visited Hawaii in early spring the sun was already getting up to 74 degrees altitude and I was able to get 400 IU's per min. More than ever I'm determined to move closer to the equator where I can both get more sunlight throughout the year, and where it will be more intense.

Here are some personal Vitamin D totals for myself from Dminder:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

July total Vit D: 110,362 IU's
Aug total Vit D: 47,884 IU's
Sept total Vit D: 31,780 IU's

I took a measure of my 25-hydroxybitamin D3 [levels on Sept 23](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rubicons-labs.17326/#post-203959) ... 40.8 ng/mL. Lower than what I was hoping for.

**Wife is on board**
About half-way through summer my wife became convinced of the benefits of Low-Carb High-Fat (LCHF) diet. Despite my promotion of it for the last few years, it took the shared experiences of some of her Facebook friends to go from being completely against it to embracing it. In the past she actually had a lot of concerns about my high fat intake and worried about giving myself a heart attack. When she saw me doing other quantum health stuff like CT, she really was not happy and thought I was nuts.

However, since getting convinced about LCHF, she also became much more open to all the different quantum health stuff that I'm interested in. I was able to get her out in the morning sun with me, mid-afternoon sun for vitamin D, drinking fluoride free water, etc. She's even been reading Dr. Kruse's stuff a bit, listening to podcasts, and learning about light and quantum physics. It's been really, really cool to see. No resistance anymore to shutting the wi-fi off at night, turning off the breakers, or similar.

Over the course of a few months her own health was positively affected and some challenges she was facing have disappeared completely. She's now fully on board for the move to the southeast US.

**Relocation**
Tentatively still planning to do a road trip from Florida to southern Texas this Christmas season. The plan will be to take a couple of weeks to drive along the gulf coast and look at places we'd want to possibly move to.

Moving is going to be quite disruptive to our lives as we'll loose all of our local connections. I anticipate some significant challenges such as acquiring a green card, work arrangements, expenses, etc. It's all really exciting though and something I want to make happen. With the recent election results...perhaps if the new president is successful at simplifying and reducing taxes, it will help make moving a no-brainer.

### Rubicon — 2016-12-30T05:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/post-205903>

I met up with shah78 last week in Progreso. He's really liking the magnetism in the center of the crater. Last I heard he's doing some epic daily 6-hour sessions in the ocean and seriously feeling the combined effects. Personally, I'm not sure I notice much difference between Progreso and PDC.

Progreso is significantly cheaper than PDC/Cancun, but at this time of year the Carribean sea is MUCH nicer than the Gulf in Progreso (murky water there)

After next week I may try out Porto Morelos or Tulum. Haven't been to either yet.

Sofar my favorite place in the Yucatan is the [south side of Isla Mujeres](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-vacation-spots.17315/#post-187906). Relatively low-density population, some jungle, an easy walk to the east side of the island for sunrises and the west side of the island for stunning sunsets. Some houses at the center of the island have clear views of both oceans. It's a neat spot.

I haven't tried biking / hiking in the Yucatan...I just stick to the shoreline.

> I have been consciously getting more sunlight since 2013 when i retired and I could lounge around naked in my back yard at lattitude 35...i walked barefoot in the frost and sunbaked naked everyday for all winter and summer for 3 years...and I improved from how I was when I was working in an office during daylight hours 5 days a week... but since moving to latitude 24 and being at the beach everyday for 2-4hrs a day since January 2017 I can say I have noticed a HUGE difference. I am solar powered now and I feel it...I am feeling really good....this is due to the strength of the sun alone. I have been eli-paleo since before 2013 and paleo and keto before that for 10 yrs....I have a magnetico since 2015...I have been drinking italian spring water since 2014....I have been doing CT intermittently for 3 yrs....minimising EMFs and blue light for 3yrs....the difference I have noticed in me and my husband (56yrs) with sunlight at latitude 24 is HUGE. I plan on holidaying at latitude closer to the equator 2-4 times a year from our current location. I can not imagine trying to be a healthy human in canada unless you live practically naked in an igloo and eat seals.

If you have money then it is easy to move somewhere else for sure. But if you don't, you just respect what you have and try to be as healthy as you can until you find the chance. Unfortunately you need money in nowadays to do some stuff. Even the bank where I live doesn't give loan.

-   [2015-01-28 - Audiogram](https://www.dropbox.com/s/cc5ifwernnzhi8d/2015-01-28%20-%20Audiogram_.pdf?dl=0) - Found mild loss of hearing in right ear, no indication for source of tinnitus
-   2015-03-01 - Ubiome ([Bacteria](https://www.dropbox.com/s/t6g3m3yz5ur3tkk/2015-03-01%20-%20Ubiome%20Bacteria_.pdf?dl=0)) ([Function](https://www.dropbox.com/s/eg0bsr7cnihzays/2015-03-01%20-%20Ubiome%20Functions_.pdf?dl=0)) - Found low diversity of gut bacteria
