# Thread: road to success- where to start?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-to-success-where-to-start.17376/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### urst131 — 2016-01-13T10:40:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-to-success-where-to-start.17376/post-186154>

Hey guys,

I haven´t introduced myself so far. My name is Chris, I´m a medstudent from Germany in my final year. I read the Epi-Paleo-Rx and researched some stuff and was absolutely blown away.
Anyways, I´d love to get some help, concerning my first steps on the road to optimal. Therefore I got some questions.

1\. I´ll be getting a lab test next week, so far I want the following tests:

\-hs-CRP
\-Cholesterol -LDL,HDL,Triglycerides
\-fasting Glucose, Hb1Ac, fasting Insulin
\-Testosterone, free Testosterone
\-Estradiol,IGF-1
\-SHBG (sex hormone Binding globulin)
\-Cortisol
\-Testosterone: Cortisol Ratio
\-DHEA-S
\-Vitamin D
\-Calcium, Magnesium, Sodium,Potassium
\-Hb
\-Feritin, Serum Iron, Total Iron Binding Capacity, Iron%Saturation
\-Vit B12, Folate
\-Homocysteine
\-TSH,FreeT3,FreeT4, reverse T3
\-Omega 6:3 Ratio

\-> do you think I should get anything else? any suggestions?

2.I´d like to start to fix my circadian rhythm. I thought about the following steps to start with:

\-see sunrise outside for 1-3 minutes every day
\-circadian rhythm app for mobile phone and laptop (f.lux)
\-no eating after sunset

\-> is there anything else you would suggest to start with? Please consider, that I´m just starting, It´s a road to optimal

3.Cold Thermogenesis
\-will start with step 1, although I started taking cold showers for 1-2 months in summer.

4.Diet
\-I´m starting with the epi-paleo-rx diet next week, I have been eating a paleo diet for about half a year now, with occasional diet breaks (e.g. Christmas)

If you have any other suggestions, I would love to hear them, thanks four your help in advance.

### Lahelada — 2016-01-13T11:39:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-to-success-where-to-start.17376/post-186156>

Hello Chris,

Willkommen. You have a good grasp of how to start.
My extra suggestions would be this

\-see sunrise outside for 10 minutes every day. You do not need to look up all the time but be outside with some
Skin exposed.Arms will do if it is very cold
\-circadian rhythm app for mobile phone and laptop (f.lux) .Cut off laptop use as early as you can before going to bed.

\-no eating after dawn. I presume you meant night. Try to avoid carbs at night even small amounts.
Do you have a pine forest,Mischwald or park near? They are optimal in this descending order. You need to get outside as much as you can as in these latitudes you will be UV deficient,especially if you are from North Germany. Sunbathe with skin exposed whenever you can.
An indoor out door pool that some Hallenbads have would be great .
Consider changing your night time light you allowed in your house to a minimum and change to red light.If you have family who would be opposed to this move still get the red lights and use them in addition to the conventional ones. Optimal would be minimal lighting though.

Viel Erfolg.

### urst131 — 2016-01-13T11:59:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-to-success-where-to-start.17376/post-186159>

Thanks for the reply.

Of course, I meant sunset  sorry for the mistake.
As for the forest, this is no problem, I will try to implement walks there, starting with twice a week? Have to be realistic here
You would suggest swimming?
Redlight bulbs for light after sunset?

### Lahelada — 2016-01-13T12:09:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-to-success-where-to-start.17376/post-186161>

Yes, to all of the above. It is not the swimming per se but exposing yourself to the temperature differences between the inside pool and outside pool water. In other words a Hallenbad mit Aussenbecken und Ausschwimmkanal.Kneipp was onto something...
Whereabouts in Germany are you?

### urst131 — 2016-01-13T12:11:03+0200

Saarbrücken, middle west

# Thread: Poor blood circulation in the extremities
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-blood-circulation-in-the-extremities.17378/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jamie Ward — 2016-01-13T13:05:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-blood-circulation-in-the-extremities.17378/post-186165>

Hi guys,

Firstly, thanks so much to @[Jack](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5/) @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) for pulling together decades of research so us non-medical professionals can benefit from all the info. This is truly an exciting new part of health and wellness and I am privileged to be a part of it. So, thanks Jack for all your efforts.

Also, thanks to al you guys for the amount of time I see you guys putting in with one another. I hope the day comes I am equally as knowledgable and can return the favour back to the community : )

Now onto me... I posted on the FB group a few days ago:

> I am interested to hear your thoughts... I am a lean, athletic guy in his late 20s who eats well, exercises and avoids artificial blue light as much as possible and tries to respect the circadian rhythm as much as possible...
>
> I've always had, since I can remember, very cold hands and feet. It is not related to diabetes as my fasted glucose hovers around 80 and HbA1c is at 4.0. Thyroid is also healthy, except slightly elevate reverse T3 26.62ng/dl - I noticed this could be leptin resistance). I've been trying to connect it to the works of Dr. [Jack](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse) and I have come up stumped. I also get head rushes easily.
>
> One thing I would note, as clearly I have a circulation problem, my MCV is 100 and I was wondering if that would prevent blood getting through the capillaries in the extremities (haemoglobin is 17.3g/dl).
>
> I appreciate this isn't a diagnostic centre but I've ran out of ideas as to what it could be. I assume it could be linked to a low DC in my body?
>
> Thanks!

I got some good responses, but I thought I would turn to the forum for anyone to help. I have had heaps of bloodwork so if you think any marker would be useful info then please ask me if I have it and I can post.
I had a really low CRP of 0.04. My Cyrex autoimmune showed up for arthertic peptide, which was interesting to see because I use to get a lot of joint issues before I lost all my weight, although I'd say this has 99% gone now.

I also had extremely high liver enzymes. I haven't got to the bottom of this yet but I think it could be down to my bodybuilding style routine as I have seen two studies showing this. In a couple of months I am going to take 2 weeks off and retest.

The main concern I wanted to look at though, as I mentioned in the FB post, was coldness in my extremities. This has always perplexed and I am not to sure what is causing it. Any ideas?

Thanks in advance

Jamie

PS - Bloodwork attached

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-13T15:20:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-blood-circulation-in-the-extremities.17378/post-186171>

Your circulatory system can be affected many ways by many alterations in its atomic lattice. Since these are the tubes your blood plasma drives through this can alter the charge contained and delivered to tissues via the blood. RBC's have 4 key frequencies in the UV and IR absorption pattern that is contained and stored in porphyrins, hemoglobin, and water. ### Martha Ray — 2019-12-06T05:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-blood-circulation-in-the-extremities.17378/post-283172>

Great thread for me Jamie as **I have very cold extremities my nose, hands and feet especially at night when I am trying to get to sleep****.** My head also get cold but I think it is healthy not to cover or cap head at night.
I am a newbie to the work of Dr. Jack Kruse and here at the OHF. I have primarily focused on more sunlight and avoiding ALAN (Artificial Light at Night) and I am hypothyroid and physically still am somewhat atrophied from a former health crisis, weight loss and inactivity. I have poor temperature adaption and poor circulation. I know very little about Water, Magnetism, CT therapy or gravity factors with health. Had been seeking health in food and supplements for the last 10 years... lots to learn but right now I am trying to decide on a height for Incline Bed Therapy. My local Musculoskeletal practitioners a chiropractor and physical therapist disagree on flat sleeping and I also have a shoe maker and comfort shoe advocate that disagree about zero drop and I am puzzled about that in relation to my feet. Very few good footwear choices today. I've wondering about my blood sugar Now which is 5.1 fasting A1c. Fasting glucose in 80's after a meal average 94... I had wondered if blood sugar was a factor. Was hypoglycemic as a teen (it was popular back in the 70's and then in my 50's I was 100 lbs heavier, had stressful work and was eating tons of sugar. Then blood sugar was high 90's so pre-diabetic. I know conventional ranges are poor indicators. I have multiple projects but right now working on information from Sophia Health Incline Bed Therapy Article, [https://www.sophiahi.com/inclined-bed-therapy/](https://www.sophiahi.com/inclined-bed-therapy/) and Andrew K Fletcher's (UK) Incline Bed Therapy Video hosted by _Patrick Timpone_ on his One Radio Network show

Andrew K. Fletchers web site is **_Inclined Bed Therapy_** [https://inclinedbedtherapy.com/](https://inclinedbedtherapy.com/)

I am stuck with **what incline to choose** from a polyurethane MATTRESS ELEVATOR product that fits under ones mattress made by
**[_Avana Comfort_ : Sleep-Support Bed Wedges, Elevators & Pillows](https://www.avanacomfort.com/)**
[https://www.avanacomfort.com](https://www.avanacomfort.com)

I am concerned about 'detox' from 6" rise tilt in a 7" head to 1" food or if new product 5" with a 4" rise on our 80" bed one may be more comfortable than the other... I am the princess and the pea when it comes to sleeping comfort all my life I have had to have everything cool, dark and quiet. Only in the past 10 years I cannot get to sleep if I am too cold especially my feet which seem to get colder when I lay down flat sleeping on my back with socks on.
**Incline Bed Therapy is supposed to be good for cold feet and many other heath benefits or what think you all?

UPDATE on 1 3 20:**
**I bought the Avana Comfort twin 7" Mattress Elevator to try out on a Relax the Back adjustable bed in a guest bedroom been using about 3 weeks and like it so far. I do slide down in it a little during the night and it negates the adjustable benefit to that bed. No matter as I do not need the adjustable** **features** **right now.**
**Will test it out longer as I still wonder about polyurethane foam even with the cotton canvas cover and** **whether** **to try to the 5" on other beds. It came in compressed and it had no chemical smell so I was happy about that.**

# Thread: Jeanne's Optimal Journal...finally.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 130
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeanne — 2016-01-13T20:44:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-186212>

After being on here almost a year and reading and reading and listening I am ready to reluctantly post my journal. I'm a pretty private person and this stuff has blown me away for a while now so I've been trying to just catch up and learn from everyone.
I'm 57 years old and live in Franklin, Tennessee. I am married and have 5 grown children. I grew up near here and genetically I am 99.9% Northern European. I grew up in a rural area and lived outside most of the time. We didn't have cell phones, video games and only 3 channels on TV so it was just what we did in the old days!  We had a huge garden that we all worked for years. My parents were extremely vibrant and healthy until got older and more "affluent" and started buying all their food from the grocery store and staying inside almost all of the time. My mom had a bad car accident in her late 30's and had back surgery. She was never the same. Multiple back surgeries later and living inside with no physical activity and she gained a ton of weight and eventually succumbed to breast cancer at 56. She had type II diabetes when she died and was on at least 20 various medications. I watched this happen and decided I would NOT go down that path. However, like most people on here my approach from everything I read at the time was diet, supplements and exercise. My father died of colon cancer (he also had diabetes) at the age of 78. Fortunately, I have always been a sun lover and continued to be outside and get sun until approx. 15 years ago when we started being told that sun would kill us all. I started staying inside more, wearing sunglasses outdoors and, of course, wearing sunscreen whenever I was exposed to the killer sun. I also started working in my husband's dental practice full time since my kids were older. So, lots of blue light. My health started declining a few years ago and I increased the supplements and started eating ketogenic and was eventually diagnosed with chronic Lyme disease. Eating a ketogenic diet helped for a bit and then menopause hit. I had terrible sleep, mood swings, gained 10 lbs and just felt tired much of the time. My doc prescribe Bio hormone replacement and it helped tremendously. After reading Jack say that using bio estrogen is not that great of an idea I have drastically cut back and use it only in small doses now when the hot flashes start up. I am still using progesterone cream. I don't have hoshimotos but I do have thyroid nodules and am taking 30 mg of Armour thyroid daily. Since hearing Jack on a podcast I have made some big changes: AM sunlight, sunlight as much as possible during the day, lots of water, fish and more fish, and I have almost eliminated all the supplements I was taking. I am also using a tanning type bed as a supplement for the sun this winter when we have those awful cloudy, overcast days. I have noticed in the past that I just feel better in the spring and summer and have always hated winter. Now, I know why. I am no longer working in my husband's office either and that one change made a HUGE difference in how I feel. I thought it was difficult because I was working with my husband which I felt was stressful but now I think it was the horrible lighting, EMF and lack of sun. Now, I feel so much better but I know I have lots more to do. First, we moved to Franklin from Memphis 7 years ago and we live in a community of 1500 houses close by. I am looking at moving to a more rural area now but this will take a little time to implement. I also want to help my husband figure out how to reduce his days in the office and his schedule so he can get more sun. There's a huge difference in his mood on the weekends as opposed to the weekdays. We are going to Jamaica in early February and I cannot wait! It helps me get through January knowing that a tropical vacation awaits.

However, adjusting his surgery schedule and work days IS under his control. He's Southern Italian too so I know he needs more sun than even I do. I know that I felt like crap when I worked in his office full time. My daughter recently quit her job as a designer in a retail store because she realized the fluorescent lights were making her sick. She told me a story of one day when there was a storm that blew out a main power supply to the mall. They had back up lighting but could only operate a few lamps around the store--no overhead lights were on all day. She said the mood of the workers AND the customers was way better that day. She talked to her store manager about light and they told her she was crazy so she just quit and is no on her own. Thanks for all you do to shine the light into the darkness!

I am thinking it is back due to excess EMF since I live in a neighborhood of people with WIFI and cell phones. I can see 10 WIFI networks in my house when I look for networks. Offsetting this EMF situation with more grounding, more sunlight and more DHA to see if this helps.

### Jeanne — 2016-02-05T14:47:15+0200

Plenty of UV during the day. UVI averages 6-9. My skins looks amazing and I have a glow. They do have Wifi but the signal is weak and only one shows up. Tinnitus is better but still there. That's where I was a year ago, though so it's not surprising. I told a couple of them about Jack. Maybe they will look further.
One thing I'm going to try to improv is my presbyopia. I always had perfect eyesight until 45 when I started to need readers. My readers have gradually increased in strength and I depend on them. I'm going to really use light to see if I can get. Some improvement.

Just returned home from 5 days in Scottsdale, Arizona visiting our son. Of course there was a UVI of 6-9 every day and we were outside in it pretty much all day. However, THIS vacation I gained a lb rather than losing like I did in Jamaica. Eating and drinking were pretty much the same. So, I have been thinking about this today and have this observation: First, there was no ocean and no beach so even though we were in the sun we weren't grounded or near the water. Second, the elevation in Scottsdale where we were was 1,000 ft which surprised me. I didn't realize Scottsdale was that high. I slept well there but last night my sleep was horrible....partly because of stupid DST back at home and partly, I think, because we had a very early flight and didn't get any morning direct sunlight before we got on an airplane.

### Jeanne — 2016-06-11T14:40:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-196350>

I absolutely LOVE mornings! And I especially love spring and summer mornings because the sun rises early and I have always been a early riser! I love waking up because I feel the sun rising and seeing the first morning light and the beautiful colors of the sky. I love hearing the birds singing and the doves cooing. It's quiet and peaceful. With my Lyme disease flaring, the mornings are also when I feel the best and can focus and think and pray. I had a great conversation with a girl a friend referred to me that has recently been diagnosed with Lyme. She's an artist and her work is unbelievable. She creates works of art using tiny pieces of paper in collage-like form and it's absolutely beautiful. The patience required for such a task is incredible to me. The first thing I identified is that her circadian rhythms are messed up because she works during the day cleaning houses to support her art career and then stays up most of the night doing her art. I explained the circadian rhythms of the body and why she needs to make some changes. She's been seeing a ND that is all supplement oriented so it's going to be a long process. I emailed her a few podcasts with Jack and his website to start and we are meeting next week. Look at her art at carolynbeehler.com!

### Jeanne — 2016-06-12T14:21:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-196412>

After years of being very strict low carb and mostly ketogenic,I am experimenting with adding carbs back into my diet in the summer after an accidental bio-hack game me some insight. I've been in the sun every day this week for hours and drinking tons of water. But on Friday afternoon I suddenly just tanked. I felt horrible after eating some wild prawns and was really hungry. So I lost my mind and ate a bunch of chocolate covered pretzels that have been in my pantry from a party two months ago and a bunch of wild cherries. Felt immediately sleepy and tired and fell asleep early but slept really well. Expecting to feel like crap Saturday morning, I actually felt very well and lost 4 lbs on the scale. I think I was just too low on the carbs for the amount of sunlight I am getting and, even in menopause, I need more carbs---maybe not chocolate covered pretzels, though.  I know Jack has discussed eating more carbs from fruit and veggies in the summer but I figured with menopause I needed to stay pretty low carb year around. I'm going to add some season fruit and veggies into the diet and see how it goes.

### Jeanne — 2016-06-22T00:13:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-197047>

I have so many thoughts and questions after that June Webinar. I thought I would write some of what I'm trying to figure out here. Lyme Disease has been kicking my butt since it started getting warm weather -- even with all the sun I am getting. I have read that Lyme symptoms does often get worse in warm weather. Why? Is this actually a good thing? Was the Lyme able to hide undetected in my body in the winter and all the sunlight has awakened my immune system causing more Lyme to die or is it something else. My CD 57 back in the winter was up to 115 and a month ago it was 46. Not good. Can I use the biohack Jack talked about with the Crispr Cas9 kits to somehow biohack this issue and get better while I am in the process of making the move further south to the Gulf? My 23 and me results have been rolling around in my head too. I have 100% northern european but my family has lived in the Kentucky/Tennessee area for at least 6 generations. How do these mutations relate to my mitochondrial environment from my mother and grandmother. My grandmother is 98 and still lives alone. She's pretty amazing. My mother died at 56 from breast cancer that spread all over her body but her downhill demise all started when she was rear-ended by a crazy driver and had back surgery. My mother had numerous constant issues starting with complications from the surgery and eventually the constant pain caused her to be indoors, inactive and on numerous pain medications leading to high blood pressure, depression, cancer and diabetes. Not that surprising. No one else on my mother's side has or had cancer. MTHFR, CBSA360A, VDR BSM and BHMT-08 all show as homozygous mutations. Was the VDR mutation a way to protect the body from the strong sunlight in the south vs northern Europe? What would be the reason for the MTHFR mutation. I'm doing research and looking at the SNPS to find out if I can figure out the possible reason. Various other heterozygous mutations exist as well. COMT, Mao-A, MTRR, BHMT and most are related to methylation, breakdown of neurotransmitters and Vit. D. Lots of work to do but I need to feel better so I can even make this move happen.

### Jeanne — 2018-07-23T21:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-246532>

After hearing Jack talk about the skin and how women should cover up if they have to be in blue light, I quit my membership to Title Boxing and bought a bag for my garage. For two weeks, I have been boxing and working out outside or in the garage with the doors open and natural light. I already notice that my heart palpitations are better. I'm not sure it's related or a coincidence. Also, I thought about how I worked in an office under blue lights and in front of a computer for years. A lot of the time, I wore a dress with my arms, chest and legs exposed but I wore mineral makeup. I think this is why the face on my skin is almost perfect with almost no age spots but my forearms, hands and chest have numerous age spots. I am still trying to sell my house and move to the Gulf. The neighborhood where I live has grown from 800 or so houses to almost 2000 in 10 years that I have been here so I am surrounded by thousands of people with 2 or more electronic devices. I can't even imagine what will happen when 5 G gets here. I also started thinking about my symptom of Lyme started soon after moving here from Memphis. Memphis is not a great place to live but our neighborhood was much smaller and more rural. I also remember going back to my integrative medicine doc after being here 3 years for a full body thermography and he was shocked at how bad it was compared to my last one. I remember him telling me I had to be under some kind of harmful stress and suggested I leave working for my husband in his dental practice. While he certainly is a stress causing individual, the stress was most likely working under those horrible lights and living in a 4 G-EMF hell hole in Franklin, TN.

### Jeanne — 2018-08-03T15:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-247374>

In 2001 I got breast implants because nursing 5 babies did a number on the girls. I was pleased with the results but 17 yeas later I wish I had never done it. I have had heart palpitations and other symptoms that have steadily worsened for over 2 years. I went back and looked at my medical history and it appears these weird symptoms of hormone imbalance, thyroid, swollen lymph nodes and many others started 6-7 years after placement. My Int. Med doc discussed the possibility of breast implant illness and I have researched and talked to women who had similar issues and had them removed. It seems 75% of women get significantly better or 100% better after explant of the implants. When I researched the symptoms of this illness, I realize that over the last 10-12 years I have experienced about 90% of them. I think when I found Jack and started implementing his recommendations and getting so much sun, water, grounding etc. I have been able to maintain but the palpitations are to the point that I would do anything to make it stop. So, on August 13 I am going in for explant and a lift procedure. I will journal my experience here for any women that may have some of these issues and have implants. I am also MTHFR 677 and some other snips affecting detox and methylation. I'm not looking forward to surgery and being put under but I am anxious to see if I start to get well after those fake boobs are out.

### Jeanne — 2018-08-08T20:29:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-247926>

So, 5 days to explant procedure. I am getting as much sun as possible every day including exposing the girls to sun. Also, doing ice packs on the boobs and future incision areas. I also had 2 50G Vitamin C IV's and have another one Sunday afternoon before the procedure. The owner of the IV clinic lives in my neighborhood so he's coming to my house and I'll do another one Monday after the surgery. Getting in bed by 9:00 every night and sleeping pretty well most nights--except when the heart palpiations wake me up.  Following Jack's suggestions for pre-surgical prep and hoping my healing process goes well!

### Jeanne — 2018-08-18T23:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-248753>

Surgery was 8/13 and was successful. Got a 50G Vit. C IV the night before and another one on the way home from the surgery center. The surgeon sent the capsule to pathology and gave me the old implants. They were not leaking and looked pretty clear. The right one did have some white spots which the doc called "calcification." I did not take any pain meds at all. The first night was not great sleep, probably due to anesthesia and lights in the surgery center but since the first night I have slept great. So far, I have noticed my brain feels clear. The normal brain fog feeling that comes with the heart palps. is gone. Vertigo is also gone but the heart palpitations have continued on and off. However, today I had zero heart palps, which is amazing! I sat in the sun and even got the girls some sun--my surgeon would not agree with that. My alt. med doc also wants me to consider some IV Detox but I am leaning towards just letting my body heal and do that itself. Once I am released in 2 weeks I am heading to the beach for a week and then again the month of October. I still haven't sold my house so I can't move yet. I am also drinking tons of spring water. Any suggestions will be appreciated.

I'm wondering now if this was Lyme disease as my doc said or sensitivity to NNEMF. Other observations being back in the Nashville area for 2 days. I have two friends that were trainers. One is a male 43 year old African American that has a very muscular physique. He just found out he has a tumor on his adrenal gland requiring surgery. ### Jeanne — 2019-07-23T17:20:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-274864>

I moved to the gulf coast permanently on June 15. It was not an easy process but I was determined to make it work. I have been trying to sell my house in Franklin, TN for 2 years and thought it was sold only to have the contract fall through. So, I decided to lease it and found a family from Chicago who has leased it for a year. I got rid of so much furniture, clothing and just crap I didn't need to move to a much smaller place. I'll deal with trying to sell it again next spring but for now it will work. I bought a little beach cottage 1/2 mile from the gulf and walk every single day--unless it rains-- for an hour. I also consistently get am sun and see the sunrise. I get some mid-day sun and spend almost every single night watching the sunset on the beach. As of this week, my heart palpitations are almost completely gone. I am also sleeping extremely well. It's easy to get fresh gulf seafood almost anywhere here and I eat oysters, shrimp, grouper and snapper all the time. I am looking forward to September when school starts and this area becomes quiet again with no people!

How do you like me now............?

### Jeanne — 2019-07-30T19:48:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-275191>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=275152)
>
> Nashville = and adenosine problem. What fucks up sleep? problems with adenosine........guess what drug is used for cardiac tachycardia's? ADENOSINE.
>
> How do you like me now............?

Adenosine problem because of 5G and population? I heard you talk about adenosine on the Q&A as something given for heart failure. That would be an IV drug? Can it be taken orally?

### Cindy Domitrovits — 2019-07-30T23:00:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-275213>

> [Jeanne said:](/goto/post?id=275191)
>
> Adenosine problem because of 5G and population? I heard you talk about adenosine on the Q&A as something given for heart failure. That would be an IV drug? Can it be taken orally?

Adenosine building up during the day is one thing that drives us to feel sleepy during the night. Then while you sleep, the adenosine is cleared out, leaving you refreshed and ready to go in the morning… for most people. I did read somewhere as well that Jack said "What fucks up sleep? problems with adenosine........guess what drug is used for cardiac tachycardia's? ADENOSINE." Not sure if it's an oral or IV drug.

### Cindy Domitrovits — 2019-07-30T23:07:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-275214>

As well if you follow your genetics from 23 and me...

There is a genetic variant in the adenosine deaminase (ADA) gene that decreases the clearance rate of adenosine. People who carry the variant may get more slow-wave, deep sleep at night, but they also may need to sleep a little longer o feel refreshed the next morning.

Check your genetic data for rs73598374 ([23andMe v4, v5](https://you.23andme.com/tools/data/?query=rs73598374); AncestryDNA):

-   C/C: normal clearance of adenosine
-   C/T: reduced clearance of adenosine, more deep sleep but may feel sleepy when waking up\[[ref](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21734253)\]
-   T/T: reduce clearance of adenosine, more deep sleep but may feel sleepy when waking up

Luckily I have C/C normal clearance of adenosine.

### Jeanne — 2019-11-27T22:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-282846>

So, today is Wednesday November 27 and the day before Thanksgiving. This will be the first time I'm here in the gulf and not in Nashville for Thanksgiving. Although I miss having all my family and grandkids around I'm happy about this decision. I moved here on June 17 permanently and have been in the sun as much as humanly possible. Today, I was sitting on the beach reflecting on my life, decisions and the present. It was 77 degrees today and sunny! I was out there for 4 hours or so. Here's what I realize and this is as honest as it gets...I was married for almost 30 years to an energy vampire/time bandit. He chose to leave the relationship and go down a very different path. I honestly wanted to make it work and put the marriage back together but I am SO damn glad that did not happen. I finally realize 3 years out of that relationship that he was making me sick...I was always walking on "eggshells" wondering what he would do next. Sometimes, when you are inside off a relationship like that you can't even see it happening. I also thought about friends who told me AFTER our divorce..."he was an asshole and I never liked how he treated you." Thanks a lot for not telling me. If someone you love or care about is in that kind of relationship don't be afraid to tell them what you think. Yeah, they may not want to be your friend after but so what?? Tell them the truth--not what they want to hear. So, bottom line. Getting out of that relationship...taking care of myself...moving out of Nashville to the Gulf of Mexico has made me better..mentally, emotionally and physically. So, I'm grateful. Grateful for the path and grateful for the journey but very grateful for where I am now. I'm also looking forward to what is ahead. Life is good. But you have to make it that way--for you.

### Jeanne — 2026-01-11T23:58:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jeannes-optimal-journal-finally.17382/post-365174>

Posting this here to update and put my thoughts down. I haven't updated in a while. I've been living 2 blocks from the gulf for 6 years from Franklin, TN. It's been great and I have felt wonderful getting lots of sun and sand. But something started last December that I think changed things. I realize they have been spraying us from the sky for years but last December they really upped their game. I can still get plenty of sun in the summer but the winters are brutal. I had tinnitus, joint pain and heart palpitations living in Franklin which was diagnosed as Lyme disease after my NP couldn't figure it out. Now, I don't think I even had Lyme disease and I think my issue was sensitivity to EMF living in a nieghborhood with over 1500 houses in a mile area. When I got in my car and drove 7 hours to the beach all the symptoms--mainly heart palps--went away.
So, since November of 2025 not only are they spraying us almost daily but we are apparently being microwaved based of what I see from Nexrad and the sky showing waves of fake clouds. Our state gov. passed laws outlawing geoengineering in July but it's not helped one bit. Florida no no longer the Sunshine State. Now my tinnitus is off the charts and heart palpitations are back and my body is sore. Joints hurt. Back pain, knee pain both come and go and move around my body. I have a metal roof but not walls. And I'm outside most of the day. I've started closing windows when the toxic fog arrives. Researching issues surrounding EMF/microwaving the sky and these are some of the common symptoms. I'm 67 so not a youngster at all but my body feels like it's aged 5 years in 1 year. I play pickle ball and am extremely sore after. This week has been unusually horible overhead but now I have lower stomach pain and headache. At my age I really didn't want to move again but it's looking like I'll have to if I want to stay alive.

# Thread: Coming Out Of My Shell....Again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 62
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-01-14T03:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-186240>

Coming out of my shell….again
Some of you will remember me from my first Journal that I posted here several months ago and I want to thank you for your support that you showed when I posted that. Based on what I have been learning recently, I have been suffering from low dopamine for a long time, and I deleted my first journal and bolted out. I’m coming back and I am really working on that aspect of my health now, as well as many other things. I know now that a good dopamine level is crucial in order for everything else to operate optimal. It’s amazing what I have been learning from Jack and the rest of you here, the webinars and Q and A’s, blogs and from my own research. I love to learn this and it’s become a true passion for me. Please bear with me here, as I am learning so much and I comprehending this so much better now, but, sometimes I have a hard time verbally expressing myself and asking questions. So, I just continue to read and learn and work on raising my dopamine, because I know that little by little, it’s all going to come together for me.
My history is as follows:
I was born on July 27th 1960 in Muskegon, Mi and I weighed 6lbs 50z. I was my Mother’s third child in 3 years and from what I know now, even though I was a “healthy” child by modern medicine, my mother was very depeleted and low on dopamine. For the most part, she never went out into nature and enjoyed it. She also worked as a surgical nurse and was indoors in artificial lighting, which as I know, was a different kind of lighting than the nurses have to work in today. My Mother is still alive, but she is in very poor health and is a very negative, angry lady and she suffers from high blood pressure and several months ago, suffered two mini strokes. Her Mother passed away at age 94, from alzheimers and her Father passed at age 80, from ling cancer.
My father was born and raised on a farm and he was a healthy child who never even had a pair of shoes until he was 12. His “shoes” were made out of potato sacks and he spent most of his childhood helping his father farm 80 acres in western Mi. He married my Mother and worked in Supervision for a company that manufactured motors, for 32 years, He is the one who spent time in nature with us kids and it was from my Grandfather and him, that I learned to master garden and enjoy the fruits of our labor. I remember him suffering from several bout of the flu and he also eventually suffered from high blood pressure and a heart attack and at 80 years old, he passed away from pancreatic cancer after spending the last three years of his life in a nursing home, due to dementia. His Father passes from parkinsons disease at age 84 and hus Mother passed fro a Stroke at age 57
My childhood was not a very happy one, except for the quality time that I spent with my Dad and both sets of my Grandparents. My Mother was physically and mentally abusive and as a result, I suffered from very low self esteem. I did learn a valuable lesson from her though. I vowed that I would not become abusive to my own children, and I kept that promise to myself. Even though I was involved in dance and gymnastics, and eventually on my high school dance team, I still felt different and out of place. I did however, develope a few close childhood friendships and made a few close friends in Jr and Sr High School. I am still in contact with them too. I did experiment with magic mushrooms on several occasion and with marijuana in Hugh School. I also drank wine on the weekends with my girlfriends. Now for some of my childhood and adult health history.
1\. I suffered a grade 2 concussion at age 6 or 7. Also, at age 2, I fell out of my crib and broke my collar bone.
2\. I had a speech impediment until age 8 for which I had regular visits with a speech therapist
3\. I was ADD and for much of my life’, until recently, I am now focusing much better.
4.At 18, I began suffering from depression, severe depression and I never did take drugs for it though. The depression did not leave me until I met my second husband at age 28. I met him in an out patient intensive therapy group, We have been together since and he understands me well and has been nothing but supportive. He is gone a lot though for his work and we spend two days per week together and talk on the phome nightly. And the time we get to spend together is that of quality.
5\. I have given birth to three daughters, one from my first miserable, abusive marriage. My first pregnancy was a very hard one. I was 21 and had morning sickness the entire time. I also developed gestational diabetes and was in labor for 24 hours and they had to give me a spinal injection and use forceps for her delivery. She weight 9lbs. 110z I suffered from debilitating migraines for 2 years following her birth and sometimes when I walked, my right leg would give out.
My second pregnancy was abreeze and normal and I delivered her with no problem. She weighed 8lbs 110z
My third and final pregnancy went well as well and she weighed 6lbs. 13oz. I had a tubal ligation following her birth.
I felt well for quite a few years after her birth, but then I gradually began to gain weight and at 5’6”, reached 190 lbs
At age 38, I was involved in a rollover accident and my seatbelt saved my life. I suffered a grade 2 concussion and lacerations and bruises.
38 is also the age that I really started to get into alternative health and I got back into gardening and consuming all organic foods and taking supplements….lots of them. I would average 20 to 30 various supplements per day and I joined the local gym and lost weight and I felt great and I even consumed a raw food diet for 6 months and then I began to crash. I added pastured meats back to my lifestyle and felt better.
Although I continued to take my supplements and eat well, I became so busy with working full time and my children’s school and activities, and volunteering in the community, hosting exchange students, etc. I was slipping and just felt like I was some kind of a machine. I kept all of this up for years and was rarely ever sick though, just the occasional cold and restless nights and at times, did feel like screaming! I know now that I was not nurturing myself enough.
By the time I was in my mid 40’s, the weight was coming on again and my blood pressure began to rise and although I felt fatigued at times and my mind would be cloudy at times, I just kept going. I began to manage a private campground as well and they had two cell towers installed on their children’s playground and they also had WIFI installed and the three routers were in my office. This is when I really began to crash…headaches, unexplained boils on my stomach, major cognitive dysfunction, body aches, vision problems and terrible fatigue like I had never experienced before! I knew I was in trouble and during my free time in the office, began doing some research and one thing led to another and I came upon information on the dangers of NNEMF exposure. My research really took off from there and I quit my job! I began to improve, but I still suffered from cognitive dysfunction, high blood pressure and fatigue. I did manage to become a Certified Holistic Nutritional Consultant after that and I worked with cancer patients. My 21 year old daughter being one of them. She was diagnosed with stage 3b hodgkins lymphoma. We went the holistic route to heal her and to this day, she is still alive, but she is stubborn and no longer as healthy as she could be.
Fast forward to 50 or so. I decided that I was going to, once and for all, take charge of my health again. I was still eating well and taking my supplements and I wasn’t very good at keeping my dental appointments though and decided to make that appointment and go. I had to have a tooth extraction done and a bridge made afterwards. It was after that extraction that I became addicted to opiates and this went on for 1 ½ years. I knew I was dying a slow death and I had to face myself in the mirror and stop this addiction or die! I went through the withdrawls cold turkey and it was living hell! But because of my experience, have been opiate free( other than my injuries) since! Although I would be lying if I said that I never had the urge again. Light, water and magnetism has made that urge stop!
By the time I was 52, I decided to join the gym again and hire a trainer and go LCHF. Not realizing what I was doing to my health then, I was training under artificial blue light 6 or 7 days per week and I also signed up for Aikido classes. I was hooked! It was then that I decided to Train towards my first NPC Figure Competition, as I was doing remarkably well with sculpting my new physique. I also tore my meniscus in Aikido class and although I could no longer go to that class, I was still going to the gym in my brace and boot and kept my crutches close by. I only trained my upper body.
After I had healed for a while, I wanted to enter my first 5k and when I was training for that, my meniscus gave out and I fell and suffered from two severe sprains to my ankle and right foot. This really set me back, but I still continued to go to the gym and train, just not as hard. I was in a boot again and I kept my crutches close by.
I decided to start going to my husbands DO. Naturopath and have blood cell analysis done. I was happy that I no longer suffered from HBP. It averages around 113/74 However, my first analysis revealed many problems and my Dr. was very concerned. Here is the list of my results from my first and subsequent analysis’s, with the exception of my last one, last September, which was pretty remarkable.
Full body Inflammation
Adrenal fatigue
Liver stress
Vitamin C deficiency
Anemia
Candida/yeast (bad case)
Parasites
Black Mold
Dehydration
Platelets clumping (Major)
Body structure was good, but by my second visit was breaking down
My monthly cycles also ended and I had never really experienced a problem with those, other than some monthly hemorrhaging.
I was experiencing incontinence and it was getting worse.
Myopia
My sense of smell was diminishing
Major cognitive dysfunction
Aches and pains
Fine motor skills with my writing was going. It was getting so that when I tried to write, my finger dexterity would give out and I couldn’t even make the connection with my mind to the word either.
Fatigue would come and go and then I would have some energy
Denial, denial, and did I say, DENIAL!!!
I wanted to Believe that I was Superwoman and nothing could bring me down! I didn’t want to end up like all of the other women in my family!
My Dr. was very upset with me that I wouldn’t stop training, so I promised that I would train only three days per week. I went from averaging about 16 to 18 hours per week, down to around 6 or 8. I was still prepping for my competition though and I was tired.
I’ve written enough for this evening and will continue with more here shortly with my healing journey after discovering Jack. I’m glad to be back and no more pretending. I’m not Super Woman…Yet!
Thanks for not giving up on me Jack! Most important though, not giving up on myself!

### Lahelada — 2016-01-14T04:21:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-186246>

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-14T19:19:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-186285>

adrenal fatigue links to a lack of UV and IR light via our surface receptors. Adrenal fatigue links to the PVN.Light information is captured exclusively by the eyes using specialized retinal photoreceptors and transduced directly to the SCN via a dedicated neural pathway, the retinohypothalamic tract (RHT). Each day the light-dark cycle resets the internal clock, which in turn synchronizes the physiology and behavior controlled by the clock.

The major biochemical correlate of the lightdark cycle is provided by the pineal melatonin rhythm. Under normal light-dark conditions, melatonin is produced only during the night, by using sunlight captured in biogenic amine during bright sunlight during the day to power the effect. Moreover it provides an internal representation of the environmental photoperiod, specifically night length.
This pathway also helps yoke the Vitamin A cycle in the photoreceptors to the environment in this way. These two cycles become correlated novelties that allow the brain to predict the future by trend setting photo chemical cycles. The trend is predictable if dopamine is also also being created. This is why dopamine and melatonin are correlated by being biogenic amines. One should never be uncorrelated without the other unless the light stimulus is very altered in some way tied to an unbalanced spectrum. The synthesis and timing of melatonin production requires an afferent full spectrum light signal from the SCN which projects to the pineal gland via the paraventricular nucleus and the superior cervical ganglion. This is why BAT is found over the posterior neck region where this ganglia exists. Light becomes able to burn BAT protons to liberate heat and energy for sleep energy to run the non linear optical programs in the brain of regeneration when the DC electric is designed to be absent when day light is absent. This helps explain why light exposure during the night also inhibits melatonin production acutely and provides an indirect assessment of light input to the SCN via the RHT. Light is a correlated novelty to the DC electric current in wakefulness as Becker found in all of hiw work on salamanders and mammals. This finding has been largely ignored by biology to human peril in our modern world.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-01-15T14:39:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-186342>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=186285)
>
> adrenal fatigue links to a lack of UV and IR light via our surface receptors. Adrenal fatigue links to the PVN.Light information is captured exclusively by the eyes using specialized retinal photoreceptors and transduced directly to the SCN via a dedicated neural pathway, the retinohypothalamic tract (RHT). Each day the light-dark cycle resets the internal clock, which in turn synchronizes the physiology and behavior controlled by the clock.
>
> The major biochemical correlate of the lightdark cycle is provided by the pineal melatonin rhythm. Under normal light-dark conditions, melatonin is produced only during the night, by using sunlight captured in biogenic amine during bright sunlight during the day to power the effect. Moreover it provides an internal representation of the environmental photoperiod, specifically night length.
> This pathway also helps yoke the Vitamin A cycle in the photoreceptors to the environment in this way. These two cycles become correlated novelties that allow the brain to predict the future by trend setting photo chemical cycles. The trend is predictable if dopamine is also also being created. This is why dopamine and melatonin are correlated by being biogenic amines. One should never be uncorrelated without the other unless the light stimulus is very altered in some way tied to an unbalanced spectrum. The synthesis and timing of melatonin production requires an afferent full spectrum light signal from the SCN which projects to the pineal gland via the paraventricular nucleus and the superior cervical ganglion. This is why BAT is found over the posterior neck region where this ganglia exists. Light becomes able to burn BAT protons to liberate heat and energy for sleep energy to run the non linear optical programs in the brain of regeneration when the DC electric is designed to be absent when day light is absent. This helps explain why light exposure during the night also inhibits melatonin production acutely and provides an indirect assessment of light input to the SCN via the RHT. Light is a correlated novelty to the DC electric current in wakefulness as Becker found in all of hiw work on salamanders and mammals. This finding has been largely ignored by biology to human peril in our modern world.

Hi Jack. I'm not speaking Chinese yet, but, I sure do understand what you said here. I purchased Becker's book many years ago and read it when I first began my research, trying to figure out my problems. It's on my bucket list of books to reread. It has helped me a lot , as I learn more, to keep referring back to the blogs, books and other resources to help connect the dots Wearing sunglasses most of my adult life and not paying attention to my cycles and I know now, taking melatonin for 15 years or so, was so detrimental to my health. I'm in the process of working on more of my journal to share my n=1 wins, etc. My adrenal stress/fatigue is one of the wins . My health was such a mess, that my Dr. had me coming in for dark field studies every four months instead of six. My last one was so good and we were both so happy that she said "You don't have to come back for six more months now." I'm due next month again. I've been so faithful with this lifestyle, and at times it hasn't been easy with my obligations, but, I've been very determined because I don't know how many years I have left here, but, want to be healthy, really healthy and make the most of my gift here and give back. Thanks again Jack!

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-01-15T16:58:48+0200

So yesterday, I had family obligations and I was on the road traveling for much of the day. I also travel with a black light in my car now, for the UV light , which I learned from Jack too. He uses one at night in his car. I have myopia and I also know that the more UV I harvest through my retina, the better I withstand the NNEMF that I'm exposed to and the more I will heal the Myopia. I also make sure that my window is partially down much of the time too. I get the benefit of CT and UV light that way. The sun is best! My myopia is gradually improving and I have gone from needing my glasses for driving, to not needing them during the day when I drive. I knew that I wasn't going to be home in time after the sun went down, which I pulled over in a Rest Area to view and enjoy. How could I not?  The sun went down and I got back into my car and rather than putting my glasses on to see, I put my special blue blockers on and I did just fine. This was actually the first time that I have been out driving in the dark since beginning this lifestyle. I have to admit that I was a little concerned about driving at night without my glasses and protecting my SCN from all of the artificial blue light, but came through with flying colors. I also forgot to mention something that I feel is important here. My last eye exam revealed the beginning of a cataract and now that I know the truth and real cause of what causes them, I'm sure that with my diligence, that will be history too. Light is my drug

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-01-16T01:36:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-186428>

My Healing Journey: So, after reading one of Jack’s blogs and doing some more research, based on what I had learned, I purchased Jack’s book and read that and began the RX. I have been living the Epi-Paleo lifestyle for 10 months now and have gone through some amazing healing, If only I would have discovered this many years ago, I wouldn’t have had to focus on food and supplements, (wasting so much $$$) and so much exercise in hopes to heal. I have now learned the truth and I know in my heart now, and based on how far I have come and all of the research too, this is what I will be doing now for the rest of my life on Earth.
Thank you Dr. Jack for all that you have taken the time to do to truly help anyone who desires this truth to heal.
I am also adding inflammation to my list above, because my body was loaded with that too.
Basically, I allowed my body the time that it needed to heal and reset itself. This meant that I had to give up my heavy training for a while and instead, go for nice walks on the beach and just practice some very soothing, gentle stretching on a daily basis and stay out of the gym. I also began doing ice face dunks and gradually worked my way, within a two week period into immersing myself into the cold water of lake Michigan. I took very well to this . I also opted for nature walks in the woods in my barefeet. I threw away all of my sunglasses, because I wouldn’t give them away to anyone after what I learned about exposing the retina to the sunlight and how healing it is. Wearing sunglasses for so many years was a huge part of my problem. I also purchased several pairs of blublockers for my protection against toxic, artificial blue light. I have recently purchased a pair for daytime use and I use them when I am on my computer or phone, along with flux, wearing them when I go into any store, etc. with fluorscent lighting as well, wearing my full blockers at night, several hours before bedtime. The only light in my home now is blacklight, which I keep on 24 hours per day now, and in the evening I use nontoxic candles and open up the fireplace for some wonderful IR as well. I have one redlight now that my husband uses when he is home. I’m also enjoying some nice outdoors fires with the added benefit of CT and retiring to my tent, or inside a short time later. I’m sleeping exceptionally well now and I dream a lot and wake up feeling rested. I’ve also noticed that I just naturally do a full body stretch upon waking, that stretches my vagus nerve. If it is still dark upon waking, my environment is blublockers and the black lights and candles. I no longer sleep with my blacklight on me because I feel as though daytime and evening use is enough. Once I see the sun rising, I step out into that and that indicates that it is daylight and activates my SCN and hormones for the day. I pay very close attention on cloudy mornings, before it has a chance to rise above the clouds. If I miss that, I use a special flashlight. When I was resetting , I would consume my BAB shortly after rising, but, no longer feel the need to eat right away. I generally am not hungry until late morning now and I will sometimes only eat a dozen raw oysters and drink my Pallegrino and perhaps a pastured egg or two. Sometimes some pastured bacon too. It all depends because I pay attention to my n=1. I generally am never hungry in the late afternoon anymore and if I am, consume a can of wild caught sardines or a small steak and a half a stick of pastured butter, but always within 4 to 5 hours of retiring. I only consume seasonal carbs that apply to my region, here in Michigan. I do not have cravings for them right now either.
Moving on to my protocol over the summer and my last Dark Field Study results last September.
Number one was to shut down the WIFI and my phone at night and when not in use. On nights that I didn’t have to run the air, pull the main breaker. I’ve had a Geocleanse in my home now for about 8 years and there is no “smart” meter on my home, nor my neighbors. I can pick op 3 wifi signals and they are poor. Within a three mile radius of my home, towers and antennas are in the doulbe digits. We have a place on lake Michigan too and there are towers and antennas in the mid 100’s, so I spend a minimal amount of time there and when I do, I CT and lay on the beach for the most part. I also spend time in the woods, naked and connected to the earth. I feel remarkable after this. I also enjoy sleeping many nights out in my tent, at home, connected to the magnetic field. I pay very strict attention to my light and dark cycles and stopped taking my nightly dose of supplement melatonin. I was a supplement Queen and stopped all but a few that Jack approves of, being Tumeric, Holy basil, rosemary, Ginger, the Chlorophyll in season and Ubi Non GMO coQ10.
I rarely ever missed a morning of getting out in the sunlight and always nude. I would use magnesium oil on my tummy and my breasts. I also would take a couple tablespoons of liquid chlorophyll before heading out. I stopped laying in a lounge chair and laid and sat in the grass. I also purchased a UFO 660nm light and used that on my naval, three to four times per week for about 20 minutes or so.
I was still going to the gym, but wearing my blublockers and covering my skin, but gave that up at the end of August and began training outdoors, in the UV and connected to nature. Within a few weeks of doing that, I noticed that I was no longer experiencing discomfort from my previous injuries. I will never step foot in a gym to Train again. You couldn’t pay me all of the $$$$ in the world, because If I don’t have my health, I really have nothing! I have cut my training down to only a few hours per week and I still have my physique and what I consider amazing for a woman who is going on 56 years young now. I will never over train again since learning the truth.
I went for my Dark Field after four months of living this lifestyle and everything in that study was perfect, with the exception of a little candida/yeast left , which I hope will be gone by the time I go back next month. My Dr. was amazed at my results and that’s when I broke my healing protocol the her. She then informed me that she had another patient who has MS and was using light and getting excellent results! BOOM! I gave her Jack’s website because she wanted to learn after seeing my results. I will be seeing her soon
My Myopia is improving, my incontinence is history, my writing has returned just fine and my fingers are working well/making the connection with words again, have not been sick a day since starting this. I did have one day that I felt like a cold was coming on, so I went out in the sun for most of the day and I felt normal again. My monthly cycle did return for a few months, but, I have not had another one now for about three months. Full menopause? My sense of smell has returned again too. My aches and pains did return, not from my injuries, and only subtle. So, I now use a Sperti UVB lamp 3 to 4x per week and that solved that. I no longer feel drained as I did in the past, my cognitive function is improving daily and I no longer am ADD like I was in the past. I can now focus and I no longer procrastinate like I was. I guess my dopamine is rising and that will eventually come too. I’m beginning to feel more sociable and it doesn’t bother me like it used to, that people are going to judge you, no matter what! This is life and we are all human folks! Jump in and make the most of it!
Other notes
Vitamin D level was 72 at the end of UVB season in Michigan
I do biohack on occasion with 160 watt solar glo
Sex is still amazing, but starting to experience some dryness, what to do?
Still doing the UFO light to the naval. Is this ok Jack?
My Quantlet will be a part of my protocol
Are there any other tests that you would like me to have done jack?
I’m also using my cyrohelmet more consistent now for CT. I made note that I do benefit from that.
I’m now doing a winter biohack with a nicotine patch that I cut up to =4mg. My n=1 approves.
I’m more in control of my emotions and I fel like my decisions are getting better. However, I am human and will have my moments and that's ok.
I’m happy to share and I hope that you find this encouraging and will share your stories too. I am starting to read other's journals now and I am learning from yours and appreciate each and everyone of you here. Thanks for sharing what you do and I am looking forward to entering more in my Journal.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-01-19T14:58:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-186732>

The entire Time series has been blowing my mind, along with the January Webinar and the Q and A, but especially Time 7, which I keep going back to and I’m sure I will do so many more times as I continue to question my past lifestyle and how I became so unhealthy. I’m not kicking myself though ,because that’s really useless and detrimental to Optimal. I’m empowering myself now and I have come, what I consider, a long way over the past 10 months. My main goals are to make my health my number one priority, to keep learning, questioning, implementing, and eventually using everything that I have been learning to help others who are willing to help themselves. I’ve learned and let go of trying to help anyone who is low on dopamine and not there yet. That has actually been very freeing for me and has helped me with my recovery.
I also wanted to mention here that the past two road trips that I have made, one being divided into two, three hour trips and then staying for two weeks in a nnemf hellhole for 2 weeks, and the most recent one day 6 hour trip, did not leave me feeling exhausted as I normally would have felt. I did just fine. I contribute this to the result of my high Vitamn D level acting as my Faraday cage. Several years ago, my level was only 28 and I had a hard time with being around the nnemf.
The past few days have been amazing for me as I have been spending more time out in the snow and cold here and getting plenty of CT in and uv light. I feel more mind clarity coming on and I am doing some of my best thinking now when I am out in this environment…..literally chilling out  I managed to Train on Sunday in nothing but my shorts and a cami and cotton socks and my Training gloves, doing some sprinting on the path that my husband has shoveled and some heavy lifting with my weights, a full body session that lasted 30 minutes. It felt beyond amazing and I wanted to keep going, but I’ve made that promise to me, that I will no longer do that. The windchill was -6 and hubby just smiled and shook his head. He supports my journey and sees how much better I am doing and he is beginning to catch on more as well.
Two more things that I forgot to mention here, and I feel are important and Jack has said, “share as much as you can” is that when I turned 40, I did get my one and only tattoo and it is located on my right shoulder. I am well aware now of the consequences of having a tattoo. Would it be a wise choice to have it removed, or, would that result in even more of a problem? It is mostly red ink and some green and black. I haven’t experienced any rash or itching around it this winter, as I have during the past winters. At age 26, I was also diagnosed with MVP and my Dr. put me on Tenormin, which I only took for a short time because it made me very sleepy. I just lived with the occasional problem of finding it hard to breath and heart flutters, eventually drinking more pure water and taking co q10 and that helped a lot. I also took my naval piercing out and that has healed well. I still wear my nose studs on occasion and earring hoops on occasion too, but not like I used to. I save those for special occasion out with my husband now. I know that even though they are of high quality, they are still magnets for nnemf. I've never been one to wear a lot of makeup and prefer to just let my face and skin breath, remaining natural and free. The cosmetic Industry is also making a lot of $$$$$$ as well
I have suffered from disordered eating in the past. from the time I was about 14 to 15, I began to starve myself and sometimes I would force myself to throw up the food that I did eat. What saved me was, I fainted in school one day and the Principal brought me home and I began counseling. That was the end of that, until I began prepping for my Figure competition. In order to get down to at least 12% body fat for the stage, I found myself once again, putting my finger down my throat and forcing myself to vomit. I knew then, it was not a good thing and that I had to stop the insanity! I was surrounded with so many ladies who were suffering from body dysmorphia and I knew that if I did not remove myself from that environment, my troubles would only escalate. I made a very wise decision to leave it.
This is all I have to share today. Peace!

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-02-04T20:43:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-188218>

It's been A few weeks since I have posted. So, I wanted to share something that I have been dealing with for years and even though trying numerous things, supplements, vinegar, tea tree oil, diet, etc. nothing would even touch what was going on with my toenails and it kept getting progressively worse. It was affecting me at the emotional level too. Well, I just wanted to share my progress picture here today. My small toes show no sign of whatever this was, either toenail fungus, or, Psoriasis, and my big toe is at least 60% better than it was. This used to be curved and thick underneath and the skin around it was very thick. I have been going to the same person for my pedicures for almost two years now and he has very lovingly been taking care of my feet for me. I even bring my own well water to fill the footbath with because I won't allow my feet to soak in fluoridate, chlorine water. He commented on how nice my nails and skin is progressing now. It took a while, but, by really following what I have learned, this is just one more health problem that is really healing nicely. I no longer feel discouraged! I feel so empowered because I feel so much better, but it's also pretty amazing when you can actually see the progress too. Don't ever become discouraged folks! Some things are going to take longer than others, but eventually you will get there, just as I am.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-02-11T03:08:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-188740>

It's time to stop by and make another note of progress here. Something else that I forgot to mention in my context and is important to share here....
For approximately the past 7 or 8 years now, my face has been very sensitive to the extreme cold and so much so, that on occasion, I would wake up with it red and swollen and small blister bumps. Sometimes my eyes would be so swollen, they would be slits and I would have to stay home and cancel any plans for several days. At other times, my cheeks would just be swollen and red. I live in Michigan and have been sleeping in my bedroom with no heat and the windows open at night. This winter, I have been sleeping outdoors in my tent quite a bit as well, plus I spend a lot of time outdoors for my CT and uv light on a daily basis. Even on cloudy days. To date, I have had absolutely no problems with any of these issues. I'm also contributing this to changing my environment and a lot of DHA.

I am also going to start a biohack with my Sperti and spray H2o2 on myself before I use it. I'll give that some time before I come back with my results.

I've also noticed that since I've really been working on my dopamine issues that I just really feel at peace and the things that used to upset me, don't like they used to. However, I've noticed now that I can't help but notice whenever I come in contact with people, rather it be on the internet, or, in person, I can tell if they are low on dopamine. This has just been my observation lately.

The biohack with the MB is coming along nicely and I'm up to 6mg 2x per day and I biohack that with a 14mg nicotine patch. I generally use the patch every other day. I love the mind clarity that the combination gives me. I still have a way to go with my cognitive function, but, I keep noticing subtle progress there.

My energy level is excellent, sleep is excellent, averaging between 7 to 8 hours per night, dreaming a lot and recalling all of them.....loving my dreams!!!

I really enjoyed the February Webinar and I'm looking forward to the Q & A coming up shortly!

Peace!
Mystic Rose

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-02-18T00:41:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-189277>

The last few days have been amazing and since my hubby is off this week, we decided to go to the lake and spend some quality time together in Nature. I feel so blessed to be with a man who prefers to be out in Nature when he's not working. People call him "The Woodsman"!  Last night, we set up our tent in the woods, by our place and we slept together in nature. This morning, once the sun was up, we sat in the sun and we devoured 4 dozen oysters and I made bacon over the fire and we made a toast with our Pellegrinos. After a while, we ventured down to the lake and we walked down the beach, hand in hand and I kept looking at the water and the ice and he read my mind.....after 28 years together, we have a tendency to do that with one another. We strolled back to our place and I packed everything that I would need for my first CT session of the year in lake Michigan. We made our way out on the ice about 15 or 20 feet and I did some Wim Hof breathing exercises and after a few minutes, in I went....fricken COLD and much different from my experience with all of my exposure to the cold air and body contact with snow, and previous CT in warmer lake Michigan temps, but fricken unbelievably AMAZING and so peaceful, like I was entering a different dimension perhaps and it's just another experience that one just has to experience for themselves to understand what I am trying to relay here. Is this what feeling like a "Superwoman" feels like? I managed to stay in for about two minutes and ventured to the shore to warm up in the healing sun and couldn't resist venturing out for several more sessions after that....I didn't want it to end. We ventured back to our place and hubby built a fire for us to warm up by and I really didn't feel like talking much because there was this transformation that was taking place within over my new experience. I fell in love with another part of Nature today and I'm going back for more of her tomorrow, bringing my CT to an entire different level. I plan on doing this often now, before she begins to once again, warm up for another season, as Spring is just around the corner.

I came home to another amazing surprise this afternoon. Time #9, another very eye opening blog and it has helped me connect more of the dots on my journey of healing. Amazing! I'm also counting down the days to my Quantlet's arrival. Hubby doesn't know it yet, but there's one coming for him too. I wouldn't have it any other way!

Peace,
Mystic Rose

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-03-05T21:23:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-190244>

It's been a few weeks since I posted. So, I just wanted to update. My Dr. broke her right wrist and she is not able to practice right now, so, I have not been able to go for my live blood analysis and my husband, who has accupuncture done on a biweekly basis, has not been able to have his needles done. My main concern is for a good recovery for her though.

I've been spending less time online, printing out more information and reading outdoors in the sun. I basically just use my Facebook as a tool now, to share what I am learning. I'm very careful as to what I give my valuable energy to and have become more aware of what sucks it out of me, Facebook being one of them.

My TriMeter arrived and I'm not pleased with the nnemf reading in my car. I've really limited the time that I spend traveling on the road too and I make sure that I combine all of my errands, etc. to only two days per week now, and even though I spend a lot of time connected to the sun and earth, to complete the DC current, I have decided to not only travel with my uv light, but, I now plan a 30 minute electron foot bath before leaving home. I plan on looking for a convertible here soon and can travel with the top down this spring, summer and into the fall.

The weather is going to start warming up here later this week and I never thought I would say this, but, I'm going to miss the snow and the cold! I've really embraced it and as a result, made it through the seasons without feeling fatigued and without even a cold. I didn't have any cravings for carbs, or, alcohol(but I am looking forward to some wine) either and no blue moods. For me, nature has given me some substantial mental health benefits too. I embraced and opened myself up to it and it's simple pleasures and it has given me inner peace like I have never experience before. One more thing that nature has taught me, is that there is nothing wrong with me. When I'm out in nature, nature displays incredible diversity in all her glory.

Something else that I have noticed now is that the hours, minutes and seconds have just basically melted away and time has just slowed right down for me. I think that living by the clock and out of my natural rhythm was a huge part of my stress. Nature has actually modeled a healthier pace of life for me. I've spent time watching the trees, plants and flowers grow s-l-o-w-l-y, deer just graze very calmly and even the rabbits and squirrels just scamper about at their own pace, to their own natural rhythm and as a result, have done the same....how amazing! Behold the beauty of nature! It has definitely brought me so much closer to my Spirit and the Spirit. It has helped me to experience the Divine! These are just a few of the reasons why I believe we are so happy in the natural world. This is why nature heals, and helps us to live lives of meaning and joy.

Sharing one of my favorites for you to enjoy. A beautiful piece by Maisey Rika. Thanks for stopping by and may peace be with you all!

"Master of the puppets limit the sun, poisons water, and destroys magnetism. To exert power and prove their existence they must slowly bleed you. As the child outlives the father, so must the puppet bury the master………" Dr. Jack Kruse

In 2002, Physiologist Henry Lukaski of the Department of Agriculture’s Human Nutrition Research Center in North Dakota (USA), established that during moderate activity, individuals with low magnesium levels use more energy not less energy—and therefore tire more quickly—than those have adequate levels.
