### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-03-16T12:48:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-190958>

It was absolutely beautiful here in western Michigan yesterday and I spent 6 hours outdoors, doing yard work, training, relaxing and reading and just taking it all in and enjoying it.

I do some of my best thinking when i am outdoors and yesterday I became very curious about some old milkweed pods that were laying in the fallen pine needles and I was examining their structure and how strong and firm they still were, even after going through the winter months, I looked over at the strong tree trunks and their branches and this led me to thinking about Boron and how I have noticed that some people here are actually taking it and also, the wash detergent booster, borax. I said to myself, ya know what, I bet you anything that the sun contains the element, boron too....I bet you anything that I am getting the boron that I need from the sun. I have learned that by spending so much time out in nature now, that if you just look around and really pay attention and curious and begin to question, it will provide you with the answers. It's all right there in front of you.

It turns out that I was right about the sun containing boron and I am getting all of the boron that I need from it. No need to take a pill or a detergent booster, because the sun , through my retina, is providing me with the boron that I need. Nature provides again!

The milkweed pod that sparked my curiousity.....also kind of resembles a half oyster shell

[http://pubs.acs.org/doi/abs/10.1021/ed005p875?journalCode=jceda8](http://pubs.acs.org/doi/abs/10.1021/ed005p875?journalCode=jceda8)

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-03-17T18:29:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-191081>

^^^^ What a wonderful message and addition to my Journal from you Jack. I'm almost one year into my journey now and it just feels so amazing to be doing so ridiculously well, and I mean that Jack! I like my Dad's quote too. Whenever someone would ask him how he was doing, he would always answer them with, "Terribly Terrific"! He had a great sense of humor.

I was just sitting outdoors and I was thinking about my almost 91 year old Mother In law who is a WWII survivor. Many in her family didn't make the bombings and she did. Her survival story is heartwrenching, but has a beautiful ending because she met her husband over there and came here and had two beautiful children, my husband being one of them. She had a thriving antique and reproduction business for almost 40 years and did a lot of her work outdoors and in the sun, out in the Country. She never wore sunglasses either, or, sunscreen. She still can't believe that people use that stuff. She's in a lovely country setting now, in a very small assisted living home and prides herself on being the only one in her little building who doesn't use a walker and stands up nice and straight. Rain, snow or sunshine, she goes outside, twice per day and walks and when the geese are there, takes some food out of the containers provided and feeds the geese and talks to them She has never worn glasses and has had no cataract surgery. She's never used a cell phone, or, computer and does not own a LED TV. She loves to listen to music and read good old fashioned romance novels and participates in all of the activities in her building. I can see it all now, how her circumstances and survival led to her thick skin and amazing inner strength. She has always been a doer and not a complainer. Anything is possible and at the rate she is going and living, I think that she is going to be a person who lives to be 100.

I switched things up today and had lightly seared lamb chops instead of oysters for my DHA and electrons, along with a couple of pastured eggs. That will be my one and only meal until tomorrow morning. I'm so connected to the Earth and Sun, that I just don't desire anything but that one meal per day. Plus, I know how my Mitochondria is loving the results from IF now. My N=1 says, YES! Always paying attention to when my body speaks to me here.

My Myopia has improved even more. I was breezing down my local Country road yesterday and could read all of the address numbers on the mailboxes, quickly and with no problem! I was really excited about that. Everything is becoming so clear now and when I have my next eye exam, I can't wait to let my Dr. know how I have been healing my Myopia.

Not much else to write here today and I'm on my way to meet a friend and enjoy some CT in Lake Michigan and take my walk on the beach today. No heavy training today.

Not the first time research like this has been done. At first, researchers found that light restored memories temporarily (for about a day), but repeated exposure resulted in memories returning for longer (up to 6 days). I like using sun light in humans with neurodegeneration to help their symptoms once they develop. If you can stop the blue light exposure and add back sunlight to your life you likely won't ever face a neuro-degenerative disorder. They are consequences of indoor living in a modern world with alien suns in a microwaved world.[http://www.sciencealert.com/scienti...st-memories-in-mice-with-alzheimer-s-symptoms](http://www.sciencealert.com/scientists-use-light-to-reactivate-lost-memories-in-mice-with-alzheimer-s-symptoms)

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-05-01T19:10:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-out-of-my-shell-again.17385/post-193963>

From one of the Members in the Quantum Facebook group.~ Nice!

Sharing some words from Jack that mean a lot to me and have helped to keep all we are learning in perspective:

\*) Make your life matter every day you have it. Become grateful for your time.

\*) Drink a lot of spring water, mineral water, or RO water. It is never gloomy, unless you allow it be so.

\*) Get serious about sharing with others regardless of their response in the past. Let them live in your new today with you.

\*) See your work as your passionate masterpiece in life.

\*) Expect the best and prepare for the worst with regards to EMF exposure and fake light. Turn off your circuit breaker every night no matter what you think you may lose in luxury. Electricity is not always our friend.

\*) Keep a journal and do not DIY it. It will cost you $$$. Trust me, I am out 150K going the DIY route.

\*) Read biographies of Tesla, Einstein, and Oppenheimer. You need perspective other than your own.

\*) Plan a schedule for your day, week, month, and year……time is your most valuable asset.

\*) Know the 5 highest priorities of your life and carry them on your person.

\*) Say no to distractions in your life or with your health always. Never compromise on you.

\*) Embrace meditation to offset EMF effects. Embrace neuro biofeedback as a way to cope.

\*) Improve your thinking every single day you rise to see the sun rise. You only see 25,000 in a life.

\*) Get a mentor to help you re train your thinking. If you want to improve your masterpiece you must improve your mind first.

\*) Begin to question paleo and its dogma. It advocates somethings that can hurt you. Turn off your technology.

\*) Get up when the sun rises and walk at the water’s edge every day you can. It is a gift to your biology.

\*) Walk bear-footed when you can, wear leather soles, get sun on your bare skin and you will eat less food naturally.

\*) Find more heroes who represent what you want for you. Hero’s are found everywhere when your mind is open.

\*) Become someone’s else hero by helping them help themselves. Avoid enabling them. Love is not enabling.

\*) Smile at everyone…….especially strangers. It increases the Schumann resonance for us all. Move naturally in nature.

\*) Hire an EMF engineer and carefully look at your home, work, car and sleeping area and that of your family.

\*) Don’t settle for anything less than excellence. Never settle for a B, C , or D when A is available.

\*) Never let someone else set your bar. Savor life’s simplest pleasures, and use your imagination to recreate you.

\*) Embrace camping and sleeping on the Earth. Take your kids camping. Build a fire and spend time with them.

\*) Spend time at museums and art galleries and tap your inner artist to find your inner masterpiece.

\*) Walk in the woods and make sure to lean on some big trees and just listen………

\*) Write thank you letters to those who’ve helped you in your life.

\*) Forgive those who’ve wronged you, no matter what they have done to you.

\*) Remember that leadership is about influence and impact, not titles, prizes awards, or accolades. Carve memories in hearts not marble

\*) Create unforgettable moments with those you love.

\*) Have 5 great friends, and tell them why they are great.

\*) Become stunningly polite, and boldly aggressive in helping those who seek your knowledge.

\*) Unplug your laptop, ipad, iphone, radio, TV, and all your energy-efficient appliances that now have smart meters.

\*) In, fact, sell your TV and never watch the news ever again……..

\*) Read from books daily. Go to the book store browse and aisle…..when you touch a book buy it without opening it. The fact you were drawn to it is enough insight that you should consider reading it to improve yourself.

\*) Avoid the average and make your nominal become phenomenal.

\*) Be content with what you have, but never settle for what you want.

\*) Pursue your dreams when others make fun of you for those dreams. They have nothing to hang their hat on so they would rather tear you down

\*) You are human and you will err but be authentic in your actions. They speak louder than your words/grammar/spelling.

\*) Be passionate about you, your loves, your work, and your health. Become the best infectious disease ever for the world.

\*) Apologize when you know you should to everyone.

\*) Embrace a moment to celebrate another’s achievements…….teach your kids that failure is their best recipe for successes.

\*) Have a vision for your life and never come off that sight.

\*) Know your strengths but embrace your weaknesses. They are what will be the hammer and anvil of your masterpiece.

\*) Focus your mind on the ideal of things and do not fret about the negative. Cut the negative from your life as soon as possible.

\*) Sit back and smile because you know you are patient and can wait for your chance to pounce. Prepare for that small window of opportunity and be ready to take full advantage of it.

\*) When things seem at the worse, you never give up because that is what a winner does.

\*) Clean up your messes quickly and prepare for the worse by planning. Things happen but we always recover because we give no quarter.

\*) Use impeccable thoughts and words to convey how much you care. No one cares what you know until the former exceeds the latter.

\*) Leave you area and go to areas that are remote and different from you would normally visit. Travel a lot more and be random about it.

\*) Talk to 3 strangers today. Force it if you must. See the beauty in things you never knew…….examine the unexamined.

\*) Honor your children and spouse.

\*) Tip well for services all the time.

\*) Become a great teammate in every aspect of your life but do not let one who needs you help fall behind.

\*) When facing your critics, smile at them. Encourage them to question everything but give no extra energy to critics.

\*) Spend time in the cold snowy mountains in leather shoes and become deeply curious to the mysteries in your life

\*) Write down your top 5 values and put it in your wallet.

\*) Shift to achieving results, and stop just allowing yourself to be busy.

\*) Innovate, iterate, and invigorate your environment. Inspire others to greatness no matter how you feel. Some day they may lift you.

\*) Pay attention to your world. Speak less to it and listen more to it………

\*) When the phone rings……let it. Put it on vibrate and watch how your life improves.

\*) Take the hand of some one you care about…..hold it, and look into their eyes and tell them what you really think about them without words………..use the power of your mind to show them what is inside of you to give to them. Then tap that moment from here on out. Become super human in a modern world that is tearing us apart.

# Thread: Morning routine

### Eilee — 2016-01-15T16:57:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine.17392/post-186357>

I'm trying to establish a morning routine and not sure if there is a correct order to things. I start work very early so don't have a lot of time but this is what I am able to do thus far:

1) 3 minute cold shower or 5 minute alternating hot/cold
2) 15 minutes of morning sun
3) sometime before 11 am - UFO light (15 minutes on area below navel/15 minutes sacral area)
4) before bed, hot tub and then 3 minute cold shower

Would love any feedback, besides the obvious that more sun would be better

Also, with regards to the UFO light, I do it in a very cold room, so outside of the area under the light, I am quite cold with goosebumps on my arms. Is this temperature differential a problem?

### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-09-30T08:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine.17392/post-202113>

Do the morning sun grounded / bare feet. Drink beige ice water. Eat big seafood breakfast.

Emily

# Thread: Email letter from Coreena to MrPinkies....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/email-letter-from-coreena-to-mrpinkies.17393/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MrPinkies — 2016-01-15T17:28:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/email-letter-from-coreena-to-mrpinkies.17393/post-186362>

I've decided to post this for her... I've decided to post it mainly because some of the things she has been though and tired, I have not dealt with.... the email reads:

Kate,

I am pretty new to Jack Kruse's website, but the ideas presented make a lot of sense. My husband started on his journey two weeks ago and I will start mine very soon. Our son is 3 1/2 and although a formal diagnosis is yet to be made (in Arkansas, it takes FOREVER!), we know he is moderately autistic. Last April we did tons of research and immediately changed our diet to GAPS and found a natural doctor. We also cleaned up our environment, but did not have all the information we have now. Fast forward 9 months and many testing and tweaks in diet and doctors....and we have transitioned to a SCD/paleo rotation diet (due to some food sensitivities - no sugar, no gluten, no grains, no starches, no nightshades, no nuts, no dairy except for camel milk). He takes a handful of supplements, nothing crazy... Probiotics, Zinc, B12, B6, B1, Methylfolate, D3 and Magnesium Citrate. We changed lightbulbs, limit tv to only a bit in the morning, try to get him outside every day. We haven't had wifi for over a year and we always put our phones on airplane mode if he is around. I still have a laptop, but I only use it when he is napping, and it's also only connected to the internet when it's connected to my phone (wired to USB hotspot on my phone). He has been drinking spring water since April and has never had fluoride toothpaste. There are a couple things in his environment I don't know how to change except to move, which just isn't an option right now. We live less than a mile away from a small airport, and there is a meter on our house (not officially a "smart meter", but its cousin). He also has a video monitor in his room, the opposite side of the room from where he sleeps. He can't open doors and he still doesn't tell us if he doesn't feel well or something is wrong, so we feel it is necessary.

After more research this last December, we decided to try Houston Homeopathy for 6 months. The first thing they addressed was parasites with Diotomaceous Earth. We use it 3 weeks on and during the full moon and one week off. They also put him on organic sulfur crystals to increase glutathione and many other functions, Lugol's iodine (topically), and Saccharomyces boulardii, He takes a few different homeopathic droppers of organ/detox support and and some pellets to help with candida, metals, and other insults specific to his history. I also started giving him a pinch of activated charcoal every night to absorb toxins from die-off. And we changed more lightbulbs. His sleep has greatly improved in the last two weeks. It's hard to know exactly what is having the most impact, or if it is all helping at the same time.

Every month since last April, and with every tweak, we have seen mostly gains (a few setbacks interspersed) in all aspects of his development. I feel like we are on the right track. I'm curious to know what/how much protein you fed your kids when starting them on Jack's protocol. Right now, my son has his homeopathic rememdies first thing in the morning, then his sulfur in some watered down cherry juice (its very bitter), and then he will have protein/fat and usually a piece of fruit, and his camel milk with his supplements. Lunch is protein/fat/veggies and another piece of fruit. He has a snack of avocado or fruit after his nap and then dinner is protein/fat/veggies and a piece of fruit for dessert. He has some more milk before bed. We've cut out so many foods due to sensitivity that his diet does not have much variety. He eats Wild Fish, Grass Fed Beef/Butter, Pastured Pork/Bacon, Pastured Chicken, Cauliflower, Brussel Sprouts, Lettuce, Onion, Fennel, Garlic, Butternut Squash, Avocado, Bananas, Apples, Olive Oil, Apple Cider Vinegar, SCD-Legal Mustard. We occasionally give him blueberries or pineapple when we can find them. This diet comes from the information we have about SCD, his own food sensitivities, and low-oxalate diet. His food sensitives are the only thing that may change in the next few months and I'm hoping we can start adding in more foods back to his diet. His two biggest sensitivies when he was tested were Coconut,Eggs, and nuts, so we don't plan on adding those back in for awhile.

He weighs 40 pounds and is 3'2" tall. I'm not exactly sure how to figure out the ratios for his protein needs. Any insight you can give me is very much appreciated! I'm also curious about your son's diet and how that works when he goes to school. I am so happy for your family!!! Sorry for the long email...

Thanks,
Coreena

### MrPinkies — 2016-01-15T17:29:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/email-letter-from-coreena-to-mrpinkies.17393/post-186363>

Hi Coreena!!!!

Thank you soo much for your email!

I have a LOT to tell you! I will address as much as I can over the next day or so.. There are some parts of your story that I haven't heard of or dealt with, but, what I can tell you is that I have an idea of where you should go and or be adding to what you are already doing.. I have to say that you are off to a GREAT start!

I'll start with the easy one... the school... I'm in Michigan, so, I know Michigan laws fairly well.. I'm not sure if what I am about to tell you is going to work in Arkansas or not, but, it is worth the effort.. Your letter to the school should say something along these lines (this is the actual letter I sent to my kids school):
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RE: kiddos full name, DOB --/--/----
Today's Date
To whom it may concern:

Ryan has been placed on the Ketogenic version of the Paleolithic Diet to help the symptoms of Sensory Processing Disorder, Autism, and hyperactivity. He is not allow to have: grains- including corn, legumes- including soy and peanut, dairy (dairy does have a few exceptions, but better safe than sorry), or anything derived from these ingredients (such as corn syrup, corn starch, popcorn, etc. to name a few), in addition to, any and all additives and or any type of food coloring. This is a high fat, moderate protein, very low carbohydrate diet that is monitored by his physician.

I, Katherine Towler, give permission for all faculty and staff members to be advised of Ryan's food sensitivities. The only food and/or beverages he can have must come from my home and sent into school with him. I will also send in spring water to limit my son's fluoride exposure as fluoride has been classified as a neuro-toxin. If I know in advance, I usually can send in the equivalent (such as ice cream, cookies, pizza, etc) that are made from ingredients that he can have.

Sincerely,
Katherine Towler
\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Few things about this note:
1\. must state type of diet, reason for special diet, and what the rules are... this is as close as you are going to get to get the school to play nice with you...
2\. must say "monitored by his physician"
3\. must give permission to advise all faculty and staff
4\. ALL food and drink must come from home
5\. must make an effort to help ease the thing about "mom, why can't I have what the others are having" thing... which again... is to get the school to play nice with you... I do make pizza and cookies when appropriate... but also PLEASE make the effort to take the time and teach your kid (even if you don't think they get it or understand it) that this helps them learn, grow, and how to be healthy... this is healthy for our bodies kind of thing... we want to be healthy and strong... My son couldn't communicate for a very very very long time.. but he sure as hell understood that his tummy felt better when we switched over to Jacks stuff...

Jack is one crazy mother fucker... he is against the grain and completely out there... and sometimes an asshole with the upmost love, but, at the same time, he will give you where to look or what to read... but I thank God that I met him... I would not be where I am today.. my son would not be where he is today.. my daughter.. my husband... Please understand things take time (starting year 5 for us)... Our kids have different gaps in skills, or if there is something I don't know, etc, so, somethings I will have you ask on the forum.. The forum here on the JK website is AMAZING... You will get answers, including from Jack... and that I TRULY believe that if effort is made, life will get better... not cured... but better.... greatly, amazingly improved better.... life is fun and full of happiness improved...

More tomorrow...

G'nite!
~Kate

### MrPinkies — 2016-01-15T17:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/email-letter-from-coreena-to-mrpinkies.17393/post-186365>

ok.. so... Our stories kind of are different.. my son is tall and very very very very skinny... We just got him up to the 3rd percentile for weight.. and we were up to the 5th percentile for weight for a while, but, we struggle to stay there.. Either way, the goal is to get our kiddos brain to have more overall energy because Autism, in very simple terms, is an energy shortage in the brain... If we were to talk different ways to get energy from our food, the two main options are: carb and fat. Fat overall wins hands down... Fat takes longer to break down in the GI track than a carb, but overall produces more energy.. VS Carb is an instant energy that doesn't last... You want to switch over from being a carb burner to a fat burner... We saw HUGE changes with this... Ditch as much as possible, if not all, of what I call "counted carbs"- fruit, starch type veggies, any kind of sweetener, anything touched by a human or factory... The only carbs I do NOT count are ones that are green- brussel sprouts, spinach, kale, seaweed, broccoli, cabbage, etc etc etc etc.... We started out with 15g of carbs per day... because of his weight issues, we up'ed it to 25- 30g per day... Carbs that you count are to be at lunchtime only. So... if you are going to eat fruit, do it at lunch time... and whatever you do, do NOT eat the counted carbs at dinner... Eat as much seafood as possible... Eat as much fat as possible- any animal fat (the favorite around here is bacon and we save the bacon fat to cook with later), macadamia nuts, coconut oil, avocado and olive oils... Let me know if you need recipes...

Straight up, I am not a fan of supplements, period. You should be able to get all the vitamins and nutrients from the food that you eat and more importantly, from the sun through your eyes and skin... Humans FUCK things up... especially when it comes to supplements... Just don't.... it's REALLY not worth it...

Last thing about food... TIMING is EVERYTHING.... no snacking... follow Leptin RX very very strictly. There is no cheating or deviating from this.

Another way to get energy is from the earth.. Kruse talks about the three legged stool- water, light, magnetism. Spend as much time in the sun (preferably sun rise or sun set), with as little clothing as possible (especially no socks or shoes), outside in the grass... no glasses/contacts in/sunglasses/hats.... do NOT put sunscreen on.. you want to soak up as much vitamin D as possible and have skin on earth as much as possible... kids and I love to lay in the grass and look at the clouds... even in the snow...

Also... buy one of these:
These are frickin' expensive, but, I would pay it million times over..... we started with the booster pad... we will soon be upgrading to the super sleep system...
[http://magneticosleep.com/](http://magneticosleep.com/)
Also, make sure you get a mattress that is less than 7"... We bought a 4" mattress for our kiddos from IKEA...
Once you do this, no more playing in the bedroom... you do not want your kid to be playing in the negative field.

After we bought it, we made a check list of things we wanted to do... so.. is our home perfect? nope... but it damn will be once we are done with it!!!!!

Get rid of the monitor... I know you feel nervous about this, but, the EMF is not worth it... your inner mom instincts will kick in... you will know when something is terribly wrong...

These suggestions about EMF is not just for him... they are for YOU too.... EMF is not something to mess with.. and while you can't see it or feel it.. it is there... It is such a HUGE topic.. which is why I suggest buying the EMF bootcamp because I thought it was well rounded and gave a lot of information that will give you the ideas of what needs to be done... I don't live with you, met you, or know everything that is going on in your home... PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE buy the EMF bootcamp...

### Coreena — 2016-01-16T00:25:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/email-letter-from-coreena-to-mrpinkies.17393/post-186422>

Kate,

Thanks for all the info. It's taken me a bit to get through it and I'm still not done with the video, but I'll get there. Yesterday we hardwired ethernet back into the house and got a landline. So that was the first big step for getting rid of EMF. Now I will buy a camera with an ethernet connection to replace his video monitor. I will look into Jack's Bootcamp, since our electrical environment is something we haven't really addressed before now.

Most of the questions I have are still about the diet. My son has texture issues and so he spits out scallops, shrimp, anything with that "springy" texture. Same with oysters. He loves fish, but I don't want to make him so much fish that he starts to hate fish. I expect that his tastes will gradually change, but I do expect a battle at the beginning of this. So when you FIRST STARTED giving your kids more seafood and less fruit, what were your strategies? I am a strong willed person so I have no problem waiting him out, but I would like this transition to be as painless as possible. Also, just from a practical standpoint, can you list a few breakfasts/lunch/dinner menus that you started them with? Also, how long did it take to do the leptin reset and what are their meals like now?

I've got more questions, but I'll leave you with those for now. Thanks!

Coreena

### MrPinkies — 2016-01-16T01:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/email-letter-from-coreena-to-mrpinkies.17393/post-186424>

Just know that to battle Autism, food is a very very very small piece compared to the EMF and environment stuff.... Once you deal with the EMF and environment stuff, sensory issues and repetitive behaviors will disappear...

We have a eat or starve approach.. so.. not gonna lie.. we went days if not weeks of eating very very little... they eventually did get hungry... We made sure they drank LOTS of water... you don't have to have seafood for every meal.. and can gradually add it in... I'm not a huge fan myself, but, I try to get it at least few times a week... On the flip side, you do have to care about where your food comes from... we do a LOT of grassfed beef, etc... chicken- organic and free range... so on and so forth... the point is DHA that is not in pill or bottle form, but, from natural sources like fish...

We did cold turkey approach with this... and there are pros and cons to it... but.. one thing I can tell you is that it did not take long for the kiddos, especially Ryan, to figure out what we were doing and why... even though he had 10 words to his vocabulary.. he knew... and then he refused the foods that didn't make him feel well... and then several months down the road, he tested the waters and broke out in hives.. and never again has he tried since....

California leads the charge in new autism cases because they embrace more technology than any other place in the USA. The U.S. Centers for Disease Control (CDC). The CDC report found that autism is now diagnosed in one in every 59 American children, representing 2 ½ times more autism in 12 years and a 15 percent increase in just two years. This is DEFINITIVE proof autism is not a GENETIC disease related to the NUCLEAR genome.

The alarming increase in autism prevalence over the past 10 years signals the need for a significant change in the federal response to addressing autism in the United States. We need advocacy for technology diets for these kids. A more accountable, effective, and strategic plan is necessary to meet the needs of those with autism and their caregivers today. Because the mtDNA changes 3-5 times more quickly it should be obvious this is a mitochondrial disease at its core.

The CDC report, “Prevalence of Autism Spectrum Disorders Among Children Aged 8 Years—Autism and Developmental Disabilities Monitoring Network, 11 Sites, United States, 2014,” states that in some of the communities represented in the network, nearly three percent of eight-year-olds had an autism diagnosis in 2014. It uses the same methodology that produced the CDC’s 2010 prevalence findings of 1 in 68 children with autism.

The extraordinary cost of autism care is expected to escalate dramatically as prevalence continues to increase. University of California Davis health economists estimates the national cost of caring for all people with autism in the billions, heading towards $1 trillion. Their forecasts for autism-related medical, nonmedical, and productivity losses were $268 billion for 2015 and $461 billion for 2025. The researchers noted that if ASD prevalence continues to increase as it has in recent years while effective interventions and preventive treatments are not identified and made widely available, the costs could reach $1 trillion by 2025.

# Thread: New member here - hoping to bring something interesting to the forums.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-hoping-to-bring-something-interesting-to-the-forums.17394/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Norway-hey — 2016-01-15T22:07:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-hoping-to-bring-something-interesting-to-the-forums.17394/post-186396>

Hi everyone,

In advance, thank you for suffering through this run-on post.

Also, thanks for letting me join the forums! I have lurked here in the past, and just recently decided to take the plunge and start posting as well.

I have been having weird health issues for a while, and have not been able to figure them out.

​

A while back I did however implement a lot of Kruse´s protocols/advice and I have honestly never felt better than during those months. But, I was foolish and did not follow up them it after a visit back home for christmas, after which my health problems returned with a vengeance.
Now I feel like making a second go of it, and I believe I have figured out the cause of my strange health issues. Or at least I hope so. Anyhow, I believe I have a rather rare genetic condition called Fructose-1,6-biphosphatase deficiency (FBPase or FDPase deficiency). I am working with my physician to figure things out, but things are moving along slowly. I don´t believe there are many, physicians in Norway that are knowledgeable about this genetic condition.

​

In short, FBPase deficiency causes problems with gluconeogensis and glycolysis after ingestion of fructose (including sucrose, half of which being fructose) and sorbitol (also cognate sugars?!), and also when liver glycogen stores are depleted (fasting). Due to the deficiency only glucose and galactose are able to sustain blood sugar levels (or, well, at least for those eating the usual diets), while the usage of other substrates (i.e. protein, glycerol, etc.) for gluconeogenesis are limited by the deficieny of FBPase.
As for treatment, the only way is through diet. This disorder can be deadly, but less so after the neonatal period, and with proper treatment it is quite benign. The usual treatment is staying away from fasting and eating lots of starches - often, which obviously sucks! Also, they usually advice sufferers to limit intake of protein and fats... I on the other hand felt that this doesn´t make much sense. Keto, making the body less dependent on glucose/sugars, makes a lot more sense to me. PPP makes even more sense!

I believe why I felt so amazing while following Kruse´s protocols last time, was that I was able to make use of ketosis and the PPP and, if I´ve understood correctly, really circumventing the problem caused by lacking enough FBPase.

I am already back in ketosis (doesn´t take much for me to get in, really) and have just started adding lots of seafood again. Also, I´ve just finished a batch of fermented vegetables (cabbage, cabbage and carrots), that I will be eating generously from. I´ve also started wearing my blue blockers and will now do my second CT session of the day. If if were not for the -17C outside, I would definitely do some grounding as well. I will also try to limit EMF - had forgotten about this and started sleeping with my iPhone close again. Definitely stopping that now.

In regards to earthing/grounding. I vaguely remember reading something about not grounding yourself to the ground in the electrical outlets while inside, being exposed to lots of EMF. It was something about acting like a receiver of EMF instead of benefitting from being grounded/earthed. I just tried it a for a little while, after which I got a headache and in turn made me remember what I´ve just written. Anyone care to comment on this?

Also, if sufferers of FBPase deficiency cannot utilize protein for gluconeogenesis (or rather, gluconeogenesis is limited by the deficiency), would that not imply that such a person eating too much protein will not as easily be kicked out of ketosis? In which case, what happens to the remaining protein? Would such an indeosyncrasy be an advantage, or a disadvantage? I am not sure about this at all, but you guys are very knowledgable so you might have a better idea and understanding of this than me. Please let me know what you think!

If you have any suggestions for things/protocols that I should add, questions, suggestions for other things, etc. please let me know. I would love to hear them!

For those interested I have included a few links of for information on FBPase:

1.  [https://books.google.no/books?id=97zoCAAAQBAJ&pg=PA104&lpg=PA104&dq=hypoglycemia in FBPase deficiency&source=bl&ots=QPwjnvuKvn&sig=oBE-6FmnulRo0bwU32SkN8oPdLw&hl=no&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwj\_\_a2yzqnKAhVpnXIKHQaTBk4Q6AEIIDAA#v=onepage&q=hypoglycemia in FBPase deficiency&f=false(this](https://books.google.no/books?id=97zoCAAAQBAJ&pg=PA104&lpg=PA104&dq=hypoglycemia+in+FBPase+deficiency&source=bl&ots=QPwjnvuKvn&sig=oBE-6FmnulRo0bwU32SkN8oPdLw&hl=no&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwj__a2yzqnKAhVpnXIKHQaTBk4Q6AEIIDAA#v=onepage&q=hypoglycemia%20in%20FBPase%20deficiency&f=false\(this) is a long one, but quite thorough and the diagnosis and treatment of FBPase)-
2.  [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10870852](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10870852) (review done in 2000)
3.  [http://journals.lww.com/jpgn/Fullte...ion\_of\_Carbohydrates\_and\_Depression\_in.6.aspx](http://journals.lww.com/jpgn/Fulltext/2005/05000/Malabsorption_of_Carbohydrates_and_Depression_in.6.aspx) (really about malabsorbation, but FBPase is needed to absorb fructose from the gut, in which case FBPase explains my depression, and general dysthymia growing up)
4.  [https://www.researchgate.net/public...sphatase\_in\_Regulating\_Appetite\_and\_Adiposity](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/224770070_The_Role_of_Liver_Fructose-16-Bisphosphatase_in_Regulating_Appetite_and_Adiposity) (interesting connection of FBPase to leptin, adipose tissue and feeding)

This last Q&A has encouraged me to post this...FINALLY.

I don't have any really big issues like Crohns, or Celiac, Fibromyalgia, or anything like that, but I do have A LOT of smaller ones. I do have sleep apnea, which I know is HUGE, but I use a CPAP, so it has helped me a great deal. I lost 40lbs on the paleo diet, and got down to 190 at 6ft tall, but my apnea remains. I was hoping to be able to get off the cpap once I lost some weight, but theres obviously a lot of other things going on. I live in Portland Oregon and it has been rainy and cloudy for 2 months now and will continue to be like that for at least another 2-4 months. I have been using a tanning bed 3x per week for 15 min each time. I do CT about 3-5x per week. I used to take buttload of supplements, but since following Dr. Kruse I only use Creatine, Niacin, low dose Aspirin, Deprenyl, a phytoplankton supplement, vitamin A, D-Ribbose, and Ubiquinol. I also just ordered some Resvertrol. Here is my most recent lab work:

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-29T16:28:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.17395/post-187617>

Cold can increase dopamine on surfaces........UV light from an equatorial environment does the same.......what is the difference between the two? O2 tensions and DHA levels.

Polar regions have higher O2 levels due to boreal forests and deep source of DHA in pelagic zone fish.

Equator is a dead zone for DHA in the sea because water is too warm and the amount of O2 is decreased because high UV lowers the quantum yield of photosynthesis. This means being more Sphinx like, seeing light grounding and eating one meal a deal is the way to lower heteroplasmy.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-29T16:35:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.17395/post-187618>

People at equator's have tight coupling of mitochondria and nucleus........why? they dont need to release a ton of IR light or heat to shrink their MINOS. They have UV light on all their food electrons that hit the fluorophore NADH at cytochrome one to control heteroplasmy rates.

As you leave tropics and head to poles you must be loosely coupled because this releases more IR heat to shrink water of the MINOS to condense the ECT to maintain electron tunneling..........the reason why ketosis and cold link is people are loosely coupled so as they liberate heat you need more electrons to go from cytochrome 2, FADH2/FAD couple to reduce oxygen to maintain life.........fats provide more electrons that carbs and the electrons they have have less UV power embedded in them...............

The key take away? The charge on the inner mitochondrial membrane is 100% tied to heteroplasmy rates in tissues.............= the diseases you get.

Published normal charges on this membrane is -180 millivolts. When you consider physics equations for currents and realize that ECT on this membrane is 4-6 nm in thickness this gives a charge of 25- 30 million volts.

Nick Lane talks about this........wallace talks about this.......and no one makes sense of it.

Now you are seeing how it works on the planet.

The environment you allow alters this balance.

It is there as a blessing! I would use only a candle if I had to wake up that early. Is there any chance to start work later?
I know the few months in my life when I had to wake up real early like that, way before sunrise, for work... it was really draining for me. I do not like it at all. I after that one experience avoided such jobs.

### Edgar Valenzuela — 2016-02-04T19:24:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.17395/post-188208>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=188207)
>
> Edgar my intuition says it is not wise to use any kind of light before the sun rises/it gets naturally light. Because we need the darkness as well to heal. I know we do.
> It is there as a blessing! I would use only a candle if I had to wake up that early. Is there any chance to start work later?
> I know the few months in my life when I had to wake up real early like that, way before sunrise, for work... it was really draining for me. I do not like it at all. I after that one experience avoided such jobs.

I am going to treat my early mornings just like I do the night time until the sun rises. I will use my fireplace, my blue blockers, and my 660nm All red UFO light until I can figure out a way to wake up later and/or move South. Thank you, Inger.

# Thread: Taking in the south shore Long Island sunrise!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taking-in-the-south-shore-long-island-sunrise.17396/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan E — 2016-01-16T16:18:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taking-in-the-south-shore-long-island-sunrise.17396/post-186479>

Hello everyone my name is Dan. I'm a 38 year old electrician from Long Island, New York.
I have been an avid Ben Greenfield podcast listener for a couple years which then led me to Dave Aspry.
I took on a strict, home made, bulletproof diet last year which was an immediate but fleeting success. Nothing against it, I have been realizing my problems are due to lifestyle. After hearing Jack a couple times I listened to everything I could find and read Epi Paleo Rx and some Pollack and Becker.
I've had sleep trouble my entire life but severe insomnia has troubled me for 9 months. It has taken me a while to realize and make changes where I need to but I am having a lot more success recently.
My main problems are adrenal fatigue, insomnia, poor cognitive function, poor verbal communication, and ADD.
My chiropractor tells me that my nervous system is switched (I go to him for supplements). I can't get out of bed in the morning and I wake up when I'd like to fall asleep at night. My job isn't very stressful but I am very active. I play volleyball 5 days per week and it keeps me out and eating late.

So what am I doing about it?
For Christmas I treated myself to a membership here. I began jumping in the bay 2 out of 3 days and staying in as long as I can. It's around 50 degrees. Now for a week I have been waking up a bit earlier to catch the sunrise. I've been sleeping like a rock for the past 4 days, so maybe my circadian rhythm is adjusting. I've also committed to doing TM at least once per day, usually twice.

"Cold dark and boring". It seems like I need to quit volleyball and concentrate on the sun and the cold water. I've been eating oysters for months, lots of sardines and scallops. My diet seems perfect except for my reliance on coffee.

Jack, thank you for all that you do, I truly appreciate it!

### Linz — 2016-01-16T16:26:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taking-in-the-south-shore-long-island-sunrise.17396/post-186480>

Welcome Dan

Volleyball = looking up into artificial lights. Great game but only on the beach!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-16T17:41:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taking-in-the-south-shore-long-island-sunrise.17396/post-186482>

Long Island is a cesspool.......not one place on the Island tested well.

### Dan E — 2016-01-16T17:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taking-in-the-south-shore-long-island-sunrise.17396/post-186486>

In light of all this new information I am very interested in getting off Long Island asap!!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-16T18:10:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taking-in-the-south-shore-long-island-sunrise.17396/post-186490>

You need to connect with Micheal Russo here. Is a member and did some work with me and moved to NC from Long Island a year ago.........his story is compelling.

### Dan E — 2016-01-16T18:12:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taking-in-the-south-shore-long-island-sunrise.17396/post-186491>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=186480)
>
> Welcome Dan
>
> Volleyball = looking up into artificial lights. Great game but only on the beach!

Yes I agree. I know a girl that plays with blue light glasses. I might have to get a nice pair that doesn't fall off.

# Thread: A new serotonin modulating antidepressant that does NOT inhibit dopamine!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-serotonin-modulating-antidepressant-that-does-not-inhibit-dopamine.17401/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2016-01-17T02:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-serotonin-modulating-antidepressant-that-does-not-inhibit-dopamine.17401/post-186536>

First off I am not condoning the use of antidepressants. Nature is our true antidepressant by far. But for those people that are sick and tired of the dopamine suppression caused by these f$%^%^g SSRI meds, there may be an option.
My doctor just prescribed me an antidepressant called "Brintellix" (vorioxetine). It is a serotonin 'modulator' but it also boosts dopamine, norepinephrine, glutamate and acetylcholine. Boosts cognitive function but most importantly- DOES NOT SUPPRESS DOPAMINE!!
You know how much we all need our dopamine!
I just started it a few days ago but I will keep you posted. I need the dopamine so I can actually APPLY the info that I learn here to get optimal. The drug is just a 'stepping stone' NOT a permanent solution.

Hopefully this helps someone struggling on those shitty SSRI's
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4296590/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4296590/)

# Thread: Harvesting UVA on skin surfaces at high latitudes for Vit D?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harvesting-uva-on-skin-surfaces-at-high-latitudes-for-vit-d.17402/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2016-01-17T03:47:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harvesting-uva-on-skin-surfaces-at-high-latitudes-for-vit-d.17402/post-186546>

**Is this a potential biohack?**
Has anyone thought about a hot-shower to get the skin pink, then while still hot/pink, run outside into the UVA light in the freezing cold?

I mention it because of the normal behavior in Scandinavia...Or taking hot spring baths outside in Iceland. In Finland, they like to take a sauna until beat-red and beat themselves with birchtree branches, then walk outside and skinny dip into a freezing lake or pool. Then literally rinse and repeat. The instant-constriction of blood-vessels in the cold air/water along with the blood brought to the skin surfaces from the sauna or hot shower, and then the UVA hitting the skin. Could this potentially harvest UVA in the dead of winter at high lattitudes to convert cholesterol to Vitamin D, or a fail due to the lack of UVB?

### Inger — 2016-01-17T09:47:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harvesting-uva-on-skin-surfaces-at-high-latitudes-for-vit-d.17402/post-186565>

Well I do this every day... I take a long hot shower and then I dip outside in my ice cold rainbarrell.. and stand a little in the outside light to suck in the rays.....
IDK if it is right or wrong but I love my ritual

### thomas — 2016-01-17T15:27:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harvesting-uva-on-skin-surfaces-at-high-latitudes-for-vit-d.17402/post-186570>

Theorically no, you need uvb to convert 7-dehydrocholesterol into vitD.
but you know what I think about science these days? ...aheum. Scientists wouldn't recognise a meteor falling on them unless there were enough papers producing evidence that it's possible ( but then there also would be a ton of other papers producing evidence of the contrary)
I guess the only real way to know is... test it for yourself and takes labs and see.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-17T21:49:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harvesting-uva-on-skin-surfaces-at-high-latitudes-for-vit-d.17402/post-186590>

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=186546)
>
> **Is this a potential biohack?**
> Has anyone thought about a hot-shower to get the skin pink, then while still hot/pink, run outside into the UVA light in the freezing cold?
>
> I mention it because of the normal behavior in Scandinavia...Or taking hot spring baths outside in Iceland. In Finland, they like to take a sauna until beat-red and beat themselves with birchtree branches, then walk outside and skinny dip into a freezing lake or pool. Then literally rinse and repeat. The instant-constriction of blood-vessels in the cold air/water along with the blood brought to the skin surfaces from the sauna or hot shower, and then the UVA hitting the skin. Could this potentially harvest UVA in the dead of winter at high lattitudes to convert cholesterol to Vitamin D, or a fail due to the lack of UVB?

Why do you think I do the Fournier effect? LOL

But what about ambient light? Will it affect my melatonin levels?

Are we supposed to or ok to use a Red LED as after sunset light (in my case with blacklights too) ?

**

### Penny — 2016-01-22T07:16:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-as-recommended-as-ambient-light-after-sunset.17403/post-187060>

I'm thinkin' that might not be such a hot idea in the summer Jans... man made light doesn't have the atomic frequencies that natural sun has:
**Electrons made to vibrate to and fro at a few hundred thousand hertz emit radio waves. What class of waves is emitted from electron vibrations of a few million billion hertz?**
visible light waves would be emitted.
Light emission from atoms involve transitions of electrons from higher to lower energy states within atom.

**What Exactly Are Fluorescence and Phosphorescence?**
Have you ever been startled by a glowing object in the darkness? Let's start by learning about fluorescence and phosphorescence, either of which was likely the source of the light you saw. Materials that emit light after being illuminated with light or electric beams are called fluorescent or phosphorescent materials. Fluorescent material is used in fluorescent lighting and TV cathode-ray tubes, phosphorescent material in wristwatch faces. These materials absorb light and then emit it at a different wavelength. Fluorescence is what occurs when a material absorbs light and then emits it almost immediately, for about 1/1 billion to 1/100,000 second, while phosphorescent materials glow more slowly, for 1/1,000 to 1/10 second. Fluorescence and phosphorescence are also called "luminescence." Electrons on the outermost orbit of atoms and molecules are what emit light as a general rule. Electrons sometimes obtain energy from an external source, causing them to jump onto a higher orbit. When they later descend to their original energy level, known as their "ground" or "normal" state, the difference of energy between two orbital (energy) levels they fall will be released as light. ("Electromagnetic wave" is generally a more accurate description than "light".) The transition to a higher energy level is called "excitation."

Only two electrons can orbit in a particular energy level. This is called the Pauli Exclusion Principle. Electrons also rotate as they orbit. The two electrons in an energy level will usually rotate in opposite directions (singlet state), though they also might rotate in the same direction (triplet state). In fluorescence, the two excited electrons are in the singlet state, while in phosphorescence, they are in the triplet state. Phosphorescence emits light longer because it takes more time for the electrons to fall to their former singlet ground state.

So, I would like to know what is different between:
1) The sun causing electrons to jump to a higher orbit
2) A man made light causing electrons to jump to a higher orbit

I assume at night when the sun is down, gravity causes the electrons to go back to their original configuration (their original shell/ground state) thus causing them to emit light - is it this light that would be different between a man made bulb and the sun?

I also read this:
[http://chemwiki.ucdavis.edu/Physica...omic\_Theory/Electrons\_in\_Atoms/Atomic\_Spectra](http://chemwiki.ucdavis.edu/Physical_Chemistry/Quantum_Mechanics/09._The_Hydrogen_Atom/Atomic_Theory/Electrons_in_Atoms/Atomic_Spectra)

When light is shone on an atom, its electrons absorb photons which cause them to gain energy and jump to higher energy levels. The higher the energy of the photon absorbed, the higher the energy level the electron jumps to. Similarly, an electron can go down energy levels by emitting a photon

**Emission Lines**
As discussed above, when an electron falls from one energy level in an atom to a lower energy level, it emits a photon of a particular wavelength and energy. When many electrons emit the same wavelength of photons it will result in a spike in the spectrum at this particular wavelength, resulting in the banding pattern seen in atomic emission spectra.

**Absorption Lines**
When an electron jumps from a low energy level to a higher level, the electron will absorb a photon of a particular wavelength. This will show up as a drop in the number of photons of this wavelength and as a black band in this part of the spectrum.

So it seems that with the sun, you would get more of a variety of absorption of photons with different wavelengths and it would have more power...
But, it also seems that since the electrons will only absorb light of certain frequencies, the rest will be ignored - so if all we care about is UVA light, what difference except for power, would it make?

That was a really good article BTW -

### Penny — 2016-01-30T19:32:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-as-recommended-as-ambient-light-after-sunset.17403/post-187682>

**Low-intensity laser irradiation at 660 nm stimulates cytochrome c oxidase in stressed fibroblast cells.**
[Houreld NN](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Houreld%20NN%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22488690)1, [Masha RT](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Masha%20RT%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22488690), [Abrahamse H](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Abrahamse%20H%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22488690).
**[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22488690#)**
**Abstract**
**BACKGROUND AND OBJECTIVE:**
Low-intensity laser irradiation (LILI) has been used to modulate a variety of biological processes, including diabetic wound healing. The mechanism of action is thought to exist primarily with the mitochondria. This study aimed to determine the effect of irradiation on normal, diabetic, and ischemic mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC) complexes.

**MATERIALS AND METHODS:**
Normal, diabetic and ischemic human skin fibroblast mitochondria were irradiated in vitro at a wavelength of 660 nm and a fluence of either 5 or 15 J/cm(2). Non-irradiated mitochondria served as controls. Enzyme activities of mitochondrial complexes I, II, III, and IV were determined immediately post-irradiation. Normal, diabetic, and ischemic cells were irradiated and adenosine triphosphate (ATP) and active mitochondria were determined by luminescence and fluorescent microscopy, respectively.

**RESULTS:**
Irradiated diabetic mitochondria at a fluence of 15 J/cm(2) showed a significant decrease in complex III activity (P < 0.05). Normal (P < 0.01) and diabetic (P < 0.05) mitochondria irradiated at either 5 or 15 J/cm(2) showed a significant increase in complex IV activity. ATP results showed a significant increase in irradiated normal cells (5 J/cm(2); P < 0.05) and diabetic cells (15 J/cm(2); P < 0.01). There was a higher accumulation of active mitochondria in irradiated cells than non-irradiated cells.

**CONCLUSION:**
Irradiation at 660 nm has the ability to influence mitochondrial enzyme activity, in particular cytochrome c oxidase. This leads to increased mitochondrial activity and ATP synthesis.

[http://jcs.biologists.org/content/127/2/388](http://jcs.biologists.org/content/127/2/388)
Light-harvesting chlorophyll pigments enable mammalian mitochondria to capture photonic energy and produce ATP

[https://books.google.com/books?id=bzxZkEcLo1kC&pg=PA995&lpg=PA995&dq=660nm mitochondria&source=bl&ots=W\_QJzXoObF&sig=IG8F\_5nT--K15WDiXOGEB-zz-Yo&hl=en&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwiprtGFiNLKAhUK4iYKHfjeDKEQ6AEIQTAF#v=onepage&q=660nm mitochondria&f=false](https://books.google.com/books?id=bzxZkEcLo1kC&pg=PA995&lpg=PA995&dq=660nm+mitochondria&source=bl&ots=W_QJzXoObF&sig=IG8F_5nT--K15WDiXOGEB-zz-Yo&hl=en&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwiprtGFiNLKAhUK4iYKHfjeDKEQ6AEIQTAF#v=onepage&q=660nm%20mitochondria&f=false)

@[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) -

-   415nm is Blue, not UV. Blue can still be used therapeutically nonetheless... it has an antimicrobial effect

googling "415nm UV light therapy" for example:

[http://www.light-therapy-led.com/acne.htm](http://www.light-therapy-led.com/acne.htm)
"LED Blue Light Therapy Bulb has no [UV](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ultraviolet) rays. The blue light has a peak wavelength of [415nm](http://eosweb.larc.nasa.gov/EDDOCS/Wavelengths_for_Colors.html) (nanometers), out of [UV](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ultraviolet) range. ... Acne blue & red LED light therapy combines the acne treating benefits of the blue LED light but also the healing benefits of the red 660nm LED light. The acne blue & red LED light treats acne and also heals acne scars."

-   660nm is Red not IR... it still can be used to enhance mitochondrial function but from my understanding doesn't penetrate very deep the way IR does and is often used for skin collagen regeneration. IR penetrates deeper about 3 inches and affects deeper tissues more... 890nm is an example of IR used in therapy

"Red light therapy uses wavelengths of light roughly between 620 nm and 700 nm. Popular wavelengths used in research and in-home products are 630 nm and 660 nm.

Visible red light is capable of penetrating the skin to a depth of about 8 to 10 mm. Once absorbed, the light energy is converted to cellular energy, stimulating the body’s natural processes on a cellular level and [kicking off a whole series of metabolic events](http://www.thorlaser.com/LLLT/how-does-LLLT-work.htm), including:

-   Increased circulation and the formation of new capillaries. [1](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=LLLT+OR+Low+level+laser+therapy+OR+Photobiomodulation+AND+circulation)
-   Increased lymph system activity.
-   Increased production of collagen and fibroblasts. [2](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=LLLT+OR+Low+level+laser+therapy+OR+Photobiomodulation+AND+fibroblasts)
-   Increased release of ATP, or raw cellular energy. [3](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=LLLT+ATP+OR+LLLT+reactive+oxygen+species+OR+LLLT+oxidative+stress+OR+Photobiomodulation+ATP+OR+Photobiomodulation+reactive+oxygen+species+OR+Photobiomodulation+oxidative+stress)
-   Increased phagocytosis, or cellular clean up.
-   Tissue granulation stimulated. [4](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=LLLT+tissue+granulation)
-   Inflammation reduced. [5](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=lllt+inflammation+or+low-level+laser+inflammation+or+photobiomodulation+inflammation)

All of these things work together to produce many benefits for you in the areas of anti-aging, the healing of wounds and injuries, and the relief of pain, as we’ll see below. But no matter what the application, red and infrared light therapy [share the same benefits overall](http://redlighttherapy.lighttherapyoptions.com/benefits-of-red-light-therapy/)." [http://redlighttherapy.lighttherapyoptions.com/red-light-therapy/](http://redlighttherapy.lighttherapyoptions.com/red-light-therapy/)

"

-   Near Infrared, also called infrared A (IR-A). Near IR spans wavelengths 760 to 1,400 nm. Most home therapy devices use these wavelengths.

Infrared light therapy is essentially the same as [red light therapy](http://redlighttherapy.lighttherapyoptions.com/red-light-therapy/), except infrared energy is invisible, and it penetrates the body deeper than red, reaching deep into soft tissues, muscles, joints, and bone." [http://redlighttherapy.lighttherapyoptions.com/infrared-light-therapy/](http://redlighttherapy.lighttherapyoptions.com/infrared-light-therapy/)

# Thread: First Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-journal.17404/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tricia McVey — 2016-01-18T03:40:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-journal.17404/post-186611>

Wasn't quite sure where to start on here. I've been an ICU nurse for going on 15 years now. I spent one year on nights, the rest has been day shift. I had pretty good health, never over weight or had the need to take any medications besides birth control pills before I had my first child at 27. I got my RN at 31. Had my son at 32. After a failed tubal ligation in 2006, I started taking birth control pills again and have continued on them due to very painful ovulation since having my tubal ligation. I say failed because I got pregnant less than a year after the ligation. In 2009 I had a few episodes of SVT during a stressful time, with syncope and I was started on Lopressor. I gained 25 lbs pretty quickly after starting and was switched to diltiazem. On my own, I stopped taking the Diltiazem in 2012. I hadn't had any episodes of SVT and I felt that it had probably been stress induced and that stress no longer was there. I have not had any SVT since. I did however continued to gain weight and starting having issues with fatigue. In 2010 and I had my TSH levels checked where they were found to be a just about 3. My primary doctor started me on Levothyroxine 25 mcg, and after a level recheck was increased to 50 mcg. I have been on Levothyroxine ever since 50 mcg. Initially it helped with my fatigue but as time has gone on I began having issues with sleep and would take Benedryl to help with that which left me very groggy all day after taking it. Between my interested in wondering what really was going on with all these sick people I was taking care of at work and my concern now about my own health since I had gained 45 lbs from 2009-2015 I began looking more closely into nutrition and in particular CAD. I saw a post on fat head from Tristan Haggard where I first saw a podcast with Jack this must have been about April or so 2015 and then I "ran into" Jack on Ivor Cummings FB page. Best thing to ever happen… So where am I now. I had started eating LCHF in late 2014 and lost about 25 lbs. I am thinner but not where I should be for me I think. I gain weight in my hips and buttocks.. I have been reading and listening to podcast as much as I can regarding circadian rhythms and sleep cycles. Because of that, I changed out all our light bulbs ( teenage daughter, 18, wasn't too happy, 13 year old more flexible) to yellow incandescent lights, some orange and one black light at night. Candles and or fire place going and Skypers glasses. They knock me out. I haven't had to take any benedryl at all and definitely feel that my sleep has improved. I do tend to wake up to pee…. changed out the night light to the low blue light one and been okay to go back to sleep. My son and husband have jumped on board, daughter still resistant. I have always loved fish and craved fish so pounding it down to up my DHA intake with all the blue light exposure especially at work. I have recently been reading about CT and plan on implementing. I did go to my Drs. a month ago and had a highly sensitive CRP done it came back .4. I asked for a VAP cholesterol test, which became an issue and my blood timed out at the lab so I need to go back to my doctors office for that or I can even swing by my lab. Now Im thinking I need more labs done, hormone levels but not sure what specifically to ask for and also unsure if my birth control pills will interfere with results I take lo loestrin. I asked for an omega 6 to 3 ratio but that too was an issue with the lab.. I need to get it straight with my doctor. They aren't regular tests he orders, he even took out his lab book for me ( very nice man) but the lab screwed it up. What else? My work environment, Im either in ICU or our procedure room. You know what that looks like… I wear contacts, need to ditch those and get blue techs, I found an optometry store that sells them right near my house. I am farsighted but between 40 till now 45, Ill be 46 on the 7th my vision has taken a dump. When I got out of nursing school in 2001 I only needed glasses to read when I was tired… now I have been wearing multifocal contacts since at least 2006. We also went all computerized charting in 2012.. uhg. Family history, Dad died in 2011 at 62 of I believe an aortic dissection from the information I got from the paramedics a few days after he died. They thought it was a PE. The way he presented I think it was a dissection, PEA, purple from the nipple line up, bleeding from his mouth, never regained a pulse. He had a history of MI and stents placed, and smoked. We had been estranged for 22 years before he died. His mother, my paternal Grandmother had sudden death also from either an MI or aortic dissection according to my Uncle when she was 51. My material Grandma had her first stroke and MI in her 40s, was on peritoneal dialysis for many years also a smoker died of hemorrhagic stroke when she was 68. My mom is still alive, was just notified of her having diverticulitis, we are also estranged. Great grandma with Colon Ca. I don't smoke, never have. Drink red wine a glass or two nightly. I live in California, about 25 minutes off the coast. Have made a point to get out in the sun without contacts since reading blog and my vitamin D level is 45 as of a month ago when I got my HS CRP done. I was surprised it was that decent.. must be all the baseball games we watch for my son. Thank you!

### Tricia McVey — 2016-01-21T06:18:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-journal.17404/post-186948>

Just found out another coworker nurse has cancer.. pancreatic with mets to her liver.. she's screwed. Two of my nurse friends had breast CA two years ago at the same time. Another one last year and my best friend from work a nurse for 25 years got ALS and past after a long battle last June.. Another nurse traveler who I became friends with also breast CA.... And this was before the whole hospital was blown up with computer screens that we are on ALL DAY. I officially cringe when I go to work.

My plan: Get blutechs asap, hopeful multifocal prescription will work. I can't think of / don't know of any hack to do while at work.. ?! I turn off artificial lights as often as I can when I am in rooms!
Ditch the contacts !!
learn more about EMF, turn off wifi at night. Schummann resonance and alpha waves blew my mind. I had never heard of this but very aware of EEGs from work.
Ditch birth control which equals pain, that will be harder to do..

Been eating a ton of seafood, which is easy I love it could eat oysters daily, any raw fish suites me
I changed out all my lights in the house, blue blocking at night.
Been relistening to podcasts, some things that went over my head are making sense now, feels good.
Getting my bare feet on my lawn in the am and at night, learned about the earth having a negative electron charge.
Listening and reading and making a choice to learn something new every day.
Listening to patients when they talk, asking questions about their environment putting pieces together. Making suggestions when appropriate.
I Want to speak up and out more about the risks we as healthcare workers have now more than ever in our modern hospital environment. They are busy bean counting I am concerned about health of all the caregivers.. MD's, Nurses, Radiology techs, all of us. If we aren't healthy everyone is screwed.

Great write up Tricia. Do most of the same!

Bluetechs: my optometrist said they can do standard bifocals, progressives are much more challenging, although Tom Doud on the forum can probably answer best. My eyesight (17 years post-Lasik) has definitley improved, especially at night. I use skypers when i drive at night. took a little getting used to doing, but worth it.

House: nothing by red and black lights. makes a huge difference.

Morning: I do the exact same thing! I get outside in pajamas and get feet on the ground, then come in for a 15 meditation session.

Work: people think of me as an alien as I have blutechs and a reptile bulb at desk. All I know is that shit works. No colds/flu at all while everyone is dropping like flies around me.

Bottom Line: over the past year I've dropped around 30 pounds and kept it off without exercising, simply by minding my light as closely as possible. I keep to pretty keto type eating pattern, but work definitely causes some issues so I eat a shit ton of chocalate and shots of apple cider vinegar with a tin of sardines thrown in.

Keep at it. It can take some time, but the alternatives are harder and less effective.

# Thread: Feedback on Rain Water Collecting
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-rain-water-collecting.17406/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kevin W — 2016-01-18T07:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-rain-water-collecting.17406/post-186623>

Hello all...
We have recently moved, improving vastly on exposure to many things, mainly bad ELF magnetic and EMF issues.

Now on a couple of acres still in Texas yet far north of the metroplex ( we have to be somewhat near for her doc, hyperbaric, etc). Here the local water is beyond poor. We of course have a good whole house filter and point of use. But let’s face it that does not quite cut it. We have no access to a good spring, the best spring water locally would be Mountain Spring water in glass bottles.

However, I was thinking of collecting rain water. Anyone have any experience with this? The main challenge to me would be letting the first part of the rain bypass being collected to clean out that initial fall through the dirtier air and clean the roof off so to speak. I am speaking of a larger system stainless steel. Ultra violet light, etc.

The rain water seems like it would be far better than the city water.

Anyone with some good advice here.
thanks in advance
Kevin

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-18T15:25:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-rain-water-collecting.17406/post-186638>

Collecting rain water can be good..........but it must be based on location. For example by the donut hole or south Atlantic anomaly this might not be wise.

### Jonathin — 2016-01-18T20:31:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-rain-water-collecting.17406/post-186659>

I've collected rainwater in 55 gal HDPE barrels with a downspout diverter. We have a metal roof so it is pretty clean but I put it though a Berkey filter to clear any toxic or microbial residue. There are other options for massive filtering.

### Kevin W — 2016-01-18T20:35:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-on-rain-water-collecting.17406/post-186660>

Thanks all...

Yep we have good filtration for sure, the city water we get, a tiny rural town has had issues with reporting and bad water in the past. Just too risky, especially with my wife's health. There are many companies in Texas that do good systems, stainless steel also and can tie it in to our existing system so then the water can be filtered as well. Plus the house has a full gutter system already in place.

I know air quality gets poor especially in Summer so I would like to divert the first part of the rain as I mentioned above if I can.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-21T22:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-men-night-day-particle-wave.17408/post-187031>

dehydration ruins many things........the EZ is a biggie but you realize that all B vitamins are water soluble. So if you are dehydrated you lose them.........and B1 is to get pyruvate into a mitochondria to meet cytochrome 1. B3 is used in cytochrome 1 (NADH), B2 is used in cytochrome two....(FADH). So dehydration also strips you of fat oxidation due to a lack of B vitamins you have to use carbs which make excessive singlet state ROS and RNS.............

# Thread: Thyroid feels funny after not eating
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-feels-funny-after-not-eating.17409/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### marlowe — 2016-01-19T07:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-feels-funny-after-not-eating.17409/post-186711>

Hey, first time poster here, so im not sure if im posting in the right place, but im definitley a beginner. I have been following a ketogenic diet for a few months now, and the last couple weeks I have been eating more seafood, shellfish, and sea weed. Ive never had any major health issues, but always been high carb eater and I am just changing diet to live better. So far I feel great!

I have been reading through a lot of the blogs on this website as well as the forums, what a wealth of knowledge!

I have had one thing that has come up that i am interested in learning more about. Sometimes when I dont eat for a while, I start to feel pressure in my thyroid. Its not painful, its just like slightly inflamed maybe, its almost like I feel its there where as normally I dont even notice it. My energy levels are good, no brain fog, and this only happens if I have been without food for 6-12 hours. I figure it might just be my thyroid becoming adapted to my new eating habits, maybe detoxing from the years of carb eating. I am curious, has anyone else had this happen and know what causes it, or knows where I can read more about this?

Thanks!

### marlowe — 2016-01-21T17:54:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-feels-funny-after-not-eating.17409/post-186996>

I think I may have figured out what happened in case anyone searches for this in the future. I got some of the Lugols 2% drops but didnt really pay attention to the proper dosing so I took a full dropper 3-4 times over the past 7 days. I believe I got the wolff-chaikoff effect from too much iodine. I am not sure why this was more noticeable when I havent eaten, and Its wierd because I have no other symptoms besides the inflamed feeling thyroid, but it seems like too much iodine is the culprit. I got some selenium to try and help balance it out, and it says this usually clears up in 10 days.

### nonchalant — 2016-01-21T20:32:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-feels-funny-after-not-eating.17409/post-187009>

OMG marlowe! Glad you're ok.
Welcome!

# Thread: Time 7 Question: Wavelength/Frequency Relationship
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-7-question-wavelength-frequency-relationship.17410/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joe Gavin — 2016-01-19T15:49:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-7-question-wavelength-frequency-relationship.17410/post-186737>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) Trying to better understand what you wrote in Time 7 based on what I researched on the wavelength/frequency relationship and Fermat/Snell :

"As the speed of light is reduced by refraction of the matter in the slower or more dense medium, the wavelength is shortened proportionately, **_but the frequency is unchanged_**; it is a characteristic of the source of the light and unaffected by medium changes. **In this way, light is powered down to become useful to a specific part of the cell at a specific time of the day when light is absent from our eye or gut."**

Standard formula is **Wavelength (lambda) x Frequency = c**, so any change in Wavelength should mean a change in Frequency to maintain the **c** equivalency

If Wavelength shortens, but Freq remains unchanged, that seems to violate the standard formula (I understand nature behaves in its own way!)

Fermat states that Light travels the Path of Least Time based on the medium it's traveling through (refraction).

The PhotoElectric Effect is hf=W+KE, with F being the Frequency.

So if I put Fermat/Snell together with PE, it appears that "frequency remains unchanged" can occur as the principles act in unison.

Am I on the right track with this understanding? I think I'm missing something ( or its just cognitive dissonance).

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-19T17:45:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-7-question-wavelength-frequency-relationship.17410/post-186745>

you are.....Snell law showed up 40 yrs before Fermat. Fermat figured his out using math.........the key for Feynman was realizing that the same principle about light could be found experimentally using geometry and math using least time..........that is why Feynman figured out how to create the sum of path technique to make sense of the photo electric interactions of QED.

Performance tuning was important back then because our processor speeds could not handle the workload. We could get by for awhile by adjusting buffers or making some code more efficient but it was always better to get a faster CPU....then everything was efficient again and you didn't have to have to tweak this process or that process and you didn't have to worry about speeding up one process but creating a bottleneck someplace else. This is kind of how I look at SNPs now. If I try to tweak my system with supplements then I may create a bottleneck someplace else. Why not give your system a faster CPU (improve redox) and let it do what it needs to do.
